div.headCT:
The
New Testament
of Our Lord and Saviour
Jesus Christ
div.p:
Translated out of the
Original Greek: and with the
Former Translations Diligently Compared
and Revised, by His Majesty’s
Special Command
Authorized King James Version

With Explanatory Notes and
Cross-References to the Standard Works
of The Church of Jesus Christ
of Latter-day Saints

Published by
The Church of Jesus Christ of Latter-day Saints
Salt Lake City, Utah, USA

div.headCT: Title: JST entitles this book “The Testimony of St. Matthew.”St Matthew
div.title: The Gospel According to

div.p: Christ is born of Mary—She conceives by the power of the Holy Ghost—Our Lord is named Jesus.

chapter.verse: The book of the generation of Jesus Christ, the son of TG Jesus Christ, Davidic Descent of.David, the son of Abraham.
Gen. 25:19.Abraham begat Isaac; and Isaac begat Jacob; and Jacob begat TG Israel, Judah, People of.Judas and his brethren;
And Judas begat Ruth 4:18; 1 Chr. 2:4 (4–15).Phares and Gen. 38:30.Zara of Thamar; and Phares begat 1 Chr. 2:9.Esrom; and Esrom begat Aram;
And Aram begat Aminadab; and Aminadab begat Naasson; and Num. 1:7.Naasson begat Salmon;
And Salmon begat Ruth 4:21.Booz of Rachab; and Booz begat Obed of Ruth; and Obed begat Jesse;
And Jesse begat David the king; and David the king begat 2 Sam. 12:24 (24–25); 1 Chr. 3:5.Solomon of her that had been the wife of Urias;
And Solomon begat Roboam; and Roboam begat Abia; and Abia begat Asa;
And Asa begat Josaphat; and Josaphat begat Joram; and Joram begat Ozias;
And Ozias begat Joatham; and Joatham begat Achaz; and Achaz begat Ezekias;
And Ezekias begat Manasses; and Manasses begat Amon; and Amon begat Josias;
And Josias begat Jechonias and his brethren, about the time they were carried away to TG Israel, Bondage of, in Other Lands.Babylon:
And after they were brought to Babylon, Jechonias begat Salathiel; and Salathiel begat 1 Chr. 3:19 (17–19); Ezra 2:2 (1–2); Hag. 1:1.Zorobabel;
And Zorobabel begat Abiud; and Abiud begat Eliakim; and Eliakim begat Azor;
And Azor begat Sadoc; and Sadoc begat Achim; and Achim begat Eliud;
And Eliud begat Eleazar; and Eleazar begat Matthan; and Matthan begat Jacob;

And Jacob begat Luke 3:23 (23–38).Joseph the husband of 1 Ne. 11:18 (14–21); Mosiah 3:8.Mary, of whom was born Luke 20:14; D&C 93:14 (3–17).Jesus, JST Matt. 1:4 … as the prophets have written, who is called Christ.who is called The Greek title “Christ” and the Hebrew title “Messiah” are synonymous, meaning “Anointed One.”Christ.

So all the generations from Abraham to David are fourteen generations; and from David until the carrying away into Babylon are fourteen generations; and from the carrying away into Babylon unto Christ are fourteen TG Book of Remembrance.generations.

¶ JST Matt. 2:1 Now, as it is written, the birth of …Now the D&C 20:1.birth of Jesus Christ was GR in this way.on this wise: When as his mother Mary was espoused to Joseph, before they came together, she was found with child of the Holy Ghost.
Then Joseph her husband, being a just man, and not willing to make her a publick TG Example.example, IE desired to release or divorce her secretly.was minded to Deut. 24:1.put her away privily.

But while he thought on these things, behold, the angel of the Lord appeared unto him in a TG Dream.dream, saying, Joseph, thou son of 2 Ne. 19:7.David, fear not to take unto thee Mary thy wife: for that which is conceived in her is of the Luke 1:35; Alma 7:10.Holy Ghost.

And she shall bring forth a son, and thou shalt call his TG Jesus Christ, Prophecies about.name Luke 2:21.
Jesus : for he shall TG Forgive; Jesus Christ, Atonement through; Jesus Christ, Mission of; Jesus Christ, Redeemer; Jesus Christ, Savior; Redemption; Salvation.save his people from their sins.
Now all this was done, that it might be fulfilled which was spoken of the Lord by the prophet, saying,

Isa. 7:14.Behold, a TG Jesus Christ, Birth of.virgin shall be with child, and shall bring forth a son, and they shall call his TG Name.name TG Jesus Christ, Prophecies about.Emmanuel, which being interpreted is, God with us.
Then Joseph being raised from sleep did as the angel of the Lord had bidden him, and took unto him his Luke 2:5.wife:
And knew her not till she had brought forth her TG Firstborn.firstborn son: and he called his name Jesus .

div.p: The wise men are directed by a star to Jesus—Joseph takes the child to Egypt—Herod slays the children in Bethlehem—Jesus is taken to Nazareth to dwell.

chapter.verse: Now when Jesus was TG Jesus Christ, Birth of.born in Bethlehem of Judæa in the days of Herod the king, behold, there came wise men from the east to Jerusalem,

Saying, JST Matt. 3:2 … Where is the child that is born, the Messiah of the Jews? …Where is he that is born Isa. 6:5; Jer. 23:5; 2 Ne. 10:14; Alma 5:50; D&C 128:22 (22–23); Moses 7:53.King of the Jews? for we have seen his Hel. 14:5 (1–2, 5); 3 Ne. 1:21.star in the east, and are come to TG Worship.worship him.
When Herod the king had heard these things, he was troubled, and all Jerusalem with him.
JST Matt. 3:4–6 (Appendix).And when he had gathered all the chief priests and scribes of the people together, he GR inquired.demanded of them where Christ should be born.
And they said unto him, In Bethlehem of Judæa: for thus it is written by the prophet,

And thou TG Jesus Christ, Prophecies about.Bethlehem, in the land of TG Israel, Judah, People of.Juda, art not the least among the princes of Juda: for out of thee shall come a 1 Chr. 5:2; Micah 5:2; John 7:42.Governor, that shall GR tend, protect, nurture.rule my people Israel.
Then Herod, when he had privily called the wise men, inquired of them diligently what time the star appeared.
And he sent them to Bethlehem, and said, Go and search diligently for the young child; and when ye have found him, bring me word again, that I may come and worship him also.
When they had heard the king, they departed; and, lo, the star, which they saw in the east, went before them, till it came and stood over where the young child was.
When they saw the star, they rejoiced with exceeding great joy.

¶ And when they were come into the house, they saw the young child with Mary his mother, and fell down, and worshipped him: and when they had opened their treasures, they presented unto him gifts; gold, and Lev. 2:1.frankincense, and myrrh.
And being warned of God in a TG Dream.dream that they should not return to Herod, they departed into their own country another way.

And when they were departed, behold, the angel of the Lord appeareth to Joseph in a dream, saying, Arise, and take the young child and his mother, and flee into Egypt, and be thou there until I bring thee word: for Herod will seek the young child to destroy him.
When he arose, he took the young child and his mother by night, and departed into Egypt:
And was there until the death of Herod: that it might be fulfilled which was spoken of the Lord by the prophet, saying, Out of TG Jesus Christ, Prophecies about.Egypt have I called my son.

¶ Then Herod, when he saw that he was GR deceived by.mocked of the wise men, GR became extremely angry.was exceeding wroth, and sent forth, and TG Tyranny.slew all the children that were in Bethlehem, and in all the GR surrounding regions.coasts thereof, from two years old and under, according to the time which he had diligently inquired of the wise men.
Then was fulfilled that which was spoken by IE Jeremiah.Jeremy the prophet, saying,
In Jer. 31:15.Rama was there a voice heard, lamentation, and weeping, and great TG Mourning.mourning, Rachel weeping for her Jer. 40:1.children, and would not be comforted, because they are not.
¶ But when Herod was dead, behold, an angel of the Lord appeareth in a JST Matt. 3:19 … vision … TG Dream.dream to Joseph in Egypt,
Saying, Arise, and take the young child and his mother, and go into the TG Israel, Land of.land of Israel: for they are dead which sought the young child’s life.
And he arose, and took the young child and his mother, and came into the land of Israel.

But when he heard that Archelaus did reign in Judæa in the GR place.room of his father Herod, he was afraid to go thither: notwithstanding, being warned of God in a TG Dream.dream, he turned aside into the parts of Galilee:

And he came and dwelt in a city called 1 Ne. 11:13.Nazareth: that it might be fulfilled which was TG Scriptures, Lost.spoken by the prophets, He shall be called a JST Matt. 3:24–26 (Appendix).Nazarene.

div.p: John the Baptist preaches in Judæa—Jesus is baptized, and the Father acclaims Him as His Beloved Son.

chapter.verse: In those days came JS—H 1:72.John the Baptist, preaching in the wilderness of Judæa,

And saying, The Greek word denotes “a change of heart or mind,” i.e., “a conversion.” Alma 7:9; 9:25 (25–26); Hel. 5:32; D&C 33:10.Repent ye: for the Luke 10:9. TG Kingdom of God, in Heaven; Kingdom of God, on Earth.kingdom of heaven GR has come. Isa. 56:1; Matt. 4:17; D&C 39:19.is at hand.

For this is he that was spoken of by the prophet See JST Luke 3:4–11 (Appendix). TG Foreordination.Esaias, saying, The Isa. 40:3; Mark 1:3; Luke 3:4; John 1:23; D&C 65:3; 88:66.voice of one crying in the wilderness, 1 Ne. 10:8 (7–10); D&C 34:6; 65:1 (1–2); 84:26.Prepare ye the way of the Lord, make his Alma 7:19 (9, 19).paths straight.
And the same John had his GR garment. See also 2 Kgs. 1:8.raiment of Zech. 13:4.camel’s hair, and a leathern girdle about his loins; and his meat was Lev. 11:22.locusts and wild honey.
Then went out to him Jerusalem, and all Judæa, and all the region round about Jordan,
And were baptized of him in Jordan, TG Confession.confessing their sins.

¶ But when he saw many of the Pharisees and Sadducees come to his See JST Matt. 9:18–21 (Appendix). Luke 7:29 (29–30).baptism, he said unto them, O GR crop of serpents. Matt. 12:34; 23:33; Alma 9:8; 10:17 (17–25).generation of vipers, who hath TG Warn.warned you to flee from the wrath to come?
JST Matt. 3:34–36 (Appendix).Bring forth therefore fruits GR appropriate to, worthy of.meet for TG Baptism, Qualifications for; Repent.repentance:
And think not to say within yourselves, We have Abraham GR as our father.to our father: for I say unto you, that God is able of these stones to raise up children unto Abraham.

And now also the axe is laid unto the root of the trees: therefore every tree which Alma 5:36 (36–41); 3 Ne. 14:19; D&C 52:18; 97:7.bringeth not forth good fruit is Luke 13:7; Jacob 5:42 (42–66).hewn down, and cast into the fire.

JST Matt. 3:38–40 (Appendix).I indeed Acts 19:3 (1–7); 2 Ne. 31:5 (5–10).baptize you with water unto TG Baptism, Qualifications for.repentance: but See JST John 1:27–34 (Appendix).he that cometh after me is mightier than I, whose shoes I am not TG Worthiness.worthy to bear: he shall TG Holy Ghost, Baptism of.baptize you with the Holy Ghost, and with D&C 19:31.fire:

Whose Jer. 51:2.fan is in his hand, and he will throughly purge his floor, and gather his wheat into the GR storehouse.garner; but he will burn up the chaff with unquenchable D&C 43:32; 63:34 (33–34); 101:66.fire.
¶ Then cometh Jesus from Galilee to Jordan unto John, to be TG Jesus Christ, Baptism of.baptized of him.
But John forbad him, saying, I have need to be baptized of thee, and comest thou to me?

JST Matt. 3:43–46 (Appendix).And Jesus answering said unto him, GR Permit it now.Suffer it to be so now: for thus it GR is fitting for us.becometh us to TG Baptism, Essential.fulfil all TG Jesus Christ, Mission of; Righteousness.righteousness. Then he suffered him.

And Jesus, when he was TG Baptism; Baptism, Immersion.baptized, went up GR immediately.straightway out of the water: and, lo, the heavens were opened unto him, and he saw the TG God, Spirit of.Spirit of God descending like a TG Holy Ghost, Dove, Sign of.dove, and lighting upon him:

And lo a Mark 9:7; Luke 9:35 (34–36). TG God, Body of, Corporeal Nature; God, Manifestations of.voice from heaven, saying, This is my Matt. 17:5; 3 Ne. 11:7; JS—H 1:17. TG Witness of the Father.beloved TG Godhead; God the Father, Elohim; Jesus Christ, Divine Sonship.Son, in whom I am well pleased.

div.p: Jesus fasts forty days and is tempted—He begins His ministry, calls disciples, and heals the sick.

chapter.verse: Then was Jesus TG Guidance, Divine.led up of the Spirit into the wilderness JST Matt. 4:1 … to be with God.to be TG Jesus Christ, Temptation of; Temptation; Test.tempted of the devil.

And when he had Mosiah 3:7. TG Fast, Fasting.fasted Ex. 24:18.forty days and forty nights, JST Matt. 4:2 … and had communed with God, he was afterwards an hungered, and was left to be tempted of the devil.he was afterward an hungred.
And when the TG Devil.tempter came to him, he said, If thou be the Son of God, command that these stones be made TG Bread.bread.

But he answered and said, It is written, Deut. 8:3; D&C 84:44 (43–44).Man shall not TG Mortality.live by TG Bread.bread alone, but by every TG Revelation.word that proceedeth out of the TG God, Body of, Corporeal Nature.mouth of God.

JST Matt. 4:5 Then Jesus was taken up into the holy city, and the Spirit setteth him on the pinnacle of the temple.Then the devil taketh him up into the Neh. 11:1.holy city, and setteth him on a pinnacle of the temple,

JST Matt. 4:6 Then the devil came unto him and said, If …And saith unto him, Matt. 27:40; D&C 20:22.If thou be the Son of God, cast thyself down: for it is written, He shall give his angels charge concerning thee: and in their hands they shall bear thee up, lest at any time thou dash thy foot against a stone.
Jesus said unto him, It is written again, Thou shalt not D&C 10:29; 24:13 (13–14). TG Jesus Christ, Temptation of; Test.tempt the Lord thy God.

JST Matt. 4:8 And again, Jesus was in the Spirit, and it taketh him …Again, the devil taketh him up into an exceeding high mountain, and sheweth him all the D&C 10:19. TG Kings, Earthly.kingdoms of the world, and the glory of them;
JST Matt. 4:9 And the devil came unto him again, and said, All …And saith unto him, All these things will I give thee, if thou wilt fall down and worship me.
Then saith Jesus unto him, Get thee hence, TG Devil.Satan: for it is written, Thou shalt TG Worship.worship the Lord thy God, and him only shalt thou serve.

Then the devil leaveth him, JST Matt. 4:11–12 And now Jesus knew that John was cast into prison, and he sent angels, and, behold, they came and ministered unto him [John]. And Jesus departed into Galilee …and, behold, TG Angels.angels came and ministered unto him.
¶ Now when Jesus had heard that John was cast into prison, he departed into Luke 4:14.Galilee;
And leaving Nazareth, he came and dwelt in Capernaum, which is upon the sea coast, in the borders of Zabulon and Nephthalim:
That it might be D&C 24:14.fulfilled which was spoken by Esaias the prophet, saying,
The land of Isa. 9:1 (1–2).Zabulon, and the land of Nephthalim, by the way of the sea, beyond Jordan, Galilee of the Gentiles;

The people which sat in D&C 57:10.darkness saw great TG Jesus Christ, Light of the World; Jesus Christ, Prophecies about.light; and to them which sat in the region and shadow of death light is sprung up.
¶ From that time Jesus began to TG Preaching.preach, and to say, TG Repent.Repent: for the TG Kingdom of God, in Heaven.kingdom of heaven GR has come. Isa. 56:1; Matt. 3:2.is at hand.
¶ And Jesus, walking by the sea of Galilee, saw two brethren, Simon called Peter, and Andrew his brother, casting a net into the sea: for they were TG Skill.fishers.

And he saith unto them, JST Matt. 4:18 … I am he of whom it is written by the prophets; follow me … TG Jesus Christ, Exemplar.Follow me, and I will make you TG Apostles; Missionary Work.fishers of men.
And they straightway left their nets, and TG Commitment.followed him.

And going on from thence, he saw other two brethren, James the son of John 21:2.Zebedee, and John his brother, in a ship with Zebedee their father, mending their nets; and he TG Called of God.called them.
And they immediately left the ship and their father, and followed him.

¶ And Jesus went about all Luke 4:44.Galilee, Matt. 9:35.teaching in their synagogues, and D&C 71:1.preaching the gospel of the kingdom, and TG Heal; Miracle.healing all manner of TG Sickness.sickness and all manner of disease among the JST Matt. 4:22 … people which believed on his name.people.

And his fame went throughout all Syria: and they brought unto him all sick people that were taken with divers diseases and torments, and those which were possessed with Matt. 8:16 (16, 28, 33); 9:32 (32–34).devils, and those which were lunatic, and those that had the GR paralysis.palsy; and he Mosiah 3:5 (5–6).healed them.
And there followed him great multitudes of people from Galilee, and from Decapolis, and from Jerusalem, and from Judæa, and from beyond Jordan.

div.p: Jesus preaches the Sermon on the Mount—Its teachings replace and transcend some aspects of the law of Moses—All are commanded to be perfect like their Father in Heaven.

chapter.verse: 3 Ne. 12:1 (1–48).And seeing the multitudes, he went up into a mountain: and when he was set, his disciples came unto him:
And he opened his mouth, and taught them, saying,

The Latin beatus is the basis of the English “beatitude,” meaning “to be fortunate,” “to be happy,” or “to be blessed.” TG Blessing.Blessed are the IE poor in pride, humble in spirit; 3 Ne. 12:3 reads “… the poor in spirit who come unto me.” James 2:5 (1–9); D&C 56:18 (18–20); 88:17. TG Contrite Heart; Humility; Poor in Spirit.poor in spirit: for theirs is the TG Kingdom of God, in Heaven.kingdom of heaven.
Blessed are they that TG Mourning; Sorrow.mourn: for they shall be TG Comfort.comforted.
Blessed are the GR gentle, forgiving, or benevolent; the Hebrew in Ps. 37:11 characterizes as the humble those who have suffered. TG Meek.meek: for they shall inherit the TG Earth, Destiny of.earth.
Blessed are they which do 3 Ne. 12:6. TG Teachable.hunger and thirst after TG Good Works; Righteousness.righteousness: for they shall be filled.
Blessed are the TG Mercy.merciful: for they shall obtain mercy.
Blessed are the TG Chastity; Cleanliness; Purity.pure in TG Heart.heart: for they shall TG God, Presence of; God, Privilege of Seeing.see God.
Blessed are the TG Contentment; Peace; Peacemakers.peacemakers: for they shall be called the TG Sons and Daughters of God.children of God.
Blessed are they which are TG Affliction; Persecution.persecuted for TG Righteousness.righteousness’ sake: for D&C 6:37; 10:55.theirs is the kingdom of heaven.
Blessed are ye, when men shall TG Malice; Reviling.revile you, and persecute you, and shall say all manner of TG Backbiting; Slander.evil against you falsely, GR on account of me.for my sake.
2 Ne. 9:18. TG Joy.Rejoice, and be exceeding glad: for great is your D&C 84:38. TG Reward.reward in heaven: for so TG Persecution.persecuted they the prophets which were before you.

¶ Ye are the See Lev. 2:13 and Num. 18:19, where salt is a token of the covenant and was part of sacrificial ritual. TG Salt.salt of the earth: but if the salt have lost his savour, wherewith shall it be salted? it is thenceforth good for nothing, but to be cast out, and to be trodden under foot of men.
Ye are the TG Mission of Early Saints.light of the world. A city that is set on an hill cannot be hid.
Neither do men light a Luke 11:33 (33–34).candle, and put it under a bushel, but on a candlestick; and it giveth light unto all that are in the house.

Let your 3 Ne. 18:24. TG Children of Light; Example; Light [noun].light so shine before men, that they may see your good 1 Pet. 2:12. TG Good Works.works, and John 15:8.glorify your Father which is in heaven.
¶ Think not that I am come to D&C 10:52 (52, 54).destroy the TG Law of Moses.law, or the prophets: I am not come to destroy, but to fulfil.
For verily I say unto you, Till heaven and earth pass, one jot or one tittle shall in no wise pass from the TG Law of Moses.law, till all be D&C 1:38.fulfilled.

JST Matt. 5:21 (Appendix).Whosoever therefore shall TG Sin.break one of these least commandments, and shall 2 Ne. 28:15 (12, 15).teach men so, he shall be called the least in the kingdom of heaven: but whosoever shall do and TG Missionary Work.teach them, the same shall be called great in the kingdom of heaven.
For I say unto you, That except your TG Righteousness.righteousness shall exceed the righteousness of the TG Scribe.scribes and Pharisees, ye shall in no case enter into the kingdom of heaven.

¶ Ye have heard that it was said by them of old time, Thou TG Commandments of God.shalt not TG Blood, Shedding of; Murder.kill; and whosoever shall kill shall be GR subject to condemnation.in danger of the judgment:

But I say unto you, That whosoever is Prov. 29:22 (21–23); 3 Ne. 12:22 (21–24). TG Anger; Rashness.angry with his brother JST Matt. 5:24 and 3 Ne. 12:22 omit the words “without a cause.”without a cause shall be GR subject to condemnation.in danger of the judgment: and whosoever shall say to his brother, A word suggesting contempt or derision in both Aramaic and Greek.Raca, shall be IE subject to the Sanhedrin.in danger of the council: but whosoever shall say, Thou fool, shall be in danger of hell fire.
Therefore if thou bring thy gift to the altar, and there rememberest that thy brother hath ought against thee;
Leave there thy gift before the altar, and go thy way; first be Matt. 18:15 (15–18). TG Forgive; Reconciliation.reconciled to thy brother, and then come and offer thy gift.

GR Quickly have kind thoughts for, or be well disposed toward. TG Communication; Disputations.Agree with thine adversary quickly, whiles thou art in the way with him; lest at any time the adversary deliver thee to the judge, and the judge deliver thee to the officer, and thou be cast into prison.
Verily I say unto thee, Thou shalt by no means come out thence, till thou hast paid GR the last penny.the uttermost farthing.
¶ Ye have heard that it was said by them of old time, Thou shalt not commit D&C 42:22 (22–26).adultery:

But I say unto you, That whosoever D&C 63:16 (16–17).looketh on a TG Body, Sanctity of; Woman.woman to Mosiah 4:30 (29–30). TG Carnal Mind; Chastity; Fornication; Lust; Motivations; Sensuality.lust after her hath committed TG Adulterer; Sexual Immorality.adultery with her already in his heart.

And if thy right eye GR cause to stumble; see also Matt. 18:6–9. TG Offense.offend thee, pluck it out, and cast it from thee: for it is profitable for thee that one of thy members should perish, and not that thy whole body should be cast into TG Damnation; Hell.hell.

And if thy right Matt. 18:8; JST Matt. 18:9 (Matt. 18:9 note a).hand offend thee, cut it off, and cast it from thee: for it is profitable for thee that one of thy members should perish, and not that thy whole body should be cast into JST Matt. 5:33–34 … hell. And now this I speak, a parable concerning your sins; wherefore, cast them from you, that ye may not be hewn down and cast into the fire.hell.
It hath been said, Whosoever shall put away his wife, let him give her a writing of TG Divorce.divorcement:

But I say unto you, That whosoever shall Ezek. 44:22.put away his D&C 42:74.wife, saving for the cause of 1 Cor. 7:10 (10–11). TG Fornication.fornication, causeth her to commit adultery: and whosoever shall marry her that is divorced committeth adultery.

¶ Again, ye have heard that it hath been said by them of old time, Thou shalt not GR break your oath, or perjure yourself. Ps. 139:20.forswear thyself, but shalt TG Dedication.perform unto the Lord thine Deut. 23:21. TG Oath.oaths:
But I say unto you, TG Honesty; Profanity; Swearing.Swear not at all; neither by heaven; for it is God’s Isa. 66:1.throne:
Nor by the earth; for it is his Isa. 60:13; Ezek. 43:7.footstool: neither by Jerusalem; for it is the Ps. 48:2.city of the great King.
Neither shalt thou swear by thy head, because thou canst not make one hair white or black.
But let your TG Communication.communication be, Yea, yea; Nay, nay: for whatsoever is more than these cometh GR from the evil one.of evil.
¶ Ye have heard that it hath been said, An Lev. 24:20; 3 Ne. 12:38 (38–40). TG Punish.eye for an eye, and a tooth for a tooth:
But I say unto you, That ye resist not TG Evil.evil: but whosoever shall smite thee on thy right Lam. 3:30. TG Patience.cheek, TG Forbear.turn to him the other also.
And if any man will sue thee at the law, and take away thy coat, let him have thy cloak also.
And whosoever shall compel thee to go a mile, go with him twain.
TG Almsgiving; Generosity; Poor.Give to him that asketh thee, and from him that would TG Borrow; Debt.borrow of thee turn not thou away.
¶ Ye have heard that it hath been said, Thou shalt D&C 112:11.love thy TG Neighbor.neighbour, and hate thine enemy.

But I say unto you, TG Love.Love your TG Enemies; Opposition.enemies, TG Retribution.bless them that TG Curse.curse you, do TG Benevolence.good to them that TG Hate.hate you, and Job 42:10; Acts 7:60 (55–60). TG Prayer.pray for them which despitefully use you, and TG Persecution.persecute you;

That ye GR may become; see Mosiah 5:7.may be the TG Sons and Daughters of God.children of your Father which is in heaven: for he maketh his sun to rise on the evil and on the good, and sendeth Job 2:10; 2 Ne. 2:11; Alma 60:13; D&C 5:22; 29:39; 122:7 (5–9).rain on the just and on the unjust.
For if ye Prov. 19:6.love them which love you, what reward have ye? do not even the publicans the same?
And if ye salute your brethren only, what do ye more than others? do not even the publicans so?

JST Matt. 5:50 Ye are therefore commanded to be perfect …Be ye therefore GR complete, finished, fully developed. D&C 67:13. TG God, the Standard of Righteousness; Godliness; Man, New, Spiritually Reborn; Man, Potential to Become like Heavenly Father; Mission of Early Saints; Objectives; Perfection.perfect, even as your TG God the Father, Elohim.Father which is in heaven is TG God, Perfection of.perfect.

div.p: Jesus continues the Sermon on the Mount—He teaches the disciples the Lord’s Prayer—They are commanded to seek first the kingdom of God and His righteousness.

chapter.verse: JST Matt. 6:1 And it came to pass that, as Jesus taught his disciples, he said unto them, Take heed … 3 Ne. 13:1 (1–34).Take heed that ye do not your GR righteousness, acts of religious devotion. TG Almsgiving; Generosity; Motivations; Poor.alms before men, to be seen of them: otherwise ye have no reward of your Father which is in heaven.

Therefore when thou doest thine alms, do not sound a trumpet before thee, as the GR pretenders; the Greek word means “a play actor,” or “one who feigns, represents dramatically, or exaggerates a part.” TG Hypocrisy.hypocrites do in the synagogues and in the streets, that they may have D&C 121:35 (34–35).glory of men. Verily I say unto you, They have their reward.
But when thou doest alms, let not thy left hand know what thy right hand doeth:
That thine TG Welfare.alms may be in secret: and thy Father which seeth in secret himself shall Luke 14:14.reward thee openly.

¶ And when thou prayest, thou shalt not be as the Alma 31:14 (14–22).hypocrites are: for they love to pray standing in the synagogues and in the corners of the streets, that they may be seen of men. Verily I say unto you, They have their reward.

But thou, when thou prayest, enter into thy Alma 33:7 (4–11); 34:26 (17–27).closet, and when thou hast 2 Kgs. 4:33.shut thy door, TG Prayer.pray to thy Father which is in TG Meditation.secret; and thy Father which TG God, Omniscience of.seeth in secret shall TG Reward.reward thee openly.
But when ye pray, use not vain Eccl. 5:2; Alma 31:20; 3 Ne. 19:24.repetitions, as the TG Heathen.heathen do: for they think that they shall be heard for their much speaking.
Be not ye therefore like unto them: for your Father D&C 84:83 (81–86). TG God, Intelligence of; God, Omniscience of.knoweth what things ye have Ps. 23:1.need of, before ye ask him.

IE It is in this way that you ought to pray.After this manner therefore TG Prayer.pray ye: Our TG God the Father, Elohim.Father which art in heaven, GR Let Thy name be sanctified.Hallowed be thy TG Name.name.
Thy TG Kingdom of God, on Earth; Millennium.kingdom come. Thy TG God, Will of.will be done GR on earth.in earth, as it is in heaven.
Give us this day our daily TG Bread.bread.
And forgive us our GR debts, offenses, faults, or sins. TG Debt.debts, as we TG Forgive.forgive our debtors.

JST Matt. 6:14 And suffer us not to be led into temptation …And Syriac: do not let us enter into temptation.lead us not into TG Temptation; Test.temptation, GR but protect us from the evil one. TG Deliver; Evil.but deliver us from evil: For thine is the kingdom, and the power, and the 1 Chr. 29:11. TG Glory.glory, for ever. Amen.
For if ye TG Forbear.forgive men their trespasses, your heavenly Father will also forgive you:
But if ye TG Forgive.forgive not men their trespasses, neither will your Father forgive your trespasses.

¶ Moreover when ye fast, be not, as the hypocrites, of a sad countenance: for they disfigure their faces, that they may appear unto men to Zech. 7:5 (5–6).fast. Verily I say unto you, They GR receive.have their reward.
But thou, when thou fastest, anoint thine head, and wash thy face;
That thou appear not unto men to TG Fast, Fasting; Motivations.fast, but unto thy Father which is in secret: and thy Father, which seeth in secret, shall TG Reward.reward thee openly.
¶ Lay not up for yourselves TG Family, Managing Finances in.treasures upon earth, where moth and rust doth corrupt, and where thieves GR dig through (as an earthen wall).break through and steal:
But lay up for yourselves Heb. 10:34; 1 Pet. 1:4 (1–16). TG Treasure.treasures in heaven, where neither moth nor rust doth corrupt, and where thieves do not break through nor TG Stealing.steal:
For where your treasure is, there will your heart be also.

The light of the body is the eye: if therefore thine 3 Ne. 13:22; Morm. 8:15; D&C 4:5; 59:1 (1–2).eye be GR healthy, sincere, without guile. JST Matt. 6:22 … single to the glory of God … TG Dedication.single, thy whole body shall be full of D&C 93:28 (28, 36–38).light.
But if thine eye be evil, thy whole body shall be full of darkness. If therefore the light that is in thee be darkness, how great is that TG Darkness, Spiritual.darkness!

¶ TG Loyalty.No man can TG Service.serve two Alma 5:39 (39–42).masters: for either he will TG Hate.hate the one, and love the other; or else he will hold to the one, and despise the other. Ye cannot serve God and TG Idolatry; Treasure; Worldliness.mammon.

JST Matt. 6:25–27 (Appendix).Therefore I say unto you, Take no GR anxious concern. Ps. 55:22; Prov. 16:3; Luke 12:22; Philip. 4:6; 3 Ne. 13:25; D&C 84:81. TG Self-Sacrifice.thought for your life, what ye shall eat, or what ye shall drink; nor yet for your body, what ye shall put on. Is not the life more than meat, and the body than raiment?
Behold the fowls of the air: for they sow not, neither do they reap, nor gather into barns; yet your heavenly Father feedeth them. Are ye not much better than they?
Which of you by taking thought can add one cubit unto his stature?
And why take ye thought for raiment? TG Meditation.Consider the lilies of the field, how they grow; they toil not, neither do they spin:
And yet I say unto you, That even Solomon in all his glory was not arrayed like one of these.

Wherefore, if God so clothe the grass of the field, which to day is, and to morrow is cast into the oven, JST Matt. 6:34 … how much more will he not provide for you, if ye are not of little faith? shall he not much more clothe you, O ye of little TG Faith.faith?
Therefore take no thought, saying, What shall we eat? or, What shall we drink? or, Wherewithal shall we be clothed?
(For after all these things do the Gentiles seek:) for your heavenly Father Hel. 8:8; D&C 84:83.knoweth that ye have need of all these things.

JST Matt. 6:38 Wherefore, seek not the things of this world but seek ye first to build up the kingdom of God, and to establish his righteousness …But 1 Kgs. 3:13; Alma 39:14 (12–14); D&C 6:7 (6–7); 11:23; 68:31 (31–32); 106:3. TG Commitment; Dedication; Priesthood, Magnifying Callings within.seek ye first the Col. 3:2. TG Mission of Early Saints; Objectives.kingdom of God, and his Prov. 21:21. TG God, the Standard of Righteousness; Righteousness.righteousness; and all these Prov. 28:10. TG Blessing.things shall be 2 Ne. 5:11 (10–13); Mosiah 2:41. TG Abundant Life.added unto you.
Take therefore no thought for the morrow: for the morrow shall take D&C 84:84.thought for the things of itself. Sufficient unto the day is the evil thereof.

div.p: Jesus concludes the Sermon on the Mount—He commands, Judge not; ask of God; beware of false prophets—He promises salvation to those who do the will of the Father.

chapter.verse: JST Matt. 7:1–2 Now these are the words which Jesus taught his disciples that they should say unto the people. Judge not unrighteously, that ye be not judged; but judge righteous judgment. Alma 41:14 (14–15); 3 Ne. 14:1 (1–27); D&C 11:12.Judge not, that ye be not TG Accountability.judged.
For with what TG Judgment.judgment ye judge, ye shall be judged: and with what Prov. 11:25; D&C 1:10.measure ye mete, it shall be Esth. 7:10; 1 Ne. 14:3; D&C 10:26 (25–27).measured to you again.

JST Matt. 7:4–8 (Appendix).And why beholdest thou the GR speck, chip, or splinter. TG Gossip.mote that is in thy brother’s eye, but considerest not the The Greek word refers to a wooden beam used in constructing houses.beam that is in thine own eye?
Or how wilt thou say to thy brother, Let me pull out the mote out of thine eye; and, behold, a beam is in thine own eye?
Thou TG Hypocrisy.hypocrite, first cast out the beam out of thine own eye; and then shalt thou see clearly to cast out the mote out of thy brother’s eye.

¶ JST Matt. 7:9–11 (Appendix).Give not that which is TG Holiness; Sacred; Sacrilege.holy unto the dogs, neither cast ye your Prov. 23:9; Matt. 15:26 (26–28); D&C 6:12 (10–12); 10:37 (36–37); 41:6.pearls before swine, lest they trample them under their feet, and turn again and rend you.

¶ JST Matt. 7:12–17 (Appendix). Isa. 58:9 (8–9); Hel. 10:5 (4–11); D&C 6:5. TG Faith; God, Access to; Prayer; Problem-Solving.Ask, and it shall be TG Revelation.given you; TG Meditation; Objectives; Study.seek, and ye shall find; TG Learn.knock, and it shall be opened unto you:
For every one that asketh receiveth; and he that TG Education.seeketh findeth; and to him that knocketh it shall be opened.
Or what man is there of you, whom if his son ask TG Bread.bread, will he give him a stone?
Or if he ask a fish, will he give him a serpent?
If ye then, GR although you are wicked.being evil, know how to give good gifts unto your children, how much more shall your Father which is in heaven give good things to them that ask him?

Therefore all things TG Good Works.whatsoever ye would that TG Marriage, Continuing Courtship in.men should Deut. 15:15 (12–15); Prov. 24:29. TG Citizenship; Courtesy; Kindness.do to you, TG Benevolence.do ye even so to them: for this is the TG Law of Moses.law and the prophets.

¶ Enter ye in at the GR narrow; see also 2 Ne. 31:17–21.strait Luke 13:24; 3 Ne. 27:33; D&C 22:4 (1–4); 43:7.gate: for wide is the gate, and broad is the way, that leadeth to D&C 132:25. TG Hell.destruction, and many there be which go in thereat:
Because Mosiah 3:17.strait is the 1 Ne. 8:20 (19–24); 2 Ne. 33:9. TG Baptism, Essential.gate, and narrow is the way, which leadeth unto Jer. 21:8.life, and few there be that find it.
¶ Beware of TG False Prophets.false prophets, which come to you in TG Sheep.sheep’s clothing, but TG Hypocrisy.inwardly they are ravening TG Guile.wolves.

Ye shall GR recognize, detect. TG Discernment, Spiritual.know them by their Prov. 20:11; Jer. 28:9 (8–9); Luke 6:44; Moro. 7:6 (5–17). TG Good Works.fruits. Do men gather grapes of thorns, or figs of thistles?

Even so every good tree bringeth forth The Greek wording carries the meaning of beautiful, precious fruit without blemish.good Matt. 12:33; 3 Ne. 14:20 (17–20).fruit; but a GR decayed, rotten, stale.corrupt tree bringeth forth GR bad, spoiled, degenerate.evil fruit.
A good tree cannot bring forth evil fruit, neither can a corrupt tree bring forth good fruit.
Every tree that bringeth not forth good Luke 3:9; John 15:2 (1–6); Jacob 5:26 (26–60); Alma 5:36; D&C 97:7.fruit is Luke 13:7.hewn down, and cast into the fire.
Wherefore by their James 2:18 (14–26); 1 Jn. 3:10 (10–18).fruits ye shall know them.

¶ Not every one that Titus 1:16. TG Hypocrisy; Sincere.saith unto me, Hosea 8:2 (1–4); Luke 6:46.Lord, Lord, shall enter into the kingdom of heaven; but he that Luke 8:21 (9–21); D&C 138:4. TG Duty; Good Works; Obedience.doeth the TG God, Will of.will of my Father which is in JST Matt. 7:30–31 … heaven. For the day soon cometh, that men shall come before me to judgment, to be judged according to their works.heaven.

Many will say to me in that day, Lord, Lord, have we not Jer. 23:25 (25–32). TG Unrighteous Dominion.prophesied in thy name? and in thy D&C 84:67. TG Name.name have cast out devils? and in thy name done many wonderful works?

JST Matt. 7:33 And then will I say, Ye never knew me … Ps. 101:4; Matt. 25:12; Mosiah 26:27 (23–27); 3 Ne. 14:23.And then will I profess unto them, I never knew you: Ps. 119:115. TG Judgment, the Last.depart from me, ye that work GR lawlessness. TG Sin.iniquity.

¶ Therefore whosoever Mosiah 4:10; Hel. 5:12 (9–12); D&C 41:5.heareth these sayings of mine, and TG Commitment; Obedience.doeth them, GR he will resemble.I will liken him unto a TG Wisdom.wise man, which D&C 6:34; 11:24.built his house upon GR the rock. 3 Ne. 11:39 (39–40).a rock:
And the D&C 90:5.rain descended, and the floods came, and the winds blew, and TG Adversity.beat upon that house; and it fell not: for it was founded upon a TG Rock.rock.
And every one that heareth these sayings of mine, and doeth them not, shall be likened unto a TG Foolishness.foolish man, which built his house upon the sand:
And the rain descended, and the floods came, and the winds blew, and beat upon that Prov. 14:11.house; and it TG Apostasy of Individuals.fell: and great was the fall of it.
And it came to pass, when Jesus had ended JST Matt. 7:36 … these sayings with his disciples, the people …these sayings, the people were Matt. 13:54.astonished at his John 7:16; 2 Ne. 31:21.doctrine:

For he taught them as one having JST Matt. 7:37 … authority from God, and not as having authority from the scribes. TG Jesus Christ, Authority of; Jesus Christ, Teaching Mode of; Teaching with the Spirit.authority, and not as GR their scribes. TG Scribe.the scribes.

div.p: Jesus heals a leper, cures the centurion’s servant and others, stills the tempest, and casts out devils—The devils enter a herd of swine.

chapter.verse: When he was come down from the mountain, great multitudes followed him.
And, behold, there came a TG Leprosy.leper and worshipped him, saying, Lord, if thou wilt, thou canst make me clean.
And Jesus put forth his hand, and touched him, saying, I will; be thou clean. And immediately his leprosy was TG Miracle.cleansed.
And Jesus saith unto him, See thou tell no man; but go thy way, shew thyself to the priest, and offer the gift that Moses commanded, for a testimony unto them.
¶ And when Jesus was entered into Capernaum, there came unto him a centurion, beseeching him,
And saying, Lord, my GR child, servant, son; see John 4:43–54.servant lieth at home sick of the GR paralysis.palsy, grievously tormented.
And Jesus saith unto him, I will come and heal him.
The centurion answered and said, Lord, I am not GR fit, qualified.worthy that thou shouldest come under my roof: but speak the word only, and my servant shall be TG Heal.healed.
For I am a man under TG Authority.authority, having soldiers under me: and I say to this man, Go, and he goeth; and to another, Come, and he cometh; and to my servant, Do this, and he doeth it.

JST Matt. 8:9 And when they that followed him, heard this, they marveled. And when Jesus …When Jesus heard it, he marvelled, and said to them that followed, Verily I say unto you, I have not found so great 3 Ne. 17:8 (5–20).faith, no, not in Israel.

And I say unto you, That Luke 13:29 (28–30); Acts 10:45; 2 Ne. 10:18 (9–18); D&C 45:9.many shall come from the east and west, and shall sit down with Abraham, and Isaac, and Jacob, in the kingdom of heaven.

But the 2 Ne. 30:2.children of the kingdom shall be cast out into Matt. 22:13 (1–14); D&C 77:8; 133:72.outer darkness: there shall be Isa. 65:14; Matt. 13:42; Rev. 19:20; D&C 112:24.weeping and gnashing of teeth.
And Jesus said unto the centurion, Go thy way; and as thou hast believed, so be it done unto thee. And his servant was healed in the selfsame hour.
¶ And when Jesus was come into Peter’s house, he saw his 1 Cor. 9:5; 1 Tim. 4:3.wife’s mother GR lying sick and feverish.laid, and sick of a fever.
And he GR took hold of.touched her hand, and the fever left her: and she arose, and ministered unto them.

¶ When the even was come, they brought unto him many that were possessed with Matt. 4:24; 9:32 (32–34).devils: and he cast out the TG Spirits, Evil or Unclean.spirits with his word, and TG Heal.healed all that were sick:
That it might be fulfilled which was spoken by Esaias the prophet, saying, GR He Himself.Himself took our TG Jesus Christ, Atonement through.infirmities, and bare our TG Sickness.sicknesses.
¶ Now when Jesus saw great multitudes about him, he gave commandment to depart unto the other side.
And a certain scribe came, and said unto him, Master, I will Luke 9:57; 2 Ne. 31:10 (10–13); Moro. 7:11; D&C 4:3; 56:2.follow thee whithersoever thou goest.
And Jesus saith unto him, The foxes have holes, and the birds of the air have nests; but the Son of man hath not where to lay his head.
And another of his disciples said unto him, Lord, GR let, allow.suffer me first to go and bury my father.
But Jesus said unto him, TG Commitment.Follow me; and let the TG Death.dead bury their dead.
¶ And when he was entered into a ship, his disciples followed him.
And, behold, there arose a great Jonah 1:4; 1 Ne. 18:13 (9–13).tempest in the sea, insomuch that the ship was covered with the waves: but he was asleep.
And his disciples came to him, and awoke him, saying, Lord, save us: we perish.

And he saith unto them, Why are ye TG Courage; Fearful.fearful, O ye of little faith? Then he arose, and Ps. 65:7; 89:9; 107:29. TG God, Power of.rebuked the winds and the sea; and there was a great calm.
But the men marvelled, saying, What manner of man is this, that even the winds and the Hel. 12:16 (8–17).sea obey him!

¶ And when he was come to the other side into the country of the Gergesenes, there met him two possessed with TG Spirits, Evil or Unclean.devils, coming out of the tombs, exceeding fierce, so that no man might pass by that way.
And, behold, they cried out, saying, What have we to do with thee, Jesus, thou Son of God? art thou come hither to torment us before GR the appointed hour.the time?
And there was a good way off from them an herd of many swine feeding.
So the devils besought him, saying, If thou cast us out, suffer us to go away into the herd of swine.

And he said unto them, Go. And when they were come out, they went into the herd of swine: and, behold, the whole herd of swine ran violently down a steep place into the sea, and perished in the waters.
And they that kept them fled, and went their ways into the city, and told every thing, and what was befallen to the possessed of the devils.
And, behold, the whole city came out to John 12:18.meet Jesus: and when they saw him, they besought him that he would depart out of their GR lands, regions.coasts.

div.p: Jesus forgives sins, heals a paralytic, and calls Matthew—Jesus eats with sinners, heals a woman who touches His garments, and raises Jairus’s daughter to life—He opens the eyes of the blind, casts out a devil, and preaches the gospel.

chapter.verse: And he entered into a ship, and passed over, and came into his own city.

And, behold, they brought to him a man sick of the GR stroke, paralysis.palsy, lying on a bed: and Jesus seeing their Luke 7:50.faith said unto the sick of the palsy; Son, be of good D&C 68:6; 112:4.cheer; thy D&C 25:3; 62:3.sins be forgiven thee.
And, behold, certain of the scribes said within themselves, This man blasphemeth.
And Jesus TG God, Omniscience of.knowing their thoughts said, Wherefore think ye evil in your hearts?
JST Matt. 9:5 For is it not easier to say, Thy sins be forgiven thee, than to say, Arise and walk?For whether is easier, to say, Thy sins be forgiven thee; or to say, Arise, and walk?

But that ye may know that the TG Jesus Christ, Son of Man.Son of man hath GR authority. TG Jesus Christ, Authority of.power on earth to TG Forgive.forgive sins, (then saith he to the sick of the palsy,) Arise, take up thy bed, and go unto thine house.
And he arose, and departed to his house.
But when the multitudes saw it, they marvelled, and glorified God, which had given such power unto men.

¶ And as Jesus passed forth from thence, he saw a man, named Luke 5:27 (27–32).Matthew, sitting at the GR tax office.receipt of custom: and he saith unto him, Follow me. And he arose, and followed him.
¶ And it came to pass, as Jesus sat at meat in the house, behold, many publicans and sinners came and sat down with him and his disciples.
And when the Pharisees saw it, they said unto his disciples, See JST Mark 3:21–25 (Appendix). Isa. 53:3 (1–3); 65:5.Why eateth your Master with publicans and sinners?
But when Jesus heard that, he said unto them, They that be whole need not a physician, but they that are sick.

But go ye and learn what that meaneth, Hosea 6:6.I GR desire.will have TG Mercy.mercy, and not sacrifice: for I am not come to 1 Tim. 1:15.call the TG Righteousness.righteous, but sinners to TG Repent.repentance.
¶ Then came to him the disciples of John, saying, Why do we and the Pharisees fast oft, but thy disciples fast not?

And Jesus said unto them, Can the children of the bridechamber mourn, as long as the bridegroom is with them? but the days will come, when the bridegroom shall be taken from them, TG Fast, Fasting.and then shall they fast.
JST Matt. 9:18–21 (Appendix).No man putteth a piece of GR unshrunken.new cloth unto an old garment, for that which is put in to fill it up taketh from the garment, and the rent is made worse.
Neither do men put new wine into old bottles: else the bottles break, and the wine runneth out, and the bottles perish: but they put new wine into new bottles, and both are preserved.

¶ While he spake these things unto them, behold, there came a certain Luke 8:41.ruler, and worshipped him, saying, JST Matt. 9:24 … My daughter is even now dying …My daughter is even now dead: but come and lay thy TG Administrations to the Sick; Hands, Laying on of.hand upon her, and she shall D&C 66:9.live.
And Jesus arose, and followed him, and so did his disciples.
¶ And, behold, a woman, which was diseased with an GR hemorrhage.issue of Lev. 15:25.blood twelve years, came behind him, and touched the hem of his garment:
For she said within herself, If I may but touch his garment, I shall be GR free from disease.whole.
But Jesus turned him about, and when he saw her, he said, Daughter, be of good comfort; thy faith hath made thee TG Heal.whole. And the woman was made whole from that hour.
And when Jesus came into the ruler’s house, and saw the minstrels and the people making a noise,
He said unto them, Give place: for the maid is not dead, but sleepeth. And they GR ridiculed Him. 1 Ne. 8:27 (26–27). TG Laughter.laughed him to scorn.
But when the people were put forth, he went in, and took her by the hand, and the maid arose.
And the fame hereof went abroad into all that land.
¶ And when Jesus departed thence, two blind men followed him, crying, and saying, Thou Son of David, have mercy on us.

And when he was come into the house, the Matt. 20:30 (30–34); John 9:1 (1–4); Mosiah 3:5; 3 Ne. 17:9 (7–10); D&C 84:69.blind men came to him: and Jesus saith unto them, GR Do you have faith that.Believe ye that I am able to do this? They said unto him, Yea, Lord.
Then touched he their eyes, saying, According to your TG Faith.faith be it unto you.
And their Ps. 146:8 (1–10).eyes were TG Heal; Miracle.opened; and Jesus GR warned them sternly.straitly charged them, saying, See that no man know it.
But they, when they were departed, Mark 7:36.spread abroad his John 12:19.fame in all that country.
¶ As they went out, behold, they brought to him a dumb man possessed with a Matt. 4:24; 8:16 (16, 28, 33).devil.
And when the devil was cast out, the dumb spake: and the multitudes marvelled, saying, It was never so seen in Israel.
But the Pharisees said, He casteth out devils through the prince of the devils.

And Jesus went about all the cities and villages, Matt. 4:23.teaching in their synagogues, and preaching the D&C 84:80 (79–80); JS—M 1:31.gospel of the kingdom, and Mosiah 3:5.healing every sickness and every disease among the people.

¶ But when he saw the multitudes, he was moved with TG Compassion.compassion on them, because GR they were harassed.they fainted, and were scattered abroad, as TG Sheep.sheep having no TG Jesus Christ, Good Shepherd; Shepherd.shepherd.
Then saith he unto his disciples, The Luke 10:2. TG Harvest.harvest truly is plenteous, but the Jacob 5:70.labourers are few;
Pray ye therefore the Lord of the harvest, that he will send forth labourers into his harvest.

div.p: Jesus instructs and empowers the Twelve Apostles and sends them forth to preach, minister, and heal the sick—Those who receive the Twelve receive the Lord.

chapter.verse: And when he had 1 Ne. 12:7 (6–7); Moro. 2:2 (1–3). TG Delegation of Responsibility.called unto him his TG Apostles.twelve disciples, he gave them GR authority over. D&C 50:32. TG Authority; Jesus Christ, Power of; Priesthood, Power of.power against TG Spirits, Evil or Unclean; Uncleanness.unclean spirits, to cast them out, and to TG Heal.heal all manner of TG Sickness.sickness and all manner of disease.
Now the names of the twelve apostles are these; The first, Simon, who is called Peter, and Andrew his brother; James the son of Zebedee, and John his brother;
Philip, and Bartholomew; Thomas, and Matthew the GR tax collector.publican; James the son of Alphæus, and Lebbæus, whose surname was Thaddæus;
Simon the Canaanite, and Judas Iscariot, who also TG Jesus Christ, Betrayal of.betrayed him.
These twelve Jesus 3 Ne. 28:34; D&C 107:35.sent forth, and commanded them, saying, Go not into the way of the Gentiles, and into any city of the Luke 9:52.Samaritans enter ye not:
But go rather to the TG Missionary Work.lost TG Sheep.sheep of the house of Israel.
And as ye go, preach, saying, The kingdom of heaven GR has come.is at hand.
Luke 9:2; D&C 84:68.Heal the sick, cleanse the TG Leprosy.lepers, raise the dead, D&C 84:67.cast out devils: GR without payment. Isa. 55:1.freely ye have received, freely TG Generosity.give.
Provide neither gold, nor silver, nor brass in your Luke 10:4; 22:35 (35–36); D&C 24:18; 84:78.purses,
Nor GR traveling bag or beggar’s bag. Mark 6:8.scrip for your journey, neither two coats, neither shoes, nor yet staves: for the workman is 1 Cor. 9:14.worthy of his Num. 18:31; Luke 10:7.meat.
And into whatsoever city or town ye shall D&C 24:15.enter, inquire who in it is worthy; and there abide till ye go thence.
And when ye come into an house, salute it.
And if the house be worthy, let your peace come upon it: but if it be not worthy, let your peace return to you.

And whosoever shall not receive you, nor hear your words, when ye depart out of that house or city, shake off the Luke 10:11 (11–12); Acts 13:51; D&C 75:20 (19–22).dust of your Acts 18:6 (5–6); D&C 60:15; 84:92 (92–93).feet.
Verily I say unto you, It shall be more tolerable for the land of Ezek. 16:48.Sodom and Gomorrha in the day of judgment, than for that city.
¶ Behold, I send you forth as Luke 10:3.sheep in the midst of wolves: be ye therefore Jacob 6:12; Alma 18:22; Morm. 9:28; D&C 111:11.wise as serpents, and GR guileless.harmless as doves.
But beware of men: for they will deliver you up to the councils, and they will scourge you in their synagogues;
And ye shall be brought before governors and Ps. 119:46; Acts 9:15; D&C 1:23; 124:3 (3, 16, 107).kings GR on account of me.for my sake, for a GR witness to.testimony against them and the Gentiles.

But when they deliver you up, GR do not be anxiously concerned. Dan. 3:16; Acts 20:24.take no thought how or what ye shall speak: for it shall be given you in that same hour what ye shall Ex. 4:12 (12–15); Mark 13:11; Luke 21:15 (14–15); D&C 24:6. TG Teaching.speak.
For it is not ye that speak, but the TG God, Spirit of; Teaching with the Spirit.Spirit of your Father which speaketh in you.
And the brother shall deliver up the brother to death, and the father the child: and the children shall rise up against their parents, and cause them to be put to death.

And ye shall be 1 Ne. 22:5. TG Hate; Martyrdom; Persecution.hated of all men GR because of my name.for my TG Name.name’s sake: but he that Mark 13:13; 3 Ne. 15:9; D&C 10:4; 53:7. TG Perseverance; Steadfastness.endureth to the end shall be saved.
But when they persecute you in this city, flee ye into another: for verily I say unto you, Ye shall not have gone over the cities of Israel, till the Son of man be come.
The John 15:20.disciple is not above his master, nor the servant above his lord.

It is enough for the disciple GR that he become.that he be as his master, and the servant as his lord. If they have called the master of the house Beelzebub, how much more shall they call them of his household?
Fear them not therefore: for there is nothing Morm. 5:8.covered, that shall not be revealed; and hid, that shall not be known.
What I tell you in darkness, that speak ye in light: and what ye hear in the ear, that preach ye upon the housetops.

And Isa. 51:7; Jer. 1:8 (7–8); Luke 12:4 (4–5).fear not them which kill the body, but are not able to kill the soul: but rather Deut. 13:8 (6–11); D&C 3:7 (6–8).fear him which is able to TG Devil.destroy both TG Soul.soul and body in TG Hell.hell.
Are not two TG God, Omniscience of.sparrows sold for a farthing? and one of them shall not fall on the ground without your Father.
But the very Luke 21:18 (17–18).hairs of your head are all numbered.
Fear ye not therefore, ye are of more Gen. 39:3 (1–6); Ps. 37:25; Rom. 8:39 (35–39); 1 Ne. 17:3 (1–5, 12–14); Mosiah 2:41; D&C 121:33.value than many TG Nature, Earth.sparrows.
Whosoever therefore shall GR solemnly covenant with, promise me. TG Witness.confess me before men, him will I confess also before my Father which is in heaven.
But whosoever shall Rom. 1:16 (15–18); 2 Tim. 2:12 (10–15); 2 Ne. 31:14 (12–21); D&C 101:5 (1–5).deny me before men, him will I also deny before my Father which is in heaven.
Think not that I am come to send John 7:43. TG Peace.peace on earth: I came not to send peace, but a sword.
For I am come to set a man at variance against his father, and the daughter against her mother, and the daughter in law against her mother in law.
And a man’s TG Persecution.foes shall be they of his own Micah 7:6.household.
He that TG Love.loveth father or mother Luke 14:26.more than me is not worthy of me: and he that 1 Sam. 2:29.loveth son or daughter more than me is not worthy of me.
And he that taketh not his Matt. 16:24; Luke 9:23; 14:27; 3 Ne. 12:30; D&C 23:6.cross, and followeth after me, is not TG Worthiness.worthy of me.

JST Matt. 10:34 He who seeketh to save his life …He that findeth his life shall GR sacrifice.lose it: and he that Luke 9:24 (23–26). TG Loyalty.loseth his TG Martyrdom; Self-Sacrifice.life for my sake shall find it.

¶ He that Luke 9:48; 10:16; D&C 84:36 (36–38). TG Priesthood, Magnifying Callings within; Teachable.receiveth you Ex. 16:8. TG Hospitality.receiveth me, and he that receiveth me receiveth him that sent me.

He that TG Sustaining Church Leaders.receiveth a prophet in the name of a prophet shall receive a prophet’s TG Reward.reward; and he that receiveth a righteous man in the name of a righteous man shall receive a righteous man’s reward.

And whosoever shall give to drink unto one of these little ones a cup of cold water only in the TG Name.name of a disciple, verily I say unto you, he shall in no wise Mark 9:41; D&C 84:90.lose his reward.

div.p: Jesus acclaims John as more than a prophet—The cities of Chorazin, Bethsaida, and Capernaum are rebuked for unbelief—The Son reveals the Father—The yoke of Christ is easy, and His burden is light.

chapter.verse: And it came to pass, when Jesus had made an end of commanding his twelve disciples, he departed thence to teach and to TG Missionary Work.preach in their cities.
Now when Luke 7:18 (18–35).John had heard in the prison the works of Christ, he sent two of his disciples,
And said unto him, Art thou he that should come, or do we look for another?
Jesus answered and said unto them, Go and shew John again those John 5:36.things which ye do hear and see:

The Ps. 146:8; Isa. 42:7.blind receive their TG Sight.sight, and the lame walk, the TG Leprosy.lepers are cleansed, and the deaf hear, the dead are raised up, and the D&C 35:15.poor have the gospel preached to them.
And blessed is he, whosoever shall not be Isa. 8:14 (14–15); Matt. 24:10.offended in me.
¶ And as they departed, Jesus began to say unto the multitudes concerning John, What went ye out into the wilderness to see? A reed shaken with the wind?
But what went ye out for to see? A man clothed in soft raiment? behold, they that wear soft clothing are in TG Kings, Earthly.kings’ houses.
But what went ye out for to see? A prophet? yea, I say unto you, and more than a Matt. 14:5; 21:26; Luke 20:6.prophet.
For this is he, of whom it is written, Behold, I send my Mal. 3:1; Mark 1:2; 1 Ne. 11:27; D&C 35:4; 45:9.messenger before thy face, which shall Isa. 40:3.prepare thy way before thee.

Verily I say unto you, Among them that are born of TG Woman.women there hath not risen a greater than John 5:35 (32–35).John the Baptist: GR but he who is less important. Luke 7:28; D&C 50:26.notwithstanding he that is least in the kingdom of heaven is greater than he.
And from the days of John the Baptist until now the kingdom of heaven suffereth violence, and GR violent men are seizing control of it, or plundering it.the violent take it by force.

JST Matt. 11:13–15 (Appendix).For all the Zech. 7:12; 1 Ne. 3:20; Mosiah 15:13. TG Jesus Christ, Prophecies about.prophets and the law IE The law and prophets foretold such violence.prophesied until John.
And if ye will receive it, this is Luke 1:17.Elias, which was for to come.
He that hath ears to Ezek. 33:31 (30–33); Heb. 5:11 (11–14); 2 Ne. 9:31; D&C 1:14.hear, let him hear.
¶ But whereunto shall I liken this generation? It is like unto children sitting in the markets, and calling unto their fellows,
And saying, We have piped unto you, and ye have not danced; we have mourned unto you, and ye have not lamented.
For John came neither eating nor drinking, and they say, He hath a devil.

The Son of man came eating and drinking, and they say, Behold a man gluttonous, and GR a drunkard.a winebibber, a friend of GR tax collectors.publicans and sinners. But wisdom is justified GR by her deeds, works.of her children.
¶ Then began he to upbraid the Alma 34:31 (31–36); 3 Ne. 9:3; D&C 84:114.cities wherein most of his mighty works were done, because they repented not:

Woe unto thee, Chorazin! woe unto thee, Bethsaida! for if the mighty works, which were done in you, had been done in Tyre and Sidon, they would have Ezek. 3:6.repented long ago in sackcloth and Job 2:8; 42:6.ashes.
But I say unto you, It shall be more D&C 45:54; 75:22.tolerable for Tyre and Sidon at the day of judgment, than for you.

And thou, Capernaum, which art TG Haughtiness.exalted unto heaven, shalt be brought down to TG Hell.hell: for if the mighty works, which have been done in thee, had been done in Sodom, it would have remained until this day.
But I say unto you, That it shall be more tolerable for the land of Ezek. 16:48.Sodom in the day of judgment, than for thee.

¶ At that time Jesus answered and said, I GR praise. Luke 10:21.thank thee, O Father, Lord of heaven and earth, because thou hast Matt. 13:11; D&C 6:11 (11–12).hid these things from the wise and TG Prudence.prudent, and hast Alma 32:23; D&C 133:58.revealed them unto GR innocent people. 2 Ne. 9:42 (42–43); Alma 32:23; 3 Ne. 26:16 (14–16); D&C 128:18.babes.
Even so, Father: for so it seemed good in thy sight.

All TG Jesus Christ, Messiah.things are delivered unto me of my Father: and no man knoweth the Son, but the Father; neither TG Knowledge.knoweth any man the Father, save the Son, JST Matt. 11:28 … and they to whom the Son will reveal himself; they shall see the Father also.and he to whomsoever the Son will Luke 10:22; John 14:6 (6–14). TG God, Privilege of Seeing.reveal him.
¶ Ps. 55:22; Isa. 55:3; D&C 10:67. TG Problem-Solving.Come unto me, all ye that TG Labor.labour and are heavy laden, and I will give you TG Rest.rest.

Take my 1 Jn. 2:6. TG Jesus Christ, Taking the Name of.yoke upon you, and D&C 32:1. TG Learn.learn of me; for I am GR gentle and humble. TG Humility; Meek.meek and TG Contrite Heart.lowly in TG Heart.heart: and ye shall find Mosiah 2:41; Alma 37:34 (33–34); D&C 54:10; 59:23. TG Comfort.rest unto your souls.
For my yoke is 1 Jn. 5:3 (1–5); Alma 37:46 (43–47).easy, and my burden is light.

div.p: Jesus proclaims Himself Lord of the Sabbath and heals on the Sabbath day—He is accused of casting out devils through the power of Beelzebub—He speaks of blasphemy against the Holy Ghost and says that an evil and adulterous generation seeks signs.

chapter.verse: At that time Jesus went on the John 5:9.sabbath day through the GR grain.corn; and his disciples were an hungred, and began to pluck the Deut. 23:25.ears of corn, and to eat.
But when the Pharisees saw it, they said unto him, Behold, thy disciples do that which is not lawful to do upon the sabbath day.
But he said unto them, Have ye not TG Jesus Christ, Teaching Mode of.read what 1 Sam. 21:6 (3–6).David did, when he was an hungred, and they that were with him;
How he entered into the house of God, and did eat the TG Bread, Shewbread.shewbread, which was not lawful for him to eat, neither for them which were with him, but only for the priests?
Or have ye not read in the law, how that on the Num. 28:9 (9–10).sabbath days the priests in the temple profane the sabbath, and are blameless?
But I say unto you, That in this place is one greater than the TG Temple.temple.
But if ye had known what this meaneth, I will have Hosea 6:6.mercy, and not sacrifice, ye would not have condemned the guiltless.
For the Son of man is Lord even of the TG Sabbath.sabbath day.
And when he was departed thence, he went into their synagogue:
¶ And, behold, there was a man which had his hand withered. And they asked him, saying, Is it lawful to heal on the sabbath days? that they might accuse him.
And he said unto them, What man shall there be among you, that shall have one sheep, and if it fall into a pit on the sabbath day, will he not lay hold on it, and lift it out?
How much then is a man better than a sheep? Wherefore it is lawful to do well on the sabbath days.
Then saith he to the man, Stretch forth thine hand. And he stretched it forth; and it was restored whole, like as the other.
¶ Then the Pharisees went out, and held a council against him, how they might destroy him.
But JST Matt. 12:13 … Jesus knew when they took counsel, and he withdrew …when Jesus knew it, he withdrew himself from thence: and great multitudes followed him, and he healed them all;
And charged them that they should not make him known:
That it might be fulfilled which was spoken by Isa. 42:1–3.Esaias the prophet, saying,

Behold my GR son.servant, whom I have TG Jesus Christ, Authority of.chosen; my TG Witness of the Father.beloved, in whom my soul is well pleased: I will put my spirit upon him, and he shall shew judgment to the Gentiles.
He shall not strive, nor GR cry for help.cry; neither shall any man hear his voice in the streets.
A bruised reed shall he not break, and smoking flax shall he not quench, till he send forth D&C 52:11.judgment unto victory.
And in his TG Jesus Christ, Taking the Name of; Name.name shall the GR nations. 2 Ne. 10:18 (9, 11, 18).Gentiles trust.
¶ Then was brought unto him one possessed with a devil, blind, and dumb: and he healed him, insomuch that the blind and dumb both spake and saw.
And all the people were amazed, and said, Is not this the son of TG Jesus Christ, Davidic Descent of.David?
But when the Pharisees heard it, they said, This fellow doth not cast out devils, but by 2 Kgs. 1:2 (2–6).Beelzebub the prince of the devils.

And Jesus knew their Ps. 139:2; Luke 11:17; John 2:25; Mosiah 24:12; D&C 6:16.thoughts, and said unto them, Every TG Governments.kingdom divided against itself is brought to desolation; and every city or house divided against itself shall not stand:
And if TG Spirits, Evil or Unclean.Satan cast out Satan, he is divided against himself; how shall then his kingdom stand?
And if I by Beelzebub cast out devils, by whom do your children cast them out? therefore they shall be your judges.

But if I cast out TG Devil.devils by the TG God, Spirit of.Spirit of God, then the kingdom of God is come unto JST Matt. 12:23 … you. For they also cast out devils by the Spirit of God, for unto them is given power over devils, that they may cast them out.you.
Or else how can one enter into a strong man’s house, and GR plunder.spoil his goods, except he first bind the strong man? and then he will spoil his house.
He that is not with me is against me; and he that gathereth not Mark 9:40; Luke 9:50; 2 Ne. 10:16.with me scattereth abroad.

¶ Wherefore I say unto you, All manner of sin and blasphemy shall be forgiven JST Matt. 12:26 … unto men who receive me and repent; but …unto men: but the TG Blaspheme; Holy Ghost, Unpardonable Sin against.blasphemy against the Holy Ghost shall TG Holy Ghost, Loss of.not be forgiven unto men.

And whosoever speaketh a word against the Son of man, it shall be forgiven him: but whosoever speaketh against the Holy Ghost, it shall not be TG Death, Spiritual, Second; Forgive.forgiven him, neither in this world, neither in the world to come.
Either make the tree good, and his Matt. 7:17 (16–20); 3 Ne. 14:20 (17–20).fruit good; or else make the tree corrupt, and his fruit corrupt: for the tree is known by his Moro. 7:16 (5–19).fruit.
O Matt. 3:7; 23:33; D&C 121:23.generation of vipers, how can ye, being evil, Luke 6:45; James 3:10 (10–12).speak good things? for out of the abundance of the heart the mouth speaketh.
A good man out of the good TG Treasure.treasure of the heart bringeth forth good things: and an evil man out of the evil treasure bringeth forth TG Evil.evil things.

But I say unto you, That every Eccl. 5:2; Eph. 5:4.idle TG Gossip.word that men shall TG Profanity.speak, they shall give TG Accountability.account thereof in the day of Alma 11:43 (43–44). TG Judgment, the Last.judgment.
For by thy Prov. 18:21.words thou shalt be justified, and by thy words thou shalt be condemned.
¶ Then certain of the scribes and of the Pharisees answered, saying, Master, we would see a John 6:30.sign from thee.
But he answered and said unto them, An evil and adulterous generation seeketh after a TG Miracle; Signs; Sign Seekers.sign; and there shall no sign be given to it, but the sign of the prophet Jonas:

For as Jonas was three days and three nights in the whale’s belly; so shall the Son of man be TG Jesus Christ, Death of; Jesus Christ, Types of, in Anticipation.three days and three nights in the heart of the earth.
The men of Jonah 3:5.Nineveh shall rise in judgment with this generation, and shall condemn it: because they TG Repent.repented at the preaching of Jonas; and, behold, a greater than Jonas is here.

The 1 Kgs. 10:1 (1–10).queen of the south shall rise up in the judgment with this generation, and shall condemn it: for she came from the uttermost parts of the earth to hear the wisdom of Solomon; and, behold, a greater than Solomon is here.
JST Matt. 12:37–38 (Appendix).When the TG Uncleanness.unclean spirit is gone out of a man, he walketh through dry places, seeking rest, and findeth none.
Then he saith, I will return into my house from whence I came out; and when he is come, he findeth it empty, swept, and garnished.

Then goeth JST Matt. 12:39 … the evil spirit, and taketh …he, and taketh with himself seven other TG Spirits, Evil or Unclean.spirits more wicked than himself, and they enter in and dwell there: and the last TG Apostasy of Individuals. state of that man is Alma 24:30.worse than the first. Even so shall it be also unto this wicked generation.
¶ While he yet talked to the people, behold, his mother and his John 7:3 (3, 5, 10); 1 Cor. 9:5. TG Jesus Christ, Family of.brethren stood GR outside.without, desiring to speak with him.
Then one said unto him, Behold, thy mother and thy brethren stand without, desiring to speak with thee.
But he answered and said unto him that told him, Who is my mother? and who are my brethren?
And he stretched forth his hand toward his disciples, and said, Behold my Mosiah 5:7. TG Marriage, Motherhood.mother and my brethren!
For whosoever shall do the Mark 3:35. TG God, Will of.will of my Father which is in heaven, the same is my TG Brotherhood and Sisterhood.brother, and sister, and mother.

div.p: Jesus explains why He teaches with parables—He gives the parables of the sower, the wheat and the tares, the grain of mustard seed, the leaven, the treasure hidden in the field, the pearl of great price, and the net cast into the sea—A prophet is not honored by his own people.

chapter.verse: The same day went Jesus out of the house, and sat by the Mark 4:1 (1–12).sea side.
And great multitudes were gathered together unto him, so that he went into a ship, and sat; and the whole multitude stood on the shore.
And he spake many things unto them in parables, saying, Behold, a sower went forth to sow;
And when he sowed, some seeds fell by the way side, and the fowls came and devoured them up:
Some fell upon IE rocky land over which a thin layer of soil is spread.stony places, where they had not much earth: and forthwith they sprung up, because they had no deepness of earth:
And when the sun was up, they were scorched; and because they had no root, they withered away.
And some fell among Jer. 4:3 (3–18).thorns; and the thorns sprung up, and choked them:
But other fell into good ground, and GR began to bring forth.brought forth fruit, some an hundredfold, some sixtyfold, some thirtyfold.
Who hath ears to hear, let him hear.
And the disciples came, and said unto him, Why speakest thou unto them in Dan. 12:10. TG Jesus Christ, Teaching Mode of.parables?

He answered and said unto them, Because it is given unto you to know the Matt. 11:25; D&C 6:11 (11–12); 19:8. TG Mysteries of Godliness.mysteries of the kingdom of heaven, but to them it is not given.

JST Matt. 13:10–11 For whosoever receiveth, to him shall be given, and he shall have more abundance; but whosoever continueth not to receive, from him shall be taken away even that he hath.For whosoever hath, to him shall be Matt. 25:29.given, and he shall have more Prov. 9:9.abundance: but whosoever hath not, from him shall be taken away even that he hath.
Therefore speak I to them in parables: because they seeing see not; and hearing they hear not, neither do they understand.

And in them is fulfilled the prophecy of Esaias, which saith, By hearing ye shall Jer. 5:21; Rom. 11:8; 2 Ne. 9:31; 16:10.hear, and shall not understand; and seeing ye shall see, and shall not perceive:

For this people’s TG Apostasy of Israel; Hardheartedness.heart is waxed gross, and their ears are dull of hearing, and their TG Spiritual Blindness.eyes they have TG Unbelief.closed; lest at any time they should see with their eyes, and hear with their ears, and should understand with their heart, and should be converted, and I should 3 Ne. 9:13; 18:32.heal them.
But blessed are your Luke 10:23.eyes, for they see: and your ears, for they hear.

For verily I say unto you, That many Heb. 11:13; 1 Pet. 1:10 (10–11).prophets and righteous men have desired to see those things which ye see, and have not seen them; and to hear those things which ye hear, and have not heard them.
¶ GR Learn, Understand.Hear ye therefore the parable of the sower.

When any one heareth the word of the kingdom, and 1 Ne. 8:23 (20–23).understandeth it not, then cometh the wicked one, and D&C 84:50 (49–53); 93:39 (38–39).catcheth away that which was sown in his heart. This is he which received seed by the way side.
But he that received the seed into Zech. 7:12.stony places, the same is he that heareth the word, and anon with joy receiveth it;

Yet hath he not root in himself, but dureth for a while: for when TG Test; Tribulation.tribulation or D&C 40:2 (1–3). TG Persecution.persecution ariseth because of the word, by and by GR he stumbles, falls away. TG Apostasy of Individuals; Offense.he is offended.

He also that received seed among the thorns is he that heareth the word; and the care of this Hel. 7:5; D&C 39:9. TG Worldliness.world, and the TG Deceit.deceitfulness of TG Treasure.riches, choke the word, and he becometh unfruitful.

But he that received seed into the good ground is he that TG Teachable.heareth the word, and JST Matt. 13:21 … understandeth and endureth; which …understandeth it; which also beareth Alma 32:42 (41–43). TG Good Works.fruit, and bringeth forth, some an hundredfold, some sixty, some thirty.
¶ Another D&C 86:1 (1–11).parable put he forth unto them, saying, The TG Kingdom of God, on Earth.kingdom of heaven is likened unto a man which sowed good Matt. 13:37 (36–43).seed in his field:
But while men slept, his enemy came and sowed TG Apostasy of the Early Christian Church.tares among the wheat, and went his way.
But when the blade was sprung up, and brought forth fruit, then appeared the tares also.
So the servants of the householder came and said unto him, Sir, didst not thou sow good seed in thy field? from whence then hath it tares?
He said unto them, An enemy hath done this. The servants said unto him, Wilt thou then that we go and gather them up?
But he said, Nay; lest while ye gather up the tares, ye root up also the wheat with them.

Let both grow together until the TG Harvest.harvest: and in the time of harvest I will say to the reapers, Gather ye together JST Matt. 13:29 … first the wheat into my barn; and the tares are bound in bundles to be burned.first the tares, and bind them in bundles to D&C 38:12.burn them: but gather the wheat into my barn.
¶ Another parable put he forth unto them, saying, The kingdom of heaven is like to a Isa. 60:22.grain of mustard seed, which a man took, and sowed in his field:

Which indeed is the least of all seeds: but when it is grown, it is the greatest among herbs, and becometh a tree, so that the TG Nature, Earth.birds of the air come and lodge in the branches thereof.
¶ Another parable spake he unto them; The kingdom of heaven is like unto GR yeast. TG Leaven.leaven, which a woman took, and hid in three measures of meal, till the whole was leavened.
All these things spake Jesus unto the multitude in parables; and without a parable spake he not unto them:

That it might be fulfilled which was spoken by the prophet, saying, I will open my mouth in Ps. 78:2; Mark 4:11 (11–12).parables; I will utter things which have been kept D&C 35:18.secret from the foundation of the world.
Then Jesus sent the multitude away, and went into the house: and his disciples came unto him, saying, Declare unto us the parable of the D&C 86:1 (1–7); 101:65 (65–67).tares of the field.
He answered and said unto them, He that soweth the good Matt. 13:24 (24–30).seed is the Son of man;
The field is the world; the good seed are the children of the kingdom; but the D&C 88:94. TG Devil, Church of.tares are the children of the wicked one;
The enemy that sowed them is the devil; JST Matt. 13:39–44 (Appendix).the TG Harvest.harvest is the end of the world; and the reapers are the angels.
As therefore the tares are gathered and burned in the fire; so shall it be in the TG World, End of.end of this world.
The Son of man shall send forth his TG Angels.angels, and they shall gather out of his kingdom all things that TG Offense.offend, and them which do iniquity;
And shall cast them into a furnace of fire: there shall be Matt. 8:12; Rev. 19:20; D&C 19:5; 29:15; 112:24.wailing and TG Pain.gnashing of teeth.
Then shall the Ps. 68:3; Alma 40:25. TG Righteousness.righteous TG Celestial Glory.shine forth as the D&C 76:70.sun in the kingdom of their Father. Who hath ears to hear, let him hear.

¶ Again, the kingdom of heaven is like unto TG Treasure.treasure hid in a field; the which when a man hath found, he hideth, and for joy thereof goeth and selleth all that he hath, and buyeth that field.
¶ Again, the kingdom of heaven is like unto a merchant man, seeking goodly pearls:
Who, when he had found one Rev. 3:18.pearl of great price, went and sold Alma 22:15.all that he had, and bought it.
¶ Again, the kingdom of heaven is like unto a net, that was cast into the sea, and Matt. 22:10 (1–14).gathered of every kind:
Which, when it was full, they drew to shore, and sat down, and gathered the good into vessels, but cast the bad away.

So shall it be at the TG World, End of.end of the JST Matt. 13:49–51 … world. And the world is the children of the wicked. The angels …world: the angels shall come forth, and GR separate.sever the wicked from among the TG Righteousness.just,
JST Matt. 13:51 … and shall cast them out into the world to be burned. There shall be wailing …And shall cast them into the furnace of fire: there shall be wailing and gnashing of teeth.
Jesus saith unto them, Have ye understood all these things? They say unto him, Yea, Lord.

Then said he unto them, Therefore every TG Scribe.scribe GR which has become a disciple in. which is instructed unto the kingdom of heaven is like unto a man that is an householder, which bringeth forth out of his treasure things new and old.
¶ And it came to pass, that when Jesus had finished these parables, he departed thence.

And when he was come into his own country, he taught them in their synagogue, insomuch that they were Matt. 7:28.astonished, and said, Whence hath this man this TG God, Wisdom of; Jesus Christ, Teaching Mode of.wisdom, and these mighty works?
Is not this the carpenter’s Mark 6:3.son? is not his mother called Mary? and his TG Jesus Christ, Family of.brethren, James, and Joses, and Simon, and Judas?
And his sisters, are they not all with us? Whence then hath this man all these things?
And they were offended in him. But Jesus said unto them, A prophet is not without TG Prophets, Rejection of.honour, save in his own country, and in his own house.
And he did not many mighty works there because of their Luke 4:23 (23–27); Ether 12:12 (6–19); Moro. 7:37 (33–39).unbelief.

div.p: John the Baptist is beheaded—Jesus feeds the five thousand and walks on the sea—Those who touch the hem of His garment are made whole.

chapter.verse: At that time Luke 9:7.Herod the tetrarch heard of the fame of Jesus,
And said unto his servants, This is John the Baptist; he is risen from the dead; and therefore mighty works do shew forth themselves in him.
¶ For Herod had laid hold on John, and bound him, and put him in prison for Herodias’ sake, his brother Philip’s wife.
