For John said unto him, It is not Lev. 18:16; 20:21. TG Divorce.lawful for thee to have her.
And GR although he desired to kill him.when he would have put him to death, he feared the multitude, because they counted him as a Matt. 11:9; 21:26; Luke 20:6.prophet.
But when Herod’s birthday was kept, the daughter of Herodias danced before them, and pleased Herod.
Whereupon he TG Vow.promised with an oath to give her whatsoever she would ask.
And she, being before instructed of her mother, said, Give me here John Baptist’s head in a GR platter.charger.
And the king was sorry: nevertheless for the oath’s sake, and them which sat with him at meat, he commanded it to be given her.
And he sent, and TG Tyranny.beheaded John in the prison.
And his head was brought in a charger, and given to the damsel: and she brought it to her mother.
And his disciples came, and took up the body, and buried it, and went and told Jesus.
¶ When Jesus heard of it, he departed thence by ship into a desert place apart: and when the people had heard thereof, they followed him on foot out of the cities.
And Jesus went forth, and saw a great multitude, and was moved with TG Compassion.compassion toward them, and he healed their TG Sickness.sick.

¶ And when it was evening, his disciples came to him, saying, This is a desert place, and the time is now past; send the multitude away, that they may go into the villages, and buy themselves GR food.victuals.
But Jesus said unto them, They need not depart; give ye them to eat.
And they say unto him, We have here but five loaves, and two fishes.
He said, Bring them hither to me.

And he commanded the multitude to sit down on the grass, and took the five loaves, and the two fishes, and looking up to heaven, he blessed, and 3 Ne. 18:5 (4–5); 20:6 (6–7).brake, and gave the loaves to his disciples, and the disciples to the multitude.
And they did all eat, and were filled: and they 2 Kgs. 4:44 (42–44); Matt. 15:37 (36–38).took up of the fragments that remained twelve baskets full.
And they that had eaten were about Mark 6:44 (32–44); Luke 9:14 (10–17); John 6:10 (1–13).five thousand men, beside women and children.
¶ And straightway Jesus constrained his disciples to get into a ship, and to go before him unto the other side, while he sent the multitudes away.
And when he had sent the multitudes away, he went up into a mountain apart to Matt. 26:36; Luke 6:12; 9:28.pray: and when the evening was come, he was there alone.
But the ship was now in the midst of the sea, tossed with waves: for the wind was contrary.
And IE between three and six in the morning.in the fourth watch of the night Jesus went unto them, walking on the sea.
And when the disciples saw him walking on the sea, they were troubled, saying, It is a spirit; and they cried out for fear.
But straightway Jesus spake unto them, saying, Be of good TG Cheerful.cheer; it is I; be not afraid.
And Peter answered him and said, Lord, if it be thou, bid me come unto thee on the water.
And he said, Come. And when Peter was come down out of the ship, he walked on the water, to go to Jesus.
But when he saw the wind boisterous, he was TG Courage; Fearful.afraid; and beginning to sink, he cried, saying, Lord, save me.
And immediately Jesus stretched forth his hand, and caught him, and said unto him, O thou of little Mark 4:40; 16:14; Luke 24:25.faith, wherefore didst thou TG Doubt.doubt?
And when they were come into the ship, the wind ceased.
Then they that were in the ship came and worshipped him, saying, Of a truth thou art the Son of God.
¶ And when they were gone over, they came into the land of Gennesaret.
And when the men of that place GR recognized Him.had knowledge of him, they sent out into all that country round about, and brought unto him all that were diseased;
And besought him that they might only touch the Mark 5:27 (25–34).hem of his garment: and as many as touched were made perfectly TG Heal.whole.

div.p: The scribes and Pharisees contend against Jesus—He heals the daughter of a gentile woman—He feeds the four thousand.

chapter.verse: Then came to Jesus TG Scribe.scribes and Pharisees, which were of Jerusalem, saying,
Why do thy disciples transgress the Alma 24:7; D&C 123:7 (7–8).tradition of the elders? for they wash not their hands when they eat bread.
But he answered and said unto them, Why do ye also transgress the commandment of God by your TG Traditions of Men.tradition?

For God commanded, saying, TG Honor; Honoring Father and Mother; Respect.Honour thy father and mother: and, He that curseth father or mother, GR he shall surely die. TG Capital Punishment.let him die the death.
But ye say, Whosoever shall say to his father or his mother, It is a Mark 7:11.gift, by whatsoever thou mightest be profited by me;
And honour not his father or his mother, he shall be free. Thus have ye made the commandment of God of none effect by your tradition.
Ye hypocrites, well did Esaias prophesy of you, saying,

This people draweth nigh unto me with their Jer. 12:2.mouth, and Isa. 29:13; Titus 1:16 (15–16); 2 Ne. 27:25 (24–25). TG Hypocrisy.honoureth me with their lips; but their Ezek. 33:31; Alma 34:28; D&C 45:29.heart is far from me.
But in vain they do Isa. 1:13.worship me, teaching for Matt. 16:12; Col. 2:22 (18–22); Titus 1:14 (13–14); 2 Ne. 28:9.doctrines the TG Apostasy of Israel.commandments of men.
¶ And he called the multitude, and said unto them, Hear, and understand:
Not that which goeth into the mouth TG Cleanliness.defileth a man; but that which cometh out of the TG Gossip; Profanity.mouth, this defileth a man.
Then came his disciples, and said unto him, Knowest thou that the Pharisees were offended, after they heard this saying?
But he answered and said, Every John 15:2 (1–2). TG Vineyard of the Lord.plant, which my heavenly Father hath not planted, shall be rooted up.
Let them alone: they be TG Spiritual Blindness.blind TG Governments; Leadership.leaders of the blind. And if the blind lead the blind, both shall fall into the ditch.
Then answered Peter and said unto him, Declare unto us this parable.
And Jesus said, Are ye also yet without understanding?
Do not ye yet understand, that whatsoever entereth in at the mouth goeth into the belly, and is cast out into the draught?
But those things which proceed out of the Mosiah 4:30 (29–30).mouth come forth from the heart; and they defile the man.

For out of the Gen. 6:5; Heb. 3:12; Hel. 12:4. TG Chastity.heart proceed evil thoughts, TG Blood, Shedding of; Murder.murders, TG Adulterer; Sexual Immorality.adulteries, TG Fornication.fornications, thefts, Ps. 27:12; Hel. 7:21 (21–22).false witness, GR slanderous statements. TG Blaspheme.blasphemies:
These are the things which TG Pollution.defile a man: but to eat with unwashen hands defileth not a man.
¶ Then Jesus went thence, and departed into the GR regions.coasts of Tyre and Sidon.
And, behold, a woman of Canaan came out of the same coasts, and cried unto him, saying, Have mercy on me, O Lord, thou Son of David; my daughter is grievously vexed with a devil.
But he answered her not a word. And his disciples came and besought him, saying, Send her away; for she crieth after us.
But he answered and said, I am not 3 Ne. 15:23. TG Jesus Christ, Messenger of the Covenant.sent but unto the lost sheep of the house of TG Israel, Scattering of.Israel.
Then came she and worshipped him, saying, Lord, help me.
But he answered and said, It is not meet to take the children’s bread, and to cast it to Prov. 23:9; Matt. 7:6; D&C 6:12 (10–12); 10:37 (36–37); 41:6.dogs.
And she said, Truth, Lord: yet the dogs eat of the crumbs which fall from their masters’ table.
Then Jesus answered and said unto her, O woman, great is thy James 5:15 (14–15).faith: be it unto thee even as thou wilt. And her daughter was made whole from that very hour.
And Jesus departed from thence, and came nigh unto the sea of Galilee; and went up into a mountain, and sat down there.
And great multitudes came unto him, having with them those that were lame, blind, dumb, maimed, and many others, and cast them down at Jesus’ feet; and he healed them:
Insomuch that the multitude wondered, when they saw the dumb to speak, the maimed to be whole, the lame to walk, and the blind to see: and they glorified the God of Israel.

¶ Then Jesus called his disciples unto him, and said, I have compassion on the multitude, because they continue with me now three days, and have nothing to eat: and I will not send them away fasting, lest they faint in the way.
And his disciples say unto him, Whence should we have so much bread in the wilderness, as to fill so great a multitude?
And Jesus saith unto them, How many loaves have ye? And they said, Seven, and a few little fishes.
And he commanded the multitude to sit down on the ground.
And he took the seven TG Bread.loaves and the fishes, and gave thanks, and brake them, and gave to his disciples, and the disciples to the multitude.
And they did all eat, and were filled: and they 2 Kgs. 4:44 (42–44); Matt. 14:20 (19–21).took up of the GR excess food.broken meat that was left seven baskets full.
And they that did eat were four thousand men, beside women and children.
And he sent away the multitude, and took ship, and came into the coasts of Magdala.

div.p: Jesus warns against the doctrine of the Pharisees and Sadducees—Peter testifies that Jesus is the Christ and is promised the keys of the kingdom—Jesus foretells His death and resurrection.

chapter.verse: The Pharisees also with the Sadducees came, and tempting desired him that he would shew them a sign from TG Heaven.heaven.
He Prov. 26:5.answered and said unto them, When it is evening, ye say, It will be fair weather: for the sky is red.

And in the morning, It will be foul weather to day: for the sky is red and GR dark, gloomy.lowring. O ye TG Hypocrisy.hypocrites, ye can discern the face of the sky; but can ye not discern the 2 Pet. 3:4 (3–10).signs of the Esth. 1:13.times?

A wicked and TG Adulterer.adulterous generation seeketh after a Mark 8:12 (11–21).sign; and there shall no sign be given unto it, but the Jonah 1:17.sign of the prophet TG Jesus Christ, Death of.Jonas. And he left them, and departed.
And when his disciples were come to the other side, they had forgotten to take bread.
¶ Then Jesus said unto them, Take heed and beware of the TG Leaven.leaven of the Pharisees and of the Sadducees.
And they reasoned among themselves, saying, It is because we have taken no bread.

JST Matt. 16:9 And when they reasoned among themselves, Jesus perceived it … Which when Jesus perceived, he said unto them, O ye of little faith, why reason ye among yourselves, because ye have brought no bread?
Do ye not yet understand, neither remember the five loaves of the five thousand, and how many baskets ye took up?
Neither the seven loaves of the four thousand, and how many baskets ye took up?
How is it that ye do not understand that I spake it not to you concerning bread, that ye should beware of the leaven of the Pharisees and of the Sadducees?
Then understood they how that he bade them not beware of the leaven of bread, but of the Matt. 15:9.doctrine of the Pharisees and of the Matt. 22:23 (23–33).Sadducees.
¶ When Jesus came into the coasts of Cæsarea Philippi, he asked his disciples, saying, Whom do men say that I the Son of man am?
And they said, Some say that thou art John the Baptist: some, IE Elijah.Elias; and others, Jeremias, or one of the prophets.
He saith unto them, But whom say IE The plural pronoun used here in the Greek indicates that Jesus asked this question of all the apostles and not just one of them.ye that I am?
And Simon Peter answered and said, Thou art the TG Loyalty.Christ, the TG Jesus Christ, Divine Sonship.Son of the TG God, Body of, Corporeal Nature.living God.

And Jesus answered and said unto him, Blessed art thou, Simon IE Son of Jonah.Bar-jona: for flesh and blood hath not TG Revelation; Testimony; Witness.revealed it unto thee, but my Father which is in heaven.

And I say also unto thee, That thou art Peter, and upon this Here is a subtle wordplay upon “Peter” (Greek petros = small rock) and “rock” (Greek petra = bedrock). Christ is the Stone of Israel. John 1:42; 1 Cor. 3:11 (9–11); 10:4; D&C 50:44; 128:10. TG Rock.rock I will build my TG Church Organization; Jesus Christ, Head of the Church.church; and the gates of TG Hell.hell shall not D&C 17:8; 21:6.prevail against it.

And I will D&C 7:7; 27:13. TG Delegation of Responsibility; God, Gifts of.give unto thee the TG Apostles; Priesthood; Priesthood, Authority; Priesthood, History of; Priesthood, Keys of.keys of the TG Kingdom of God, on Earth.kingdom of heaven: and whatsoever thou shalt Hel. 10:7 (5–11); D&C 138:58 (58–60). TG Genealogy and Temple Work; Marriage, Celestial; Salvation for the Dead; Sealing.bind on earth shall be bound in heaven: and whatsoever thou shalt loose on earth shall be loosed in heaven.
Then charged he his disciples that they should tell no man that he was Jesus the TG Jesus Christ, Messiah.Christ.

¶ From that time forth began Jesus to shew unto his disciples, how that he must go unto Jerusalem, and suffer many things of the elders and chief priests and scribes, and be killed, and be TG Jesus Christ, Resurrection.raised again the third day.
Then Peter took him, and began to rebuke him, saying, Be it far from thee, Lord: this shall not be unto thee.
But he turned, and said unto Peter, Get thee behind me, Satan: thou art an offence unto me: for thou Hel. 10:4 (4–5).savourest not the things that be of God, but those that be of men.

¶ JST Matt. 16:25–29 (Appendix).Then said Jesus unto his disciples, If any man will come after me, let him TG Self-Mastery.deny himself, and take up his Matt. 10:38; 3 Ne. 12:30.cross, and Philip. 3:17 (13–21); 2 Ne. 31:12 (12–13).follow JST Matt. 16:25–26 … me. And now for a man to take up his cross, is to deny himself all ungodliness, and every worldly lust, and keep my commandments.me.
For whosoever will save his life shall lose it: and whosoever will TG Self-Mastery; Self-Sacrifice.lose his life for my sake shall TG Abundant Life.find it.

For what is a man 1 Sam. 12:21; Prov. 13:7; Eccl. 1:3 (2–3).profited, if he shall Job 27:8. TG Selfishness; Treasure.gain the whole TG Worldliness.world, and Luke 9:25.lose his own TG Mortality; Soul.soul? or what shall a man give in exchange for his soul?

For the TG Jesus Christ, Son of Man.Son of man shall come in the TG Glory; Jesus Christ, Glory of; Jesus Christ, Second Coming.glory of his Father with his angels; and then he shall TG Jesus Christ, Judge; Judgment, the Last; Retribution; Reward.reward every man according to his Prov. 24:12. TG Good Works.works.
Verily I say unto you, There be some standing here, which shall not taste of TG Translated Beings.death, till they see the Son of man coming in his TG Millennium.kingdom.

div.p: Jesus is transfigured before Peter, James, and John on the mount—Jesus heals a lunatic, tells of His coming death, and pays taxes in a miraculous manner.

chapter.verse: And after six days Jesus taketh D&C 128:20.Peter, James, and John his brother, and bringeth them up into an high mountain apart,

And was Mark 9:2 (2–13); Luke 9:29 (28–36); John 1:14; 2 Pet. 1:16 (16–19). TG Jesus Christ, Glory of; Transfiguration.transfigured before them: and his face did shine as the sun, and his raiment was white as the light.
And, behold, there appeared unto them D&C 63:21; 110:11. TG Priesthood, Keys of; Translated Beings.Moses and IE Elijah. 1 Kgs. 17:1; Luke 4:25; D&C 110:13 (13–16).Elias talking with him.
Then answered Peter, and said unto Jesus, Lord, it is good for us to be here: if thou wilt, let us make here three tabernacles; one for thee, and one for Moses, and one for Elias.

While he yet spake, behold, a bright Ex. 24:15.cloud overshadowed them: and behold a TG God, Body of, Corporeal Nature.voice out of the cloud, which said, This is my Matt. 3:17; 3 Ne. 11:7; JS—H 1:17. TG Witness of the Father.beloved TG God; Godhead; God the Father, Elohim; Jesus Christ, Divine Sonship.Son, in whom I am well pleased; hear ye him.
And when the disciples heard it, they fell on their face, and were sore Ex. 3:6; Deut. 5:5.afraid.
And Jesus came and touched them, and said, Arise, and be not afraid.
And when they had lifted up their eyes, they saw no man, save Jesus only.
And as they came down from the mountain, Jesus charged them, saying, Tell the TG Vision.vision to no Mark 9:9 (9–13).man, until the Son of man be risen again from the TG Death.dead.
And his disciples asked him, saying, Why then say the scribes that Elias must first come?
JST Matt. 17:10–14 (Appendix).And Jesus answered and said unto them, Elias truly shall first come, and D&C 27:6; 77:14; 110:13–16. TG Dispensations; Restoration of the Gospel.restore all things.
But I say unto you, That Elias is come already, and they knew him not, but have done unto him whatsoever they GR desired.listed. Likewise shall also the Son of man suffer of them.
Then the disciples understood that he spake unto them of John the Baptist.
¶ And when they were come to the multitude, there came to him a certain man, kneeling down to him, and saying,
Lord, have mercy on my son: for he is lunatic, and sore vexed: for ofttimes he GR throws himself.falleth into the fire, and oft into the water.
And I brought him to thy disciples, and they could not cure him.
Then Jesus answered and said, O TG Unbelief.faithless and Deut. 32:5.perverse generation, how long shall I be with you? how long shall I suffer you? bring him hither to me.
And Jesus rebuked the devil; and he departed out of him: and the child was cured from that very hour.
Then came the disciples to Jesus apart, and said, Why could not we cast him out?

And Jesus said unto them, Because of your GR little faith. TG Doubt.unbelief: for verily I say unto you, If ye have TG Faith.faith as a grain of mustard seed, ye shall say unto this Ether 12:30; Moses 7:13.mountain, Remove hence to yonder place; and it shall remove; and nothing shall be TG God, Power of.impossible unto you.
Howbeit this D&C 84:67 (65–73).kind goeth not out but by prayer and TG Fast, Fasting.fasting.
¶ And while they abode in Galilee, Jesus said unto them, The Son of man shall be TG Jesus Christ, Betrayal of.betrayed into the hands of men:
And they shall kill him, and the third day he shall be TG Jesus Christ, Resurrection.raised again. And they were exceeding sorry.
¶ And when they were come to Capernaum, they that received tribute money came to Peter, and said, Doth not your master pay D&C 58:21 (21–22).tribute?

He saith, Yes. And when he was come into the house, Jesus GR spoke to him first.prevented him, saying, What thinkest thou, Simon? of whom do the TG Kings, Earthly.kings of the earth take custom or tribute? of their own children, or of strangers?
Peter saith unto him, Of strangers. Jesus saith unto him, Then are the children free.

Notwithstanding, lest we should offend them, go thou to the sea, and cast an hook, and take up the fish that first cometh up; and when thou hast opened his mouth, thou shalt find a piece of Ex. 30:13; 38:26.money: that take, and TG Citizenship.give unto them for me and thee.

div.p: Jesus explains how we are to treat our offending brethren—The Son of Man came to save that which was lost—All of the Twelve receive the keys of the kingdom—Jesus explains why we should forgive.

chapter.verse: At the same time came the disciples unto Jesus, saying, Who is the Luke 9:46.greatest in the kingdom of heaven?
And Jesus called a little child unto him, and set him in the midst of them,

And said, Verily I say unto you, Except ye be converted, and become as little 1 Cor. 14:20; Mosiah 3:19. TG Children; Salvation of Little Children.children, ye shall not enter into the kingdom of heaven.
Whosoever therefore shall TG Humility; Meek.humble himself as this little TG Children.child, the same is greatest in the kingdom of heaven.
And whoso shall receive one such little child in my TG Name.name receiveth me.

But whoso shall GR cause to stumble. D&C 121:19 (19–22). TG Offense.offend one of these little ones which believe in me, it were better for him that a millstone were hanged about his neck, and that he were drowned in the depth of the sea.
¶ Woe unto the world because of TG Sin.offences! for it must needs be that offences come; but woe to that man by whom the offence cometh!

Wherefore if thy hand or thy foot offend thee, cut them off, and cast them from thee: it is better for thee to enter into life halt or maimed, rather than having two hands or two feet to be cast into everlasting fire.

And if thine eye offend thee, pluck it out, and cast it from thee: it is better for thee to enter into life with one eye, rather than having two eyes to be cast into JST Matt. 18:8–9 … hellfire. And a man’s hand is his friend, and his foot, also; and a man’s eye, are they of his own household.hell fire.

Take heed that ye despise not one of these TG Children; Family, Children, Responsibilities toward.little ones; for I say unto you, That in heaven their angels do always behold the face of my Father which is in heaven.

For the TG Jesus Christ, Son of Man.Son of man is come to TG Jesus Christ, Mission of; Life, Sanctity of; Worth of Souls.save that which was JST Matt. 18:11 … lost, and to call sinners to repentance; but these little ones have no need of repentance, and I will save them.lost.
How think ye? if a man have an hundred TG Sheep.sheep, and one of them be gone astray, doth he not leave the ninety and nine, and goeth into the mountains, and seeketh that which is gone astray?
And if so be that he find it, verily I say unto you, he rejoiceth more of that sheep, than of the ninety and nine which went not astray.
Even so it is not the will of your Father which is in heaven, that one of these TG Children.little ones should Mosiah 28:3.perish.

¶ Moreover if thy brother shall TG Offense.trespass against thee, go and TG Reproof.tell him his Matt. 5:24 (23–24); Luke 17:3 (3–4); D&C 42:88 (88–92).fault between thee and him alone: if he shall hear thee, thou hast gained thy brother.
But if he will not hear thee, then take with thee one or two more, that in the mouth of two or three 2 Ne. 29:8. TG Witness.witnesses every word may be established.
And if he shall neglect to hear them, tell it unto the 1 Cor. 6:1; D&C 42:81.church: but if he Titus 3:10.neglect to hear the TG Church.church, let him be unto thee as an heathen man and a publican.
Verily I say unto you, Whatsoever ye shall TG Apostles; Priesthood, Keys of; Sealing.bind on earth shall be bound in heaven: and whatsoever ye shall loose on earth shall be loosed in heaven.

Again I say unto you, That if two of you shall TG Common Consent.agree on earth as D&C 6:32; 42:3.touching any thing that they shall TG Prayer.ask, it shall be done for them of my Father which is in heaven.
For where two or three are TG Church.gathered TG Unity.together in my TG Name.name, there am I in the D&C 29:5; 32:3; 45:59; 49:27.midst of them.
¶ Then came Peter to him, and said, Lord, how oft shall my brother sin against me, and I TG Forbear.forgive him? till seven times?
Jesus saith unto him, I say not unto thee, Until seven times: but, Until D&C 98:40 (39–48). TG Patience.seventy times seven.
¶ Therefore is the kingdom of heaven likened unto a certain king, which would GR settle accounts with. TG Accountability; Stewardship.take account of his TG Servant.servants.
And when he had begun to reckon, one was brought unto him, which owed him ten thousand talents.
But forasmuch as he had not to pay, his lord commanded him to be 2 Kgs. 4:1.sold, and his wife, and children, and all that he had, and payment to be made.
The servant therefore fell down, and worshipped him, saying, Lord, have patience with me, and I will pay thee all.
Then the lord of that servant was moved with compassion, and loosed him, and forgave him the debt.

But the same servant went out, and found one of his fellowservants, which owed him IE approximately three months’ wages of a poor working man.an hundred pence: and he laid hands on him, and took him by the throat, saying, Pay me that thou owest.
And his fellowservant fell down at his feet, and besought him, saying, Have patience with me, and I will pay thee all.
And he would not: but went and cast him into prison, till he should pay the debt.
So when his fellowservants saw what was done, they were very GR distressed.sorry, and came and told unto their lord all that was done.
Then his lord, after that he had called him, said unto him, O thou wicked servant, I forgave thee all that TG Debt.debt, because thou desiredst me:
TG Ingratitude.Shouldest not thou also have had TG Compassion; Mercy.compassion on thy fellowservant, even as I had GR compassion.pity on thee?
And his lord was wroth, and delivered him to the tormentors, till he should pay all that was due unto him.
So likewise shall my heavenly Father do also unto you, if ye from your hearts TG Forgive.forgive not every one his brother their trespasses.

div.p: Jesus teaches about marriage and divorce—Eternal life is for those who keep the commandments—The Twelve Apostles will judge the house of Israel.

chapter.verse: And it came to pass, that when Jesus had finished these sayings, he departed from Galilee, and came into the coasts of Judæa beyond Jordan;
And great multitudes followed him; and he healed them there.
¶ The Pharisees also came unto him, tempting him, and saying unto him, Is it lawful for a man to GR divorce.put away his wife for every cause?
And he answered and said unto them, Have ye not read, that he which TG Man, Physical Creation of.made them at the beginning made them male and female,
And said, For this cause shall a man leave father and mother, and shall TG Family, Love within; Marriage, Celestial.cleave to his wife: and they twain shall be one flesh?
Wherefore they are no more twain, but one flesh. What therefore God hath TG Marriage, Marry.joined together, let not man GR divide, separate.put asunder.
They say unto him, Why did Moses then command to give a GR certificate of divorce.writing of TG Divorce.divorcement, and to put her away?
He saith unto them, Moses because of the TG Hardheartedness.hardness of your hearts suffered you to put away your TG Family, Eternal.wives: but from the beginning it was not so.

And I say unto you, Whosoever shall put away his wife, except it be for TG Fornication.fornication, and shall marry another, committeth TG Sexual Immorality.adultery: and whoso marrieth her which is put away doth commit adultery.
¶ His disciples say unto him, If the case of the man be so with his wife, it is not good to marry.
But he said unto them, All men cannot receive this saying, save they to whom it is given.

For there are some eunuchs, which were so born from their mother’s womb: and there are some eunuchs, which were made eunuchs of men: and there be eunuchs, which have made themselves eunuchs for the kingdom of heaven’s sake. He that is able to receive it, let him receive it.

¶ Then were there brought unto him little children, that he should put his TG Hands, Laying on of.hands on them, and pray: and the disciples rebuked JST Matt. 19:13 … them, saying, There is no need, for Jesus hath said, Such shall be saved.them.
But Jesus said, GR Allow, Permit.Suffer little D&C 137:10. TG Children; Salvation of Little Children.children, and forbid them not, to come unto me: for of such is the kingdom of heaven.
And he laid his hands on them, and departed thence.
¶ And, behold, one came and said unto him, Good Master, what good thing shall I do, that I may have TG Eternity.eternal life?
And he said unto him, Why callest thou me good? there is none TG God, Perfection of.good but one, that is, God: but if thou wilt enter into Prov. 4:22.life, keep the 1 Ne. 22:31.commandments.

He saith unto him, Which? Jesus said, Thou shalt do no TG Blood, Shedding of; Murder.murder, Thou shalt not commit TG Adulterer.adultery, Thou shalt not TG Stealing.steal, Thou shalt not bear Prov. 25:18.false witness,
TG Family, Children, Duties of; Honoring Father and Mother.Honour thy father and thy mother: and, Thou shalt TG Citizenship; Love.love thy TG Fellowshipping; Neighbor.neighbour as thyself.
The young man saith unto him, All these things have I kept from my youth up: what lack I yet?

Jesus said unto him, If thou wilt be TG Commitment; Perfection.perfect, go and sell that thou hast, and TG Almsgiving; Sacrifice.give to the TG Poor.poor, and thou shalt have TG Treasure.treasure in heaven: and come and Alma 22:15.follow me.
But when the young man heard that saying, he went away sorrowful: for he had GR many.great possessions.
¶ Then said Jesus unto his disciples, Verily I say unto you, That a 2 Ne. 9:30; Jacob 2:19 (16–19); D&C 6:7 (5–7).rich man shall GR with difficulty.hardly enter into the kingdom of heaven.
And again I say unto you, It is easier for a camel to go through the eye of a needle, than for a Prov. 18:11.rich man to enter into the kingdom of God.
When his disciples heard it, they were exceedingly amazed, saying, Who then can be saved?

JST Matt. 19:26 But Jesus beheld their thoughts, and said unto them, With men this is impossible; but if they will forsake all things for my sake, with God whatsoever things I speak are possible.But Jesus beheld them, and said unto them, With men this is impossible; but with God all things are Job 42:2. TG God, Power of.possible.
¶ Then answered Peter and said unto him, Behold, we have forsaken all, and followed thee; what shall we have therefore?

And Jesus said unto them, Verily I say unto you, That ye which have followed me, in the JST Matt. 19:28 … resurrection …regeneration when the Son of man shall sit in the throne of his glory, ye also shall sit upon twelve thrones, Morm. 3:18 (18–20); D&C 29:12. TG Apostles; Judgment, the Last.judging the twelve tribes of Israel.

And every one that hath D&C 19:36. TG Self-Sacrifice.forsaken houses, or brethren, or sisters, or father, or mother, or wife, or children, or lands, for my TG Name.name’s sake, shall receive an hundredfold, and shall inherit TG Exaltation.everlasting life.
But many that are D&C 29:30.first shall be last; and the last shall be first.

div.p: Jesus gives the parable of the laborers in the vineyard—He foretells His crucifixion and resurrection—He came to give His life as a ransom for many.

chapter.verse: For the kingdom of heaven is like unto a man that is an householder, which went out early in the morning to hire TG Labor.labourers into his vineyard.
And when he had agreed with the labourers for a penny a day, he sent them into his TG Vineyard of the Lord.vineyard.
And he went out about the third hour, and saw others standing Alma 38:12.idle in the marketplace,
And said unto them; Go ye also into the vineyard, and whatsoever is right I will give you. And they went their way.
Again he went out about the sixth and ninth hour, and did likewise.
And about the D&C 33:3 (1–3).eleventh hour he went out, and found others standing idle, and saith unto them, Why stand ye here all the day idle?
They say unto him, Because no man hath hired us. He saith unto them, Go ye also into the vineyard; and whatsoever is right, that shall ye receive.
So when even was come, the lord of the vineyard saith unto his steward, Call the D&C 39:13.labourers, and give them their TG Wages.hire, beginning from the last unto the first.
And when they came that were hired about the eleventh hour, they received every man a penny.
But when the first came, they supposed that they should have received more; and they likewise received every man a penny.
And when they had received it, they murmured against the goodman of the house,
Saying, These last have wrought but one hour, and thou hast made them equal unto us, which have borne the burden and heat of the day.
But he answered one of them, and said, Friend, I do thee no wrong: didst not thou agree with me for a penny?
Take that thine is, and go thy way: I will give unto this last, even as unto thee.
Is it not lawful for me to do what I will with mine own? Is thine eye evil, because I am good?
So the Jacob 5:63.last shall be first, and the first last: for many be TG Called of God; Foreordination.called, but few chosen.
¶ And Jesus going up to Jerusalem took the twelve disciples apart in the way, and said unto them,

Behold, we go up to Jerusalem; and the Son of man shall be TG Jesus Christ, Betrayal of.betrayed unto the chief priests and unto the scribes, and they shall condemn him to TG Jesus Christ, Death of.death,

And shall deliver him to the Gentiles to TG Mocking.mock, and to scourge, and to TG Jesus Christ, Crucifixion of; Martyrdom.crucify him: and the third day he shall TG Jesus Christ, Resurrection.rise again.
¶ Then came to him the mother of Zebedee’s children with her sons, worshipping him, and desiring a certain thing of him.
And he said unto her, What wilt thou? She saith unto him, Grant that these my two sons may sit, the one on thy right hand, and the other on the left, in thy kingdom.

But Jesus answered and said, Ye know not what ye ask. Are ye able to drink of the cup that I shall drink of, and to be baptized with the baptism that I am baptized with? They say unto him, We are able.

And he saith unto them, Ye shall drink indeed of my cup, and be baptized with the baptism that I am baptized with: but to sit on my right hand, and on my left, is not mine to give, but it shall be given to them for whom it is prepared of my TG Godhead.Father.
And when the ten heard it, they were moved with indignation against the two brethren.
But Jesus called them unto him, and said, Ye know that the princes of the Gentiles exercise dominion over them, and they that are great exercise authority upon them.
But it shall not be so among you: but whosoever will be TG Leadership.great among you, let him be your TG Priesthood, Magnifying Callings within.minister;
And whosoever GR desires to be.will be chief among you, let him be your TG Humility; Self-Sacrifice; Servant; Service.servant:

Even as the Moses 6:57.Son of man came not to be ministered unto, but to minister, and to TG God, Gifts of.give his life a D&C 138:2 (2–4). TG Jesus Christ, Atonement through; Jesus Christ, Redeemer; Redemption.ransom for many.
And as they departed from Jericho, a great multitude followed him.

¶ And, behold, two Matt. 9:28 (28–31); Mark 10:46 (46–52); John 9:1 (1–4); Mosiah 3:5; 3 Ne. 17:9 (7–10); D&C 84:69.blind men sitting by the way side, when they heard that Jesus passed by, cried out, saying, Have mercy on us, O Lord, thou Son of David.
And the multitude rebuked them, because they should hold their peace: but they cried the more, saying, Have mercy on us, O Lord, thou Son of David.
And Jesus stood still, and called them, and said, What will ye that I shall do unto you?
They say unto him, Lord, that our eyes may be opened.
So Jesus had TG Compassion.compassion on them, and touched their eyes: and immediately their eyes received TG Sight.sight, and they followed him.

div.p: Jesus rides in triumph into Jerusalem—He cleanses the temple, curses the fig tree, and discusses authority—He gives the parables of the two sons and the wicked husbandmen.

chapter.verse: And when they drew nigh unto Jerusalem, and were come to Bethphage, unto the mount of Olives, then sent Jesus two disciples,
Saying unto them, Go into the village GR in front of you.over against you, and straightway ye shall find an ass tied, and a colt with her: loose them, and bring them unto me.
And if any man say ought unto you, ye shall say, The Lord hath need of them; and straightway he will send them.
All this was done, that it might be fulfilled which was spoken by the prophet, saying,
Tell ye the daughter of Sion, Behold, thy Isa. 62:11; Zech. 9:9 (9–11).King cometh unto thee, meek, and sitting upon an ass, and a colt the foal of an ass.
And the disciples went, and did as Jesus commanded them,

JST Matt. 21:5 … and brought the colt, and put on it their clothes; and Jesus took the colt and sat thereon; and they followed him.And brought the ass, and the colt, and put on them their clothes, and they set him thereon.
And a very great multitude spread their garments in the way; others cut down branches from the trees, and strawed them in the way.
And the multitudes that went before, and that followed, cried, saying, Luke 19:40.Hosanna to the Son of David: Ps. 118:25–26.Blessed is he that cometh in the name of the Lord; Hosanna in the highest.
And when he was come into Jerusalem, all the city was moved, saying, Who is this?
And the GR crowds kept saying.multitude said, This is Jesus the prophet of Nazareth of Galilee.
¶ And Jesus went into the temple of God, and cast out all them that sold and bought in the temple, and overthrew the tables of the moneychangers, and the seats of them that sold doves,
And said unto them, It is written, My Ps. 27:4. TG Temple.house shall be called the house of TG Prayer.prayer; but ye have made it a Jer. 7:11.den of thieves.
And the blind and the lame came to him in the temple; and he healed them.

And when the chief priests and scribes saw the wonderful things that he did, and the JST Matt. 21:13 … children of the kingdom crying …children crying in the temple, and saying, Hosanna to the Son of David; they were sore displeased,
And said unto him, Hearest thou what these say? And Jesus saith unto them, Yea; have ye never read, Out of the mouth of babes and sucklings thou hast perfected praise?
¶ And he left them, and went out of the city into Bethany; and he lodged there.
Now in the morning as he returned into the city, he hungered.

And when he saw a fig tree GR by the road.in the way, he came to it, and found nothing thereon, but leaves only, and said unto it, Let no TG Vineyard of the Lord.fruit grow on thee henceforward for ever. And GR immediately; see also Mark 11:20–24.presently the fig tree withered away.
And when the disciples saw it, they marvelled, saying, How soon is the fig tree withered away!

Jesus answered and said unto them, Verily I say unto you, If ye have TG Faith.faith, and TG Doubt.doubt not, ye shall not only do this which is done to the fig tree, but also if ye shall say unto this TG God, Power of.mountain, Be thou removed, and be thou cast into the sea; it shall be done.
And all TG Blessing.things, whatsoever ye shall 3 Ne. 18:20; Morm. 9:21.ask in TG Prayer.prayer, believing, ye shall receive.

¶ And when he was come into the temple, the chief priests and the elders of the people came unto him as he was teaching, and said, By what TG Authority.authority doest thou these things? and who gave thee this authority?
And Jesus answered and said unto them, I also will ask you one thing, which if ye tell me, I in like wise will tell you by what TG Jesus Christ, Authority of.authority I do these things.
The baptism of John, whence was it? from heaven, or of men? And they reasoned with themselves, saying, If we shall say, From heaven; he will say unto us, Why did ye not then believe him?
But if we shall say, Of men; we fear the people; for all hold John as a Matt. 11:9; 14:5; Luke 20:6.prophet.
And they answered Jesus, and said, We cannot tell. And he said unto them, Neither tell I you by what authority I do these things.
¶ But what think ye? A certain man had two sons; and he came to the first, and said, Son, go Jacob 5:71 (70–71).work to day in my vineyard.
He answered and said, GR I don’t desire to go.I will not: but afterward he repented, and went.
And he came to the second, and said likewise. And he answered and said, I go, sir: and TG Hypocrisy.went D&C 41:5.not.
Whether of them twain did the will of his father? They say unto him, The first. Jesus saith unto them, Verily I say unto you, That the publicans and the harlots go into the kingdom of God before you.

For John came unto you in the way of TG Righteousness.righteousness, and ye TG Unbelief.believed him not: but the Luke 3:12; 7:29 (28–30).publicans and the harlots believed him: JST Matt. 21:32–34 … and ye, afterward, when ye had seen me, repented not, that ye might believe him. For he that believed not John concerning me, cannot believe me, except he first repent. And except ye repent, the preaching of John shall condemn you in the day of judgment …and ye, when ye had seen it, repented not afterward, that ye might believe him.

¶ JST Matt. 21:34–35 … And again, hear another parable; for unto you that believe not, I speak in parables; that your unrighteousness may be rewarded unto you. Behold, there was …Hear another parable: There was a certain householder, which planted a vineyard, and hedged it round about, and digged a winepress in it, and built a tower, and let it out to TG Stewardship; Watchman.husbandmen, and went into a far country:
And when the time of the fruit drew near, he sent his servants to the husbandmen, that they might receive the fruits of it.
And the husbandmen took his Matt. 23:30 (29–38).servants, and TG Prophets, Rejection of.beat one, and killed another, and stoned another.
Again, he sent other servants more than the first: and they did unto them likewise.
But last of all he sent unto them his son, saying, They will reverence my son.
But when the husbandmen saw the son, they said among themselves, This is the John 11:53 (47–53); Heb. 1:2.heir; come, let us kill him, and let us seize on his inheritance.
And they caught him, and cast him out of the vineyard, and slew him.
When the lord therefore of the vineyard cometh, what will he do unto those husbandmen?
They say unto him, He will miserably destroy those wicked men, and will let out his TG Vineyard of the Lord.vineyard unto other husbandmen, which shall render him the fruits in their seasons.

Jesus saith unto them, Did ye never read in the scriptures, The TG Cornerstone; Jesus Christ, Prophecies about; Rock.stone which the builders Luke 9:22; 1 Pet. 2:7.rejected, the same is become the head of the Zech. 10:4.corner: this is the Lord’s doing, and it is marvellous in our eyes?

Therefore say I unto you, The Isa. 5:5 (1–7); Luke 14:24 (21–24); Acts 13:46; 1 Ne. 13:26; D&C 90:3 (1–5).kingdom of God shall be Dan. 2:44.taken from you, and Isa. 48:11.given to a TG Nations.nation bringing forth the fruits thereof.
Isa. 8:14 (13–15); Luke 2:34; Rom. 9:32 (28–33); 1 Pet. 2:8 (6–8).And whosoever shall fall on this stone shall be broken: but on whomsoever it shall fall, it will grind him to powder.
JST Matt. 21:47–56 (Appendix).And when the chief priests and Pharisees had heard his parables, they perceived that he spake of them.
But when they sought to lay hands on him, they feared the multitude, because they took him for a prophet.

div.p: Jesus gives the parable of the marriage of the king’s son—Pay tribute to Cæsar and to God—Worldly marriages endure in this life only—The first commandment is to love the Lord—Jesus asks, What think ye of Christ?

chapter.verse: And Jesus answered and spake unto them again by parables, and said,
The kingdom of heaven is like unto a certain king, which GR gave a wedding celebration.made a Luke 5:34; Rev. 19:7 (7–9); 21:2; D&C 65:3.marriage for his son,
And sent forth his servants to call them that were bidden to the wedding: and GR they did not want to come.they would not come.
Again, he sent forth other servants, saying, Tell them which are bidden, Behold, I have prepared my dinner: my oxen and my fatlings are killed, and all things are ready: come unto the marriage.
But they made light of it, and went their ways, one to his farm, another to his merchandise:
And the remnant took his servants, and entreated them spitefully, and slew them.
But when the king heard thereof, he was wroth: and he sent forth his armies, and destroyed those murderers, and burned up their city.
Then saith he to his servants, The wedding is ready, but they which were bidden were not worthy.
Go ye therefore into the highways, and as many as ye shall find, bid to the marriage.
So those servants went out into the highways, and Matt. 13:47 (47–53).gathered together all as many as they found, both bad and good: and the wedding was furnished with guests.
¶ And when the king came in to see the guests, he saw there a man which had not on a wedding Rev. 19:8.garment:
And he saith unto him, Friend, how camest thou in hither not having a wedding garment? And he was speechless.

Then said the king to the servants, Bind him hand and foot, and take him away, and cast him into Matt. 8:12 (11–12); D&C 77:8; 133:72 (71–73).outer darkness; there shall be weeping and gnashing of teeth.
For many are D&C 121:34 (34–40).called, but few are JST Matt. 22:14 … chosen; wherefore all do not have on the wedding garment.chosen.
¶ Then went the Pharisees, and took counsel how they might Mark 12:13 (13–17); Luke 11:54; 20:20 (19–26).entangle him in his talk.

And they sent out unto him their disciples with the Herodians, saying, Master, we know that thou art true, and teachest the way of God in truth, IE you court no man’s favor.neither carest thou for any man: for thou Prov. 24:23.regardest not the person of men.
Tell us therefore, What thinkest thou? Is it D&C 58:22 (20–23).lawful to give tribute unto Cæsar, or not?
But Jesus perceived their wickedness, and said, Why GR are you testing. TG Test.tempt ye me, ye hypocrites?
Shew me the tribute money. And they brought unto him a penny.
And he saith unto them, Whose is this image and superscription?
They say unto him, Cæsar’s. Then saith he unto them, TG Citizenship.Render therefore unto TG Governments.Cæsar the things which are Cæsar’s; and unto God the things that are God’s.
When they had heard these words, they marvelled, and left him, and went their way.
¶ The same day came to him the Matt. 16:12 (1, 6, 11–12); Acts 23:8.Sadducees, which say that there is no resurrection, and asked him,
Saying, Master, Moses said, If a man die, having no children, his Deut. 25:5; Mark 12:19.brother shall Ruth 3:13.marry his wife, and raise up seed unto his brother.
Now there were with us seven brethren: and the first, when he had married a wife, deceased, and, having no issue, left his wife unto his brother:
Likewise the second also, and the third, unto the seventh.
And last of all the woman died also.
Therefore in the resurrection whose wife shall she be of the seven? for they all had her.
Jesus answered and said unto them, Ye do err, not knowing the D&C 138:1 (1–4). TG Jesus Christ, Teaching Mode of; Scriptures, Study of.scriptures, nor the TG God, Power of.power of God.
For in the TG Resurrection.resurrection they neither TG Marriage, Marry; Marriage, Temporal.marry, nor are given in marriage, but are as the D&C 131:4 (1–4); 132:16 (15–17).angels of God in heaven.
But as touching the resurrection of the dead, have ye not read that which was spoken unto you by God, saying,
I am the Gen. 32:9; 1 Ne. 19:10; D&C 136:21.God of Abraham, and the God of Isaac, and the God of Jacob? God is not the God of the dead, but of the living.
And when the multitude heard this, they were astonished at his doctrine.
¶ But when the Pharisees had heard that he had put the Sadducees to silence, they were gathered together.
Then one of them, which was a lawyer, asked him a question, TG Test.tempting him, and saying,
Master, which is the great commandment in the law?
Jesus said unto him, Thou shalt TG Dedication; God, Love of; Love.love the Lord thy God with all thy TG Heart.heart, and with all thy soul, and with all thy TG Mind.mind.
This is the first and great TG Commandments of God.commandment.
And the second is like unto it, Thou shalt TG Love.love thy neighbour as thyself.
On these two commandments hang all the Mark 12:33 (13–37); Rom. 13:10 (8–10). TG Law of Moses.law and the prophets.
¶ While the Pharisees were gathered together, Jesus asked them,
Saying, What think ye of Christ? whose son is he? They say unto him, The Son of TG Jesus Christ, Davidic Descent of.David.
He saith unto them, How then doth David in spirit call him Lord, saying,
The Lord said unto my Lord, Sit thou on my right hand, till I make thine enemies thy footstool?
If David then call him Lord, how is he his son?
And no man was able to answer him a word, neither GR dared.durst any man from that day forth ask him any more questions.

div.p: Jesus pronounces woes upon the scribes and Pharisees—They will be held responsible for killing the prophets—They will not escape the damnation of hell.

chapter.verse: Then spake Jesus to the multitude, and to his disciples,
Saying, The TG Scribe.scribes and the Pharisees sit in Moses’ The Greek word connotes a chair of judgment and instruction.seat:
All therefore whatsoever they bid you observe, that observe and do; but do not ye after their works: for they say, and do not.
For they bind heavy burdens and grievous to be borne, and lay them on men’s shoulders; but they themselves will not move them with one of their fingers.
But all their works they do for to be seen of men: they GR enlarge their phylacteries.make broad their Ex. 13:9 (9, 16); Deut. 6:8.phylacteries, and enlarge the Num. 15:38.borders of their garments,
And love the Luke 11:43; 14:7.uppermost rooms at feasts, and the chief seats in the synagogues,
And greetings in the markets, and to be called of men, Rabbi, JST Matt. 23:4 … Rabbi, (which is master).Rabbi.
But be not ye called Hebrew and Aramaic: Master, or, My master.Rabbi: for one is your Col. 4:1 (1–11).Master, even Christ; and all ye are brethren.

JST Matt. 23:6 And call no one your creator upon the earth, or your heavenly Father; for one is your creator and heavenly Father, even he who is in heaven.And call no man your father upon the earth: for one is your Father, which is in heaven.
Neither be ye called masters: for one is your Master, JST Matt. 23:7 … even he whom your heavenly Father sent, which is Christ; for he hath sent him among you that ye might have life. even Christ.
But he that is Mark 10:44 (42–45); Luke 22:24 (24–30). TG Leadership.greatest among you shall be your TG Servant.servant.
And whosoever shall TG Haughtiness; Pride.exalt himself shall be D&C 49:10.abased; and he that shall James 1:9 (8–16); D&C 112:3; 124:114. TG Humility.humble himself shall be exalted.

¶ But woe unto you, TG Scribe.scribes and TG Apostasy of Israel.Pharisees, GR actors.hypocrites! for ye shut up the kingdom of heaven against men: for ye neither go in yourselves, neither suffer ye them that are entering to go in.
Woe unto you, scribes and Pharisees, hypocrites! for ye TG Injustice.devour widows’ houses, and for a pretence make long prayer: therefore ye shall receive the greater TG Damnation.damnation.

Woe unto you, scribes and Pharisees, hypocrites! for ye compass sea and land to make one proselyte, and when he is made, ye make him twofold more the child of hell JST Matt. 23:12 … than he was before, like unto yourselves.than yourselves.
Woe unto you, ye TG Spiritual Blindness.blind guides, which say, Whosoever shall swear by the temple, it is nothing; but whosoever shall swear by the gold of the temple, he is a debtor!
Ye fools and blind: for whether is greater, the gold, or the temple that sanctifieth the gold?
And, Whosoever shall swear by the altar, it is nothing; but whosoever sweareth by the gift that is upon it, he is guilty.
Ye fools and blind: for whether is greater, the gift, or the altar that sanctifieth the gift?
Whoso therefore shall swear by the altar, sweareth by it, and by all things thereon.
And whoso shall swear by the temple, sweareth by it, and by him that dwelleth therein.
And he that shall swear by heaven, sweareth by the throne of God, and by him that sitteth thereon.

Woe unto you, scribes and Pharisees, TG Hypocrisy.hypocrites! for ye pay TG Tithing.tithe of mint and GR dill.anise and cummin, and have GR abandoned.omitted the weightier matters of the law, TG Justice.judgment, TG Mercy.mercy, and faith: these ought ye to have done, and not to leave the other undone.

Ye blind guides, which strain at a gnat, and swallow a JST Matt. 23:21 … camel; who make yourselves appear unto men that ye would not commit the least sin, and yet ye yourselves, transgress the whole law.camel.

Woe unto you, scribes and Pharisees, hypocrites! for ye make clean the outside of the cup and of the platter, but within they are full of GR rapacity, greediness.extortion and GR self-indulgence, lack of self-control. TG Temperance.excess.
Thou blind Pharisee, TG Purification; Wash.cleanse first that which is within the cup and platter, that the outside of them may be clean also.

Woe unto you, scribes and Pharisees, hypocrites! for ye are like unto GR whitewashed tombs. Acts 23:3.whited Luke 11:44.sepulchres, which indeed appear TG Beauty.beautiful outward, but are within full of dead men’s bones, and of all TG Cleanliness.uncleanness.
Even so ye also outwardly appear D&C 10:37.righteous unto men, but within ye are full of TG Guile.hypocrisy and iniquity.
Woe unto you, scribes and Pharisees, hypocrites! because ye build the tombs of the prophets, and garnish the sepulchres of the righteous,
And say, If we had been in the days of our fathers, we would not have been partakers with them in the blood of the Matt. 21:35 (33–42).prophets.
Wherefore ye be witnesses unto yourselves, that ye are the children of them which TG Martyrdom.killed the TG Prophets, Rejection of.prophets.
Fill ye up then the Judg. 2:19.measure of your Acts 7:51 (51–52); Hel. 13:25 (25–29).fathers.
Ye serpents, ye Matt. 3:7; 12:34.generation of vipers, how can ye escape the TG Damnation; Punish.damnation of hell?

¶ Wherefore, behold, I send unto you D&C 1:38; 43:25.prophets, and wise men, and TG Scribe.scribes: and some of them ye shall kill and crucify; and some of them shall ye scourge in your synagogues, and persecute them from city to city:

That upon you may come all the righteous TG Martyrdom.blood shed upon the earth, from the blood of TG Righteousness.righteous Abel unto the blood of Zacharias son of Barachias, whom ye slew between the Luke 11:51.temple and the altar.

Verily I say unto you, All these things shall come upon this JST Matt. 23:33–35 … generation. Ye bear testimony against your fathers, when ye, yourselves, are partakers of the same wickedness. Behold your fathers did it through ignorance, but ye do not; wherefore, their sins shall be upon your heads.generation.

O TG Jerusalem.Jerusalem, Jerusalem, thou that TG Prophets, Rejection of.killest the prophets, and stonest them which are sent unto thee, how often GR have I desired to gather.would I have 3 Ne. 10:6 (4–6); D&C 29:2; 43:24 (24–25). TG Israel, Gathering of.gathered thy children together, even as a hen gathereth her chickens under her wings, and ye D&C 138:21 (21–22). TG Rebellion; Stubbornness.would not!
Behold, your house is left unto you Ps. 69:25; Jer. 12:7; 22:5.desolate.
JS—M 1:1.For I say unto you, Ye shall not see me henceforth, till ye shall say, Ps. 118:26.Blessed is he that cometh in the name of the Lord.

div.p: Jesus foretells the doom of Jerusalem and the destruction of the temple—Great calamities will precede His Second Coming—He gives the parable of the fig tree.

chapter.verse: And Jesus went out, and departed from the temple: and his disciples came to him for to JS—M 1:2.shew him the buildings of the D&C 45:20 (15–21). TG Temple.temple.

And Jesus said unto them, D&C 45:18.See ye not all these things? verily I say unto you, There shall not be left here one Luke 19:44 (41–44); JS—M 1:3 (1–55).stone upon another, that shall not be thrown down.

¶ And as he sat upon the mount of Olives, the disciples came unto him privately, saying, Tell us, when shall these things be? and what shall be the Luke 21:7 (7–36); D&C 45:16 (16–75).sign of thy coming, and of the end of the JS—M 1:4 adds “or the destruction of the wicked, which is the end of the world.” D&C 45:22. TG Last Days; World, End of.world?
And Jesus answered and said unto them, Take heed that no man Col. 2:18 (16–23); D&C 49:23 (22–24). TG Lying.deceive you.
For many shall come in my TG Name.name, saying, I am TG False Christs.Christ; and shall Mark 13:6. TG Apostasy of the Early Christian Church.deceive many.
And ye shall hear of TG War.wars and rumours of wars: see that ye be not GR frightened. D&C 45:35 (34–35).troubled: for all these things must come to pass, but the end is not yet.
For TG Last Days; Nations.nation shall rise against nation, and kingdom against kingdom: and there shall be TG Drought.famines, and pestilences, and earthquakes, in divers places.
All these are the beginning of TG Sorrow.sorrows.
Then shall they deliver you up to be afflicted, and shall TG Martyrdom.kill you: and ye shall be TG Persecution.hated of all nations GR on account of my name.for my name’s sake.
And then shall many be Isa. 8:14 (14–15); Matt. 11:6.offended, and shall betray one another, and shall hate one another.
And many TG Apostasy of the Early Christian Church; False Prophets.false prophets shall rise, and shall deceive many.
And because D&C 45:27.iniquity shall abound, the love of many shall wax TG Apostasy of Individuals.cold.
But he that shall TG Perseverance; Steadfastness.endure unto the end, the same shall be saved.

And this TG Gospel; Restoration of the Gospel.gospel of the TG Kingdom of God, on Earth.kingdom shall be preached in all the world for a witness unto all nations; and then shall the D&C 29:23; 43:31.end come.

When ye therefore shall see the TG Abomination of Desolation.abomination of desolation, spoken of by Daniel the prophet, stand in the TG Jerusalem.holy place, (whoso readeth, let him D&C 57:9.understand:)
Then let them which be in Judæa TG Israel, Scattering of.flee into the mountains:
Let him which is on the housetop not come down to take any thing out of his house:
Neither let him which is in the field return back to take his clothes.
And Luke 23:29.woe unto them that are with child, and to them that give suck in those days!
But pray ye that your flight be not in the winter, neither on the sabbath day:
For then shall be great JS—M 1:18. TG Tribulation.tribulation, such as was not since the beginning of the world to this time, no, nor ever shall be.
And except those days should be shortened, there should no flesh be saved: but for the elect’s sake those TG Time.days shall be shortened.
Then if any man shall say unto you, Lo, here is Christ, or there; D&C 49:22.believe it not.

For there shall arise TG Apostasy of the Early Christian Church.false TG False Christs.Christs, and TG False Prophets.false prophets, and shall shew great Rev. 16:14 (13–14). TG Signs; Sign Seekers.signs and wonders; insomuch that, if it were possible, they shall Rev. 13:14 (11–18). TG Deceit.deceive the very D&C 29:7 (7–8); 84:34 (33–34).elect.
Behold, I have told you before.
Wherefore if they shall say unto you, Behold, he is in the desert; go not forth: behold, he is in the D&C 49:22 (22–23).secret chambers; believe it not.
For as the D&C 45:36 (36–44).lightning cometh out of the Ezek. 43:2.east, and shineth even unto the west; so shall also the coming of the Son of man be.
For wheresoever the carcase is, there will the eagles be D&C 29:8 (7–8); JS—M 1:27.gathered together.

¶ Immediately after the tribulation of those TG Day of the Lord.days shall the TG Astronomy.sun be Isa. 13:10; Ezek. 32:7; 34:12 (11–19); Joel 2:10; Rev. 6:12.darkened, and the moon shall not give her light, and the stars shall fall from heaven, and the powers of the heavens shall be shaken:

And then shall appear the D&C 88:93. TG Jesus Christ, Second Coming.sign of the Son of man in TG Heaven.heaven: and then shall all the tribes of the earth Zech. 12:12; Rev. 1:7 (7–8).mourn, and they shall see the TG Jesus Christ, Son of Man.Son of man coming in the clouds of heaven with TG Jesus Christ, Power of.power and great TG Jesus Christ, Glory of.glory.

And he shall send his TG Angels.angels with a great sound of a Isa. 27:13; 1 Thes. 4:16; D&C 49:23 (23–28).trumpet, and they shall gather together his Ps. 147:2 (1–20); JS—M 1:27. TG Election.elect from the four winds, from one end of Deut. 30:4.heaven to the other.
Now learn a parable of the D&C 35:16.fig tree; When his branch is yet tender, and putteth forth leaves, ye know that summer is nigh:
So likewise ye, when ye shall see all these things, know that GR He is near.it is near, even at the D&C 45:63.doors.
Verily I say unto you, This JS—M 1:34 (31–35).generation shall not pass, till all these Isa. 10:22 (21–34); D&C 1:7.things be D&C 45:21.fulfilled.
Heaven and earth shall D&C 29:23 (22–29).pass away, but my D&C 1:38 (37–39).words shall not Josh. 21:45; 1 Kgs. 8:56; D&C 45:23.pass away.
¶ But of that D&C 39:21; 49:7.day and TG Jesus Christ, Second Coming.hour knoweth no man, no, not the TG Angels.angels of heaven, but my Father only.
But as the days of IE Noah. Gen. 7:1; Luke 17:27 (26–27); 3 Ne. 22:9.Noe were, so shall also the coming of the Son of man be.
For as in the days that were before the TG Worldliness.flood they were eating and drinking, marrying and giving in marriage, until the day that Noe entered into the ark,
And knew not until the flood came, and took them all away; so shall also the coming of the Son of man be.
Then shall two be in the field; the one shall be taken, and the other left.
Two women shall be grinding at the mill; the one shall be taken, and the other left.
¶ TG Millennium, Preparing a People for; Watch.Watch therefore: for ye know not what hour your Lord doth come.
But know this, that if the goodman of the house had known in what watch the thief would come, he would have watched, and would not have suffered his house to be Ex. 22:2 (1–4).broken up.
Therefore be ye also TG Procrastination.ready: for in such an Luke 12:40; D&C 45:2; 51:20.hour GR when you do not expect Him.as ye think not the Son of man cometh.

Who then is a D&C 51:19.faithful and D&C 72:4.wise D&C 58:26 (26–29); 84:86 (85–86); 107:100 (99–100). TG Stewardship.servant, whom his lord hath made ruler over his household, to give them meat in due season?
Blessed is that TG Servant.servant, whom his lord when he cometh shall find so doing.
Verily I say unto you, That he shall make him ruler over all his goods.
But and if that evil servant shall say in his heart, My lord D&C 45:26.delayeth his coming;
And shall begin to smite his fellowservants, and to eat and drink with the drunken;
The lord of that servant shall come in a day when he looketh not for him, and in an hour that he is not aware of,
And shall cut him asunder, and appoint him his portion with the hypocrites: there shall be weeping and gnashing of teeth.

div.p: Jesus gives the parables of the ten virgins, the talents, and the sheep and the goats.

chapter.verse: JST Matt. 25:1 And then, at that day, before the Son of man comes, the kingdom of …Then shall the kingdom of heaven be likened unto ten D&C 45:56 (56–59); 63:54.virgins, which took their Lev. 24:2.lamps, and went forth to meet the bridegroom.
And five of them were wise, and five were foolish.
They that were foolish took their lamps, and took no oil with them:
But the wise took oil in their vessels with their lamps.
While the bridegroom tarried, they all D&C 35:21.slumbered and TG Sleep.slept.
And at midnight there was a cry made, Behold, the D&C 88:92; 133:10.bridegroom cometh; go ye out to meet him.
Then all those virgins arose, and trimmed their Luke 12:35; D&C 33:17.lamps.
And the foolish said unto the wise, Give us of your oil; for our lamps GR are going out.are gone out.
But the wise answered, saying, Not so; lest there be not enough for us and you: but go ye rather to them that sell, and buy for yourselves.
And while they went to buy, the bridegroom came; and they that were TG Millennium, Preparing a People for; Procrastination.ready went in with him to the marriage: and the door was GR locked.shut.
Afterward came also the other virgins, saying, Lord, Lord, open to us.
But he answered and said, JST Matt. 25:11 … Verily I say unto you, Ye know me not.Verily I say unto you, I Matt. 7:23.know you not.
TG Watch.Watch therefore, for ye know neither the day nor the hour wherein the Son of man cometh.
¶ For the kingdom of heaven is as a man travelling into a far country, who called his own servants, and delivered unto them his goods.
And unto one he Mark 4:24 (23–25).gave five TG Talents; Work, Value of.talents, to another two, and to another one; to every man according to his several ability; and straightway took his journey.
Then he that had received the five talents went and traded with the same, and made them other five talents.
And likewise he that had received two, he also gained other two.
But he that had received one went and digged in the earth, and hid his lord’s money.
After a long time the lord of those servants cometh, and GR settled accounts. D&C 72:3 (3–5).reckoneth with them.
And so he that had received five talents came and brought other five talents, saying, Lord, thou GR entrustedst.deliveredst unto me five talents: behold, I have gained beside them five talents more.

His lord said unto him, Well done, thou good and faithful TG Servant.servant: thou hast been Luke 16:10; D&C 132:53.faithful over a few things, I will make thee Rev. 3:21; D&C 132:20. TG Leadership; Stewardship.ruler over many things: enter thou into the TG Joy.joy of thy lord.
He also that had received two talents came and said, Lord, thou deliveredst unto me two talents: behold, I have gained two other talents beside them.

His lord said unto him, Well done, good and D&C 52:13. TG Trustworthiness.faithful servant; thou hast been faithful over a few things, I will make thee ruler over many things: enter thou into the joy of thy lord.
Then he which had received the one talent came and said, Lord, I knew thee that thou art an GR strict.hard man, reaping where thou hast not sown, and gathering where thou hast not strawed:
And I was D&C 60:2 (2–3, 13).afraid, and went and hid thy talent in the earth: lo, there thou hast that is thine.
His lord answered and said unto him, Thou wicked and TG Laziness.slothful servant, thou knewest that I TG Harvest.reap where I sowed not, and gather where I have not strawed:
Thou oughtest therefore to have put my money to the exchangers, and then at my coming I should have received mine own with TG Usury.usury.
Take therefore the D&C 82:18.talent from him, and give it unto him which hath ten talents.

For unto every one that hath shall be Matt. 13:12.given, and he shall have D&C 82:3.abundance: but from him that hath not shall be Alma 12:11 (9–11); D&C 1:33; 60:3 (2–3).taken away even that which he hath.
And cast ye the unprofitable servant into outer darkness: there shall be weeping and gnashing of teeth.
¶ When the TG Jesus Christ, Son of Man.Son of man shall come in his TG Jesus Christ, Second Coming; Millennium.glory, and all the holy angels with him, then shall he sit upon the throne of his glory:

And before him shall be gathered all TG Nations.nations: and he shall TG Jesus Christ, Judge; Judgment, the Last; Separation.separate them one from another, as a TG Shepherd.shepherd divideth his TG Sheep.sheep from the goats:
And he shall set the sheep on his D&C 29:27.right hand, but the goats on the left.

Then shall the King say unto them on his Moses 7:56 (56–57).right hand, Come, ye TG Blessing.blessed of my Father, TG Exaltation; Reward.inherit the Luke 12:32; Rev. 22:5.kingdom prepared for you from the foundation of the world:
For I was an TG Poor.hungred, and ye TG Almsgiving; Generosity.gave me meat: I was thirsty, and ye gave me drink: I was a TG Hospitality; Stranger.stranger, and ye took me in:
Naked, and ye clothed me: I was sick, and ye GR took care of, cared for.visited me: I was in Heb. 13:3.prison, and ye came unto me.
Then shall the righteous answer him, saying, Lord, when saw we thee an hungred, and fed thee? or thirsty, and gave thee drink?
When saw we thee a stranger, and took thee in? or naked, and clothed thee?
Or when saw we thee TG Health.sick, or in prison, and came unto thee?

And the King shall answer and say unto them, Verily I say unto you, Inasmuch as ye have Mosiah 2:17; D&C 42:38.done it unto one of the TG Good Works; Neighbor; Poor.least of these my TG Welfare.brethren, ye have done it unto me.

Then shall he say also unto them on the D&C 19:5.left hand, D&C 29:28.Depart from me, ye GR who have come under a curse. (See also 2 Ne. 9:16; D&C 76:31–38, 43–48.) TG Curse.cursed, into everlasting TG Hell.fire, GR which has been prepared.prepared for the devil and his angels:
For I was an Job 22:7 (6–7).hungred, and ye gave me no meat: I was thirsty, and ye gave me no drink:
I was a stranger, and ye took me not in: naked, and ye clothed me not: sick, and in prison, and ye visited me not.
Then shall they also answer him, saying, Lord, when saw we thee an hungred, or athirst, or a stranger, or naked, or sick, or in prison, and did not minister unto thee?
Then shall he answer them, saying, Verily I say unto you, Inasmuch as ye did it not to one of the Prov. 14:31.least of these, ye did it not to me.
And these shall go away into D&C 19:11 (6–12).everlasting TG Damnation; Punish.punishment: but the TG Righteousness.righteous into TG Resurrection.life TG Eternal Life.eternal.

div.p: Jesus is anointed—He keeps the Passover and institutes the sacrament—He suffers in Gethsemane, is betrayed by Judas, and is taken before Caiaphas—Peter denies that he knows Jesus.

chapter.verse: And it came to pass, when Jesus had finished all these sayings, he said unto his disciples,
Ye know that after two days is the feast of the TG Passover.passover, and the Son of man is TG Jesus Christ, Betrayal of.betrayed to be TG Martyrdom.crucified.
Then assembled together the chief priests, and the scribes, and the elders of the people, unto the palace of the high priest, who was called John 11:49 (47–52).Caiaphas,
And GR plotted.consulted that they might take Jesus by GR treachery, cunning. Matt. 27:20 (11–26).subtilty, and Mark 14:1 (1–2); Luke 22:2 (1–2).kill him.
But they said, Not on the feast day, lest there be an TG Rioting and Reveling.uproar among the people.
¶ Now when Jesus was in Bethany, in the house of Simon the leper,
There came unto him a woman having an alabaster box of very precious John 11:2.ointment, and poured it on his head, as he sat at meat.
But when his disciples saw it, they had indignation, saying, To what purpose is this TG Waste.waste?
For this ointment might have been sold for much, and given to the poor.
When Jesus understood it, he said unto them, Why trouble ye the woman? for she hath TG Benevolence.wrought a good work upon me.
For ye have the poor always with you; but me ye have not always.
For in that she hath poured this ointment on my body, she did it GR to prepare me for my burial.for my burial.
Verily I say unto you, Wheresoever this gospel shall be preached in the whole world, there shall also this, that this woman hath done, be told for a memorial of her.
¶ Then one of the twelve, called Judas Iscariot, went unto the chief priests,
And said unto them, What will ye give me, and I will deliver him unto you? And they covenanted with him for Ex. 21:32 (28–32).thirty pieces of silver.
And from that time he sought opportunity to TG Jesus Christ, Betrayal of.betray him.
¶ Now the first day of the feast of TG Bread, Unleavened.unleavened bread the disciples came to Jesus, saying unto him, Where wilt thou that we prepare for thee to eat the TG Passover.passover?
And he said, Go into the city GR to a certain man.to such a man, and say unto him, The Master saith, My John 7:6.time is at hand; I will keep the passover at thy house with my disciples.
And the disciples did as Jesus had appointed them; and they made ready the Ex. 12:27 (1–51).passover.
Now when the even was come, he sat down with the twelve.
And as they did eat, he said, Verily I say unto you, that one of you shall betray me.
And they were exceeding sorrowful, and began every one of them to say unto him, Lord, is it I?
And he answered and said, He that dippeth his hand with me in the dish, the same shall betray me.

The Son of man goeth as it is TG Jesus Christ, Prophecies about.written of him: but woe unto that man by whom the TG Jesus Christ, Son of Man.Son of man is betrayed! GR it would have been good.it had been good for that man if he had not been Luke 22:22; Acts 2:23.born.
Then Judas, which betrayed him, answered and said, Master, is it I? He said unto him, Thou hast said.

¶ JST Matt. 26:22, 24–25 (Appendix).And as they were eating, Jesus took TG Bread; Bread of Life; Jesus Christ, Types of, in Memory.bread, JST Matt. 26:22 … and brake it, and blessed it, and gave to his disciples, and said, Take, eat; this is in remembrance of my body which I give a ransom for you.and blessed it, and brake it, and gave it to the disciples, and said, Take, eat; this is my TG Sacrament.body.
And he took the 1 Cor. 10:16 (16–17).cup, and gave thanks, and gave it to them, saying, GR All of you drink from it. TG Sacrament.Drink ye all of it;

For this is my Ex. 24:8 (3–8); Deut. 32:14; Heb. 9:16 (15–22). TG Jesus Christ, Atonement through.blood of the new GR covenant. TG Covenants.testament, which is shed for many for the TG Jesus Christ, Redeemer; Remission of Sins.remission of sins.

But I say unto you, I will not Mark 14:25; Luke 22:18.drink henceforth of this fruit of the vine, until that day when I JST Matt. 26:26 … shall come and drink it … D&C 27:5 (5–14).drink it new with you in my Father’s kingdom.
And when they had sung an TG Singing.hymn, they went out into the mount of Olives.

Then saith Jesus unto them, All ye shall be TG Offense.offended because of me this night: for it is written, I will GR strike, hit, slay.smite the Zech. 13:7.shepherd, and the sheep of the flock shall be scattered abroad.
But after I am TG Jesus Christ, Resurrection.risen again, I will go before you into Galilee.
Peter answered and said unto him, Though all men shall be offended because of thee, yet will I never be Luke 22:33–34, 56–62.offended.
Jesus said unto him, Verily I say unto thee, That this night, before the cock crow, thou shalt deny me thrice.
Peter said unto him, Though I should die with thee, yet will I not deny thee. Likewise also said all the disciples.
¶ Then cometh Jesus with them unto a place called TG Jesus Christ, Atonement through.Gethsemane, and saith unto the disciples, Sit ye here, while I go and Matt. 14:23; Luke 6:12; 9:28.pray yonder.
And he took with him Peter and the two sons of Zebedee, and GR began to be distressed and troubled.began to be sorrowful and very heavy.
Then saith he unto them, My soul is GR deeply grieved. TG Pain; Sorrow.exceeding sorrowful, even unto death: tarry ye here, and GR stay awake.watch with me.

And he went a little further, and fell on his face, and Heb. 5:7. TG Prayer.prayed, saying, O my TG God the Father, Elohim.Father, if it be possible, let this Mark 14:36 (32–42); Mosiah 3:7; D&C 19:18 (16–19).cup pass from me: nevertheless not as I GR wish, desire. Isa. 50:5. TG Agency.will, but as TG Godhead.thou TG Obedience; Self-Mastery. wilt .
And he cometh unto the disciples, and findeth them TG Sleep.asleep, and saith unto Peter, What, GR are you so powerless that you could not stay awake with me …could ye not watch with me one hour?
TG Watch.Watch and TG Prayer.pray, that ye enter not into TG Temptation.temptation: the spirit indeed is willing, but the flesh is weak.
He went away again the second time, and prayed, saying, O my Father, if this cup may not pass away from me, except I drink it, thy Acts 21:14. TG God, Will of.will be done.
And he came and found them asleep again: for their eyes were heavy.
And he left them, and went away again, and prayed the third time, saying the same words.

Then cometh he to his disciples, and saith unto them, Sleep on now, and take your rest: behold, the hour is at hand, and the Son of man is TG Jesus Christ, Betrayal of.betrayed into the hands of sinners.
Rise, let us be going: behold, he is at hand that doth betray me.
¶ And while he yet spake, lo, Judas, one of the twelve, came, and with him a great multitude with swords and staves, from the chief priests and elders of the people.
Now he that betrayed him gave them a sign, saying, Whomsoever I shall kiss, that same is he: hold him fast.
And GR immediately.forthwith he came to Jesus, and said, Hail, master; and kissed him.
And Jesus said unto him, Friend, wherefore art thou come? Then came they, and laid hands on Jesus, and took him.
And, behold, one of them which were with Jesus stretched out his hand, and drew his sword, and struck a John 18:10.servant of the high priest’s, and smote off his ear.
Then said Jesus unto him, Put up again thy sword into his place: for all they that take the sword shall TG Punish; Retribution.perish with the sword.
Thinkest thou that I cannot now pray to my Father, and he shall presently give me more than twelve legions of angels?
But how then shall the 2 Ne. 9:7 (5–10); Mosiah 3:15 (11–17).scriptures be fulfilled, that thus it must be?

In that same hour said Jesus to the multitudes, Are ye come out as against a thief with swords and staves for to take me? I sat daily with you Luke 21:37; John 8:2 (1–2). TG Teaching.teaching in the temple, and ye laid no hold on me.
But all this was done, that the scriptures of the prophets might be fulfilled. Then all the disciples forsook him, and fled.
¶ And they that had laid hold on Jesus led him away to John 18:13 (13, 24). TG Jesus Christ, Trials of.Caiaphas the high priest, where the scribes and the elders were assembled.
But Peter followed him afar off unto the high priest’s palace, and went in, and sat with the servants, to see the end.
Now the chief priests, and elders, and all the council, sought false TG Slander.witness against Jesus, GR so that they might put Him to death.to put him to death;
But found none: yea, though many false witnesses came, yet found they none. At the last came two false Deut. 19:15 (15–19).witnesses,
And said, This fellow said, I am able to destroy the Matt. 27:40; Mark 14:58; John 2:19 (18–22).temple of God, and to build it in three days.
And the high priest arose, and said unto him, Answerest thou nothing? what is it which these witness against thee?

But Jesus GR was silent, “kept silent.”held his Isa. 53:7.peace. And the high priest answered and said unto him, I GR charge (you) under oath (“cause you to swear”).adjure thee by the TG Oath; Swearing.living God, that thou tell us whether thou be the Christ, the Son of God.

Jesus saith unto him, Thou hast said: nevertheless I say unto you, Hereafter shall ye see the TG Jesus Christ, Son of Man.Son of man sitting on the right hand of TG Celestial Glory.power, and TG Jesus Christ, Second Coming.coming in the clouds of heaven.
Then the high priest rent his clothes, saying, He hath spoken TG Blaspheme.blasphemy; what further need have we of witnesses? behold, now ye have heard his blasphemy.
What think ye? They answered and said, He is TG Guilt.guilty JST Matt. 26:67 … and worthy of death.of TG Jesus Christ, Death of.death.
Then did they Isa. 50:6.spit in his face, and buffeted him; and others smote him with the palms of their hands,
Saying, Prophesy unto us, thou Christ, Who is he that Luke 22:64.smote thee?
¶ Now Peter sat without in the palace: and a GR servant-girl.damsel came unto him, saying, Thou also wast with Jesus of Galilee.
But he denied before them all, saying, I know not what thou sayest.
And when he was gone out into the porch, another maid saw him, and said unto them that were there, This fellow was also with Jesus of Nazareth.
And again he denied with an oath, I do not know the man.
And after a while came unto him they that stood by, and said to Peter, Surely thou also art one of them; for thy speech GR reveals you.bewrayeth thee.
