Then began he to curse and to swear, saying, I know not the man. And immediately the GR rooster crowed.cock crew.
And Peter remembered the word of Jesus, which said unto him, Before the cock crow, thou shalt TG Honesty.deny me thrice. And he went out, and wept bitterly.

div.p: Jesus is accused and condemned before Pilate—Barabbas is released—Jesus is mocked, crucified, and buried in the tomb of Joseph of Arimathæa.

chapter.verse: When the morning was come, all the chief priests and elders of the people took counsel against Jesus to put him to death:
And when they had bound him, they led him away, and delivered him to TG Jesus Christ, Trials of.Pontius Pilate the governor.
¶ Then Judas, which had betrayed him, when he saw that he was condemned, repented himself, and brought again the thirty pieces of silver to the chief priests and elders,

Saying, I have sinned in that I have TG Jesus Christ, Betrayal of.betrayed the innocent TG Blood, Shedding of.blood. And they said, What is that to us? GR that is your affair. JST Matt. 27:5 … See thou to it; thy sins be upon thee.see thou to that .

And he cast down the pieces of silver in the temple, and departed, and went JST Matt. 27:6 … and hanged himself on a tree. And straightway he fell down, and his bowels gushed out, and he died.and Acts 1:18 (15–20).hanged himself.
And the chief priests took the silver pieces, and said, It is not lawful for to put them into the treasury, because it is the price of blood.
And they took counsel, and bought with them the Zech. 11:13 (12–13).potter’s field, to bury TG Stranger.strangers in.
Wherefore that field was called, The field of blood, unto this day.

Then was fulfilled that which was spoken by Jeremy the prophet, saying, And they took the TG Jesus Christ, Betrayal of.thirty pieces of silver, the price of him that was valued, whom they of the children of Israel did value;
And gave them for the potter’s field, as the Lord appointed me.

And Jesus stood before the governor: and the governor asked him, saying, Art thou the King of the Jews? And Jesus said unto him, JST Matt. 27:12 … Thou sayest truly; for thus it is written of me.Thou sayest.
And when he was accused of the chief priests and elders, he answered TG Jesus Christ, Trials of.nothing.
Then said Pilate unto him, Hearest thou not how many things they witness against thee?
And he answered him to never a word; insomuch that the governor marvelled greatly.
Now at that feast the governor was GR accustomed.wont to Luke 23:17.release unto the people a prisoner, whom they GR desired.would.
And they had then a notable prisoner, called Barabbas.
Therefore when they were gathered together, Pilate said unto them, Whom will ye that I release unto you? Barabbas, or Jesus which is called Christ?
For he knew that for TG Envy.envy they had TG Jesus Christ, Betrayal of.delivered him.
¶ When he was set down on the judgment seat, his wife sent unto him, saying, Have thou nothing to do with that just man: for I have suffered many things this day in a TG Dream.dream because of him.
But the chief TG Priestcraft.priests and elders Matt. 26:4 (3–4); Acts 3:13; 13:28; 2 Ne. 10:5 (3–6).persuaded the multitude that they should GR request.ask Barabbas, and destroy Jesus.
The governor answered and said unto them, Whether of the twain will ye that I release unto you? They said, Barabbas.
Pilate saith unto them, What shall I do then with Jesus which is called Christ? They all say unto him, Let him be 2 Ne. 10:3.crucified.
And the governor said, Why, what evil hath he done? But they cried out GR exceedingly, beyond measure.the more, saying, Let him be crucified.

¶ When Pilate saw that he GR was accomplishing.could prevail nothing, but that rather a TG Rioting and Reveling.tumult was made, he took water, and washed his hands before the multitude, saying, I am innocent of the blood of this John 18:38 (28–38).just person: see ye to it.
Then answered all the people, and said, His Ps. 69:27; Acts 5:28; 2 Ne. 6:10 (8–11).blood be on us, and on our children.
¶ Then released he Barabbas unto them: and when he had Isa. 50:6. TG Cruelty.scourged Jesus, he delivered him to be crucified.
Then the soldiers of the governor took Jesus into the GR governor’s house, residence.common hall, and gathered unto him the whole band of soldiers.
And they stripped him, and put on him a JST Matt. 27:30 … purple …scarlet robe.

¶ And when they had GR woven.plaited a crown of thorns, they put it upon his head, and a GR stalk, staff.reed in his right hand: and they bowed the knee before him, and mocked him, saying, Hail, King of the Jews!
And they 1 Ne. 19:9.spit upon him, and took the reed, and smote him on the head.
And after that they had mocked him, they took the robe off from him, and put his own raiment on him, and led him away to crucify him.
And as they came out, they found a man of Cyrene, Simon by name: him they compelled to bear his cross.
And when they were come unto a place called Golgotha, that is to say, a place of JST Matt. 27:35 … burial …a skull,
¶ They gave him Ps. 69:21.vinegar to drink mingled with gall: and when he had tasted thereof, he would not drink.

And they TG Jesus Christ, Crucifixion of.crucified him, and IE they divided His clothes among themselves.parted his garments, casting lots: that it might be fulfilled which was spoken by the prophet, They parted my Ps. 22:18.garments among them, and upon my vesture did they cast lots.
And sitting down they watched him there;
And set up over his head his accusation written, This Is Jesus the King of the Jews.
Then were there two thieves crucified with him, one on the right hand, and another on the left.
¶ And they that passed by TG Reviling.reviled him, wagging their heads,

And saying, Thou that destroyest the Matt. 26:61; Mark 14:58; John 2:19 (18–22).temple, and buildest it in three days, save thyself. If thou be the Matt. 4:6; D&C 20:22.Son of God, come down from the cross.
Likewise also the chief priests mocking him, with the scribes and elders, said,
He saved others; himself he cannot save. If he be the King of Israel, let him now come down from the cross, and we will believe him.
He Ps. 22:8.trusted in God; let him TG Deliver.deliver him now, if he will have him: for he said, I am the TG Jesus Christ, Divine Sonship.Son of God.
The thieves also, which were crucified with him, GR insulted, reproached Him.cast the same in his teeth.
Now from the sixth hour there was TG Darkness, Physical.darkness over all the land unto the ninth hour.
And about the ninth hour Jesus cried with a loud voice, saying, Eli, Eli, lama sabachthani? that is to say, Ps. 22:1.My God, my God, why hast thou D&C 121:1 (1–8).forsaken me?
Some of them that stood there, when they heard that, said, This man calleth for IE Elijah.Elias.
And straightway one of them ran, and took a sponge, and filled it with TG Jesus Christ, Prophecies about.vinegar, and put it on a reed, and gave him to drink.
The rest said, Let be, let us see whether Elias will come to save him.
¶ Jesus, when he had cried again with JST Matt. 27:54 … a loud voice, saying, Father, it is finished, thy will is done, yielded up the ghost.a loud voice, yielded up the ghost.

And, behold, the TG Jesus Christ, Types of, in Anticipation; Veil.veil of the temple was GR torn into two pieces.rent in twain from the top to the bottom; and the earth did 3 Ne. 8:6 (5–12); Moses 7:56 (55–56).quake, and the rocks rent;
And the TG Jesus Christ, Prophecies about.graves were opened; and many D&C 129:1.bodies of the TG Saints.saints GR who had died.which slept TG Resurrection.arose,
And came out of the graves after his resurrection, and went into the Neh. 11:1.holy city, and appeared unto many.
Now when the centurion, and they that were with him, watching Jesus, saw the earthquake, and those things that were done, they feared greatly, saying, Truly this was the Son of God.
And many TG Woman.women were there beholding afar off, which followed Jesus from Galilee, ministering unto him:
Among which was Mary Magdalene, and Mary the mother of James and GR Joseph.Joses, and the mother of Zebedee’s children.
When the even was come, there came a rich man of Arimathæa, named John 19:38.Joseph, who also himself was Jesus’ John 8:31.disciple:
He went to Pilate, and GR asked for, requested.begged the body of Jesus. Then Pilate commanded the body to be delivered.
And when Joseph had taken the body, he wrapped it in a clean linen cloth,
And laid it in his own new tomb, which he had hewn out in the rock: and he rolled a great John 11:38 (37–40); 20:1.stone to the door of the 1 Ne. 19:10.sepulchre, and departed.
And there was Mary Magdalene, and the other Mary, sitting GR in front of, before.over against the sepulchre.
¶ Now the next day, that followed the day of the TG Passover.preparation, the chief priests and Pharisees came together unto Pilate,
Saying, Sir, we remember that that John 2:19.deceiver said, while he was yet alive, After three days I will rise again.

Command therefore that the sepulchre be made sure until the third day, lest his disciples come by night, and steal him away, and say unto the people, He is risen from the dead: so the last GR deception.error shall be worse than the first.
Pilate said unto them, Ye have a watch: go your way, make it as sure as ye can.
So they went, and made the sepulchre sure, sealing the stone, and setting a watch.

div.p: Christ the Lord is risen—He appears to many—He has all power in heaven and earth—He sends the Apostles to teach and baptize all nations.

chapter.verse: GR After the sabbath.In the end of the TG Sabbath.sabbath, as it began to dawn toward the first day of the week, came Mary Magdalene and the other Mary to see the sepulchre.
And, behold, there was a great earthquake: for JST Matt. 28:2 … two angels …the TG Angels.angel of the Lord descended from heaven, and came and rolled back the stone from the door, and sat upon it.
JST Matt. 28:3 And their countenance was like lightning, and their raiment white as snow …His D&C 20:6.countenance was like lightning, and his raiment white as snow:
And for fear of him the keepers did shake, and became as dead men.

JST Matt. 28:4 And the angels answered and said unto the women, Fear not ye; for we know …And the angel answered and said unto the women, Fear not ye: for I know that ye seek Jesus, which was crucified.
He is not here: for he is TG Jesus Christ, Resurrection; Resurrection.risen, as he said. Come, see the place where the TG Jesus Christ, Lord.Lord lay.
And go quickly, and tell his disciples that he is risen from the dead; and, behold, he goeth before you into Galilee; there shall ye see him: lo, I have told you.
And they departed quickly from the sepulchre with fear and great joy; and did run to bring his disciples word.
¶ And as they went to tell his disciples, behold, TG Jesus Christ, Appearances, Postmortal.Jesus met them, saying, All hail. And they came and held him by the feet, and worshipped him.
Then said Jesus unto them, Be not afraid: go tell my brethren that they go into Galilee, and there shall they see me.
¶ Now when they were going, behold, some of the GR guard.watch came into the city, and GR reported.shewed unto the chief priests all the things that were done.
And when they were assembled with the elders, and had taken counsel, they gave GR much money.large money unto the soldiers,
Saying, Say ye, His disciples came by night, and stole him away while we slept.
And if this come to the governor’s ears, we will persuade him, and GR keep you out of trouble.secure you.
So they took the money, and did as they were taught: and this saying is commonly reported among the Jews until this day.
¶ Then the eleven disciples went away into Galilee, into a mountain where Jesus had appointed them.
And when they saw him, they worshipped him: but some TG Doubt.doubted.

And TG Jesus Christ, Appearances, Postmortal.Jesus came and spake unto them, saying, All Heb. 2:8; 1 Ne. 9:6. TG God, Power of; Jesus Christ, Messiah; Jesus Christ, Power of; Priesthood, Power of.power is given unto me in heaven and in earth.

¶ Go ye therefore, and GR preach to, make disciples of (meaning “make Christians in all nations”). TG Apostles; Israel, Mission of; Missionary Work; Mission of Early Saints; Mission of Latter-day Saints; Teaching.teach all TG Nations.nations, TG Baptism; Baptism, Essential.baptizing them in the name of the TG Godhead.Father, and of the Son, and of the Holy Ghost:

The Greek text suggests this would be post-baptismal teaching. TG Prophets, Mission of.Teaching them to TG Baptism, Qualifications for.observe all things whatsoever I have Jer. 1:7 (7–10).commanded you: and, lo, D&C 30:11; 31:13; 61:10; 62:9. TG Guidance, Divine; Protection, Divine.I am with you alway, even unto the D&C 24:8; 132:49. TG World, End of.end of the world. Amen.

div.headCT: Title: JST entitles this book “The Testimony of St. Mark.”St Mark
div.title: The Gospel According to

div.p: Jesus is baptized by John—He preaches the gospel, calls disciples, casts out devils, heals the sick, and cleanses a leper.

chapter.verse: The beginning of the TG Gospel.gospel of Jesus Christ, the Son of God;
As it is written in the prophets, Behold, I send my Mal. 3:1; Matt. 11:10.messenger before thy face, which shall prepare thy way before thee.
The Matt. 3:3; Luke 3:4; John 1:23.voice of one crying in the wilderness, Prepare ye the way of the Lord, make his paths straight.
John did baptize in the wilderness, and TG Missionary Work.preach the TG Baptism.baptism of TG Repent.repentance for the TG Remission of Sins.remission of sins.

And there went out unto him all the land of Judæa, and they of Jerusalem, and were all TG Baptism, Immersion.baptized of him in the river of Jordan, TG Baptism, Qualifications for.confessing their sins.
And John was TG Clothing.clothed with 2 Kgs. 1:8.camel’s hair, and with a girdle of a skin about his loins; and he did eat Lev. 11:22.locusts and wild honey;
And preached, saying, There cometh one mightier than I after me, the latchet of whose shoes I am not worthy to stoop down and unloose.
I indeed have baptized you with water: JST Mark 1:6 … but he shall not only baptize you with water, but with fire, and the Holy Ghost.but he shall baptize you with the Holy Ghost.
And it came to pass in those days, that Jesus came from Nazareth of Galilee, and was TG Baptism; Baptism, Essential; Jesus Christ, Baptism of.baptized of John in Jordan.
And straightway coming TG Baptism, Immersion.up out of the water, he saw the heavens opened, and the Spirit like a TG Holy Ghost, Dove, Sign of.dove descending upon him:
And there came a voice from heaven, saying, Thou art my beloved Son, in whom I am well pleased.

JST Mark 1:10–11 And immediately the Spirit took him into the wilderness. And he was there in the wilderness forty days, Satan seeking to tempt him; and was with …And immediately the Spirit driveth him into the GR desert.wilderness.
And he was there in the wilderness forty days, tempted of Satan; and was with the wild beasts; and the angels ministered unto him.
Now after that John was put in prison, Jesus came into Galilee, TG Jesus Christ, Teaching Mode of.preaching the gospel of the kingdom of God,
And saying, The TG Time.time is fulfilled, and the D&C 33:10 (1–18). TG Kingdom of God, in Heaven.kingdom of God GR has arrived.is at hand: TG Repent.repent ye, and TG Faith.believe the gospel.
Now as he walked by the sea of Galilee, he saw Simon and Andrew his brother casting a net into the sea: for they were fishers.
And Jesus said unto them, Come ye after me, and I will make you to become TG Apostles; Missionary Work.fishers of men.
And straightway they forsook their nets, and followed him.
And when he had gone a little further thence, he saw James the son of Zebedee, and John his brother, who also were in the ship mending their nets.
And straightway he called them: and they left their father Zebedee in the ship with the hired servants, and went after him.
And they went into Capernaum; and straightway on the sabbath day he entered into the synagogue, and TG Jesus Christ, Teaching Mode of.taught.
And they were astonished at his doctrine: for he taught them as one that had TG Authority; Jesus Christ, Authority of.authority, and not as the TG Scribe.scribes.
And there was in their synagogue a man with an TG Spirits, Evil or Unclean.unclean spirit; and he cried out,

Saying, Let us alone; GR what business do you have with us …what have we to do with thee, thou Jesus of Nazareth? art thou come to destroy us? I know thee who thou art, the TG Holiness.Holy One of God.
And Jesus TG Heal.rebuked him, saying, Hold thy peace, and come out of him.
And when the unclean spirit had Mark 9:20 (14–29).torn him, and cried with a loud voice, he came out of him.

And they were all amazed, insomuch that they questioned among themselves, saying, What thing is this? what new doctrine is this? for with TG Authority; Jesus Christ, Authority of.authority commandeth he even the unclean spirits, and they do obey him.
And immediately his fame spread abroad throughout all the region round about Galilee.
And forthwith, when they were come out of the synagogue, they entered into the house of Simon and Andrew, with James and John.
But Simon’s wife’s mother lay sick of a fever, and GR immediately.anon they tell him of her.
And he came and took her by the hand, and TG Heal.lifted her up; and immediately the fever left her, and she ministered unto them.
And at even, when the sun did set, they brought unto him all that were diseased, and them that were possessed with devils.
And all the city was gathered together at the door.

And he TG Heal; Miracle.healed many that were sick of divers diseases, and cast out many Mosiah 3:6 (5–6). TG Spirits, Evil or Unclean.devils; and GR did not allow.suffered not the devils to speak, because they knew him.
And in the morning, rising up a great while before day, he went out, and departed into a solitary place, and there prayed.
And Simon and they that were with him followed after him.
And when they had found him, they said unto him, All men seek for thee.
And he said unto them, Let us go into the next towns, that I may TG Jesus Christ, Mission of; Teaching.preach there also: for therefore came I forth.
And he preached in their synagogues throughout all Galilee, and cast out Luke 4:36 (31–37); D&C 35:9 (6–12).devils.
And there came a TG Leprosy.leper to him, beseeching him, and kneeling down to him, and saying unto him, If thou wilt, thou canst make me GR pure.clean.
And Jesus, moved with TG Compassion.compassion, put forth his hand, and touched him, and saith unto him, I will; be thou clean.
And as soon as he had spoken, immediately the leprosy departed from him, and he was cleansed.
And he GR warned him sternly.straitly charged him, and forthwith sent him away;
And saith unto him, See thou say nothing to any man: but go thy way, shew thyself to the priest, and offer for thy cleansing those things which Moses commanded, for a testimony unto them.

But he went out, and began to publish it much, and to GR spread widely.blaze abroad the matter, insomuch that Jesus could no more openly enter into the city, but was without in desert places: and they came to him from every quarter.

div.p: Jesus forgives sins, heals a paralytic, eats with tax gatherers and sinners, and announces that He is Lord of the Sabbath.

chapter.verse: And again he entered into Capernaum after some days; and it was noised that he was OR at home.in the house.
And straightway many were gathered together, insomuch that there was no room to receive them, no, not so much as about the door: and he TG Jesus Christ, Teaching Mode of.preached the word unto them.
And they come unto him, bringing one sick of the palsy, which was GR carried.borne of four.
And when they could not come nigh unto him for the press, they uncovered the roof where he was: and when they had broken it up, they let down the bed wherein the sick of the palsy lay.
When Jesus saw their faith, he said unto the sick of the palsy, Son, thy sins be Luke 7:48; James 5:15 (14–15).forgiven thee.
But there were certain of the scribes sitting there, and reasoning in their hearts,
Why doth this man thus speak TG Blaspheme.blasphemies? who can forgive sins but God only?
And immediately when Jesus perceived in his spirit that they so reasoned within themselves, he said unto them, Why reason ye these things in your hearts?
Whether is it easier to say to the sick of the palsy, Thy sins be forgiven thee; or to say, Arise, and take up thy bed, and walk?
But that ye may know that the TG Jesus Christ, Son of Man.Son of man hath power on earth to forgive sins, (he saith to the sick of the palsy,)
I say unto thee, Alma 15:10 (4–11); 3 Ne. 9:13 (10–22).Arise, and take up thy 3 Ne. 7:22.bed, and go thy way into thine house.
And immediately he arose, took up the bed, and went forth before them all; insomuch that they were all amazed, and glorified God, saying, We never saw it on this fashion.
And he went forth again by the sea side; and all the multitude resorted unto him, and he taught them.
And as he passed by, he saw Levi the son of Alphæus sitting at the GR tax office.receipt of custom, and said unto him, Follow me. And he arose and followed him.
And it came to pass, that, as Jesus sat at meat in his house, many publicans and sinners sat also together with Jesus and his disciples: for there were many, and they followed him.
And when the scribes and Pharisees saw him eat with Luke 15:1 (1–2).publicans and sinners, they said unto his disciples, How is it that he eateth and drinketh with publicans and sinners?
When Jesus heard it, he saith unto them, They that are Moro. 8:8 (8–27).whole have no need of the physician, but they that are sick: I came not to call the righteous, but sinners to repentance.
And the disciples of John and of the Pharisees used to Luke 5:33 (33–38).fast: and they come and say unto him, Why do the disciples of John and of the Pharisees fast, but thy disciples fast not?
And Jesus said unto them, Can the children of the bridechamber fast, while the Luke 5:35.bridegroom is with them? as long as they have the D&C 65:3.bridegroom with them, they cannot fast.
But the days will come, when the bridegroom shall be taken away from them, and then shall they fast in those days.
No man also seweth a piece of GR unshrunken or unsized.new cloth on an old garment: else the new piece that filled it up taketh away from the old, and the rent is made worse.
And no man putteth new wine into old bottles: else the new wine doth burst the bottles, and the wine is spilled, and the bottles will be marred: but new wine must be put into new bottles.
And it came to pass, that he went through the corn fields on the sabbath day; and his disciples began, as they went, GR occasionally picking some grain.to pluck the ears of corn.
And the Pharisees said unto him, Behold, why do they on the sabbath day that which is not lawful?
And he said unto them, Have ye never read what David did, when he had need, and was an hungred, he, and they that were with him?
How he went into the house of God in the days of Abiathar the high priest, and did eat the shewbread, which is not lawful to eat but for the priests, and gave also to them which were with him?
And he said unto them, The TG Sabbath.sabbath was made for man, and not man for the sabbath:
JST Mark 2:26–27 (Appendix).Therefore the TG Jesus Christ, Son of Man.Son of man is Lord also of the sabbath.

div.p: Jesus heals on the Sabbath day—He chooses and ordains the Twelve Apostles—He asks, Can Satan cast out Satan?—Jesus speaks of blasphemy against the Holy Ghost and identifies those who believe as being members of His family.

chapter.verse: And he entered again into the synagogue; and there was a man there which had a withered hand.
And they GR watched carefully, maliciously.watched him, whether he would heal him on the sabbath day; that they might accuse him.
And he saith unto the man which had the withered hand, Stand forth.
And he saith unto them, Is it lawful to do good on the TG Sabbath.sabbath days, or to do evil? to save life, or to kill? But they GR remained silent.held their peace.

And when he had looked round about on them with TG God, Indignation of.anger, being grieved for the GR insensibility, dullness. TG Cruelty.hardness of their hearts, he saith unto the man, Stretch forth thine hand. And he stretched it out: and his hand was restored whole as the other.
And the Pharisees went forth, and straightway took TG Counsel.counsel with the Herodians against him, how they might destroy him.
But Jesus withdrew himself with his disciples to the sea: and a great multitude from Galilee followed him, and from Judæa,
And from Jerusalem, and from Idumæa, and from beyond Jordan; and they about Tyre and Sidon, a great multitude, when they had heard what great things he did, came unto him.
And he spake to his disciples, that a small GR boat.ship should wait on him because of the multitude, lest they should throng him.
For he had healed many; insomuch that they GR impetuously rushed at Him.pressed upon him for to touch him, as many as had plagues.
And TG Spirits, Evil or Unclean.unclean spirits, when they saw him, fell down before him, and cried, saying, Thou art the Son of God.
And he straitly charged them that they should not make him known.
And he goeth up into a mountain, and calleth unto him whom he GR desired.would: and they came unto him.

And he TG Apostles; Jesus Christ, Authority of; Priesthood, Authority; Priesthood, History of; Priesthood, Ordination.ordained twelve, that they should be with him, and that he might send them forth to TG Missionary Work.preach,
And to have GR authority. TG Priesthood; Priesthood, Power of.power to heal TG Sickness.sicknesses, and to cast out TG Devil.devils:
And Simon he surnamed Peter;
And James the son of Zebedee, and John the brother of James; and he surnamed them Boanerges, which is, The sons of Luke 9:54.thunder:
And Andrew, and Philip, and Bartholomew, and Matthew, and Thomas, and James the son of Alphæus, and Thaddæus, and Simon the Canaanite,
And Judas Iscariot, which also betrayed him: and they OR went home.went into an house.
And the multitude cometh together again, so that they could not so much as Mark 6:31 (30–36).eat bread.
And when his friends heard of it, they went out to lay hold on him: for they said, He is GR out of his senses.beside himself.
¶ And the scribes which came down from Jerusalem said, He hath Beelzebub, and by the prince of the devils casteth he out devils.
And he called them unto him, and said unto them in parables, How can Satan cast out Satan?
And if a kingdom be divided against itself, that kingdom cannot stand.
And if a house be divided against itself, that house cannot stand.
And if Satan rise up against himself, and be divided, he cannot stand, but hath an end.
No man can enter into a strong man’s house, and spoil his goods, except he will first bind the strong man; and then he will spoil his house.
JST Mark 3:21–25 (Appendix).Verily I say unto you, All sins shall be forgiven unto the sons of men, and blasphemies GR however so many.wherewith soever they shall blaspheme:
But he that shall TG Holy Ghost, Unpardonable Sin against.blaspheme against the Holy Ghost hath never TG Forgive.forgiveness, but is in danger of TG Damnation; Eternity; Punish.eternal damnation:
Because they said, He hath an unclean spirit.
¶ There came then his TG Jesus Christ, Family of.brethren and his mother, and, standing OR outside.without, sent unto him, calling him.
And the multitude sat about him, and they said unto him, Behold, thy mother and thy brethren without seek for thee.
And he answered them, saying, Who is my mother, or my brethren?
And he looked round about on them which sat about him, and said, Behold my mother and my brethren!
For whosoever shall do the Matt. 12:50.will of God, the same is my brother, and my sister, and mother.

div.p: Jesus gives the parables of the sower, the candle under a bushel, the seed growing secretly, and the mustard seed—He stills the tempest.

chapter.verse: And he began again to teach by the Matt. 13:1.sea side: and there was gathered unto him a great multitude, so that he entered into a ship, and sat in the sea; and the whole multitude was by the sea on the land.
And he taught them many things by parables, and said unto them in his 3 Ne. 11:39 (31–41).doctrine,
Hearken; Behold, there went out a D&C 86:2 (2–3).sower to sow:
And it came to pass, as he sowed, some fell by the way side, and the fowls of the air came and devoured it up.
And some fell on stony ground, where it had not much earth; and immediately it sprang up, because it had no depth of earth:
But when the sun was up, it was scorched; and because it had no root, it withered away.
And some fell among thorns, and the thorns grew up, and choked it, and it yielded no fruit.
And other fell on good ground, and did yield fruit that sprang up and increased; and brought forth, some thirty, and some sixty, and some an hundred.
And he said unto them, He that hath ears to hear, let him hear.
And when he was JST Mark 4:9 … alone with the twelve, and they that believed in him, they that were …alone, they that were about him with the twelve asked of him the parable.

And he said unto them, Unto you it is given to know the TG Mysteries of Godliness.mystery of the kingdom of God: but unto them that are without, all these things are done in Matt. 13:35 (34–35); Luke 8:10. TG Jesus Christ, Teaching Mode of.parables:

That seeing they may see, and not TG Spiritual Blindness.perceive; and hearing they may hear, and not understand; lest at any time they should be converted, and their sins should be TG Forgive.forgiven them.
And he said unto them, Know ye not this parable? and how then will ye know all parables?
¶ The sower soweth the word.
And these are they by the way side, where the word is sown; but when they have heard, TG Devil.Satan cometh immediately, and taketh away the word that was sown in their hearts.
And these are they likewise which are sown on stony ground; who, when they have heard the word, immediately D&C 40:2.receive it with gladness;

And have no root in themselves, and so endure but for a time: afterward, when 1 Ne. 8:28 (24–28). TG Affliction.affliction or TG Persecution.persecution ariseth for the word’s sake, immediately they are offended.
And these are they which are sown among thorns; such as hear the word,
And the cares of this world, and the deceitfulness of TG Treasure.riches, and the TG Lust.lusts of other things entering in, choke the word, and it becometh unfruitful.
And these are they which are sown on good ground; such as hear the word, and receive it, and bring forth fruit, some thirtyfold, some sixty, and some an hundred.
¶ And he said unto them, Is a 3 Ne. 18:24.candle brought to be put under a bushel, or under a bed? and not to be set on a candlestick?
For there is nothing hid, which shall not be manifested; neither was any thing kept secret, but that it should come abroad.
See JST Isa. 42:19–23 (Appendix).If any man have ears to hear, let him hear.

And he said unto them, Take heed what ye hear: with what measure ye mete, it shall be measured to you: JST Mark 4:20 … and unto you that continue to receive, shall more be given …and unto you that hear shall more be Matt. 25:15; Luke 8:18.given.

JST Mark 4:20 … for he that receiveth, to him shall be given; but he that continueth not to receive, from him shall …For he that hath, to him shall be given: and he that hath not, from him shall be D&C 43:10; 60:3.taken even that which he hath.
¶ And he said, So is the kingdom of God, as if a man should cast seed into the ground;
And should sleep, and rise night and day, and the seed should spring and 1 Cor. 3:6 (6–7).grow up, he knoweth not how.
For the earth bringeth forth TG Nature, Earth.fruit of herself; first the blade, then the ear, after that the full corn in the ear.
But when the fruit is brought forth, immediately he putteth in the sickle, because the TG Harvest.harvest is come.
¶ And he said, Whereunto shall we liken the kingdom of God? or with what comparison shall we compare it?
It is like a grain of mustard seed, which, when it is sown in the earth, is less than all the seeds that be in the earth:
But when it is sown, it groweth up, and becometh greater than all herbs, and shooteth out great branches; so that the fowls of the air may lodge under the shadow of it.
And with many such parables spake he the word unto them, as they were JST Mark 4:26 … able to bear; but without …able to hear it .
But without a parable spake he not unto them: and when they were alone, he expounded all things to his disciples.
And the same day, when the even was come, he saith unto them, Let us pass over unto the other side.
And when they had sent away the multitude, they took him even as he was in the ship. And there were also with him other little ships.
And there arose a great storm of wind, and the waves beat into the ship, so that it was now full.
And he was in the hinder part of the ship, asleep on a pillow: and they awake him, and say unto him, Master, carest thou not that we perish?
And he arose, and rebuked the wind, and said unto the sea, TG Peace.Peace, be still. And the wind ceased, and there was a great Ps. 89:9.calm.
And he said unto them, Why are ye so Matt. 14:31; Mark 16:14; Luke 24:25.fearful? how is it that ye have no Alma 44:4 (1–5); D&C 4:5 (1–7).faith?
And they feared exceedingly, and said one to another, What manner of man is this, that even the wind and the sea obey him?

div.p: Jesus casts out a legion of devils, who then enter the swine—A woman is healed by touching Jesus’ clothes—He raises Jairus’s daughter from the dead.

chapter.verse: And they came over unto the other side of the sea, into the country of the Gadarenes.
And when he was come out of the ship, immediately there met him out of the tombs a man with an unclean spirit,
Who had his dwelling among the tombs; and no man could bind him, no, not with chains:

Because that he had been often bound with fetters and chains, and the chains had been GR broken, taken apart.plucked asunder by him, and the fetters broken in pieces: neither GR was any man strong enough to tame him.could any man tame him.
And always, night and day, he was in the mountains, and in the tombs, crying, and cutting himself with stones.
But when he saw Jesus afar off, he ran and worshipped him,
And cried with a loud voice, and said, What have I to do with thee, Jesus, thou Son of the most high God? I OR implore.adjure thee by God, that thou torment me not.
For he said unto him, Come out of the man, thou TG Spirits, Evil or Unclean.unclean TG Heal.spirit.
And he asked him, What is thy name? And he answered, saying, My name is Legion: for we are many.
And he besought him much that he would not send them away out of the country.
Now there was there nigh unto the mountains a great herd of swine feeding.
And all the devils besought him, saying, Send us into the swine, that we may enter into them.

And forthwith Jesus gave them leave. And the unclean spirits went out, and entered into the swine: and the herd ran violently down a steep place into the sea, (they were about two thousand;) and were OR drowned.choked in the sea.
And they that fed the swine fled, and told it in the city, and in the country. And they went out to see what it was that was done.
And they come to Jesus, and see him that was possessed with the 1 Ne. 11:31; Morm. 9:24.devil, and had the legion, sitting, and clothed, and in his right TG Mind.mind: and they were afraid.
And they that saw it told them how it befell to him that was possessed with the devil, and also concerning the swine.
And they began to pray him to depart out of their coasts.
And when he was come into the ship, he that had been possessed with the devil prayed him that he might be with him.
Howbeit Jesus suffered him not, but saith unto him, Go home to thy friends, and tell them how great things the Lord hath done for thee, and hath had TG Compassion.compassion on thee.
And he departed, and began to Luke 8:39.publish in Decapolis how great things Jesus had done for him: and all men did marvel.
And when Jesus was passed over again by ship unto the other side, much people gathered unto him: and he was nigh unto the sea.
And, behold, there cometh one of the rulers of the synagogue, Jairus by name; and when he saw him, he fell at his feet,

And besought him greatly, saying, My little daughter lieth at the point of death: I pray thee, come and lay thy TG Administrations to the Sick; Hands, Laying on of.hands on her, that she may be healed; and she shall live.
And Jesus went with him; and much people followed him, and thronged him.
And a certain woman, which had an OR hemorrhage.issue of blood twelve years,
And had suffered many things of many physicians, and had spent all that she had, and was nothing bettered, but rather grew worse,
When she had heard of Jesus, came in the press behind, and touched his Matt. 14:36.garment.
For she said, If I may touch but his clothes, I shall be whole.
And straightway the fountain of her blood was dried up; and she felt in her body that she was healed of that plague.
And Jesus, immediately knowing in himself that GR power, strength. Luke 6:19 (17–19); 8:46 (41–48).virtue had gone out of him, turned him about in the press, and said, Who touched my clothes?
And his disciples said unto him, Thou seest the multitude thronging thee, and sayest thou, Who touched me?
And he looked round about to see her that had done this thing.
But the woman fearing and trembling, knowing what was done in her, came and fell down before him, and told him all the truth.
And he said unto her, Daughter, thy Hel. 15:9 (9–10); D&C 46:19.faith hath made thee whole; go in TG Peace of God.peace, and be whole of thy plague.
While he yet spake, there came from the ruler of the synagogue’s house certain which said, Thy daughter is dead: why troublest thou the Master any further?
As soon as Jesus heard the word that was spoken, he saith unto the ruler of the synagogue, Be not afraid, only GR exercise faith.believe.
And he suffered no man to follow him, save Peter, and James, and John the brother of James.
And he cometh to the house of the ruler of the synagogue, and seeth the tumult, and them that wept and wailed greatly.
And when he was come in, he saith unto them, Why make ye this OR uproar, tumult.ado, and weep? the damsel is not dead, but sleepeth.

And they OR ridiculed Him.laughed him to scorn. But when he had put them all out, he taketh the father and the mother of the damsel, and them that were with him, and entereth in where the damsel was lying.
And he took the damsel by the hand, and said unto her, Talitha cumi; which is, being interpreted, Damsel, I say unto thee, TG Death, Power over.arise.
And straightway the damsel TG Heal.arose, and walked; for she was of the age of twelve years. And they were astonished with a great astonishment.
And he GR strongly warned them.charged them straitly that no man should know it; and commanded that something should be given her to eat.

div.p: Jesus sends forth the Twelve—John the Baptist is beheaded by Herod—Our Lord feeds the five thousand, walks on the water, and heals multitudes.

chapter.verse: And he went out from thence, and came into his own country; and his disciples follow him.

And when the sabbath day was come, he began to teach in the synagogue: and many hearing him were astonished, saying, From whence hath this man these things? and what Eccl. 9:16.wisdom is this which is given unto him, that even such mighty works are wrought by his hands?

Is not this the Matt. 13:55.carpenter, the son of Mary, the TG Jesus Christ, Family of.brother of James, and Joses, and of Juda, and Simon? and are not his sisters here with us? And they were offended at him.
But Jesus said unto them, A prophet is not without honour, but in his own country, and among his own kin, and in his own house.
And he could there do no Morm. 9:18 (18–21).mighty work, save that he TG Hands, Laying on of.laid his TG Administrations to the Sick.hands upon a few sick folk, and healed them.
And he marvelled because of their OR lack of faith.unbelief. And he went round about the villages, teaching.
¶ And he called unto him the TG Apostles.twelve, and began to send them forth by Luke 10:1; D&C 42:6.two and two; and gave them power over unclean spirits;
And commanded them that they should take nothing for their journey, save a staff only; no Matt. 10:10 (9–11).scrip, no bread, no money in their purse:
But be shod with sandals; and not put on two coats.
And he said unto them, In what place soever ye enter into an house, there abide till ye depart from that place.

And whosoever shall not receive you, nor hear you, when ye depart thence, shake off the Luke 10:11; D&C 24:15.dust under your feet for a testimony against them. Verily I say unto you, It shall be more tolerable for Sodom and Gomorrha in the day of judgment, than for that city.
And they went out, and TG Preaching.preached that men should repent.
And they cast out many devils, and Ex. 31:11. TG Administrations to the Sick; Anointing.anointed with oil many that were sick, and healed them.
And king Luke 9:7 (7–9).Herod heard of him; (for his name was spread abroad:) and he said, That John the Baptist was risen from the dead, and therefore mighty works do shew forth themselves in him.
Others said, That it is Elias. And others said, That it is a prophet, or as one of the prophets.
But when Herod heard thereof, he said, It is John, whom I beheaded: he is risen from the dead.
For Herod himself had sent forth and laid hold upon John, and bound him in prison for Herodias’ sake, his brother Philip’s wife: for he had married her.
For John had said unto Herod, It is not lawful for thee to have thy brother’s wife.
Therefore Herodias had a quarrel against him, and would have killed him; but she could not:

For Herod feared John, knowing that he was a GR righteous and holy man.just man JST Mark 6:21 … and a holy man, and one who feared God and observed to worship him; and when he heard him he did many things for him, and heard …and an holy, and GR protected.observed him; and when he heard him, he did many things, and heard him gladly.
And when a convenient day was come, that Herod on his birthday made a supper to his GR nobles, military commanders, and prominent men.lords, high captains, and chief estates of Galilee;
And when the daughter of the said Herodias came in, and danced, and pleased Herod and them that sat with him, the king said unto the damsel, Ask of me whatsoever thou wilt, and I will give it thee.
And he TG Swearing.sware unto her, Whatsoever thou shalt ask of me, I will give it thee, unto the Esth. 5:3.half of my kingdom.
And she went forth, and said unto her mother, What shall I ask? And she said, The head of John the Baptist.
And she came in straightway with TG Haste.haste unto the king, and asked, saying, I will that thou give me GR at once.by and by in a OR platter.charger the head of TG Martyrdom.John the Baptist.
And the king was exceeding sorry; yet for his TG Vow.oath’s sake, and for their sakes which sat with him, GR he did not desire to refuse.he would not reject her.
And immediately the king sent an executioner, and commanded his head to be brought: and he went and beheaded him in the prison,
And brought his head in a charger, and gave it to the damsel: and the damsel gave it to her mother.
And when his disciples heard of it, they came and took up his corpse, and laid it in a tomb.
And the apostles gathered themselves together unto Jesus, and told him all things, both what they had done, and what they had taught.

And he said unto them, Come ye yourselves apart into a JST Mark 6:32 … solitary …desert place, and rest a while: for there were many coming and going, and they had no Mark 3:20.leisure so much as to eat.
And they departed into a JST Mark 6:33 … solitary …desert place by ship privately.
And the people saw them departing, and many knew him, and ran afoot thither out of all cities, and outwent them, and came together unto him.

And Jesus, when he came out, saw much people, and was moved with TG Compassion.compassion toward them, because they were as sheep not having a TG Shepherd.shepherd: and he began to TG Jesus Christ, Teaching Mode of.teach them many things.

And when the day was now far spent, his disciples came unto him, and said, JST Mark 6:36 … This is a solitary place, and now the time for departure is come …This is a desert place, and now the time is far passed:
Send them away, that they may go into the country round about, and into the villages, and buy themselves bread: for they have nothing to eat.

He answered and said unto them, Give ye them to eat. And they say unto him, Shall we go and buy two hundred GR denarii; one denarius was a workman’s daily wage.pennyworth of bread, and give them to eat?
He saith unto them, How many loaves have ye? go and see. And when they knew, they say, Five, and two fishes.
And he commanded them to make all sit down by companies upon the green grass.
And they sat down in ranks, by hundreds, and by fifties.

And when he had taken the five loaves and the two fishes, he looked up to heaven, and blessed, and brake the loaves, and gave them to his disciples to set before them; and the two fishes divided he among them all.
And they did all eat, and were filled.
And they took up twelve baskets full of the fragments, and of the fishes.
And they that did eat of the loaves were about Matt. 14:21 (13–21); Luke 9:14 (10–17); John 6:10 (1–13).five thousand men.
And straightway he constrained his disciples to get into the ship, and to go to the other side before unto Bethsaida, while he sent away the people.
And when he had GR bid them farewell.sent them away, he departed into a mountain to pray.
And when even was come, the ship was in the midst of the sea, and he alone on the land.

And he saw them GR struggling at their oars.toiling in rowing; for the wind was contrary unto them: and about the fourth watch of the night he cometh unto them, walking upon the sea, and would have passed by them.
But when they saw him walking upon the sea, they supposed it had been a spirit, and cried out:
For they all saw him, and were OR terrified.troubled. And immediately he talked with them, and saith unto them, Be of good cheer: it is I; be not afraid.
And he went up unto them into the ship; and the wind ceased: and they were sore amazed in themselves beyond measure, and wondered.
For they OR did not understand. 1 Cor. 2:11.considered not the miracle of the loaves: for their heart was TG Hardheartedness.hardened.
And when they had passed over, they came into the land of Gennesaret, and drew to the shore.
And when they were come out of the ship, straightway they knew him,
And ran through that whole region round about, and began to carry about in beds those that were sick, where they heard he was.

And whithersoever he entered, into villages, or cities, or country, they laid the sick in the streets, and besought him that they might touch if it were but the border of his garment: and as many as touched him were made whole.

div.p: Jesus reproves the Pharisees for their false traditions and ceremonies—He casts a devil out of the daughter of a Greek woman—He opens the ears and loosens the tongue of a person with an impediment.

chapter.verse: Then came together unto him the Pharisees, and certain of the scribes, which came from Jerusalem.
And when they saw some of his disciples eat bread with defiled, that is to say, with unwashen, hands, they found fault.
For the Pharisees, and all the Jews, except they TG Purification.wash their hands oft, eat not, holding the tradition of the elders.
And when they come from the market, except they wash, they eat not. And many other things there be, which they have received to hold, as the washing of cups, and pots, brasen vessels, and of tables.
Then the Pharisees and scribes asked him, Why walk not thy disciples according to the tradition of the elders, but eat bread with TG Wash.unwashen hands?

He answered and said unto them, Well hath Esaias prophesied of you hypocrites, as it is written, This people TG Apostasy of Israel; Hypocrisy.honoureth me with their lips, but their heart is far from me.
Howbeit in vain do they worship me, teaching for doctrines the commandments of men.
For laying aside the commandment of God, ye hold the TG Traditions of Men.tradition of men, as the washing of pots and cups: and many other such like things ye do.
And he said unto them, Full well ye reject the commandment of God, that ye may keep your own tradition.
JST Mark 7:10–12 (Appendix).For Moses said, Honour thy father and thy mother; and, Whoso TG Curse; Honoring Father and Mother.curseth father or mother, let him die the death:
But ye say, If a man shall say to his father or mother, It is Matt. 15:5.Corban, that is to say, a gift, by whatsoever thou mightest be profited by me; he shall be free.
And ye suffer him no more to do ought for his father or his mother;
Making the word of God of none effect through your tradition, which ye have delivered: and many such like things do ye.
¶ And when he had called all the people unto him, he said unto them, Hearken unto me every one of you, and understand:

There is nothing from without a man, that entering into him can JST Mark 7:15 … defile him, which is food; but the things which come out of him; those are they that defile the man, that proceedeth forth out of the heart.defile him: but the things which come out of him, those are they that Titus 1:15; 2 Ne. 19:17; D&C 93:35.defile the man.
If any man have ears to hear, let him hear.
And when he was entered into the house from the people, his disciples asked him concerning the parable.
And he saith unto them, Are ye so without understanding also? Do ye not perceive, that whatsoever thing from without entereth into the man, it cannot defile him;
Because it entereth not into his heart, but into the belly, and goeth out into the draught, purging all meats?
And he said, That which cometh out of the man, that James 3:6; D&C 88:121.defileth the man.
For from within, out of the 1 Jn. 2:16.heart of men, proceed evil thoughts, TG Adulterer.adulteries, fornications, murders,
TG Stealing.Thefts, TG Covet.covetousness, TG Wickedness.wickedness, TG Deceit; Fraud.deceit, lasciviousness, an evil eye, blasphemy, pride, foolishness:
All these TG Motivations.evil things come from within, and Mosiah 4:30.defile the man.

¶ And from thence he arose, and went into the borders of Tyre and Sidon, and entered into an house, JST Mark 7:22–23 … and would that no man should come unto him. But he could not deny them; for he had compassion upon all men.and would have no man know it: but he could not be hid.
For a certain woman, whose young daughter had an unclean spirit, heard of him, and came and fell at his feet:
The woman was a Greek, a Syrophenician by nation; and she besought him that he would cast forth the devil out of her daughter.
But Jesus said unto her, Let the JST Mark 7:26 … children of the kingdom first …children first be filled: for it is not meet to take the children’s bread, and to cast it unto the D&C 41:6.dogs.
And she answered and said unto him, Yes, Lord: yet the dogs under the table eat of the children’s crumbs.
And he said unto her, For this saying go thy way; the devil is gone out of thy daughter.
And when she was come to her house, she found the 1 Ne. 11:31.devil gone out, and her daughter laid upon the bed.
¶ And again, departing from the coasts of Tyre and Sidon, he came unto the sea of Galilee, through the midst of the coasts of Decapolis.
And they bring unto him one that was deaf, and had an impediment in his speech; and they beseech him to put his TG Administrations to the Sick; Hands, Laying on of.hand upon him.
And he took him aside from the multitude, and put his fingers into his ears, and he spit, and touched his tongue;
And looking up to heaven, he sighed, and saith unto him, Ephphatha, that is, Be opened.
And straightway his ears were TG Miracle.opened, and the string of his tongue was loosed, and he spake plain.
And he charged them that they should tell no man: but the more he charged them, so much the more a great deal they Matt. 9:31.published it;
And were beyond measure astonished, saying, He hath done all things well: he maketh both the deaf to hear, and the dumb to speak.

div.p: Jesus feeds the four thousand—He counsels, Beware of the leaven of the Pharisees—He heals a blind man in Bethsaida—Peter testifies that Jesus is the Christ.

chapter.verse: In those days the multitude being very great, and having nothing to eat, Jesus called his disciples unto him, and saith unto them,
I have compassion on the multitude, because they have now been with me three days, and have nothing to eat:
And if I send them away fasting to their own houses, they will faint by the way: for GR some.divers of them came from far.
And his disciples answered him, From whence can a man satisfy these men with bread here in the wilderness?
And he asked them, How many loaves have ye? And they said, Seven.
And he commanded the people to sit down on the ground: and he took the seven loaves, and gave thanks, and brake, and gave to his disciples to set before them; and they did set them before the people.
And they had a few small fishes: and he blessed, and commanded to set them also before them.
So they did eat, and were filled: and they took up of the broken meat that was left seven baskets.
And they that had eaten were about four thousand: and he sent them away.
¶ And straightway he entered into a ship with his disciples, and came into the parts of Dalmanutha.
And the Pharisees came forth, and began to question with him, seeking of him a D&C 46:9; 63:7 (7–11).sign from heaven, tempting him.
And he sighed deeply in his spirit, and saith, Why doth this generation seek after a Matt. 16:4 (1–12).sign? verily I say unto you, There shall no sign be given unto this generation.
And he left them, and entering into the ship again departed to the other side.
¶ Now the disciples had forgotten to take bread, neither had they in the ship with them more than one loaf.
And he charged them, saying, Take heed, beware of the leaven of the Pharisees, and of the leaven of Herod.
And they reasoned among themselves, saying, It is because we have no bread.

And when Jesus knew it, he saith unto them, Why reason ye, because ye have no bread? perceive ye not yet, neither understand? GR do you still have hardened hearts?have ye your heart yet TG Hardheartedness.hardened?
Having eyes, see ye not? and having ears, hear ye not? and do ye not remember?
When I brake the five loaves among five thousand, how many baskets full of fragments took ye up? They say unto him, Twelve.
And when the seven among four thousand, how many baskets full of fragments took ye up? And they said, Seven.
And he said unto them, How is it that ye do not understand?
¶ And he cometh to Bethsaida; and they bring a blind man unto him, and besought him to touch him.

And he took the blind man by the hand, and led him out of the town; and when he had spit on his eyes, and put his TG Administrations to the Sick; Hands, Laying on of.hands upon him, he asked him if he saw OR anything.ought.
And he looked up, and said, I see men as trees, walking.
After that he put his hands again upon his eyes, and made him look up: and he was restored, and saw OR everything.every man clearly.
And he sent him away to his house, saying, Neither go into the town, nor tell it to any in the town.
¶ And Jesus went out, and his disciples, into the towns of Cæsarea Philippi: and by the way he asked his disciples, saying unto them, Whom do men say that I am?
And they answered, John the Baptist: but some say, Elias; and others, One of the prophets.
And he saith unto them, But whom say ye that I am? And Peter answereth and saith unto him, Thou art the TG Jesus Christ, Messiah.Christ.
And he charged them that they should Luke 9:21.tell no man of him.

And he began to TG Jesus Christ, Teaching Mode of.teach them, that the Son of man must suffer many things, and be rejected of the elders, and of the chief priests, and scribes, and be killed, and after three days rise again.
And he spake that saying openly. And Peter took him, and began to rebuke him.

But when he had turned about and looked on his disciples, he rebuked Peter, saying, Get thee behind me, Alma 12:5 (5–6, 34).Satan: for GR you do not consider, cherish.thou savourest not the things that be of God, but the things that be of men.

¶ And when he had called the people unto him with his disciples also, he said unto them, Whosoever will come after me, let him TG Self-Mastery.deny himself, and take up his cross, and TG Jesus Christ, Exemplar.follow me.

JST Mark 8:37–38 (Appendix).For whosoever will save his life shall lose it; but whosoever shall Luke 9:24; JST Luke 9:24–25 (Appendix).lose his Heb. 11:35; JST Heb. 11:35 (Heb. 11:35 note b); D&C 103:28 (27–28). TG Martyrdom.life for my sake and the gospel’s, the same shall save it.
For what shall it profit a man, if he shall Jer. 45:5.gain the whole world, and lose his own soul?
Or what shall a man give in exchange for his JST Mark 8:39–40 … soul? Therefore deny yourselves of these, and be not ashamed of me.soul?

Whosoever therefore shall be 1 Ne. 8:28 (24–28). TG Shame.ashamed of me and of my words in this adulterous and sinful generation; of him also shall the TG Jesus Christ, Son of Man.Son of man be ashamed, when he cometh in the glory of his Father with the holy JST Mark 8:42–43 (Appendix).angels.

div.p: Jesus is transfigured on the mountain—He casts out an unclean spirit—He teaches concerning His death and resurrection, who will be greatest, and the condemnation of those who offend His little ones.

chapter.verse: And he said unto them, Verily I say unto you, That there be some of them that stand here, which shall not taste of death, till they have seen the kingdom of God come with power.

¶ And after Luke 9:28 (28–36).six days Jesus taketh with him Peter, and James, and John, JST Mark 9:1 … who asked him many questions concerning his sayings; and Jesus leadeth them …and leadeth them up into an high mountain apart by themselves: and he was Matt. 17:2 (1–13); Luke 9:29 (28–36).transfigured before them.
And his raiment became shining, exceeding white as snow; so as no fuller on earth can white them.
And there appeared unto them Elias with JST Mark 9:3 … Moses, or in other words, John the Baptist and Moses …Moses: and they were talking with Jesus.
And Peter answered and said to Jesus, Master, it is good for us to be here: and let us make three tabernacles; one for thee, and one for Moses, and one for Elias.
For he wist not what to say; for they were sore afraid.
And there was a cloud that overshadowed them: and a TG God, Manifestations of.voice came out of the cloud, saying, This is my Matt. 3:17; Luke 9:35 (34–36).beloved Son: hear him.
And suddenly, when they had looked round about, they saw no man any more, save Jesus only with themselves.
And as they came down from the mountain, he charged them that they should tell no Matt. 17:9 (9–12).man what things they had seen, till the Son of man were risen from the dead.
And they kept that saying with themselves, questioning one with another what the John 20:9.rising from the dead should mean.
¶ And they asked him, saying, Why say the scribes that Elias must first come?

And he answered and told them, Elias verily cometh first, JST Mark 9:10 … and prepareth all things; and teacheth you of the prophets; how …and TG Dispensations.restoreth all things; and how it is written of the TG Jesus Christ, Prophecies about.Son of man, that he must TG Jesus Christ, Crucifixion of.suffer many things, and be set at Isa. 53:3.nought.

But I say unto you, That Elias is indeed come, and they have done unto him whatsoever they GR willed, chose, intended.listed, as it is written of JST Mark 9:11 … him; and he bore record of me, and they received him not. Verily this was Elias.him.
¶ And when he came to his disciples, he saw a great multitude about them, and the scribes questioning with them.
And straightway all the people, when they beheld him, were greatly amazed, and running to him saluted him.
And he asked the scribes, What question ye with them?
And one of the multitude answered and said, Master, I have brought unto thee my son, which hath a dumb spirit;

And wheresoever he taketh him, he GR dashes on the ground, convulses, lacerates.teareth him: and he foameth, and gnasheth with his teeth, and pineth away: and I spake to thy disciples that they should cast him out; and they could not.
He answereth him, and saith, O faithless generation, how long shall I be with you? how long shall I suffer you? bring him unto me.
And they brought him unto him: and when he saw him, straightway the spirit Mark 1:26.tare him; and he fell on the ground, and wallowed foaming.
And he asked his father, How long is it ago since this came unto him? And he said, Of a child.
And ofttimes it hath cast him into the fire, and into the waters, to destroy him: but if thou canst do any thing, have TG Compassion.compassion on us, and help us.
Jesus said unto him, If thou canst believe, all things are Dan. 3:29. TG God, Power of.possible to him that TG Faith.believeth.
And straightway the father of the child cried out, and said with tears, Lord, I believe; help thou mine TG Doubt.unbelief.

When Jesus saw that the people came running together, he TG Heal.rebuked the TG Spirits, Evil or Unclean.foul spirit, saying unto him, Thou dumb and deaf spirit, I charge thee, come out of him, and enter no more into him.
And the spirit cried, and rent him sore, and came out of him: and he was as one dead; insomuch that many said, He is dead.
But Jesus took him by the hand, and Acts 3:7.lifted him up; and he arose.
And when he was come into the house, his disciples asked him privately, Why could not we cast him out?
And he said unto them, This kind can come forth by nothing, but by prayer and TG Problem-Solving.fasting.
¶ And they departed thence, and passed through Galilee; and he would not that any man should know it.

For he taught his disciples, and said unto them, The TG Jesus Christ, Son of Man.Son of man is delivered into the hands of men, and they shall kill him; and after that he is killed, he shall TG Jesus Christ, Resurrection.rise the third day.
But they understood not that saying, and were afraid to ask him.
¶ And he came to Capernaum: and being in the house he asked them, What was it that ye GR reasoned, disputed, pondered.disputed among yourselves by the way?
But they held their peace: for by the way they had TG Disputations.disputed among themselves, who should be the Luke 9:46.greatest.
And he sat down, and called the twelve, and saith unto them, If any man desire to be Prov. 18:17.first, the same shall be last of all, and TG Meek.servant of all.
And he took a child, and set him in the midst of them: and when he had taken him in his arms, he said unto them,

JST Mark 9:34–35 Whosoever shall humble himself like one of these children, and receiveth me, ye shall receive in my name. And whosoever shall receive me, receiveth not me only, but him that sent me, even the Father.Whosoever shall receive one of such TG Children; Salvation of Little Children.children in my name, receiveth me: and whosoever shall receive me, receiveth not me, but him that sent me.
¶ And John answered him, saying, Master, we saw one Luke 11:19; Acts 19:13.casting out devils in thy name, and he followeth not us: and we forbad him, because he followeth not us.
But Jesus said, Forbid him not: for there is no man which shall do a TG Miracle.miracle in my TG Name.name, that can lightly speak evil of me.
For he that is not against us is Matt. 12:30; Luke 9:50.on our part.
For whosoever shall give you a cup of water to drink in my name, because ye belong to Christ, verily I say unto you, he shall not Matt. 10:42; D&C 84:90.lose his TG Reward.reward.
And whosoever shall TG Offense.offend one of these little ones that believe in me, it is better for him that a millstone were hanged about his neck, and he were cast into the sea.

JST Mark 9:40–48 (Appendix).And if thy hand offend thee, TG Self-Mastery.cut it off: it is better for thee to enter into life maimed, than having two hands to go into TG Hell.hell, into the fire that never shall be quenched:
Where their worm dieth not, and the fire is not quenched.
And if thy foot GR cause thee to stumble.offend thee, cut it off: it is better for thee to enter halt into life, than having two feet to be cast into hell, into the fire that never shall be quenched:
Where their worm dieth not, and the fire is not quenched.
And if thine eye offend thee, pluck it out: it is better for thee to enter into the kingdom of God with one eye, than having two eyes to be cast into hell fire:
Where their Isa. 66:24; D&C 76:44.worm dieth not, and the fire is not quenched.
For every one shall be TG Salt.salted with fire, and every sacrifice shall be salted with salt.
Salt is good: but if the salt have lost his saltness, wherewith will ye season it? Have salt in yourselves, and have 1 Thes. 5:13. TG Peace.peace one with another.

div.p: Jesus teaches the higher law of marriage—He blesses little children—Jesus counsels the rich young man, foretells His own death, and heals blind Bartimæus.

chapter.verse: And he arose from thence, and cometh into the coasts of Judæa by the farther side of Jordan: and the people resort unto him again; and, as he was wont, he taught them again.
¶ And the Pharisees came to him, and asked him, Is it lawful for a man to put away his wife? tempting him.
And he answered and said unto them, What did Moses command you?
And they said, Moses suffered to write a bill of divorcement, and to put her away.
And Jesus answered and said unto them, For the hardness of your heart he wrote you this precept.
But from the beginning of the creation God made them male and female.
For this cause shall a TG Marriage, Husbands.man leave his father and mother, and cleave to his TG Marriage, Wives.wife;
And they twain shall be one flesh: so then they are no more twain, but one flesh.
What therefore God hath TG Family, Eternal; Genealogy and Temple Work; Marriage, Marry.joined together, let not man put TG Divorce.asunder.
And in the house his disciples asked him again of the same matter.
And he saith unto them, Whosoever shall put away his wife, and marry another, committeth adultery against her.
And if a woman shall put away her husband, and be married to another, she committeth adultery.
¶ And they brought young children to him, that he should touch them: and his disciples rebuked those that brought them.
But when Jesus saw it, he was much displeased, and said unto them, Suffer the little Moro. 8:8 (5–9). TG Example.children to come unto me, and forbid them not: for of such is the kingdom of God.
Verily I say unto you, Whosoever shall not receive the kingdom of God as a little TG Meek.child, he shall not enter therein.
And he took them up in his arms, put his hands upon them, and 3 Ne. 17:21. TG Salvation of Little Children.blessed them.
¶ And when he was gone forth into the way, there came one running, and kneeled to him, and asked him, Good Master, what shall I do that I may inherit eternal life?
And Jesus said unto him, Why callest thou me good? there is none Ether 4:12 (11–12).good but one, that is, God.

Thou knowest the commandments, Do not commit D&C 66:10.adultery, Do not TG Murder.kill, Do not TG Stealing.steal, Do not bear false witness, TG Fraud.Defraud not, TG Honoring Father and Mother.Honour thy father and mother.
And he answered and said unto him, Master, all these have I observed from my youth.

Then Jesus beholding him loved him, and said unto him, One thing thou lackest: go thy way, sell whatsoever thou hast, and TG Charity; Generosity.give to the poor, and thou shalt have treasure in heaven: and come, take up the cross, and TG Jesus Christ, Exemplar.follow me.
And he was sad at that saying, and went away grieved: for he had great possessions.
¶ And Jesus looked round about, and saith unto his disciples, How hardly shall they that have riches enter into the kingdom of God!

And the disciples were astonished at his words. But Jesus answereth again, and saith unto them, Children, how hard is it for them that Jacob 2:18 (17–19).trust in TG Treasure.riches to enter into the kingdom of God!
It is easier for a camel to go through the eye of a needle, than for a rich man to enter into the kingdom of God.
And they were astonished out of measure, saying among themselves, Who then can be saved?

And Jesus looking upon them saith, JST Mark 10:26 … With men that trust in riches, it is impossible; but not impossible with men who trust in God and leave all for my sake, for with such all these things are possible.With men it is impossible, but not with God: for with God all things are TG God, Power of.possible.
¶ Then Peter began to say unto him, Lo, we have left all, and have followed thee.
And Jesus answered and said, Verily I say unto you, There is no man that hath left house, or brethren, or sisters, or father, or mother, or wife, or children, or lands, for my sake, and the gospel’s,
But he shall receive an TG Reward.hundredfold now in this time, houses, and brethren, and sisters, and mothers, and children, and lands, with persecutions; and in the world to come eternal life.

JST Mark 10:30–31 But there are many who make themselves first, that shall be last, and the last first. This he said, rebuking Peter …But many that are Jacob 5:63; Ether 13:12 (10–12).first shall be last; and the last first.

¶ And they were in the way going up to Jerusalem; and Jesus went before them: and they were amazed; and as they followed, they were afraid. And he took again the twelve, and began to tell them what things should happen unto him,

Saying, Behold, we go up to Jerusalem; and the TG Jesus Christ, Son of Man.Son of man shall be delivered unto the chief priests, and unto the TG Scribe.scribes; and they shall condemn him to death, and shall deliver him to the Gentiles:
And they shall TG Mocking.mock him, and shall scourge him, and shall spit upon him, and shall kill him: and the third day he shall TG Jesus Christ, Resurrection.rise again.
¶ And James and John, the sons of Zebedee, come unto him, saying, Master, we would that thou shouldest do for us whatsoever we shall desire.
And he said unto them, What would ye that I should do for you?
They said unto him, Grant unto us that we may sit, one on thy right hand, and the other on thy left hand, in thy glory.
But Jesus said unto them, Ye know not what ye ask: can ye drink of the cup that I drink of? and be baptized with the TG Jesus Christ, Baptism of.baptism that I am baptized with?
And they said unto him, We can. And Jesus said unto them, Ye shall indeed drink of the cup that I drink of; and with the baptism that I am baptized withal shall ye be baptized:
But to sit on my right hand and on my left hand is not mine to give; but it shall be given to them for whom it is prepared.
And when the ten heard it, they began to be much displeased with James and John.
But Jesus called them to him, and saith unto them, Ye know that they which are accounted to rule over the Gentiles exercise lordship over them; and their great ones exercise authority upon them.
But so shall it not be among you: but whosoever will be D&C 50:26 (26–27).great among you, shall be your minister:
And whosoever of you will be the Matt. 23:11; Luke 22:24 (24–30); 2 Ne. 9:5.chiefest, shall be servant of all.
For even the Son of man came not to be ministered unto, but to minister, and to give his life a Rom. 4:25.ransom for many.
¶ And they came to Jericho: and as he went out of Jericho with his disciples and a great number of people, blind Matt. 20:30 (29–34).Bartimæus, the son of Timæus, sat by the highway side begging.
And when he heard that it was Jesus of Nazareth, he began to cry out, and say, Jesus, thou Son of David, have mercy on me.
And many charged him that he should hold his peace: but he cried the more a great deal, Thou Son of David, have mercy on me.
And Jesus stood still, and commanded him to be called. And they call the 1 Ne. 17:51.blind man, saying unto him, Be of good comfort, rise; he calleth thee.
And he, casting away his garment, rose, and came to Jesus.
And Jesus answered and said unto him, What wilt thou that I should do unto thee? The blind man said unto him, Lord, that I might receive my sight.
And Jesus said unto him, Go thy way; thy faith hath GR saved, preserved, healed thee.made thee whole. And immediately he received his sight, and followed Jesus in the way.

div.p: Jesus rides into Jerusalem amid shouts of hosanna—He curses a fig tree, drives the money changers from the temple, and confounds the scribes on the matter of authority.

chapter.verse: And when they came nigh to Jerusalem, unto Bethphage and Bethany, at the mount of Olives, he sendeth forth two of his disciples,
And saith unto them, Go your way into the village over against you: and as soon as ye be entered into it, ye shall find a colt tied, whereon never man sat; loose him, and bring him.
And if any man say unto you, Why do ye this? say ye that the Lord hath need of him; and straightway he will send him hither.
And they went their way, and found the colt tied by the door without in a place where two ways met; and they loose him.
And certain of them that stood there said unto them, What do ye, loosing the colt?
And they said unto them even as Jesus had commanded: and they let them go.
And they brought the Zech. 9:9.colt to Jesus, and cast their garments on him; and he sat upon him.
And many spread their garments in the way: and others cut down branches off the trees, and strawed them in the way.
And they that went before, and they that followed, cried, saying, Hosanna; Ps. 118:26.Blessed is he that cometh in the name of the Lord:

JST Mark 11:11–12 That bringeth the kingdom of our father David; Blessed is he that cometh in the name of the Lord …Blessed be the kingdom of our father David, that cometh in the name of the Lord: Hosanna in the highest.
And Jesus entered into Jerusalem, and into the temple: and when he had looked round about upon all things, and now the eventide was come, he went out unto Bethany with the twelve.
¶ And on the morrow, when they were come from Bethany, he was hungry:
And seeing a fig tree afar off having leaves, he came, if haply he might find any thing thereon: and when he came to it, he found nothing but leaves; for the time of figs was not yet.
And Jesus answered and said unto it, No man eat fruit of thee hereafter for ever. And his disciples heard it.

¶ And they come to Jerusalem: and Jesus went into the temple, and began to cast out them that sold and bought in the temple, and overthrew the tables of the moneychangers, and the seats of them that sold doves;
And would not suffer that any man should carry any vessel through the temple.
And he taught, saying unto them, Is it not written, My house shall be called of all nations the house of prayer? but ye have made it a den of thieves.
And the scribes and chief priests heard it, and sought how they might TG Jesus Christ, Betrayal of.destroy him: for they feared him, because all the people was astonished at his doctrine.
And when even was come, he went out of the city.
¶ And in the morning, as they passed by, they saw the fig tree dried up from the roots.
And Peter calling to remembrance saith unto him, Master, behold, the fig tree which thou cursedst is withered away.
And Jesus answering saith unto them, Have TG Faith.faith in God.

For verily I say unto you, That whosoever shall say unto this mountain, Be thou removed, and be thou cast into the sea; and shall not TG Doubt.doubt in his heart, but shall believe that those things which he saith shall come to pass; he shall have whatsoever he saith.
Therefore I say unto you, What things soever ye desire, when ye TG Prayer.pray, 3 Ne. 18:20; D&C 29:6.believe that ye receive them, and ye shall have them.
And when ye stand TG Prayer.praying, forgive, if ye have ought against any: that your Father also which is in heaven may TG Forgive.forgive you your trespasses.
But if ye do not D&C 64:9 (7–10).forgive, neither will your Father which is in heaven forgive your trespasses.
¶ And they come again to Jerusalem: and as he was walking in the temple, there come to him the chief priests, and the scribes, and the elders,
And say unto him, By what TG Priesthood, Authority.authority doest thou these things? and who gave thee this authority to do these things?
And Jesus answered and said unto them, I will also ask of you one question, and answer me, and I will tell you by what authority I do these things.
The baptism of John, was it from heaven, or of men? answer me.
And they reasoned with themselves, saying, If we shall say, From heaven; he will say, Why then did ye not believe him?
But if we shall say, Of men; they feared the people: for all men counted John, that he was a prophet indeed.
And they answered and said unto Jesus, We cannot tell. And Jesus answering saith unto them, Neither do I tell you by what authority I do these things.

div.p: Jesus gives the parable of the wicked husbandmen—He speaks of paying taxes, celestial marriage, the two great commandments, the divine sonship of Christ, and the widow’s mites.

chapter.verse: And he began to speak unto them by parables. A certain man planted a vineyard, and set an hedge about it, and digged a place for the winefat, and built a tower, and let it out to husbandmen, and went into a far country.
And at the season he sent to the husbandmen a servant, that he might receive from the husbandmen of the fruit of the vineyard.
And they caught him, and beat him, and sent him away empty.
And again he sent unto them another servant; and at him they cast stones, and wounded him in the head, and sent him away shamefully handled.
And again he sent another; and him they killed, and many others; beating some, and killing some.
Having yet therefore one son, his wellbeloved, he sent him also last unto them, saying, They will reverence my son.
But those husbandmen said among themselves, This is the heir; come, let us kill him, and the inheritance shall be ours.
And they took him, and killed him, and cast him out of the vineyard.
What shall therefore the lord of the vineyard do? he will come and destroy the husbandmen, and will give the TG Vineyard of the Lord.vineyard unto others.
And have ye not read this scripture; The TG Cornerstone.stone which the builders rejected is become the head of the corner:
This was the Lord’s doing, and it is marvellous in our eyes?
And they sought to lay hold on him, but feared the people: for they knew that he had spoken the parable against them: and they left him, and went their way.
¶ And they send unto him certain of the Pharisees and of the Herodians, to Matt. 22:15 (15–22); Luke 11:54 (53–54); 20:20 (19–26).catch him in his words.

And when they were come, they say unto him, Master, we know that thou art true, and carest for no man: for thou regardest not the person of men, but teachest the way of God in truth: Is it lawful to give tribute to Cæsar, or not?
Shall we give, or shall we not give? But he, knowing their hypocrisy, said unto them, Why TG Test.tempt ye me? bring me a penny, that I may see it.
And they brought it. And he saith unto them, Whose is this image and superscription? And they said unto him, Cæsar’s.
And Jesus answering said unto them, Render to Cæsar the things that are Cæsar’s, and to God the things that are God’s. And they marvelled at him.
