chapter.verse: And it came to pass, that on one of those days, as he taught the people in the temple, and preached the gospel, the chief priests and the scribes came upon him with the elders,
And spake unto him, saying, Tell us, by what authority doest thou these things? or who is he that gave thee this authority?
And he answered and said unto them, I will also ask you one thing; and answer me:
The baptism of John, was it from heaven, or of men?
And they reasoned with themselves, saying, If we shall say, From heaven; he will say, Why then believed ye him not?
But and if we say, Of men; all the people will stone us: for they be persuaded that John was a Matt. 11:9; 14:5; 21:26.prophet.
And they answered, that they could not tell whence it was.
And Jesus said unto them, Neither tell I you by what authority I do these things.
Then began he to speak to the people this parable; A certain man planted a TG Vineyard of the Lord.vineyard, and let it forth to GR farmers.husbandmen, and went into a far country for a long time.
And at the season he sent a servant to the husbandmen, that they should give him of the fruit of the vineyard: but the husbandmen beat him, and sent him away empty.
And again he sent another servant: and they beat him also, and entreated him shamefully, and sent him away empty.
And again he sent a third: and they wounded him also, and cast him out.
Then said the lord of the vineyard, What shall I do? I will send my beloved son: it may be they will reverence him when they see him.
But when the husbandmen saw him, they reasoned among themselves, saying, This is the heir: come, let us kill him, that the inheritance may be ours.
So they cast him out of the vineyard, and killed him. What therefore shall the lord of the vineyard do unto them?
He shall come and destroy these husbandmen, and shall give the vineyard to others. And when they heard it, they said, GR May it not be.God forbid.
And he beheld them, and said, What is this then that is written, The TG Rock.stone which the builders rejected, the same is become the head of the corner?
Whosoever shall fall upon that stone shall be broken; but on whomsoever it shall fall, it will GR scatter like chaff.grind him to powder.
¶ And the chief priests and the scribes the same hour sought to lay hands on him; and they feared the people: for they perceived that he had spoken this parable against them.

And they watched him, and sent forth spies, which should feign themselves just men, that they might Matt. 22:15 (15–22); Mark 12:13 (13–17).take hold of his words, that so they might deliver him unto the power and authority of the governor.
And they asked him, saying, Master, we know that thou sayest and teachest rightly, neither acceptest thou the person of any, but teachest the way of God truly:
Is it lawful for us to give GR taxes. D&C 58:21 (21–22); 98:9 (4–10).tribute unto Cæsar, or no?
But he perceived their Alma 10:17; D&C 10:25 (21–27).craftiness, and said unto them, Why tempt ye me?
Shew me a penny. Whose image and superscription hath it? They answered and said, Cæsar’s.
And he said unto them, Render therefore unto D&C 58:22 (21–23); 63:26 (25–29).Cæsar the things which be Cæsar’s, and unto God the things which be God’s.
And they could not take hold of his words before the people: and they marvelled at his answer, and held their peace.
¶ Then came to him certain of the Sadducees, which deny that there is any resurrection; and they asked him,

Saying, Master, Moses wrote unto us, If any man’s Deut. 25:5.brother die, having a wife, and he die without children, that his Mark 12:19.brother should take his wife, and raise up seed unto his brother.
There were therefore seven brethren: and the first took a wife, and died without children.
And the second took her to wife, and he died childless.
And the third took her; and in like manner the seven also: and they left no children, and died.
Last of all the woman died also.
Therefore in the resurrection whose wife of them is she? for seven had her to wife.
And Jesus answering said unto them, The children of this world TG Marriage, Marry; Marriage, Temporal.marry, and are given in marriage:

But they which shall be accounted worthy to obtain JST Luke 20:35 … that world, through resurrection from …that world, and the TG Objectives; Resurrection.resurrection from the dead, neither marry, nor are given in D&C 132:16 (7–39).marriage:

Neither can they die any more: for they are equal unto the D&C 131:2 (1–4); 132:17 (5–17). TG Angels.angels; and are the TG Sons and Daughters of God.children of God, being the children of the resurrection.
Now that the dead are raised, even Moses shewed at the Ex. 3:2 (2–6).bush, when he calleth the Lord the God of Abraham, and the God of Isaac, and the God of Jacob.
For he is not a God of the Rom. 14:9. TG Spirits, Disembodied.dead, but of the living: for all live unto him.
¶ Then certain of the scribes answering said, Master, thou hast well said.
And after that they durst not ask him any D&C 84:116. question at all.
And he said unto them, How say they that Christ is David’s Matt. 1:16 (1–16); D&C 93:14 (3–17).son?
And David himself saith in the book of Psalms, The Lord said unto my Ps. 110:1.Lord, Sit thou on my right hand,
Till I make thine enemies thy footstool.
David therefore calleth him Lord, how is he then his son?
¶ Then in the audience of all the people he said unto his disciples,

Beware of the 2 Ne. 28:16; Alma 10:27.scribes, which desire to walk in long robes, and love greetings in the markets, and the GR first or most honorable. Luke 11:43.highest seats in the synagogues, and the chief rooms at feasts;
Which devour widows’ houses, and for a shew make long prayers: the same shall receive greater damnation.

div.p: Jesus foretells the destruction of the temple and of Jerusalem—He tells of the signs to precede His Second Coming and gives the parable of the fig tree.

chapter.verse: And he looked up, and saw the Mosiah 4:23; D&C 56:16.rich men casting their gifts into the treasury.
And he saw also a certain poor widow casting in thither two D&C 64:34 (22, 34).mites.
And he said, Of a truth I say unto you, that this poor widow hath cast in more than they all:
For all these have of their abundance cast in unto the offerings of God: but she of her penury hath cast in all the living that she had.
¶ And as some spake of the temple, how it was adorned with goodly stones and gifts, he said,
As for these things which ye behold, the days will come, in the which there shall not be left one D&C 45:20.stone upon another, that shall not be thrown down.
And they asked him, saying, Master, but when shall these things be? and what Matt. 24:3 (3–46); D&C 45:16 (16–75); JS—M 1:4 (4–55).sign will there be when these things shall come to pass?
And he said, Take heed that ye be not deceived: for many shall come in my name, saying, I am Christ; and the time draweth near: go ye not therefore after them.
But when ye shall hear of wars and commotions, be not terrified: for these things must first come to pass; but the end is not by and by.
Then said he unto them, 1 Ne. 22:14 (13–14).Nation shall rise against nation, and kingdom against kingdom:
And great earthquakes shall be in divers places, and famines, and 2 Ne. 6:15; Mosiah 12:4; D&C 97:26 (22–26).pestilences; and fearful sights and great signs shall there be from heaven.

But before all these, they shall lay their hands on you, and TG Persecution.persecute you, delivering you up to the synagogues, and into prisons, being brought before kings and rulers for my name’s sake.
And it shall turn to you for a testimony.
Settle it therefore in your hearts, not to GR practice, prepare. TG Meditation.meditate before what ye shall D&C 84:85.answer:

For I will give you a mouth and Isa. 50:4; Matt. 10:19 (16–20); Acts 6:10; D&C 11:21. TG Wisdom.wisdom, which all your adversaries shall not be able to GR speak against, oppose, contradict.gainsay nor resist.
And ye shall be betrayed both by parents, and brethren, and kinsfolks, and friends; and some of you shall they cause to be put to death.
And ye shall be hated of all men for my name’s D&C 98:13; 101:35.sake.
But there shall not an Matt. 10:30 (29–31); Alma 40:23; D&C 29:25.hair of your head perish.
In your TG Patience.patience GR preserve, win mastery over.possess ye your souls.
And when ye shall see Luke 19:43.Jerusalem compassed with Ezek. 38:16 (15–16); Rev. 16:16.armies, then know that the D&C 45:19 (18–21). TG Abomination of Desolation.desolation thereof is nigh.
Then let them which are in Judæa flee to the mountains; and let them which are in the midst of it depart out; and let not them that are in the GR districts or regions.countries enter thereinto.
For these be the days of vengeance, that all things which are written may be fulfilled.
But woe unto them that are with child, and to them that give suck, in those days! for there shall be great distress in the land, and wrath upon this people.

And they shall fall by the edge of the sword, and shall be Dan. 12:7. TG Israel, Scattering of.led away TG Israel, Bondage of, in Other Lands.captive into all TG Israel, Twelve Tribes of.nations: and Jerusalem shall be Dan. 9:27 (24–27).trodden down of the D&C 45:25. TG Gentiles.Gentiles, until the times of the Gentiles be fulfilled.

¶ JST Luke 21:24–26 (Appendix).And there shall be signs in the sun, and in the TG Astronomy.moon, and in the stars; and upon the earth distress of nations, with perplexity; the sea and the waves roaring;
Men’s hearts D&C 45:26; 88:91.failing them for fear, and for looking after those things which are coming on the earth: for the powers of heaven shall be Isa. 34:4.shaken.
And then shall they see the Son of man coming in a Dan. 7:13; D&C 34:7.cloud with power and great glory.
And when these things begin to come to pass, then look up, and lift up your heads; for your D&C 35:26.redemption draweth nigh.
And he spake to them a parable; Behold the D&C 35:16 (15–16); 45:37 (34–38).fig tree, and all the trees;
When they now shoot forth, ye see and know of your own selves that summer is now nigh at hand.
So likewise ye, when ye see these things come to pass, know ye that the kingdom of God is nigh at hand.
Verily I say unto you, JST Luke 21:32 … this generation, the generation when the times of the Gentiles be fulfilled, shall not …This generation shall not pass away, till all be fulfilled.
Heaven and earth shall pass away: but my words shall not pass away.

¶ And take heed to yourselves, lest at any time your hearts be overcharged with GR debauchery. TG Rioting and Reveling.surfeiting, and drunkenness, and TG Worldliness.cares of this life, and so that Luke 17:24; 1 Thes. 5:4.day come upon you TG Procrastination.unawares.
For as a Rev. 3:3; 16:15; D&C 63:15.snare shall it come on all them that dwell on the face of the whole earth.

TG Watch.Watch ye therefore, and TG Prayer.pray always, JST Luke 21:36 … and keep my commandments, that ye …that ye may be accounted TG Worthiness.worthy to escape all these things that shall come to pass, and to stand before the JST Luke 21:36 … Son of man when he shall come clothed in the glory of his Father.Son of man.
And in the day time he was Matt. 26:55; John 8:2 (1–2).teaching in the temple; and at night he went out, and abode in the mount that is called the mount of Olives.
And all the people came early in the morning to him in the temple, for to hear him.

div.p: Jesus institutes the sacrament—He suffers in Gethsemane and is betrayed and arrested—Peter denies knowing Him—Jesus is smitten and mocked.

chapter.verse: Now the feast of unleavened bread drew nigh, which is called the TG Passover.Passover.
And the chief 2 Ne. 10:5. TG Priestcraft.priests and TG Scribe.scribes sought how they might Matt. 26:4 (2–5); Mark 14:1 (1–2); 2 Ne. 10:3 (3–6).kill him; for they feared the people.
¶ Then entered Satan into Judas surnamed Iscariot, being of the number of the twelve.
And he went his way, and GR talked, conferred.communed with the chief priests and captains, how he might betray him unto them.
And they were glad, and covenanted to give him Zech. 11:12.money.
And he promised, and sought opportunity to betray him unto them in the absence of the multitude.
¶ Then came the day of TG Bread, Unleavened.unleavened bread, when the TG Passover.passover must be killed.
And he sent Mark 14:13 (12–16).Peter and John, saying, Go and prepare us the passover, that we may eat.
And they said unto him, Where wilt thou that we prepare?
And he said unto them, Behold, when ye are entered into the city, there shall a man meet you, bearing a pitcher of water; follow him into the house where he entereth in.
And ye shall say unto the GR master; i.e., head of house or family.goodman of the house, The Master saith unto thee, Where is the guestchamber, where I shall eat the passover with my disciples?
And he shall shew you a large upper room furnished: there make ready.
And they went, and found as he had said unto them: and they made ready the passover.
And when the hour was come, he sat down, and the twelve apostles with him.
And he said unto them, With desire I have desired to eat this passover with you before I suffer:

For I say unto you, I will not any more eat thereof, JST Luke 22:16 … until it be fulfilled which is written in the prophets concerning me. Then I will partake with you, in the …until it be fulfilled in the kingdom of God.
And he took the cup, and gave thanks, and said, Take this, and divide it among yourselves:
For I say unto you, I will not Matt. 26:29; Mark 14:25; D&C 27:5.drink of the fruit of the vine, until the kingdom of God shall come.

¶ And he took D&C 20:77.bread, and gave thanks, and brake it, and gave unto them, saying, This is my Mark 14:22; JST Mark 14:20–25 (Appendix); John 6:53. TG Jesus Christ, Atonement through.body which is TG Jesus Christ, Redeemer.given for you: this do in TG Jesus Christ, Types of, in Memory.remembrance of me.
Likewise also the D&C 20:78.cup after supper, saying, This cup is the new GR covenant.testament in my blood, which is shed for you.
¶ But, behold, the hand of him that betrayeth me is with me on the table.
And truly the Son of man goeth, as it was GR appointed, decreed. TG Foreordination.determined: but woe unto that man by whom he is Matt. 26:24; Acts 2:23.betrayed!
And they began to inquire among themselves, which of them it was that should do this thing.
¶ And there was also a TG Strife.strife among them, which of them should be accounted the Matt. 23:11; Mark 10:44 (42–45); D&C 50:26.greatest.
And he said unto them, The TG Kings, Earthly.kings of the Gentiles exercise lordship over them; and they that exercise authority upon them are called benefactors.
But ye shall not be so: but he that is greatest among you, let him be as the younger; and he that is chief, as he that doth Mosiah 2:18 (14, 18–19).serve.
For whether is greater, he that sitteth at meat, or he that serveth? is not he that sitteth at meat? but I am among you as he that TG Meek; Self-Sacrifice.serveth.
Ye are they which have TG Perseverance.continued with me in my TG Jesus Christ, Temptation of; Test.temptations.
And I appoint unto you a kingdom, as my Father hath TG Authority.appointed unto me;
That ye may Luke 14:15; Rev. 19:9.eat and drink at my table in my kingdom, and sit on thrones Morm. 3:18 (18–20); D&C 29:12. TG Apostles.judging the twelve tribes of Israel.

¶ And the Lord said, Simon, Simon, behold, JST Luke 22:31 … Satan hath desired you, that he may sift the children of the kingdom as wheat. TG Devil.Satan hath desired to TG Test. have you, that he may sift you as wheat:

But I have TG Prayer.prayed for thee, that thy faith fail not: and when thou art Mosiah 3:19. TG Conversion.converted, D&C 108:7. TG Fellowshipping; Missionary Work; Sustaining Church Leaders.strengthen thy brethren.
And he said unto him, Lord, I am Matt. 26:33.ready to go with thee, both into prison, and to death.
And he said, I tell thee, Peter, the cock shall not crow this day, before that thou shalt Matt. 26:34 (30–35).thrice deny that thou knowest me.
And he said unto them, When I sent you without Matt. 10:9 (9–10); D&C 84:78.purse, and scrip, and shoes, lacked ye any thing? And they said, Nothing.
Then said he unto them, But now, he that hath a purse, let him take it, and likewise his scrip: and he that hath no sword, let him sell his garment, and buy one.
For I say unto you, that this that is written must yet be accomplished in me, And he was reckoned among the Isa. 53:12.transgressors: for the things concerning me have an end.
And they said, Lord, behold, here are two swords. And he said unto them, It is enough.
¶ And he came out, and went, as he was GR accustomed.wont, to the mount of Olives; and his disciples also followed him.
And when he was at the place, he said unto them, Pray that ye enter not into TG Temptation.temptation.
And he was withdrawn from them about a stone’s cast, and kneeled down, and prayed,
Saying, Father, if thou be willing, D&C 19:18.remove this cup from me: nevertheless not my Moses 4:2 (1–4). TG God, Will of.will, but thine, be done.
And there appeared an TG Angels.angel unto him from heaven, strengthening him.

And being in an TG Pain.agony he prayed more earnestly: JST Luke 22:44 … and he sweat as it were great drops of blood …and his sweat was as it were great drops of Mosiah 3:7; D&C 19:18. TG Jesus Christ, Atonement through.blood falling down to the ground.
And when he rose up from prayer, and was come to his disciples, he found them sleeping for sorrow,
And said unto them, Why sleep ye? rise and pray, lest ye enter into temptation.
¶ And while he yet spake, behold a multitude, and he that was called Judas, one of the twelve, went before them, and drew near unto Jesus to kiss him.
But Jesus said unto him, Acts 1:16.Judas, TG Jesus Christ, Betrayal of.betrayest thou the Son of man with a Prov. 27:6.kiss?
When they which were about him saw what would follow, they said unto him, Lord, shall we smite with the sword?
¶ And one of them smote the servant of the high priest, and cut off his right ear.
And Jesus answered and said, Suffer ye thus far. And he touched his ear, and TG Heal.healed him.
Then Jesus said unto the chief priests, and captains of the temple, and the elders, which were come to him, Be ye come out, as against a thief, with swords and staves?
When I was daily with you in the temple, ye stretched forth no hands against me: but this is your hour, and the power of TG Darkness, Spiritual.darkness.
¶ Then took they him, and led him, and brought him into the high priest’s house. And Peter followed afar off.
And when they had kindled a fire in the midst of the GR courtyard.hall, and were set down together, Peter sat down among them.
But a certain maid beheld him as he sat by the fire, and earnestly looked upon him, and said, This man was also with him.
And he denied him, saying, Woman, I know him not.
And after a little while another saw him, and said, Thou art also of them. And Peter said, Man, I am not.
And about the space of one hour after another confidently affirmed, saying, Of a truth this fellow also was with him: for he is a Galilæan.
And Peter said, Man, I know not what thou sayest. And immediately, while he yet spake, the cock crew.
And the TG Jesus Christ, Lord.Lord turned, and looked upon Peter. And Peter remembered the word of the Lord, how he had said unto him, Before the cock crow, thou shalt deny me thrice.
And Peter went out, and wept bitterly.
¶ And the men that held Jesus TG Jesus Christ, Trials of; Mocking.mocked him, and smote him.
And when they had Matt. 26:68.blindfolded him, they struck him on the face, and asked him, saying, Prophesy, who is it that smote thee?
And many other things blasphemously spake they against him.
¶ And as soon as it was day, IE the Sanhedrin.the elders of the people and the chief priests and the scribes came together, and led him into their council, saying,
Art thou the TG Jesus Christ, Trials of.Christ? tell us. And he said unto them, If I tell you, ye will not believe:
And if I also ask you, ye will not answer me, nor let me go.
GR From now on.Hereafter shall the TG Jesus Christ, Son of Man.Son of man sit on the right hand of the power of God.
Then said they all, Art thou then the Son of God? And he said unto them, Ye say that I am.
And they said, What need we any further TG Jesus Christ, Trials of.witness? for we ourselves have heard of his own mouth.

div.p: Jesus is taken before Pilate, then to Herod, and then to Pilate again—Barabbas is released—Jesus is crucified between two thieves—He is buried in the tomb of Joseph of Arimathæa.

chapter.verse: And the whole multitude of them arose, and led him unto Pilate.
And they began to accuse him, saying, We found this fellow perverting the nation, and forbidding to give tribute to D&C 63:26.Cæsar, saying that he himself is Christ a Alma 5:50.King.
And Pilate asked him, saying, Art thou the King of the Jews? And he answered him and said, Thou sayest it.
Then said Pilate to the chief priests and to the people, I find no TG Jesus Christ, Trials of.fault in this man.
And they were the more fierce, saying, He stirreth up the people, teaching throughout all Jewry, beginning from Galilee to this place.
When Pilate heard of Galilee, he asked whether the man were a Galilæan.
And as soon as he knew that he belonged unto Herod’s jurisdiction, he sent him to Herod, who himself also was at Jerusalem at that time.

¶ And when Herod saw Jesus, he was exceeding glad: for he was desirous to see him of a long season, because he had heard many things of him; and he hoped to have seen some TG Miracle; Sign Seekers.miracle done by him.
Then he questioned with him in many words; but he Mosiah 14:7; 15:6.answered him nothing.
And the chief priests and TG Scribe.scribes stood and vehemently accused him.
And Herod with his men of war set him at nought, and TG Mocking.mocked him, and arrayed him in a gorgeous robe, and sent him again to Pilate.
¶ And the same day Pilate and Herod were made friends together: for before they were at enmity between themselves.
¶ And Pilate, when he had called together the chief priests and the rulers and the people,

Said unto them, Ye have brought this man unto me, as one that perverteth the people: and, behold, I, having examined him before you, have found no fault in this man touching those things whereof ye accuse him:
No, nor yet Herod: for I sent you to him; and, lo, nothing worthy of death is done GR by.unto him.
I will therefore chastise him, and release him.
(For of necessity he must Matt. 27:15 (15–26).release one unto them at the feast.)
And they cried out all at once, saying, Away with this man, and release unto us Barabbas:
(Who for a certain sedition made in the city, and for John 18:40.murder, was cast into prison.)
Pilate therefore, willing to release Jesus, spake again to them.
But they cried, saying, TG Jesus Christ, Trials of.Crucify him, crucify him.
And he said unto them the third time, Why, what evil hath he done? I have found Mark 15:9 (6–15).no cause of death in him: I will therefore chastise him, and let him go.
And they were GR urging.instant with loud voices, GR demanding.requiring that he might be 2 Ne. 10:3 (3–6).crucified. And the voices of them and of the chief priests prevailed.
And Pilate gave sentence that it should be as they GR demanded.required.
And he released unto them him that for sedition and Acts 3:14.murder was cast into prison, whom they had desired; but he delivered Jesus to their will.
And as they led him away, they laid hold upon one Simon, a Cyrenian, coming out of the country, and on him they laid the cross, that he might bear it after Jesus.
¶ And there followed him a great company of people, and of women, which also bewailed and lamented him.
But Jesus turning unto them said, Daughters of TG Jerusalem.Jerusalem, weep not for me, but weep for yourselves, and for your children.
For, behold, the days are coming, in the which they shall say, Blessed are the Matt. 24:19.barren, and the wombs that never bare, and the paps which never gave suck.
Then shall they begin to say to the Hosea 10:8; Rev. 6:16 (14–17).mountains, Fall on us; and to the hills, Cover us.

For if they do these things in a D&C 135:6.green tree, what shall be done in the JST Luke 23:31–32 … dry tree? This he spake, signifying the scattering of Israel, and the desolation of the heathen, or in other words, the Gentiles.dry?
And there were also two other, GR criminals. Isa. 53:9.malefactors, led with him to be put to death.
And when they were come to the place, which is called Calvary, there they crucified him, and the malefactors, one on the right hand, and the other on the left.

¶ Then Luke 6:28; Acts 7:60 (53–60).said Jesus, Father, TG Benevolence; Forgive; Mercy.forgive them; for they know not what JST Luke 23:35 … they do (Meaning the soldiers who crucified him,) …they do. And they parted his Ps. 22:18.raiment, and cast lots.

And the people stood beholding. And the rulers also with them Ps. 22:7.derided him, saying, He saved others; let him save himself, if he be Christ, the TG Jesus Christ, Relationships with the Father.chosen of God.
And the soldiers also TG Mocking.mocked him, coming to him, and offering him GR sour wine. Ps. 69:21.vinegar,
And saying, If thou be the king of the Jews, save thyself.
And a superscription also was written over him in letters of Greek, and Latin, and Hebrew, This Is the D&C 45:53 (51–53). King of the Mosiah 15:5. Jews.
¶ And one of the malefactors which were hanged GR blasphemed, reproached.railed on him, saying, If thou be Christ, save thyself and us.
But the other answering rebuked him, saying, Dost not thou fear God, seeing thou art in the same condemnation?
And we indeed justly; for we receive the due TG Reward.reward of our deeds: but this man hath done nothing amiss.
And he said unto Jesus, Lord, remember me when thou comest into thy kingdom.
And Jesus said unto him, Verily I say unto thee, To day shalt thou be with me in John 20:17; Alma 40:21 (11–14, 21). TG Immortality; Paradise; Spirits, Disembodied.paradise.
And it was about the 3 Ne. 8:19.sixth hour, and there was a Amos 8:9; Hel. 14:20. TG Darkness, Physical.darkness over all the GR earth, region, land, or country.earth until the ninth hour.
And the sun was darkened, and the veil of the temple was rent in the GR middle.midst.

¶ And when Jesus had cried with a loud voice, he said, Father, into thy hands I TG Commitment.commend my Ps. 31:5; John 10:18 (17–18). TG Man, a Spirit Child of Heavenly Father.spirit: and having said thus, he GR expired, ceased breathing, or died.gave up the TG Death.ghost.
Now when the centurion saw what was done, he glorified God, saying, Certainly this was a TG Righteousness.righteous man.
And all the people that came together to that sight, beholding the things which were done, smote their breasts, and returned.
And all his acquaintance, and the women that followed him from Galilee, stood afar off, beholding these things.
¶ And, behold, there was a man named Joseph, a IE member of the Sanhedrin, senator.counsellor; and he was a good man, and a just:
(The same had not consented to the counsel and deed of them;) he was of Arimathæa, a city of the Jews: who also himself waited for the kingdom of God.
This man went unto Pilate, and GR requested.begged the body of Jesus.
And he took it down, and wrapped it in linen, and laid it in a John 19:41; 1 Ne. 19:10; 2 Ne. 25:13.sepulchre that was hewn in stone, wherein never man before was laid.
And that day was the TG Passover.preparation, and the sabbath drew on.
And the women also, which came with him from Galilee, followed after, and beheld the Luke 24:1 (1–3).sepulchre, and how his body was laid.
And they returned, and prepared spices and ointments; and TG Rest.rested the sabbath day according to the commandment.

div.p: Angels announce the resurrection of Christ—He walks on the Emmaus road—He appears with a body of flesh and bones, eats food, testifies of His divinity, and promises the Holy Ghost—He ascends into heaven.

chapter.verse: Now upon the first day of the week, very early in the morning, they came unto the Luke 23:55.sepulchre, bringing the spices which they had prepared, and certain others with them.
JST Luke 24:2–4 (Appendix).And they found the stone rolled away from the sepulchre.
And they entered in, and found not the body of the Lord Jesus.
And it came to pass, as they were much perplexed thereabout, behold, two men stood by them in shining garments:
And as they were afraid, and bowed down their faces to the earth, they said unto them, Why seek ye the living among the dead?
He is not here, but is TG Jesus Christ, Resurrection.risen: remember how he spake unto you when he was yet in Galilee,
Saying, The Son of man must be delivered into the hands of sinful men, and be crucified, and the third day rise again.
And they remembered his words,
And returned from the sepulchre, and told all these things unto the eleven, and to all the rest.
It was Mary Magdalene, and Joanna, and Mary the mother of James, and other women that were with them, which told these things unto the apostles.
And their words seemed to them as idle tales, and they believed them not.
Then arose Peter, and ran unto the sepulchre; and stooping down, he beheld the linen clothes laid by themselves, and departed, wondering in himself at that which was come to pass.
¶ And, behold, two of them went that same day to a village called Emmaus, which was from Jerusalem The distance here indicated is about 7½ miles or 12 kilometers. about threescore furlongs.
And they talked together of all these things which had happened.
And it came to pass, that, while they OR talked.communed together and reasoned, TG Jesus Christ, Appearances, Postmortal.Jesus himself drew near, and went with them.
But their eyes were GR restrained. Heb. 13:2; Ether 3:25; D&C 25:4.holden that they should not know him.
And he said unto them, What manner of communications are these that ye have one to another, as ye walk, and are sad?
And the one of them, whose name was John 19:25.Cleopas, answering said unto him, Art thou only a stranger in Jerusalem, and hast not known the things which are come to pass there in these days?
And he said unto them, What things? And they said unto him, Concerning Jesus of Nazareth, which was a prophet mighty in deed and word before God and all the people:
And how the chief priests and our rulers delivered him to be condemned to death, and have crucified him.
But we trusted that it had been he which should have redeemed Israel: and beside all this, to day is the third day since these things were done.
Yea, and certain women also of our company made us astonished, which were early at the sepulchre;
And when they found not his body, they came, saying, that they had also seen a vision of angels, which said that he was alive.
And certain of them which were with us went to the sepulchre, and found it even so as the women had said: but him they saw not.
Then he said unto them, O GR unwise.fools, and slow of Matt. 14:31; Mark 4:40; 16:14.heart to Luke 1:20 (19–20); 12:29; Alma 22:7.believe all that the prophets have spoken:
Ought not Christ to have suffered these things, and to enter into his glory?

And beginning at John 5:46.Moses and all the Acts 3:18 (18–21); 1 Pet. 1:10 (1–16).prophets, he TG Scriptures, Study of.expounded unto them in all the TG Jesus Christ, Prophecies about.scriptures the things concerning himself.
And they drew nigh unto the village, whither they went: and he made as though he would have gone further.
But they constrained him, saying, Abide with us: for it is toward evening, and the day is far spent. And he went in to tarry with them.
And it came to pass, as he sat at meat with them, he took bread, and blessed it, and brake, and gave to them.
And their eyes were opened, and they knew him; and he vanished out of their sight.

And they said one to another, Did not our D&C 9:8. TG Heart.heart TG Teaching with the Spirit.burn within us, while he TG Jesus Christ, Teaching Mode of.talked with us by the way, and while he opened to us the scriptures?
And they rose up the same hour, and returned to Jerusalem, and found the eleven gathered together, and them that were with them,
Saying, The Lord is risen indeed, and hath TG Jesus Christ, Appearances, Postmortal.appeared to 1 Cor. 15:5 (3–8).Simon.
And they told what things were done in the way, and how he was known of them in breaking of bread.
¶ And as they thus spake, Jesus himself TG Jesus Christ, Appearances, Postmortal.stood in the midst of them, and saith unto them, D&C 19:23.Peace be unto you.
But they were terrified and affrighted, and supposed that they had seen a spirit.
And he said unto them, Why are ye troubled? and why do OR doubts, hesitations.thoughts arise in your hearts?

Behold my hands and my feet, that it is I myself: 3 Ne. 11:14 (12–15).handle me, and see; for a TG Man, Potential to Become like Heavenly Father; Spirits, Disembodied.spirit hath not D&C 129:2. TG God, Body of, Corporeal Nature; Jesus Christ, Appearances, Postmortal; Resurrection.flesh and bones, as ye TG God, Knowledge about.see me have.
And when he had thus spoken, he shewed them his hands and his feet.
And while they yet believed not for joy, and GR marveled.wondered, he said unto them, Have ye here any meat?
And they gave him a piece of a broiled fish, and of an honeycomb.
And he took it, and did eat before them.

And he said unto them, These are the words which I spake unto you, while I was yet with you, that all things must be TG Jesus Christ, Prophecies about.fulfilled, which were written in the law of Moses, and in the prophets, and in the psalms, concerning me.
Then opened he their TG Understanding.understanding, that they might understand the scriptures,
And said unto them, Thus it is written, and thus it GR was necessary for, proper for.behoved Christ to suffer, and to TG Resurrection.rise from the dead the third day:

And that TG Repent.repentance and D&C 137:6. TG Forgive; Remission of Sins.remission of sins should be TG Apostles; Mission of Early Saints.preached in his name among all nations, beginning at Jerusalem.
And ye are Acts 1:8; 22:15 (14–16); D&C 27:12. TG Witness.witnesses of these things.
¶ And, behold, I send the TG Promise.promise of my Father upon you: but tarry ye in the city of Jerusalem, until ye be OR clothed, invested. D&C 20:8; 38:32 (32, 38).endued with power from on high.
¶ And he led them out as far as to Bethany, and he lifted up his hands, and blessed them.
And it came to pass, while he blessed them, he was parted from them, and TG Jesus Christ, Ascension of.carried up into heaven.
And they worshipped him, and returned to Jerusalem with great joy:
And were continually in the Acts 2:46.temple, praising and blessing God. Amen.

div.headCT: Title: JST entitles this book “The Testimony of St. John.”St John
div.title: The Gospel According to

div.p: Christ is the Word of God—He created all things and was made flesh—John baptizes Jesus and testifies that He is the Lamb of God—John, Andrew, Simon, Philip, and Nathanael believe in Christ and follow Him.

chapter.verse: JST John 1:1–34 (Appendix).In the D&C 76:39. TG Jesus Christ, Firstborn; Jesus Christ, Foreordained.beginning was the Word, and the TG Jesus Christ, Messenger of the Covenant; Jesus Christ, Relationships with the Father.Word was with God, and the TG Jesus Christ, Jehovah.Word was TG Jesus Christ, Authority of.God.
The same was in the 1 Jn. 1:1. TG Man, Antemortal Existence of.beginning with God.
All things were TG Creation; God, Power of; Jesus Christ, Creator; Jesus Christ, Mission of; Jesus Christ, Power of.made by him; and without him was not any thing made that was made.
In him was 1 Jn. 5:11 (10–21).life; and the life was the TG Jesus Christ, Light of the World; Light [noun].light of men.
And the D&C 6:21; 34:2; 45:7.light shineth in TG Darkness, Spiritual.darkness; and the darkness 1 Cor. 2:11 (10–14); D&C 45:29.comprehended it not.
¶ There was a man sent from God, whose name was 1 Ne. 11:27.John.
The same came for a John 19:35 (35–37).witness, to bear D&C 66:7.witness of the Light, that all men through him might John 20:31 (30–31).believe.
He was not that Light, but was sent to bear witness of that Light.
That was the true John 12:46. TG Intelligence; Jesus Christ, Light of the World; Light of Christ.Light, which TG Discernment, Spiritual.lighteth D&C 84:46.every man that cometh into the world.
He was in the TG World.world, and the TG Creation.world was TG Jesus Christ, Creator.made by him, and the world Isa. 53:3.knew him not.
He came unto his own, and his own 3 Ne. 9:16; D&C 6:21.received him not.

But as many as D&C 25:1. TG Teachable.received him, to them gave he GR authority, right, privilege. D&C 11:30. TG Jesus Christ, Power of.power to become the D&C 39:4 (3–6). TG Sons and Daughters of God.sons of God, even to them that believe on his TG Name.name:
Which were TG Holy Ghost, Baptism of.born, not of blood, nor of the will of the flesh, nor of the will of man, but of God.

And the Word was made TG Flesh and Blood; God, Manifestations of; Jesus Christ, Birth of; Jesus Christ, Condescension of.flesh, and TG God, Presence of.dwelt among us, (and we Matt. 17:2 (1–9); Luke 1:2; John 19:35; 21:24; Acts 5:32; 26:16; 2 Pet. 1:16 (16–19); D&C 93:11.beheld his Heb. 1:3. TG Glory; Jesus Christ, Glory of.glory, the glory as of the TG Jesus Christ, Divine Sonship.only D&C 76:23.begotten of the Father,) full of D&C 66:12.grace and truth.
¶ John bare John 5:33.witness of him, and cried, saying, This was he of whom I spake, He that cometh after me is preferred before me: for he was before me.
And of his Col. 2:10.fulness have all we received, and D&C 93:12.grace for grace.
JST John 1:17–18 (Appendix).For the TG Law of Moses.law was given by Moses, but TG Grace.grace and TG Truth.truth came by Jesus Christ.

No D&C 67:11; 84:22.man hath TG God, Privilege of Seeing.seen God JST John 1:19 … at any time, except he hath borne record of the Son; for except it is through him no man can be saved.at any time; the only begotten Son, which is in the bosom of the Father, he hath Luke 10:22.declared him.
¶ And this is the record of Luke 3:15 (15–16).John, when the Jews sent priests and Levites from Jerusalem to ask him, Who art thou?
And he confessed, and denied not; but confessed, I am not the Christ.
And they asked him, What then? Art thou Mal. 4:5 (5–6).Elias? And he saith, I am not. Art thou that prophet? And he answered, No.
Then said they unto him, Who art thou? that we may give an answer to them that sent us. What sayest thou of thyself?
He said, I am the Matt. 3:3; Mark 1:3; Luke 3:4.voice of one crying in the wilderness, Make D&C 84:28.straight the D&C 65:1.way of the Lord, as said the prophet Esaias.
And they which were sent were of the Pharisees.
And they asked him, and said unto him, Why baptizest thou then, if thou be not that Christ, nor Elias, neither that prophet?
John answered them, saying, I baptize with D&C 52:10.water: but there standeth one among you, whom ye know not;
JST John 1:28 (Appendix).He it is, who coming after me is preferred before me, whose GR sandal strap.shoe’s latchet I am not worthy to unloose.
These things were done in 1 Ne. 10:9.Bethabara beyond Jordan, where John was baptizing.

¶ The next day John seeth Jesus coming unto him, and saith, Behold the TG Jesus Christ, Lamb of God; Jesus Christ, Redeemer; Jesus Christ, Types of, in Anticipation; Passover.Lamb of God, which taketh away the TG Jesus Christ, Atonement through; Redemption; Sin.sin of the world.
This is he of whom I said, After me cometh a man which is preferred before me: for he was before me.
And I knew him not: but that he should be made manifest to Israel, therefore am I come baptizing with water.
And John D&C 93:11.bare record, saying, I saw the TG Jesus Christ, Baptism of.Spirit descending from heaven like a TG Holy Ghost, Dove, Sign of.dove, and it abode upon him.

JST John 1:32 And I knew him; for he who sent me …And I knew him not: but he that sent me to baptize with water, the same said unto me, Upon whom thou shalt see the Spirit descending, and remaining on him, the same is he which baptizeth with the TG Holy Ghost, Baptism of.Holy Ghost.
And I saw, and bare TG Testimony.record that this is the Son of God.
¶ Again the next day after John stood, and two of his disciples;
And looking upon Jesus as he walked, he saith, Behold the TG Passover.Lamb of God!
And the two disciples heard him speak, and they followed Jesus.
Then Jesus turned, and saw them following, and saith unto them, What seek ye? They said unto him, Rabbi, (which is to say, being interpreted, Master,) where dwellest thou?
He saith unto them, Come and see. They came and saw where he dwelt, and abode with him that day: for it was about the tenth hour.
One of the two which heard John speak, and TG Apostles.followed him, was Andrew, Simon Peter’s brother.
He first findeth his own brother Simon, and saith unto him, We have found the TG Jesus Christ, Messiah.Messias, which is, being interpreted, the Christ.

And he brought him to Jesus. And when Jesus beheld him, he said, Thou art Simon the son of Jona: thou shalt be called GR Stone, Pebble. JST John 1:42 … Cephas, which is, by interpretation, a seer, or a stone. And they were fishermen. And they straightway left all, and followed Jesus. Matt. 16:18; Luke 6:14; Gal. 2:9.Cephas, which is by interpretation, A stone.
¶ The day following Jesus would go forth into Galilee, and findeth Philip, and saith unto him, Follow me.
Now Philip was of Bethsaida, the city of Andrew and Peter.

Philip findeth Nathanael, and saith unto him, We have found him, of whom TG Jesus Christ, Prophecies about.Moses in the law, and the prophets, did TG Record Keeping.write, Jesus of Nazareth, the son of Joseph.
And Nathanael said unto him, Can there any good thing come out of Nazareth? Philip saith unto him, Come and see.
Jesus saw D&C 41:11.Nathanael coming to him, and saith of him, Behold an Israelite indeed, in whom is no GR deceit, fraud. TG Guile.guile!
Nathanael saith unto him, Whence knowest thou me? Jesus answered and said unto him, Before that Philip called thee, when thou wast under the fig tree, I saw thee.
Nathanael answered and saith unto him, Rabbi, thou art the TG God, Body of, Corporeal Nature.Son of God; thou art the TG Jesus Christ, Messiah.King of Israel.
Jesus answered and said unto him, Because I said unto thee, I saw thee under the fig tree, believest thou? thou shalt see greater things than these.
And he saith unto him, Verily, verily, I say unto you, Hereafter ye shall see heaven open, and the angels of God ascending and descending upon the Son of man.

div.p: Jesus turns water into wine in Cana—He attends the Passover, cleanses the temple, foretells His death and resurrection, and performs miracles.

chapter.verse: And the JST John 2:1 … third day of the week …third day there was a marriage in Cana of Galilee; and the mother of Jesus was there:
And both Jesus was called, and his disciples, to the marriage.
And when they wanted wine, the mother of Jesus saith unto him, They have no wine.

Jesus saith unto her, JST John 2:4 … Woman, what wilt thou have me to do for thee? that will I do; for mine hour is not yet come. TG Woman.Woman, what have I to do with thee? mine hour is not yet come.
His mother saith unto the servants, Whatsoever he saith unto you, do it.
And there were set there six waterpots of stone, after the manner of the TG Purification.purifying of the Jews, containing two or three firkins apiece.
Jesus saith unto them, Fill the waterpots with water. And they filled them up to the brim.
And he saith unto them, Draw out now, and bear unto the governor of the feast. And they bare it.
When the ruler of the feast had tasted the water that was made wine, and knew not whence it was: (but the servants which drew the water knew;) the governor of the feast called the bridegroom,
And saith unto him, Every man at the beginning doth set forth good wine; and when men have well drunk, then that which is worse: but thou hast kept the good wine until now.
This John 4:54.beginning of TG Miracle.miracles did Jesus in Cana of Galilee, and manifested forth his glory; and his disciples believed on him.
¶ After this he went down to Capernaum, he, and his mother, and his TG Jesus Christ, Family of.brethren, and his disciples: and they continued there not many days.
¶ And the Jews’ passover was at hand, and Jesus went up to Jerusalem,
And found in the temple those that sold oxen and sheep and doves, and the changers of Ps. 69:9.money sitting:
And when he had made a GR whip. Matt. 21:12–16.scourge of small cords, he drove them all out of the temple, and the sheep, and the oxen; and poured out the changers’ money, and overthrew the tables;
And said unto them that sold doves, Take these things hence; make not my Father’s house an house of merchandise.
And his disciples remembered that it was written, The TG Zeal.zeal of thine house hath GR consumed me.eaten me up.
¶ Then answered the Jews and said unto him, What TG Sign Seekers.sign shewest thou unto us, seeing that thou doest these things?
Jesus answered and said unto them, Destroy this Matt. 26:61; 27:40, 63 (62–66); Mark 14:58.temple, and in three days I will TG Jesus Christ, Resurrection; Resurrection.raise it up.
Then said the Jews, Forty and six years was this TG Temple.temple in building, and wilt thou rear it up in three days?
But he spake of the temple of his body.
When therefore he was risen from the dead, his disciples remembered that he had said this unto them; and they believed the scripture, and the word which Jesus had said.
¶ Now when he was in Jerusalem at the TG Passover.passover, in the feast day, many believed in his TG Name.name, when they saw the TG Miracle; Signs.miracles which he did.
But Jesus did not GR entrust.commit himself unto them, because he Luke 6:8.knew all JST John 2:24 … things, men ,
And needed not that any should testify of man: for he Matt. 12:25; Luke 11:17; John 6:61.knew what was in D&C 62:1.man.

div.p: Jesus tells Nicodemus that men must be born again—God so loved the world that He sent His Only Begotten Son to save men—John the Baptist testifies that he that believes on the Son has everlasting life.

chapter.verse: There was a man of the Pharisees, named John 7:50; 19:39.Nicodemus, a John 7:26.ruler of the Jews:

The same came to Jesus by night, and said unto him, Rabbi, we know that thou art a teacher come from God: for no man can do these TG Miracle.miracles that thou doest, except Ex. 8:19; Luke 11:20; Acts 2:22; 10:38.God be with him.
Jesus answered and said unto him, Verily, verily, I say unto thee, Except a man be TG Man, New, Spiritually Reborn.born GR from above, anew.again, he cannot D&C 56:18.see the kingdom of God.
Nicodemus saith unto him, How can a man be born when he is old? can he enter the second time into his mother’s womb, and be born?

Jesus answered, Verily, verily, I say unto thee, Except a man be TG Holy Ghost, Baptism of.born of TG Baptism; Baptism, Essential.water and of the TG Holy Ghost, Mission of.Spirit, he cannot 1 Cor. 6:9; 15:50.enter into the kingdom of God.
That which is born of the flesh is flesh; and that which is born of the Spirit is spirit.
Marvel not that I said unto thee, Ye must be TG Man, Natural, Not Spiritually Reborn.born again.

The GR wind, spirit.wind bloweth where it listeth, and thou hearest the sound thereof, but canst not tell whence it cometh, and whither it goeth: so is every one that is born of the Eccl. 11:5.Spirit.
Nicodemus answered and said unto him, How can these things be?
Jesus answered and said unto him, Art thou a master of Israel, and knowest not these things?

The Greek construction suggests that verses 11–21 contain a direct quotation. This testimony of Jesus was given to a member of the Sanhedrin.Verily, verily, I say unto thee, We speak that we do know, and TG Testimony.testify that we have seen; and ye receive not our witness.
If I have told you earthly things, and ye TG Faith.believe not, how shall ye believe, if I tell you of heavenly things?
And no man hath TG Jesus Christ, Ascension of.ascended up to heaven, but he that John 6:62. TG Jesus Christ, Condescension of.came down from heaven, even the Moses 6:57.Son of man which is in heaven.

¶ And as Moses lifted up the 2 Ne. 25:20. TG Jesus Christ, Prophecies about; Jesus Christ, Types of, in Anticipation; Symbolism.serpent in the wilderness, even so must the TG Jesus Christ, Son of Man.Son of man be lifted up:
That whosoever believeth in him should not perish, but have eternal life.

¶ For TG God the Father, Elohim.God so TG God, Love of; Love; Mercy; Worth of Souls.loved the TG World.world, that he TG God, Gifts of; Jesus Christ, Redeemer.gave his Gen. 22:2 (1–14); Jacob 4:5.only begotten TG Jesus Christ, Divine Sonship.Son, that whosoever TG Faith.believeth in him should not perish, but have D&C 45:5.everlasting 1 Jn. 4:9.life.

For God D&C 49:5; 132:24 (24, 59). TG Jesus Christ, Messenger of the Covenant.sent not his Son into the world to Luke 9:56 (54–56).condemn the world; but that the world through him might be TG Jesus Christ, Atonement through.saved.

¶ He that believeth on him is not condemned: but he that TG Unbelief.believeth not is condemned already, because he hath not believed in the TG Name.name of the only begotten JST John 3:18 … Son of God, which before was preached by the mouth of the holy prophets; for they testified of me.Son of God.

And this is the condemnation, that TG Light [noun]; Light of Christ.light is come into the world, and men loved TG Darkness, Spiritual; Secret Combinations.darkness rather than light, because their D&C 10:21; 29:45.deeds were evil.
For every one that doeth TG Evil.evil TG Hate.hateth the light, neither cometh to the light, lest his deeds should be reproved.
But he that TG Good Works.doeth TG Truth.truth cometh to the TG Discernment, Spiritual.light, that his deeds may be made manifest, that they are wrought in God.
¶ After these things came Jesus and his disciples into the land of Judæa; and there he tarried with them, and John 4:2 (1–2); JST John 4:1–4 (Appendix).baptized.
¶ And John also was baptizing in Ænon near to Salim, because there was much TG Baptism, Immersion.water there: and they came, and were TG Baptism.baptized.
For John was not yet cast into prison.
¶ Then there arose a question between some of John’s disciples and the Jews about purifying.

And they came unto John, and said unto him, Rabbi, he that was with thee beyond Jordan, to whom thou barest witness, behold, the same baptizeth, JST John 3:27 … and he receiveth of all people who come unto him.and all men come to him.
John answered and said, A man can James 1:17 (17–18).receive nothing, except it be given him from heaven.
Ye yourselves bear me witness, that I said, I am not the Christ, but that I am sent before him.
He that hath the bride is the bridegroom: but the friend of the bridegroom, which standeth and heareth him, rejoiceth greatly because of the bridegroom’s voice: this my joy therefore is fulfilled.
He must increase, but I must decrease.
He that cometh from John 8:23.above is above all: he that is of the earth is earthly, and speaketh of the earth: he that cometh from heaven is above all.
And what he hath seen and heard, that he TG Testimony.testifieth; and no man receiveth his testimony.
He that hath received his testimony hath OR declared that, certified that.set to his TG Seal.seal that God is true.

For he whom God hath sent Isa. 61:1. TG Jesus Christ, Teaching Mode of.speaketh the words of God: JST John 3:34 … for God giveth him not the Spirit by measure, for he dwelleth in him, even the fullness.for God giveth not the Spirit by measure unto him.
The Father loveth the TG Jesus Christ, Divine Sonship.Son, and hath TG Jesus Christ, Authority of.given all TG Jesus Christ, Power of.things into his hand.

JST John 3:36 And he who believeth on the Son hath everlasting life; and shall receive of his fullness. But he who believeth not the Son, shall not receive of his fullness; for the wrath of God is upon him.He that believeth on the Son hath TG Eternal Life.everlasting life: and he that GR disbelieves, disobeys, is uncompliant to.believeth not the Son shall not see life; but the TG God, Indignation of.wrath of God abideth on him.

div.p: Jesus teaches a woman of Samaria—All must worship the Father in spirit and truth—Those who harvest souls gain eternal life—Many Samaritans believe—Jesus heals a nobleman’s son.

chapter.verse: JST John 4:1–4 (Appendix).When therefore the Lord knew how the Pharisees had heard that Jesus made and baptized more disciples than John,
(Though Jesus himself John 3:22 (22, 26).baptized not, but his disciples,)
He left Judæa, and departed again into Galilee.
And he must needs go through Samaria.
Then cometh he to a city of Samaria, which is called Sychar, near to the parcel of ground that Jacob gave to his son TG Israel, Joseph, People of.Joseph.
Now Jacob’s well was there. Jesus therefore, being wearied with his journey, sat thus on the well: and it was about the sixth hour.
There cometh a woman of Samaria to draw water: Jesus saith unto her, Give me to drink.
(For his disciples were gone away unto the city to buy meat.)

Then saith the woman of Samaria unto him, How is it that thou, being a Jew, askest drink of me, which am a woman of Samaria? for the Jews have no dealings with the 2 Kgs. 17:29; Luke 10:33.Samaritans.

Jesus answered and said unto her, If thou knewest the TG God, Gifts of.gift of God, and who it is that saith to thee, Give me to drink; thou wouldest have asked of him, and he would have given thee TG Living Water.living water.
The woman saith unto him, Sir, thou hast nothing to draw with, and the well is deep: from whence then hast thou that living water?
Art thou greater than our father Jacob, which gave us the well, and drank thereof himself, and his children, and his cattle?
Jesus answered and said unto her, Whosoever drinketh of this water shall thirst again:

But whosoever drinketh of the water that I shall give him shall never Ps. 42:2 (1–3); 143:6 (5–12); Isa. 55:1 (1–3); John 6:35; 7:37 (37–39).thirst; but the water that I shall give him shall be in him a well of water D&C 63:23.springing up into TG Immortality.everlasting life.
The woman saith unto him, Sir, give me this water, that I thirst not, neither come hither to draw.
Jesus saith unto her, Go, call thy husband, and come hither.
The woman answered and said, I have no husband. Jesus said unto her, Thou hast well said, I have no husband:
For thou hast had five husbands; and he whom thou now hast is not thy husband: in that saidst thou truly.
The woman saith unto him, Sir, I perceive that thou art a 1 Ne. 22:20.prophet.
Our fathers worshipped in Deut. 11:29.this mountain; and ye say, that in Jerusalem is the place where men ought to worship.
Jesus saith unto her, Woman, believe me, the hour cometh, when ye shall neither in this mountain, nor yet at Jerusalem, worship the Father.
Ye worship ye D&C 93:19 (1–20). TG Ignorance.know not what: we know what we worship: for salvation is of the Jews.
But the hour cometh, and now is, when the true worshippers shall TG Worship.worship the Father in spirit and in Ps. 145:18 (1–21).truth: for the Father seeketh such to worship him.

JST John 4:26 For unto such hath God promised his Spirit. And they who worship him, must worship in spirit and in truth.God is a D&C 93:33; 130:22.Spirit: and they that worship him must worship him in spirit and in TG Truth.truth.
The woman saith unto him, I know that TG Jesus Christ, Messiah.Messias cometh, which is called Christ: when he is come, he will Deut. 18:18.tell us all things.

Jesus saith unto her, The term I Am used here in the Greek is identical with the Septuagint usage in Ex. 3:14 which identifies Jehovah. John 8:58. TG Jesus Christ, Jehovah.I that speak unto thee am he.
¶ And upon this came his disciples, and marvelled that he talked with the woman: yet no man said, What seekest thou? or, Why talkest thou with her?
The woman then left her waterpot, and went her way into the city, and saith to the men,
Come, see a man, which told me all things that ever I did: is not this the Christ?
Then they went out of the city, and came unto him.
¶ In the mean while his disciples prayed him, saying, Master, eat.
But he said unto them, I have meat to eat that ye know not of.
Therefore said the disciples one to another, Hath any man brought him ought to eat?

Jesus saith unto them, My meat is to do the Luke 2:49; John 6:38–39; 8:29; see JST Matt. 27:54 … Father, it is finished, thy will is done … TG God, Will of.will of him that sent me, and to finish his John 9:4 (1–4).work.

Say not ye, There are yet four months, and then cometh harvest? behold, I say unto you, Lift up your eyes, and look on the Alma 26:5; D&C 4:4; 33:3.fields; for they are white already to TG Missionary Work.harvest.
And he that reapeth receiveth TG Wages.wages, and gathereth fruit unto life eternal: that both he that soweth and he that reapeth may D&C 18:16 (13–16); 50:22 (17–22).rejoice together.
And herein is that saying true, One 1 Cor. 3:6 (5–8).soweth, and another reapeth.
I sent you to TG Harvest.reap that whereon ye bestowed no labour: JST John 4:40 … the prophets have labored …other men laboured, and ye are entered into their labours.
¶ And many of the Samaritans of that city believed on him for the saying of the woman, which testified, He told me all that ever I did.
So when the Samaritans were come unto him, they besought him that he would tarry with them: and he abode there two days.
And many more believed because of his own word;

And said unto the woman, Now we believe, not because of thy saying: for we have heard him ourselves, and know that this is indeed the TG Jesus Christ, Messiah.Christ, the D&C 66:1.Saviour of the world.
¶ Now after two days he departed thence, and went into Galilee.
For Jesus himself testified, that a prophet hath no honour in his own country.
Then when he was come into Galilee, the Galilæans received him, having seen all the things that he did at Jerusalem at the feast: for they also went unto the feast.
So Jesus came again into Cana of Galilee, where he made the water wine. And there was a certain nobleman, whose son was sick at Luke 4:23 (23–24).Capernaum.
When he heard that Jesus was come out of Judæa into Galilee, he went unto him, and besought him that he would come down, and heal his son: for he was at the point of death.
Then said Jesus unto him, Except ye see TG Sign Seekers.signs and wonders, ye will not believe.
The nobleman saith unto him, Sir, come down ere my child die.
Jesus saith unto him, Go thy way; thy son TG Heal.liveth. And the man believed the word that Jesus had spoken unto him, and he went his way.
And as he was now going down, his servants met him, and told him, saying, Thy son liveth.
Then inquired he of them the hour when he began to amend. And they said unto him, Yesterday at the seventh hour the fever left him.
So the father knew that it was at the same hour, in the which Jesus said unto him, Thy son liveth: and GR he himself.himself believed, and his whole house.
This is again the John 2:11.second miracle that Jesus did, when he was come out of Judæa into Galilee.

div.p: Jesus heals an invalid on the Sabbath—He explains why men must honor the Son—Jesus promises to take the gospel to the dead—Man is resurrected, judged, and assigned his glory by the Son—Jesus obeys the divine law of witnesses.

chapter.verse: After this there was The Koine Greek manuscripts of the Gospels (Byzantine) read “the feast,” implicitly the Passover. See also Matt. 26:5. Some earlier manuscripts do not make this identification. TG Passover.a feast of the Jews; and Jesus went up to Jerusalem.
Now there is at Jerusalem by the sheep market a pool, which is called in the Hebrew tongue Bethesda, having five porches.
In these lay a great multitude of impotent folk, of blind, halt, withered, waiting for the moving of the water.
For an angel went down at a certain season into the pool, and troubled the water: whosoever then first after the troubling of the water stepped in was made whole of whatsoever disease he had.
And a certain man was there, which had an infirmity thirty and eight years.
When Jesus saw him lie, and knew that he had been now a long time in that case, he saith unto him, Wilt thou be made whole?
The impotent man answered him, Sir, I have no man, when the water is troubled, to put me into the pool: but while I am coming, another steppeth down before me.
Jesus saith unto him, John 7:23.Rise, take up thy bed, and walk.
And immediately the man was made TG Heal.whole, and took up his bed, and walked: and on the same day was the Matt. 12:1 (1–14).sabbath.
¶ The Jews therefore said unto him that was cured, It is the Luke 6:2. TG Sabbath.sabbath day: it is not lawful for thee to carry thy bed.
He answered them, He that made me whole, the same said unto me, Take up thy bed, and walk.
Then asked they him, What man is that which said unto thee, Take up thy bed, and walk?
And he that was healed GR knew.wist not who it was: for Jesus had conveyed himself away, a multitude being in that place.
Afterward Jesus findeth him in the temple, and said unto him, Behold, thou art made whole: sin no more, lest a worse thing come unto thee.
The man departed, and told the Jews that it was Jesus, which had made him whole.
And therefore did the Jews TG Persecution.persecute Jesus, and John 7:1.sought to slay him, because he had done these things on the sabbath day.
¶ But Jesus answered them, My Father TG God, Works of.worketh hitherto, and I John 9:4.work.

Therefore the Jews sought the more to kill him, because he not only had broken the sabbath, but said also that God was his TG God, Body of, Corporeal Nature.Father, making himself John 10:33; 19:7; D&C 88:107. TG Jesus Christ, Relationships with the Father.equal with God.

Then answered Jesus and said unto them, Verily, verily, I say unto you, The Son can do nothing of himself, but what he John 8:28.seeth the TG Godhead.Father do: for what things soever he doeth, these also doeth the Son likewise.
For the Father loveth the Son, and sheweth him all things that himself doeth: and he will shew him greater works than these, that ye may marvel.
For as the Father TG Resurrection.raiseth up the dead, and GR causes to become alive. 1 Cor. 15:45. TG God, Power of.quickeneth them ; even so the Son quickeneth whom he will.
For the Father judgeth no man, but hath TG Jesus Christ, Authority of.committed all TG Judgment; Judgment, the Last.judgment unto the Son:
That all men should TG Honor; Respect.honour the Son, even as they honour the Father. He that Luke 10:16.honoureth not the Son honoureth not the Father which hath sent him.

Verily, verily, I say unto you, He that heareth my word, and TG Faith.believeth on him that sent me, hath everlasting John 20:31.life, and shall not come into D&C 20:15.condemnation; but is passed from John 8:51; 11:26; D&C 63:49.death unto 1 Jn. 3:14.life.

Verily, verily, I say unto you, The hour is coming, and now is, when the TG Death; Genealogy and Temple Work; Salvation for the Dead; Spirits, Disembodied.dead shall Isa. 24:22; 61:1.hear the voice of the Son of God: and they that hear shall TG Immortality.live.
For as the Father hath life in himself; so hath he given to the TG Jesus Christ, Divine Sonship.Son to have John 6:57.life in himself;

And hath given him TG Authority; Delegation of Responsibility; Jesus Christ, Authority of.authority to execute TG Jesus Christ, Judge.judgment also, because he is the TG Jesus Christ, Son of Man.Son of man.
Marvel not at this: for the hour is coming, in the which all that are in the Dan. 12:2.graves shall D&C 76:16 (16–17).hear his voice,

And shall D&C 29:26.come forth; JST John 5:29 … they who have done good, in the resurrection of the just; and they who have done evil, in the resurrection of the unjust.they that have done good, unto the TG Resurrection.resurrection of life; and they that have done TG Sin.evil, unto the resurrection of TG Damnation; Hell.damnation.

I can of mine own self do nothing: as I hear, I judge: and my judgment is TG God, Justice of.just; because I seek not mine own 2 Ne. 31:7 (7–10). TG Agency.will, but the TG God, Will of.will of the Father which hath sent me.
If I bear witness of myself, my witness is not true.
¶ There is another that beareth TG Holy Ghost, Source of Testimony.witness of me; and I know that the TG Witness.witness which he witnesseth of me is true.
Ye sent unto D&C 35:4; 84:27 (27–28).John, and he bare John 1:15 (7, 15, 19).witness unto the truth.

JST John 5:35 And he received not his testimony of man, but of God, and ye yourselves say that he is a prophet, therefore ye ought to receive his testimony. These things I say …But I receive not testimony from man: but these things I say, that ye might be saved.
He was a burning and a shining Matt. 11:11 (7–15).light: and ye were willing for a season to rejoice in his TG Light [noun].light.

¶ JST John 5:37 But I have a greater witness than the testimony of John …But I have GR the greater witness than John’s.greater witness than that of John: for the TG God, Works of.works which the Father hath given me to finish, the same Matt. 11:4; John 10:25.works that I do, bear witness of me, that the Father hath TG Jesus Christ, Messenger of the Covenant.sent me.
And the Father himself, which hath sent me, hath borne TG Witness of the Father.witness of me. Ye have neither heard his voice at any time, nor TG God, Privilege of Seeing.seen his shape.
And ye have not his word abiding in you: for whom he hath sent, him ye TG Unbelief.believe not.
¶ TG Education; Learn; Scriptures, Study of; Study.Search the scriptures; for in them ye think ye have eternal life: and they are they which TG Scriptures, Value of; Testimony.testify of me.
And ye will not come to me, that ye might have D&C 66:2.life.
I receive not John 7:18. TG Honor.honour from men.
But I know you, that ye have not the TG God, Love of; Love.love of God in you.
I am come in my TG Jesus Christ, Authority of.Father’s TG Authority; Name.name, and ye D&C 132:25.receive me not: if another shall come in his own name, him ye will receive.
How can ye believe, which receive Luke 18:9; 1 Thes. 2:6; D&C 76:61.honour one of another, and seek not the TG God, the Standard of Righteousness.honour that cometh from God only?
Do not think that I will accuse you to the Father: there is one that accuseth you, even Moses, in whom ye trust.
For had ye believed Luke 24:27.Moses, ye would have believed me: for he TG Jesus Christ, Prophecies about.wrote of me.
But if ye believe not his TG Scriptures, Study of.writings, how shall ye believe my words?

div.p: Jesus feeds the five thousand—He walks on the sea—He is the living manna sent from God—Salvation is gained by eating living bread—Jesus explains how men eat His flesh and drink His blood—Peter testifies that Jesus is the Messiah.

chapter.verse: After these things Jesus went over the sea of Galilee, which is the sea of Tiberias.
And a great multitude followed him, because they saw his miracles which he did on them that were diseased.
And Jesus went up into a mountain, and there he sat with his disciples.
And the TG Passover.passover, a feast of the Jews, was nigh.
¶ When Jesus then lifted up his eyes, and saw a great company come unto him, he saith unto Philip, Whence shall we buy bread, that these may eat?
And this he said to prove him: for he himself knew what he would do.
Philip answered him, Two hundred pennyworth of bread is not sufficient for them, that every one of them may take a little.
One of his disciples, Andrew, Simon Peter’s brother, saith unto him,
There is a lad here, which hath five barley loaves, and two small fishes: but what are they among so many?
And Jesus said, Make the men sit down. Now there was much grass in the place. So the men sat down, in number about Matt. 14:21 (13–21); Mark 6:44 (32–44); Luke 9:14 (10–17).five thousand.

And Jesus took the loaves; and when he had given D&C 46:32; 59:7.thanks, he distributed to the disciples, and the disciples to them that were set down; and likewise of the fishes as much as they would.
When they were filled, he said unto his disciples, Gather up the fragments that remain, that nothing be TG Waste.lost.
Therefore they gathered them together, and filled twelve baskets with the fragments of the five barley loaves, which remained over and above unto them that had eaten.

Then those men, when they had seen the 3 Ne. 8:1; 20:7 (1–9); Morm. 9:18 (18–19).miracle that Jesus did, said, This is of a truth that TG Jesus Christ, Prophecies about.prophet that should come into the world.
¶ When Jesus therefore perceived that they would come and take him by force, to make him a John 18:36 (33, 36–37).king, he departed again into a mountain himself alone.
And when even was now come, his disciples went down unto the sea,
And entered into a ship, and went over the sea toward Capernaum. And it was now dark, and Jesus was not come to them.
And the sea arose by reason of a great wind that blew.
So when they had rowed about five and twenty or thirty furlongs, they see Jesus walking on the sea, and drawing nigh unto the ship: and they were D&C 67:3.afraid.
But he saith unto them, It is I; be not afraid.
Then they willingly received him into the ship: and immediately the ship was at the land whither they went.

¶ The day following, when the people which stood on the other side of the sea saw that there was none other boat there, save that one whereinto his disciples were entered, and that Jesus went not with his disciples into the boat, but that his disciples were gone away alone;
(Howbeit there came other boats from Tiberias nigh unto the place where they did eat bread, after that the Lord had given thanks:)
When the people therefore saw that Jesus was not there, neither his disciples, they also took shipping, and came to Capernaum, seeking for Jesus.
And when they had found him on the other side of the sea, they said unto him, Rabbi, when camest thou hither?

Jesus answered them and said, Verily, verily, I say unto you, Ye seek me, JST John 6:26 … not because ye desire to keep my sayings, neither because ye saw the miracles …not because ye saw the miracles, but because ye did eat of the loaves, and were filled.

TG Labor; Objectives.Labour not for the meat which perisheth, but for that meat which endureth unto everlasting life, which the TG Jesus Christ, Son of Man.Son of man shall give unto you: for him hath God the Father TG Sealing.sealed.
Then said they unto him, What shall we do, that we might work the works of God?
Jesus answered and said unto them, This is the work of God, that ye TG Faith.believe on him whom he hath sent.
They said therefore unto him, What Matt. 12:38; Jacob 7:13 (13–21); Alma 30:43 (43–60); D&C 46:9 (8–9).sign shewest thou then, that we may see, and believe thee? what dost thou work?
Our fathers did eat manna in the desert; as it is written, He gave them Ps. 78:24.bread from heaven to eat.
Then Jesus said unto them, Verily, verily, I say unto you, Moses gave you not that bread from heaven; but my Father giveth you the true bread from heaven.
For the bread of God is he which cometh down from heaven, and giveth life unto the world.
Then said they unto him, Lord, evermore give us this bread.
And Jesus said unto them, I am the TG Bread of Life.bread of life: he that cometh to me shall never hunger; and he that believeth on me shall never John 4:14; 7:37 (37–39).thirst.
But I said unto you, That ye also have seen me, and believe not.
All that the Father 3 Ne. 15:24; D&C 27:14; 50:41 (41–42); 84:63.giveth me shall come to me; and him that cometh to me I will in no wise John 17:2.cast out.
For I TG Jesus Christ, Condescension of.came down from heaven, not to do mine own TG Jesus Christ, Mission of.will, but the TG God, Will of.will of him that sent me.

And this is the Luke 2:49.Father’s 3 Ne. 27:13 (13–16).will which hath sent me, that of all which he hath given me I should lose John 17:12; 18:9.nothing, but should 3 Ne. 15:1; D&C 5:35.raise it up again at the last day.

And this is the will of him that sent me, that every one which seeth the Son, and TG Faith.believeth on him, may have TG Eternal Life.everlasting life: and I will raise him up JST John 6:40 … in the resurrection of the just at the last day.at the last day.
The Jews then murmured at him, because he said, I am the bread which came down from heaven.
And they said, Is not this Jesus, the son of Luke 4:22.Joseph, whose father and mother we know? how is it then that he saith, I came down from heaven?
Jesus therefore answered and said unto them, TG Murmuring.Murmur not among yourselves.
JST John 6:44 (Appendix).No man can Isa. 55:3.come to me, except the Father which hath sent me 1 Cor. 12:3; 1 Pet. 1:2; 3 Ne. 27:14; D&C 29:7.draw him: and I will raise him up at the last day.

It is written in the prophets, And they shall be all 1 Thes. 4:9; 1 Jn. 2:27 (20, 27).taught of God. Every man therefore that hath heard, and hath learned of the TG Witness of the Father.Father, cometh unto me.
Not that any man hath seen the Father, save he which is of God, he hath TG God, Manifestations of; God, Privilege of Seeing; Revelation.seen the Father.
Verily, verily, I say unto you, He that TG Faith.believeth on me hath TG Eternal Life; Exaltation.everlasting life.
I am that bread of life.
Your fathers did eat 1 Ne. 17:28; Mosiah 7:19.manna in the wilderness, and are dead.
This is the bread which cometh down from heaven, that a man may eat thereof, and not die.

I am the living TG Jesus Christ, Types of, in Anticipation.bread which came down from heaven: if any man eat of this bread, he shall live for ever: and the TG Bread of Life; Jesus Christ, Types of, in Memory.bread that I will give is my Heb. 10:10.flesh, which I will TG God, Gifts of.give for the TG Jesus Christ, Atonement through.life of the world.
The Jews therefore strove among themselves, saying, How can this man give us his flesh to eat?
Then Jesus said unto them, Verily, verily, I say unto you, Except ye Mark 14:22; Luke 22:19.eat the flesh of the TG Jesus Christ, Son of Man.Son of man, and drink his blood, ye have no life in you.

Whoso eateth my TG Sacrament.flesh, and drinketh my TG Blood, Symbolism of.blood, hath eternal life; JST John 6:54 … and I will raise him up in the resurrection of the just at the last day.and I will TG Resurrection.raise him up at the last day.
For my flesh is meat indeed, and my blood is drink indeed.
He that eateth my flesh, and drinketh my blood, 1 Jn. 3:24.dwelleth in me, and I in him.
As the living Father hath TG Jesus Christ, Messenger of the Covenant.sent me, and I live by the Father: so he that eateth me, even he shall John 5:26.live by me.
This is that bread which came down from heaven: not as your fathers did eat manna, and are dead: he that eateth of this bread shall live for ever.
These things said he in the synagogue, as he taught in Capernaum.
Many therefore of his disciples, when they had heard this, said, This is an hard saying; who can hear it?
When Jesus John 2:25.knew in himself that his disciples murmured at it, he said unto them, Doth this TG Offense.offend you?
What and if ye shall see the TG Jesus Christ, Son of Man.Son of man John 3:13. TG Jesus Christ, Ascension of.ascend up where he was before?
It is the 2 Cor. 3:6.spirit that quickeneth; the flesh profiteth nothing: the words that I speak unto you, they are spirit, and they are life.
But there are some of you that believe not. For Jesus knew from the beginning who they were that believed not, and who should John 13:11.betray him.
And he said, Therefore said I unto you, that no man can come unto me, JST John 6:65 … except he doeth the will of my Father who hath sent me.except it were given unto him of my Father.
¶ From that time many of his TG Apostasy of Individuals.disciples went back, and TG Apostasy of the Early Christian Church.walked no more with him.
Then said Jesus unto the twelve, Will ye also go away?
Then Simon Peter answered him, Lord, to whom shall we go? thou hast the John 17:8; Acts 5:20; Moses 6:59.words of eternal life.
And GR we have had faith and have known that.we believe and are sure that thou art that Christ, the TG God, Body of, Corporeal Nature.Son of the living God.
Jesus answered them, Have not I 1 Ne. 12:7 (6–7).chosen you twelve, and one of you is a devil?
He spake of Judas Iscariot the son of Simon: for he it was that should betray him, being one of the twelve.

div.p: Jesus’ kinsmen do not believe—He teaches His Father’s doctrine and proclaims His divine sonship—Truth may be known through obedience—Jesus offers living water to all people—The people have various opinions concerning Him.

chapter.verse: After these things Jesus walked in Galilee: for he would not walk in Jewry, because the Jews John 5:16 (16, 18).sought to John 11:53 (53–54).kill him.
Now the Jews’ Lev. 23:34 (34–43).feast of tabernacles was at hand.
His Matt. 12:46; 1 Cor. 9:5.brethren therefore said unto him, Depart hence, and go into Judæa, that thy disciples also may see the works that thou doest.
For there is no man that doeth any thing in secret, and he himself seeketh to be known openly. If thou do these things, shew thyself to the world.
For neither did his brethren believe in him.
Then Jesus said unto them, My Matt. 26:18.time is not yet come: but your time is alway ready.
The world cannot hate you; but me it 1 Ne. 16:2 (1–3).hateth, because I testify of it, that the works thereof are TG Evil.evil.
Go ye up unto this feast: I go not up yet unto this feast; for my time is not yet full come.
When he had said these words unto them, he abode still in Galilee.
¶ But when his brethren were gone up, then went he also up unto the feast, not openly, but as it were in secret.
Then the Jews sought him at the feast, and said, Where is he?
And there was much murmuring among the people concerning him: for some said, He is a good man: others said, Nay; but he deceiveth the people.
Howbeit no man spake openly of him for John 19:38.fear of the Jews.
¶ Now about the midst of the feast Jesus went up into the temple, and taught.
And the Jews Luke 2:46 (46–47).marvelled, saying, How knoweth this man letters, having never learned?

Jesus answered them, and said, My Matt. 7:28; John 8:28; 2 Ne. 31:21. TG Gospel; Jesus Christ, Teaching Mode of.doctrine is not mine, but his that TG Jesus Christ, Authority of; Jesus Christ, Messenger of the Covenant.sent me.
If any man will TG Good Works; Obedience.do his TG God, Will of.will, he shall TG Discernment, Spiritual; Testimony; Truth.know of the doctrine, whether it be of God, or whether I speak of myself.

He that speaketh of himself seeketh his own John 5:41 (41–43).glory: but he that seeketh his John 8:50; D&C 88:67; Moses 4:2 (1–3).glory that sent him, the same is true, and no unrighteousness is in him.
Did not Moses give you the law, and yet none of you keepeth the law? Why go ye about to kill me?
The people answered and said, Thou hast a devil: who goeth about to kill thee?
Jesus answered and said unto them, I have done one work, and ye all marvel.
Moses therefore gave unto you TG Circumcision.circumcision; (not because it is of Moses, but of the fathers;) and ye on the sabbath day circumcise a man.

If a man on the TG Sabbath.sabbath day receive circumcision, that the law of Moses should not be broken; are ye angry at me, because I have made a man every whit John 5:8 (8–9).whole on the sabbath day?
