TG Judgment.Judge not according to JST John 7:24 … your traditions, but judge …the Isa. 11:3.appearance, but judge righteous judgment.
Then said some of them of Jerusalem, Is not this he, whom they seek to kill?
But, lo, he speaketh boldly, and they say nothing unto him. Do the John 3:1 (1–2).rulers know indeed that this is the very Christ?
Howbeit we know this man whence he is: but when Christ cometh, no man knoweth whence he is.
Then cried Jesus in the temple as he taught, saying, Ye both know me, and ye know whence I am: and I am not come of myself, but he that sent me is TG Truth.true, whom ye know not.
But I know him: for I am from him, and he hath John 13:3. TG Jesus Christ, Authority of; Jesus Christ, Messenger of the Covenant.sent me.
Then they John 10:39.sought to take him: but no man laid hands on him, because his John 12:23 (23–27).hour was not yet come.
And many of the people believed on him, and said, When Christ cometh, will he do more miracles than these which this man hath done?
¶ The Pharisees heard that the people murmured such things concerning him; and the Pharisees and the chief priests sent officers to take him.
Then said Jesus unto them, Yet a little while am I with you, and then I go unto him that sent me.
Ye shall John 13:33 (33, 36).seek me, and shall not find me: and D&C 29:29; 76:112 (51–112).where I am, 1 Pet. 3:22. thither ye D&C 25:15.cannot come.
Then said the Jews among themselves, Whither will he go, that we shall not find him? will he go unto the dispersed among the GR Greeks.Gentiles, and teach the Gentiles?
What manner of saying is this that he said, Ye shall seek me, and shall not find me: and where I am, thither ye cannot come?

In the last day, that great day of the feast, Jesus stood and cried, saying, If any man Ps. 42:2 (1–3); 143:6 (5–12); Isa. 55:1 (1–3); John 4:14 (13–15); 6:35.thirst, let him come unto me, and TG Living Water.drink.
He that believeth on me, as the scripture hath said, out of his belly shall flow rivers of living water.

(But this spake he of the TG Holy Ghost, Gift of.Spirit, which they that believe on him should receive: JST John 7:39 … for the Holy Ghost was promised unto them who believe, after that Jesus was glorified.)for the Holy Ghost was not yet given; because that Jesus was not yet glorified.)
¶ Many of the people therefore, when they heard this saying, said, Of a truth this is the John 9:17; 1 Ne. 22:20 (20–21).Prophet.
Others said, This is the Christ. But some said, Shall Christ come out of Galilee?

Hath not the scripture said, That 1 Chr. 5:2; Matt. 2:6.Christ cometh of the seed of TG Jesus Christ, Davidic Descent of.David, and out of the town of 1 Sam. 16:1; Micah 5:2; Luke 2:4. TG Jesus Christ, Prophecies about.Bethlehem, where David was?
So there was a Matt. 10:34.division among the people because of him.
And some of them would have taken him; but no man laid hands on him.
¶ Then came the officers to the chief priests and Pharisees; and they said unto them, Why have ye not brought him?
The officers answered, Never man TG Teaching with the Spirit.spake like this man.
Then answered them the Pharisees, Are ye also Jacob 4:14 (13–14).deceived?
Have any of the rulers or of the Pharisees believed on him?
But this people who knoweth not the law are TG Curse.cursed.
John 3:1.Nicodemus saith unto them, (he that came to Jesus by night, being one of them,)
Doth our law judge any man, before it hear him, and know what he doeth?
They answered and said unto him, Art thou also of Galilee? Search, and look: for out of Galilee ariseth no prophet.
And every man went unto his own house.

div.p: The woman taken in adultery is brought before Christ—Christ is the Light of the world—He again proclaims that He is the Messiah—The true children of Abraham believe in Christ—Jesus says, Before Abraham was I, Jehovah.

chapter.verse: Jesus went unto the mount of Olives.
And early in the morning he came again into the Matt. 26:55; Luke 21:37.temple, and all the people came unto him; and he sat down, and taught them.
And the scribes and Pharisees brought unto him a TG Woman.woman taken in TG Adulterer.adultery; and when they had set her in the midst,
They say unto him, Master, this woman was taken in adultery, in the very act.
Now Moses in the Lev. 20:10.law commanded us, that such should be stoned: but what sayest thou?
This they said, tempting him, that they might have to accuse him. But Jesus stooped down, and with his finger TG Jesus Christ, Teaching Mode of.wrote on the ground, as though he heard them not.
So when they continued asking him, he lifted up himself, and said unto them, He that is without 3 Ne. 14:5 (1–5).sin among you, let him Deut. 17:7.first cast a TG Gossip.stone at her.
And again he stooped down, and wrote on the ground.

And they which heard it, being convicted by their own TG Conscience.conscience, went out one by one, beginning at the eldest, even unto the last: and Jesus was left alone, and the woman standing in the midst.
When Jesus had lifted up himself, and saw none but the woman, he said unto her, Woman, where are those thine accusers? hath no man condemned thee?

She said, No man, Lord. And Jesus said unto her, Neither do I TG Benevolence; Mercy.condemn thee: go, and D&C 42:25.sin no JST John 8:11 … more. And the woman glorified God from that hour, and believed on his name.more.

¶ Then spake Jesus again unto them, saying, I am the 1 Jn. 1:5. TG Jesus Christ, Light of the World; Light [noun]; Light of Christ.light of the world: he that followeth me shall not TG Walking in Darkness.walk in TG Darkness, Spiritual.darkness, but shall have the light of life.
The Pharisees therefore said unto him, Thou bearest record of thyself; thy record is not true.

Jesus answered and said unto them, Though I bear record of myself, yet my record is true: for I know John 16:28.whence I came, and whither I go; but ye cannot tell John 9:29.whence I come, and whither I go.
Ye judge after the flesh; I John 12:47.judge no man.
And yet if I TG Jesus Christ, Judge.judge, my TG Judgment.judgment is true: for I am not John 8:29; 16:32.alone, but I and the Father that sent me.
It is also written in your law, that the TG Witness.testimony of two men is true.
I am one that bear witness of myself, and the TG Godhead; God the Father, Elohim.Father that sent me beareth TG Witness of the Father.witness of me.
Then said they unto him, Where is thy Father? Jesus answered, Ye neither know me, nor my Father: if ye had John 14:7.known me, ye should have known my Father also.
These words spake Jesus in the treasury, as he taught in the temple: and no man laid hands on him; for his hour was not yet come.
Then said Jesus again unto them, I go my way, and ye shall seek me, and shall die in your Moro. 10:26 (24–26). TG Sin.sins: whither I go, ye cannot come.
Then said the Jews, Will he kill himself? because he saith, Whither I go, ye cannot come.
And he said unto them, Ye are from John 3:31 (13, 31).beneath; I am from above: ye are of this TG World.world; I am not of this world.
I said therefore unto you, that ye shall D&C 138:32 (31–34).die in your sins: for if ye TG Faith.believe not that I am he, ye shall die in your sins.
Then said they unto him, Who art thou? And Jesus saith unto them, Even the same that I said unto you from the beginning.
I have many things to say and to judge of you: but he that sent me is true; and I speak to the world those things which I have TG Revelation.heard of him.
They understood not that he spake to them of the Father.

Then said Jesus unto them, When ye have lifted up the Son of man, then shall ye know that I am he, and that I do John 5:19.nothing of myself; but as my John 7:16 (16–18). TG Authority.Father hath TG Delegation of Responsibility.taught me, I Deut. 18:18.speak these things.

And he that TG Jesus Christ, Authority of.sent me is with me: the Father hath not left me John 8:16; 16:32. TG Jesus Christ, Relationships with the Father.alone; for I TG Obedience.do always those things that John 4:34.please him.
As he spake these words, many John 12:42 (11, 42).believed on him.
Then said Jesus to those Jews which believed on him, If ye TG Commitment; Perseverance; Steadfastness.continue in my word, then are ye my Matt. 27:57.disciples indeed;
And ye shall TG Conversion; Testimony.know the D&C 50:25. TG Education; Gospel; Truth.truth, and the Moro. 10:5.truth shall make you 3 Ne. 21:4. TG Bondage, Spiritual; Liberty.free.
¶ They answered him, We be TG Abrahamic Covenant.Abraham’s seed, and were never in bondage to any man: how sayest thou, Ye shall be made free?
Jesus answered them, Verily, verily, I say unto you, Whosoever D&C 29:40.committeth TG Sin.sin is the Rom. 6:16; Alma 12:11 (9–11, 17). TG Servant.servant of sin.
And the servant abideth not in the house for ever: but the Son abideth ever.
If the Son therefore shall make you TG Bondage, Spiritual; Liberty.free, ye shall be free indeed.
I know that ye are Abraham’s seed; but ye seek to kill me, because my word hath no place in you.
I speak that which I have seen with my Father: and ye do that which ye have seen with your father.
They answered and said unto him, Abraham is our father. Jesus saith unto them, If ye were TG Abrahamic Covenant.Abraham’s TG Seed of Abraham.children, ye would do the TG Good Works.works of Abraham.
But now ye seek to kill me, a man that hath told you the truth, which I have heard of God: this did not Abraham.
Ye do the deeds of your father. Then said they to him, We be not born of fornication; we have one Father, even God.

Jesus said unto them, If God were your Father, ye would love me: for I proceeded forth and John 16:28 (27–28, 30); 17:8; Gal. 4:4.came from God; neither came I of myself, but he TG Jesus Christ, Messenger of the Covenant.sent me.
Why do ye not understand my speech? even because ye cannot JST John 8:43 … bear …hear my word.

Ye are of your father the 1 Jn. 3:10 (10–18). TG Devil; Devil, Church of.devil, and the TG Lust.lusts of your father ye will do. He was a TG Murder.murderer from the beginning, and abode not in the TG Truth.truth, because there is no truth in him. When he speaketh a TG Lying.lie, he speaketh of his own: for he is a TG Honesty.liar, and the father of it.
And because I tell you the truth, ye believe me not.
Which of you GR convicts, reproves.convinceth me of Heb. 4:15.sin? And if I say the truth, why do ye not believe me?

JST John 8:47 He that is of God receiveth God’s words; ye therefore receive them not, because ye are not of God.He that is of God Dan. 12:10; 1 Jn. 4:6 (5–6); 2 Ne. 33:10 (10–11).heareth God’s words: ye therefore hear them not, because ye are not of God.
Then answered the Jews, and said unto him, Say we not well that thou art a Samaritan, and hast a Mosiah 3:9.devil?
Jesus answered, I have not a devil; but I TG God, the Standard of Righteousness; Honoring Father and Mother; Respect.honour my Father, and ye do dishonour me.
And I seek not mine own John 7:18.glory: there is one that seeketh and judgeth.
Verily, verily, I say unto you, If a man keep my saying, he shall never see John 5:24; 11:26; D&C 63:49. TG Death, Spiritual, First; Death, Spiritual, Second.death.
Then said the Jews unto him, Now we know that thou hast a devil. Abraham is dead, and the prophets; and thou sayest, If a man keep my saying, he shall never D&C 42:46 (46–47).taste of death.
Art thou greater than our father Abraham, which is dead? and the prophets are dead: whom makest thou thyself?
Jesus answered, If I honour myself, my honour is nothing: it is my TG God, Body of, Corporeal Nature.Father that TG Honor.honoureth me; of whom ye say, that he is your God:
Yet ye have not known him; but I know him: and if I should say, I know him not, I shall be a liar like unto you: but I know him, and keep his saying.
Your father Gen. 22:8 (8–14); Hel. 8:17 (16–19).Abraham TG Joy.rejoiced to TG Jesus Christ, Appearances, Antemortal.see my day: and he saw it, and was glad.
Then said the Jews unto him, Thou art not yet fifty years old, and hast thou seen Abraham?

Jesus said unto them, Verily, verily, I say unto you, TG Man, Antemortal Existence of.Before Abraham was, The term I Am used here in the Greek is identical with the Septuagint usage in Ex. 3:14 which identifies Jehovah. (See also John 4:26.) TG Jesus Christ, Jehovah.I am.
Then took they up John 10:31.stones to cast at him: but Jesus hid himself, and went out of the temple, going through the midst of them, and so Luke 4:30.passed by.

div.p: Jesus, on the Sabbath, heals a man born blind—The Jews accuse Him of Sabbath violation—He lectures them on spiritual blindness.

chapter.verse: And as Jesus passed by, he saw a man which was Matt. 9:28 (28–31); 20:30 (30–34); Mosiah 3:5; 3 Ne. 17:9 (7–10); D&C 84:69.blind from his birth.
And his disciples asked him, saying, Master, who did sin, this TG Man, Antemortal Existence of.man, or his parents, that he was born blind?
Jesus answered, Neither hath this man sinned, nor his parents: but that the TG God, Works of.works of God should be made John 11:4.manifest in him.

I must John 5:17.work the John 4:34. TG Good Works.works of him that sent me, JST John 9:4 … while I am with you; the time cometh when I shall have finished my work, then I go unto the Father.while it is Rom. 13:12 (11–12); Alma 34:33.day: the TG Darkness, Spiritual.night cometh, when no man can work.
As long as I am in the world, I am the TG Jesus Christ, Light of the World; Light of Christ.light of the world.
When he had thus spoken, he spat on the ground, and made clay of the spittle, and he anointed the eyes of the blind man with the clay,
And said unto him, Go, wash in the pool of Neh. 3:15; Isa. 8:6.Siloam, (which is by interpretation, Sent.) He went his way therefore, and washed, and came seeing.
¶ The neighbours therefore, and they which before had seen him that he was blind, said, Is not this he that sat and begged?
Some said, This is he: others said, He is like him: but he said, I am he.
Therefore said they unto him, How were thine eyes opened?

He answered and said, A man that is called Jesus made clay, and anointed mine eyes, and said unto me, Go to the pool of Siloam, and 2 Kgs. 5:10.wash: and I went and washed, and I received TG Sight.sight.
Then said they unto him, Where is he? He said, I know not.
¶ They brought to the Pharisees him that aforetime was blind.
And it was the TG Sabbath.sabbath day when Jesus made the clay, and TG Heal.opened his eyes.
Then again the Pharisees also asked him how he had received his sight. He said unto them, He put clay upon mine eyes, and I washed, and do see.

Therefore said some of the Pharisees, This man is not of God, because he keepeth not the sabbath day. Others said, How can a man that is a sinner do such miracles? And there was a division among them.
They say unto the blind man again, What sayest thou of him, that he hath opened thine eyes? He said, He is a John 7:40 (40–44).prophet.
But the Jews did not believe concerning him, that he had been blind, and received his sight, until they called the parents of him that had received his sight.
And they asked them, saying, Is this your son, who ye say was born blind? how then doth he now see?
His parents answered them and said, We know that this is our son, and that he was born blind:
But by what means he now seeth, we know not; or who hath opened his eyes, we know not: he is of age; ask him: he shall speak for himself.
These words spake his parents, because they feared the Jews: for the Jews had agreed already, that if any man did confess that he was Christ, he should be put out of the synagogue.
Therefore said his parents, He is of age; ask him.
Then again called they the man that was blind, and said unto him, Give God the praise: we know that this man is a sinner.
He answered and said, Whether he be a sinner or no, I know not: one thing I know, that, whereas I was blind, now I see.
Then said they to him again, What did he to thee? how opened he thine eyes?
He answered them, I have told you already, and ye did not hear: wherefore would ye hear it again? will ye also be his disciples?
Then they TG Reviling.reviled him, and said, Thou art his disciple; but we are Moses’ disciples.
We know that God spake unto Moses: as for this fellow, we know not from John 8:14.whence he is.
The man answered and said unto them, Why herein is a marvellous thing, that ye know not from whence he is, and yet he hath opened mine eyes.
Now we know that God heareth not sinners: but if any man be a worshipper of God, and doeth his will, him he heareth.
Since the world began was it not heard that any man opened the eyes of one that was born JST John 9:32 … blind, except he be of God.blind.
If this man were not of Acts 2:22.God, he could do nothing.
They answered and said unto him, Thou wast altogether born in sins, and dost thou teach us? And they cast him out.
Jesus heard that they had cast him out; and when he had found him, he said unto him, Dost thou believe on the Son of God?
He answered and said, Who is he, Lord, that I might believe on him?
And Jesus said unto him, Thou hast both seen him, and it is he that talketh with thee.
And he said, Lord, I believe. And he worshipped him.
¶ And Jesus said, For TG Jesus Christ, Judge.judgment I am come into this world, that they which Luke 5:32; 1 Tim. 1:13, 15.see not might see; and that they which see might be made blind.
And some of the Pharisees which were with him heard these words, and said unto him, Are we blind also?
Jesus said unto them, If ye were TG Spiritual Blindness.blind, ye should have no John 15:22 (19–27).sin: but now ye say, We TG Accountability.see; therefore your TG Sin.sin remaineth.

div.p: Jesus is the Good Shepherd—He gained power over death from His Father—He promises to visit His other sheep—He proclaims, I am the Son of God.

chapter.verse: Verily, verily, I say unto you, He that entereth not by the door into the sheepfold, but climbeth up some other way, the same is a thief and a robber.
But he that entereth in by the door is the shepherd of the TG Sheep.sheep.
To him the porter openeth; and the sheep hear his D&C 35:21.voice: and he calleth his own sheep by name, and Isa. 55:4.leadeth them out.
And when he putteth forth his own sheep, he goeth before them, and the sheep follow him: for they TG Discernment, Spiritual.know his TG Revelation.voice.
And a stranger will they not follow, but will flee from him: for they know not the voice of TG Stranger.strangers.
This parable spake Jesus unto them: but they understood not what things they were which he spake unto them.
Then said Jesus unto them again, Verily, verily, I say unto you, I am the John 14:6 (4–7).door of the sheep.
All that ever came before me JST John 10:8 … who testified not of me are thieves …are thieves and robbers: but the sheep did not hear them.
I am the door: by me if any man enter in, he shall be saved, and shall go in and out, and find pasture.
The thief cometh not, but for to TG Stealing.steal, and to kill, and to destroy: I am come that they might have D&C 66:2.life, and that they might have it more TG Abundant Life; Happiness.abundantly.
I am the good TG Shepherd.shepherd: the good Ezek. 34:2.shepherd giveth his life for the sheep.

But he that is an hireling, and not the shepherd, whose own the sheep are not, seeth the wolf coming, and TG Leadership.leaveth the sheep, and fleeth: and the wolf catcheth them, and scattereth the sheep.
The TG Wages.hireling fleeth, because he is an hireling, and careth not for the sheep.
I am the good TG Jesus Christ, Good Shepherd.shepherd, and Ex. 33:12 (12, 17); 1 Cor. 8:3.know my 3 Ne. 18:31. sheep , and am known of mine.

As the Father knoweth me, even so TG Jesus Christ, Relationships with the Father.know I the Father: and I lay down my 1 Jn. 3:16 (10–18). TG Jesus Christ, Atonement through; Martyrdom.life for the sheep.

And 3 Ne. 15:21 (11–24); 16:1 (1–3); D&C 10:59. TG Israel, Scattering of.other TG Book of Mormon; Israel, Joseph, People of; Israel, Ten Lost Tribes of; Sheep.sheep I have, which are not of this fold: them also I must bring, and they shall hear my voice; and there shall be Ezek. 37:22.one fold, and one shepherd.
Therefore doth my Father TG God, Love of.love me, because I TG Self-Sacrifice.lay down my life, that I might TG Jesus Christ, Resurrection.take it again.

No man Luke 23:46.taketh it from me, but I lay it down of myself. I have TG Death, Power over; God, Power of.power to lay it down, and I have GR authority, full power.power to take it again. This commandment have I TG Jesus Christ, Authority of.received of my Father.
¶ There was a division therefore again among the Jews for these sayings.
And many of them said, He hath a devil, and is mad; why hear ye him?
Others said, These are not the words of him that hath a devil. Can a devil open the eyes of the blind?
¶ And it was at Jerusalem the feast of the dedication, and it was winter.
And Jesus walked in the temple in Solomon’s 1 Kgs. 6:3; Acts 3:11.porch.
Then came the Jews round about him, and said unto him, How long dost thou make us to TG Doubt.doubt? If thou be the Christ, tell us plainly.
Jesus answered them, I told you, and ye TG Faith; Unbelief.believed not: the John 5:36. TG Miracle.works that I do in my Father’s TG Name.name, they bear witness of me.
But ye believe not, because ye are not of my sheep, as I said unto you.
My TG Sheep.sheep hear my voice, and I know them, and they D&C 38:22.follow me:
And I give unto them eternal life; and they shall never perish, neither shall any man pluck them out of my hand.
My Father, which John 17:2 (2–12); D&C 50:41.gave them me, is greater than all; and no man is able to pluck them out of my Father’s hand.
I and my Father are D&C 93:3 (1–6). TG Godhead; Unity.one.
Then the Jews took up John 8:59.stones again to stone him.
Jesus answered them, Many good works have I shewed you from my Father; for which of those works do ye stone me?
The Jews answered him, saying, For a good work we stone thee not; but for TG Blaspheme.blasphemy; and because that thou, being a man, makest thyself John 5:18 (17–18); 19:7; 1 Tim. 3:16.God.
Jesus answered them, Is it not written in your law, I said, Ye are TG Man, Potential to Become like Heavenly Father; Sons and Daughters of God.gods?
If he called them gods, unto whom the word of God came, and the scripture cannot be broken;

Say ye of him, whom the Father hath TG Sanctification.sanctified, and TG Jesus Christ, Messenger of the Covenant.sent into the world, Thou blasphemest; because I said, I am the TG Jesus Christ, Divine Sonship.Son of God?
If I do not the works of my Father, believe me not.
But if I do, though ye believe not me, TG Faith.believe the works: that ye may know, and believe, that the TG Godhead.Father is in me, and I in him.
Therefore they John 7:30.sought again to take him: but he escaped out of their hand,
And went away again beyond Jordan into the place where John at first baptized; and there he abode.
And many resorted unto him, and said, John did no miracle: but all things that John spake of this man were true.
And many believed on him there.

div.p: Jesus testifies that He is the Resurrection and the Life—Mary and Martha testify of Him—He raises Lazarus from the dead—Caiaphas speaks prophetically of the death of Jesus.

chapter.verse: Now a certain man was sick, named Lazarus, of Bethany, the town of Mary and her sister Luke 10:38; John 12:2.Martha.

JST John 11:2 And Mary, his sister, who anointed the Lord with ointment and wiped his feet with her hair, lived with her sister Martha, in whose house her brother Lazarus was sick.(It was that Mary which anointed the TG Jesus Christ, Lord.Lord with Matt. 26:7 (6–13).ointment, and wiped his feet with her hair, whose brother Lazarus was sick.)
Therefore his sisters sent unto him, saying, Lord, behold, he whom thou lovest is sick.
When Jesus heard that, he said, This sickness is not unto death, but for the 3 Ne. 12:16; Ether 12:4.glory of God, that the Son of God might be John 9:3.glorified thereby.
Now Jesus loved Martha, and her sister, and Lazarus.
When he had heard therefore that he was sick, he abode two days still in the same place where he was.
Then after that saith he to his disciples, Let us go into Judæa again.
His disciples say unto him, Master, the Jews of late sought to stone thee; and goest thou thither again?
Jesus answered, Are there not twelve hours in the day? If any man TG Walking with God.walk in the day, he stumbleth not, because he seeth the light of this world.
But if a man walk in the night, he stumbleth, because there is no light in him.
These things said he: and after that he saith unto them, Our friend Lazarus sleepeth; but I go, that I may 2 Kgs. 4:31.awake him out of sleep.
Then said his disciples, Lord, if he sleep, he shall GR be cured, saved.do well.
Howbeit Jesus spake of his death: but they thought that he had spoken of taking of rest in sleep.
Then said Jesus unto them plainly, Lazarus is dead.
And I am glad for your sakes that I was not there, to the intent ye may TG Faith.believe; nevertheless let us go unto him.

Then said Thomas, which is called Didymus, unto his fellowdisciples, Let us also go, that we may die with JST John 11:16 … him; for they feared lest the Jews should take Jesus and put him to death, for as yet they did not understand the power of God.him.
Then when Jesus came, JST John 11:17 … to Bethany, to Martha’s house, Lazarus had already been in the grave four days.he found that he had lain in the grave four days already.
Now Bethany was nigh unto Jerusalem, about fifteen GR stadium—607 English feet, about 185 meters.furlongs off:
And many of the Jews came to Martha and Mary, to comfort them concerning their brother.
Then Martha, as soon as she heard that Jesus was coming, went and met him: but Mary sat still in the house.
Then said Martha unto Jesus, Lord, if thou hadst been here, my brother had not died.
But I know, that even now, whatsoever thou wilt ask of God, God will TG God, Gifts of.give it thee.
Jesus saith unto her, Thy brother shall rise again.
Martha saith unto him, I know that he shall rise again in the resurrection at the last day.

Jesus said unto her, I am the TG Jesus Christ, Resurrection; Resurrection.resurrection, and the TG Jesus Christ, Atonement through.life: he that TG Faith.believeth in me, though he were TG Death.dead, yet shall he Alma 37:46 (46–47); Hel. 8:15; 3 Ne. 15:9.live:
And whosoever liveth and believeth in me shall never John 5:24; 8:51; D&C 42:46 (44, 46); 63:49.die. Believest thou this?
She saith unto him, Yea, Lord: I believe that thou art the Christ, the TG God, Body of, Corporeal Nature.Son of God, which should come into the world.
And when she had so said, she went her way, and called Mary her sister secretly, saying, The Master is come, and calleth for thee.
As soon as she heard that, she arose quickly, and came unto him.
Now Jesus was not yet come into the town, but was in that place where Martha met him.
The Jews then which were with her in the house, and comforted her, when they saw Mary, that she rose up hastily and went out, followed her, saying, She goeth unto the grave to weep there.
Then when Mary was come where Jesus was, and saw him, she fell down at his feet, saying unto him, Lord, if thou hadst been here, my brother had not died.
When Jesus therefore saw her weeping, and the Jews also weeping which came with her, he groaned in the spirit, and was troubled,
And said, Where have ye laid him? They said unto him, Lord, come and see.
Jesus wept.
Then said the Jews, Behold how he D&C 42:45.loved him!
And some of them said, Could not this man, which opened the eyes of the blind, have caused that even this man should not have died?
Jesus therefore again groaning in himself cometh to the grave. It was a cave, and a Matt. 27:60.stone lay upon it.
Jesus said, Take ye away the stone. Martha, the sister of him that was dead, saith unto him, Lord, by this time he stinketh: for he hath been dead four days.
Jesus saith unto her, Said I not unto thee, that, if thou wouldest believe, thou shouldest see the Ex. 16:7.glory of God?
Then they took away the stone from the place where the dead was laid. And Jesus lifted up his eyes, and said, Father, I TG Thanksgiving.thank thee that thou hast heard me.
And I knew that thou hearest me always: but because of the people which stand by I said it, that they may believe that thou hast TG Jesus Christ, Messenger of the Covenant.sent me.
And when he thus had spoken, he cried with a loud voice, TG God, Power of.Lazarus, come forth.

And he that was 2 Kgs. 8:1. TG Death, Power over.dead came forth, bound hand and foot with graveclothes: and his face was bound about with a John 20:7.napkin. Jesus saith unto them, Loose him, and let him go.
Then many of the Jews which came to Mary, and had seen the things which Jesus did, believed on him.
But some of them went their ways to the Pharisees, and told them what things Jesus had done.
¶ Then gathered the chief 2 Ne. 10:5.priests and the Pharisees a council, and said, What do we? for this man doeth many 3 Ne. 19:35.miracles.
If we let him thus alone, all men will believe on him: and the Romans shall come and take away both our place and nation.
And one of them, named Matt. 26:3 (3–5).Caiaphas, being the TG Priesthood, Aaronic.high priest that same year, said unto them, Ye know nothing at all,
Nor consider that it is expedient for us, that one man should TG Jesus Christ, Trials of.die for the people, and that the whole nation perish not.
And this spake he not of himself: but being high priest that year, he prophesied that Jesus should die for that nation;
And not for that nation only, but that also he should TG Israel, Gathering of.gather together in one the TG Sons and Daughters of God.children of God that were scattered abroad.
Then from that day forth they took Matt. 21:38 (33–46). TG Counsel.counsel together for to put him to John 7:1.death.
Jesus therefore walked no more openly among the Jews; but went thence unto a country near to the wilderness, into a city called Ephraim, and there continued with his disciples.
¶ And the Jews’ passover was nigh at hand: and many went out of the country up to Jerusalem before the passover, to TG Purification.purify themselves.
Then sought they for Jesus, and spake among themselves, as they stood in the temple, What think ye, that he will not come to the feast?
Now both the chief priests and the Pharisees had given a commandment, that, if any man knew where he were, he should shew it, that they might take him.

div.p: Mary anoints Jesus’ feet—His triumphal entry into Jerusalem is recounted—He foretells His death—To receive Christ is to receive the Father.

chapter.verse: Then Jesus six days before the passover came to Bethany, where Lazarus was which had been dead, whom he raised from the dead.
There they made him a supper; and Luke 10:38; John 11:1 (1, 5).Martha served: but Lazarus was one of them that sat at the table with him.
Then took Mary a pound of ointment of spikenard, very costly, and anointed the feet of Jesus, and wiped his feet with her hair: and the house was filled with the odour of the ointment.
Then saith one of his disciples, Judas Iscariot, Simon’s son, which should betray him,
Why was not this ointment sold for three hundred pence, and given to the poor?
This he said, not that he cared for the poor; but because he was a Mosiah 2:36 (36–39).thief, and had the GR purse, money bag. John 13:29.bag, and bare what was put therein.

Then said Jesus, Let her alone: JST John 12:7 … for she hath preserved this ointment until now, that she might anoint me in token of my burial.against the day of my burying hath Mark 14:9.she kept this.
For the poor always ye have with you; but me ye have not always.
Much people of the Jews therefore knew that he was there: and they came not for Jesus’ sake only, but that they might see Lazarus also, whom he had raised from the dead.
¶ But the chief priests consulted that they might put Lazarus also to death;
Because that by reason of him many of the Jews went away, and believed on Jesus.
¶ On the next day much people that were come to the feast, when they heard that Jesus was coming to Jerusalem,
Took branches of palm trees, and went forth to meet him, and cried, Hosanna: Blessed is the King of Israel that cometh in the name of the Lord.
And Jesus, when he had found a young ass, sat thereon; as it is written,
Fear not, daughter of Sion: behold, thy Zech. 9:9.King cometh, sitting on an ass’s colt.

These things Luke 9:45; 18:34 (31–34).understood not his disciples at the first: but when Jesus was glorified, then remembered they that these things were written of him, and that they had done these things unto him.
The people therefore that was with him when he called Lazarus out of his grave, and raised him from the dead, bare record.
For this cause the people also Matt. 8:34.met him, for that they heard that he had done this D&C 63:10 (7–12).miracle.
The Pharisees therefore said among themselves, Perceive ye how ye prevail nothing? behold, the Matt. 9:31 (27–31).world is gone after him.
¶ And there were certain Acts 17:4 (1–9).Greeks among them that came up to Acts 8:27.worship at the feast:
The same came therefore to Philip, which was of Bethsaida of Galilee, and desired him, saying, Sir, we would see Jesus.
Philip cometh and telleth Andrew: and again Andrew and Philip tell Jesus.
¶ And Jesus answered them, saying, The John 7:30.hour is come, that the Son of man should be TG Jesus Christ, Mission of.glorified.
Verily, verily, I say unto you, Except a GR grain, seed.corn of wheat fall into the ground and 1 Cor. 15:36 (35–38).die, it abideth alone: but if it TG Death.die, it bringeth forth much fruit.
He that loveth his See JST Luke 9:24–25 (Appendix).life shall lose it; and he that TG Hate.hateth his life in this world shall keep it unto life eternal.
If any man serve me, let him 2 Ne. 31:12 (12–13).follow me; and where I am, there shall also my servant be: if any man serve me, him will my Father honour.
Now is my soul troubled; and what shall I say? Father, save me from this hour: but for this cause came I unto this hour.
Father, glorify thy name. Then came there a TG Godhead; Witness of the Father.voice from heaven, saying, I have both D&C 45:4.glorified it, and will glorify it again.
The people therefore, that stood by, and heard it, said that it thundered: others said, An TG Angels.angel spake to him.
Jesus answered and said, This voice came not because of me, but for your sakes.
Now is the judgment of this world: now shall the TG Devil.prince of this world be cast out.
And I, if I be TG Jesus Christ, Atonement through; Jesus Christ, Crucifixion of.lifted up from the earth, will D&C 18:11.draw all men unto me.
This he said, signifying what TG Jesus Christ, Death of.death he should die.
The people answered him, We have heard out of the law that 2 Sam. 7:16.Christ abideth for ever: and how sayest thou, The Son of man must be lifted up? who is this Son of man?

Then Jesus said unto them, Yet a little while is the TG Light of Christ.light with you. Walk while ye have the TG Light [noun].light, lest darkness come upon you: for he that walketh in TG Darkness, Spiritual; Walking in Darkness.darkness knoweth not whither he goeth.
While ye have light, TG Faith.believe in the light, that ye may be the TG Children of Light.children of light. These things spake Jesus, and departed, and did hide himself from them.
¶ But though he had done so many TG Miracle; Signs.miracles before them, yet they Mosiah 3:9 (9–11).believed not on him:
That the saying of Esaias the prophet might be fulfilled, which he spake, Lord, who hath Isa. 53:1; Rom. 10:16.believed our report? and to whom hath the arm of the Lord been revealed?
Therefore they could not believe, because that Esaias said again,

He hath TG Spiritual Blindness.blinded their eyes, and TG Hardheartedness.hardened their heart; that they should not see with their eyes, nor understand with their heart, and be converted, and I should heal them.
These things said Esaias, when he TG Jesus Christ, Appearances, Antemortal.saw his TG Glory.glory, and spake of him.

¶ Nevertheless among the chief Acts 6:7.rulers also many John 8:30 (30–31).believed on him; but because of the Pharisees they did not Prov. 29:25; John 19:38.confess him, lest they should be put out of the synagogue:
For they loved the Mark 12:38 (38–40); 1 Cor. 1:26 (26–31); 1 Ne. 8:28 (26–28); Alma 11:24; D&C 3:7 (5–8).praise of men more than the praise of God.
¶ Jesus cried and said, He that TG Faith.believeth on me, believeth not on me, but on him that sent me.
And he that seeth me seeth him that sent me.
I am come a John 1:9 (4, 9).light into the world, that whosoever believeth on me should not abide in darkness.
And if any man hear my words, and believe not, I judge him not: for I came not to Luke 4:19; John 8:15.judge the world, but to save the world.

He that D&C 39:9.rejecteth me, and receiveth not my words, hath one that TG Judgment.judgeth him: the TG Gospel.word that I have spoken, the same shall TG Judgment, the Last.judge him in the last day.
For I have not spoken of myself; but the Father which TG Jesus Christ, Authority of.sent me, he gave me a commandment, what I should say, and what I should speak.
And I know that his commandment is life everlasting: whatsoever I Deut. 18:18.speak therefore, even as the 3 Ne. 15:18.Father said unto me, so I TG Jesus Christ, Teaching Mode of.speak.

div.p: Jesus washes the feet of the Twelve—He identifies Judas as His betrayer—He commands them to love one another.

chapter.verse: Now before the feast of the Deut. 16:1.passover, when Jesus knew that his hour was come that he should depart out of this world unto the Father, having loved his own which were in the world, he TG God, Love of.loved them unto the end.
And supper being ended, the devil having now put into the D&C 10:15 (13, 15); 63:28.heart of Judas Iscariot, Simon’s son, to betray him;
Jesus TG God, Omniscience of.knowing that the Father had given all TG Jesus Christ, Power of.things into his hands, and that he was John 7:29.come from God, and went to God;
He riseth from supper, and laid aside his garments; and took a towel, and girded himself.
After that he poureth water into a basin, and began to TG Wash.wash the disciples’ feet, and to wipe them with the towel wherewith he was girded.
Then cometh he to Simon Peter: and Peter saith unto him, Lord, dost thou wash my feet?
Jesus answered and said unto him, What I do thou knowest not now; but thou shalt know hereafter.
JST John 13:8–10 (Appendix).Peter saith unto him, Thou shalt never wash my feet. Jesus answered him, If I TG Purification.wash thee not, thou hast no part with me.
Simon Peter saith unto him, Lord, not my feet only, but also my hands and my head.
Jesus saith to him, He that is washed needeth not save to wash his feet, but is TG Purity.clean every whit: and ye are D&C 38:10.clean, but not all.
For he knew who should John 6:64.betray him; therefore said he, Ye are not D&C 66:3.all clean.
So after he had washed their feet, and had taken his garments, and was set down again, he said unto them, Know ye what I have done to you?
Ye call me GR Teacher.Master and Lord: and ye say well; for so I am.
If I then, your Lord and GR Teacher.Master, have TG Courtesy.washed your feet; ye also ought to TG Humility.wash one another’s TG Jesus Christ, Teaching Mode of.feet.
For I have given you an TG Example; God, the Standard of Righteousness; Jesus Christ, Exemplar; Leadership.example, that ye should do as I have done to you.
Verily, verily, I say unto you, The servant is not greater than his lord; neither he that is sent greater than he that sent him.
If ye know these things, TG Happiness.happy are ye if ye do them.
¶ I speak not of you all: I know whom I have chosen: but that the scripture may be fulfilled, He that eateth bread with me hath lifted up his heel against me.
Now I tell you before it come, that, when it is come to pass, ye may believe that I am JST John 13:19 … the Christ. he .
Verily, verily, I say unto you, He that receiveth whomsoever I send D&C 39:5; 84:37.receiveth me; and he that 1 Jn. 4:6 (1–6).receiveth me receiveth him that sent me.
When Jesus had thus said, he was troubled in spirit, and testified, and said, Verily, verily, I say unto you, that one of you shall TG Jesus Christ, Betrayal of.betray me.
Then the disciples looked one on another, doubting of whom he spake.
Now there was leaning on Jesus’ bosom one of his John 20:2; 21:24 (20–24).disciples, whom Jesus loved.
Simon Peter therefore beckoned to him, that he should ask who it should be of whom he spake.
He then lying on Jesus’ breast saith unto him, Lord, who is it?
Jesus answered, He it is, to whom I shall give a GR morsel, mouthful.sop, when I have dipped it. And when he had dipped the sop, he gave it to Judas Iscariot, the son of Simon.
And after the sop TG Devil.Satan entered into him. Then said Jesus unto him, That thou doest, do quickly.
Now no man at the table knew for what intent he spake this unto him.
For some of them thought, because Judas had the John 12:6.bag, that Jesus had said unto him, Buy those things that we have need of against the feast; or, that he should give something to the poor.
He then having received the sop went immediately out: and it was night.
¶ Therefore, when he was gone out, Jesus said, Now is the TG Jesus Christ, Son of Man.Son of man glorified, and God is glorified in him.
If God be glorified in him, God shall also glorify him in himself, and shall straightway glorify him.
Little children, yet a little while I am with you. Ye shall John 7:34 (33–36).seek me: and as I said unto the Jews, Whither I go, ye cannot come; so now I say to you.
A new commandment I give unto you, That ye TG Love.love one another; as I have loved you, that ye also TG Family, Love within.love one another.
By this shall all men know that ye are my disciples, if ye have TG Fellowshipping; God, Love of.love one to another.
¶ Simon Peter said unto him, Lord, whither goest thou? Jesus answered him, Whither I go, thou canst not follow me now; but thou shalt follow me afterwards.
Peter said unto him, Lord, why cannot I TG Commitment.follow thee now? I will lay down my life for thy sake.
Jesus answered him, Wilt thou lay down thy life for my sake? Verily, verily, I say unto thee, The cock shall not crow, till thou hast denied me thrice.

div.p: Jesus speaks of many mansions—He says that He is the way, the truth, and the life and that to see Him is to see the Father—He promises the first and second Comforters.

chapter.verse: Let not your heart be D&C 50:41 (41–42).troubled: ye believe in God, believe also in me.
In my Father’s TG Heaven.house are many D&C 59:2; 98:18. TG Eternal Life; Telestial Glory.mansions: if it were not so, I would have told you. I go to prepare a place for you.
And if I go and prepare a place for you, I will Acts 1:11 (9–11).come again, and receive you unto myself; that D&C 27:18; 132:23.where 1 Thes. 4:17; Rev. 22:4 (3–5).I am, there ye may be also.
And whither I go ye know, and the way ye know.
Thomas saith unto him, Lord, we know not whither thou goest; and how can we know the way?

Jesus saith unto him, I am the Ex. 18:20; 33:13; 1 Sam. 12:23 (16–23); Ps. 25:4; John 10:7 (1–15). TG Example.way, the TG Truth.truth, and the life: no man Matt. 11:27; Luke 10:22. TG God, Access to.cometh unto the Father, but by me.
If ye had John 8:19; 1 Jn. 2:23; D&C 88:50.known me, ye should have known my Father also: and from henceforth ye know him, and have seen him.
Philip saith unto him, Lord, shew us the Father, and it sufficeth us.

Jesus saith unto him, Have I been so long time with you, and yet hast thou not known me, Philip? he that hath seen me hath seen the TG God, Body of, Corporeal Nature.Father; and how sayest thou then, Shew us the Father?
Believest thou not that I am in the D&C 93:3.Father, and the Father in me? the words that I speak unto you I speak not of myself: but the Father that dwelleth in me, he doeth the works.
Believe me that I am D&C 50:43.in the TG Jesus Christ, Relationships with the Father.Father, and the Father in me: or else believe me for the very works’ sake.

Verily, verily, I say unto you, He that Acts 9:41 (36–43); 4 Ne. 1:5 (5, 32–33). TG Faith.believeth on me, the works that I do shall he do also; and greater works than these shall he do; because I TG Jesus Christ, Ascension of.go unto my TG God the Father, Elohim.Father.
And whatsoever ye shall D&C 6:14; 29:6. TG Prayer.ask in my TG Name.name, that will I do, that the Father may be glorified in the Son.
If ye shall ask any thing in my name, I will do it.
¶ If ye TG God, Love of; God, the Standard of Righteousness; Love.love me, TG Duty; Obedience.keep my TG Commandments of God; Commitment; Good Works.commandments.
And I will pray the Father, and he shall give you another TG Holy Ghost, Comforter.Comforter, that he may D&C 20:77.abide with you for ever;
Even the Spirit of truth; whom the world cannot receive, because it seeth him not, neither knoweth him: but ye know him; for he dwelleth with you, and shall be in you.
I will not leave you GR orphans. TG Comfort.comfortless: I will John 16:32; 2 Tim. 4:17 (16–17). TG God, Privilege of Seeing.come to you.
Yet a little while, and the world seeth me no more; but ye see me: because I live, ye shall live also.
At that day ye shall know that I am in my Father, and ye in me, and I in you.

He that hath my commandments, and TG Obedience.keepeth them, he it is that loveth me: and he that loveth me shall be TG God, Love of.loved of my Father, and I will love him, and will TG God, Privilege of Seeing; Revelation.manifest myself to him.
Judas saith unto him, not Iscariot, Lord, how is it that thou wilt manifest thyself unto us, and not unto the world?

Jesus answered and said unto him, If a man love me, he will keep my words: and my Father will TG God, Love of.love him, and we will come unto him, and make our 1 Jn. 3:24; Rev. 3:20; D&C 130:3.abode with him.
He that loveth me not keepeth not my sayings: and the word which ye hear is not mine, but the Father’s which sent me.
These things have I spoken unto you, being yet present with you.

But the TG Holy Ghost, Comforter.Comforter, which is the TG Guidance, Divine.Holy Ghost, whom the Father will send in my TG Name.name, he shall TG God, Omniscience of; Holy Ghost, Gifts of; Holy Ghost, Mission of; Inspiration; Learn; Teaching.teach you all things, and bring all things to your remembrance, whatsoever I have said unto you.
TG Peace of God.Peace I leave with you, my TG Contentment; Peace.peace I give unto you: not as the world giveth, give I unto you. Let not your heart be TG Sorrow.troubled, neither let it be afraid.

Ye have heard how I said unto you, I go away, and come again unto you. If ye loved me, ye would rejoice, because I said, I TG Jesus Christ, Ascension of.go unto the Father: for my TG Godhead.Father is greater than I.
And now I have told you before it come to pass, that, when it is come to pass, ye might TG Faith.believe.

Hereafter I will not talk much with you: JST John 14:30 … for the prince of darkness, who is of this world, cometh, but hath no power over me, but he hath power over you.for the TG Devil.prince of this TG Worldliness.world cometh, and hath nothing in me.
But that the world may know that I love the Father; and as the Father gave me TG Jesus Christ, Authority of.commandment, even so I do. Arise, let us go hence.

div.p: Jesus is the vine; His disciples are the branches—He discourses on the perfect law of love—His servants have been chosen and ordained by Him—The world hates and fights true religion—He promises the Comforter, the Spirit of Truth.

chapter.verse: I am the true Gen. 49:11; 1 Ne. 15:15.vine, and my Father is the husbandman.

Every Matt. 15:13 (13–14).branch in me that beareth not Matt. 7:19 (15–20); Luke 3:9.fruit he taketh away: and every branch that beareth fruit, he GR purifies. TG Test.purgeth it, that it may bring forth more fruit.
Now ye are clean through the word which I have spoken unto you.
1 Jn. 2:6; D&C 35:18; 43:3; 50:24.Abide in me, and I in you. As the branch cannot bear fruit of itself, except it abide in the vine; no more can ye, except ye abide in me.
I am the TG Vineyard of the Lord.vine, ye are the branches: He that abideth in me, and I in him, the same bringeth forth much fruit: for without Philip. 4:13.me ye can do nothing.
If a man Col. 1:23.abide not in me, he is cast forth as a branch, and is withered; and men gather them, and cast them into the fire, and they are burned.
If ye TG Problem-Solving.abide in me, and my words abide in you, ye shall TG Prayer.ask what ye will, and it shall be done unto you.
Herein is my Father Isa. 49:3; Matt. 5:16.glorified, that ye bear much fruit; so shall ye be my disciples.
As the Father hath TG God, Love of.loved me, so have I loved you: continue ye in my love.
If ye 1 Ne. 17:35; Mosiah 2:4; D&C 95:12. TG Obedience.keep my commandments, ye shall abide in my TG Love.love; even as I have kept my Father’s commandments, and abide in his love.
These things have I spoken unto you, that my joy might remain in you, and that your TG Joy.joy might be full.
This is my commandment, That ye TG Love.love one another, as I have D&C 6:20.loved you.
Greater 1 Jn. 3:16 (10–18). TG Love.love hath no man than this, that a man lay down his TG Life, Sanctity of; Martyrdom.life for his TG Brotherhood and Sisterhood; Friendship.friends.
Ye are my D&C 84:63.friends, if ye do whatsoever I TG Commandments of God.command you.

Henceforth I call you not servants; for the servant knoweth not what his lord doeth: but I have called you Luke 12:4.friends; for all things that I have John 16:12; 3 Ne. 15:16 (13–18).heard of my Father I have made TG Revelation.known unto you.

Ye have not chosen me, but I have TG Apostles; Called of God; Election; Priesthood, Qualifying for.chosen you, and TG Authority; Church Organization; Priesthood, Authority; Priesthood, History of; Priesthood, Melchizedek.ordained you, that ye should go and bring forth TG Missionary Work; Mission of Early Saints.fruit, and that your fruit should remain: that whatsoever ye shall ask of the Father in my D&C 18:18.name, he may give it you.
These things I command you, that ye love one another.
If the world TG Hate.hate you, ye know that it hated me before it hated you.
If ye were of the TG World; Worldliness.world, the world would Luke 6:26.love his own: but because ye are not of the world, but I have chosen you out of the world, therefore the world hateth you.

Remember the word that I said unto you, The Matt. 10:24 (24–25).servant is not greater than his lord. If they have D&C 6:29. TG Persecution.persecuted me, they will also persecute you; if they have kept my saying, they will keep yours also.
But all these things will they do unto you for my name’s sake, because they know not him that sent me.
If I had not come and spoken unto them, they had not had John 9:41 (39–41); 2 Ne. 9:25 (25–27); D&C 82:3 (3–4).sin: but now they have no IE pretext, excuse.cloak for their sin.
He that hateth me hateth my Father also.
If I had not done among them the works which none other man did, they had not had Alma 32:19 (17–19).sin: but now have they both seen and hated both me and my Father.
But this cometh to pass, that the word might be fulfilled that is written in their law, They TG Hate.hated me without a cause.

But when the TG Comfort; Holy Ghost, Comforter.Comforter is come, whom I will send unto you from the Father, even the Spirit of truth, which proceedeth from the Father, he shall TG Holy Ghost, Mission of; Holy Ghost, Source of Testimony.testify of me:
And ye also shall bear TG Witness.witness, because ye have been with me from the beginning.

div.p: Jesus discourses on the mission of the Holy Ghost—He tells of His death and resurrection, announces that He is the Son of God, and says that He has overcome the world.

chapter.verse: These things have I spoken unto you, that ye should not be TG Offense.offended.
They shall put you out of the synagogues: yea, the time cometh, that whosoever TG Persecution.killeth you will think that he doeth God service.
And these things will they do unto you, because they have not Moses 4:6.known the Father, nor me.
But these things have I told you, that when the time shall come, ye may remember that I told you of them. And these things I said not unto you at the beginning, because I was with you.
But now I Luke 5:35.go my way to him that sent me; and none of you asketh me, Whither goest thou?
But because I have said these things unto you, sorrow hath filled your heart.

Nevertheless I tell you the truth; It is GR beneficial, profitable, advantageous.expedient for you that I go away: for if I go not away, the TG Holy Ghost, Comforter.Comforter will not come unto you; but if I depart, I will send him unto you.
And when he is come, he will TG Holy Ghost, Mission of; Reproof.reprove the world of sin, and of TG Righteousness.righteousness, and of judgment:
Of TG Sin.sin, because they believe not on me;
Of righteousness, because I go to my Father, and ye see me no more;
Of judgment, because the prince of this world is judged.
I have yet many things to John 15:15; 3 Ne. 15:16 (13–18).say unto you, but ye cannot Heb. 5:11 (11–12); 3 Ne. 17:2 (2–4); D&C 50:40; 78:18 (17–18).bear them now.

Howbeit when he, the TG Conscience.Spirit of truth, is come, he will TG God, Omniscience of; Holy Ghost, Gifts of; Holy Ghost, Mission of; Inspiration; Learn.guide you into all TG God, Intelligence of; Truth.truth: for he shall not speak of himself; but whatsoever he shall hear, that shall he speak: and he will TG Revelation.shew you things to come.
He shall Acts 3:13. TG Holy Ghost, Mission of.glorify me: for he shall receive of mine, and shall shew it unto you.
All 3 Ne. 28:10; D&C 76:59; 84:38 (37–38).things that the TG Jesus Christ, Divine Sonship.Father hath are mine: therefore said I, that he shall take of mine, and shall shew it unto you.
A little while, and ye shall not see me: and again, a little while, and ye shall D&C 35:21; 38:8.see me, because I go to the Father.

Then said some of his disciples among themselves, What is this that he saith unto us, A little while, and ye shall not see me: and again, a little while, and ye shall see me: and, Because I go to the Father?
They said therefore, What is this that he saith, A little while? we cannot tell what he saith.

Now Jesus knew that they were desirous to ask him, and said unto them, Do ye inquire among yourselves of that I said, A little while, and ye shall not see me: and again, a little while, and ye shall see me?
Verily, verily, I say unto you, That ye shall weep and lament, but the world shall rejoice: and ye shall be sorrowful, but your TG Pain; Patience.sorrow shall be turned into TG Joy.joy.

A TG Marriage, Motherhood.woman when she is in travail hath sorrow, because her hour is come: but as soon as she is delivered of the child, she remembereth no more the anguish, for joy that a man is born into the world.
And ye now therefore have TG Sorrow.sorrow: but I will see you again, and your heart shall rejoice, and your TG Joy.joy no man taketh from you.

And in that day ye shall ask me JST John 16:23 … nothing but it shall be done unto you. Verily, verily, I say …nothing. Verily, verily, I say unto you, Whatsoever ye shall TG Prayer.ask the Father in my name, he will give it you.
Hitherto have ye asked nothing in my name: D&C 66:9.ask, and ye shall receive, that your joy may be full.
These things have I spoken unto you in GR figurative discourse, similitudes.proverbs: but the time cometh, when I shall no more speak unto you in proverbs, but I shall shew you plainly of the Father.
At that day ye shall ask in my name: and I say not unto you, that I will pray the Father for you:
For the Father himself TG God, Love of.loveth you, because ye have loved me, and have TG Faith.believed that I came out from God.
I John 8:42; 17:8; Gal. 4:4.came John 8:14.forth from the TG Man, Antemortal Existence of.Father, and am come into the world: again, I leave the world, and go to the Father.
His disciples said unto him, Lo, now speakest thou plainly, and speakest no proverb.
Now are we sure that thou TG God, Omniscience of; God, Perfection of.knowest all things, and needest not that any man should ask thee: by this we believe that thou camest forth from God.
Jesus answered them, Do ye now believe?
Behold, the hour cometh, yea, is now come, that ye shall be scattered, every man to his own, and shall leave me alone: and yet I am not John 8:16, 29; 14:18.alone, because the Father is with me.

These things I have spoken unto you, that in me ye might have TG Peace of God.peace. In the TG Earth, Purpose of; World; Worldliness.world ye shall have TG Affliction; Test; Tribulation.tribulation: but be of good TG Happiness.cheer; I have Rev. 12:11 (10–12); D&C 50:41; 76:107.overcome the world.

div.p: Jesus offers the great Intercessory Prayer—He is glorified by gaining eternal life—He prays for His Apostles and all the Saints—He explains how the Father and Son are one.

chapter.verse: These words spake Jesus, and lifted up his eyes to heaven, and said, Father, the hour is come; glorify thy TG Jesus Christ, Divine Sonship.Son, that thy Son also may TG Jesus Christ, Mission of.glorify thee:

As thou hast given him TG Jesus Christ, Authority of; Jesus Christ, Power of.power over all flesh, that he should give John 6:37.eternal life to as many as thou hast John 10:29 (27–29); D&C 50:41.given him.

And this is TG Eternal Life.life 1 Jn. 1:2; 2:25; D&C 132:24.eternal, that they might Jer. 9:3; Hosea 2:20; 1 Jn. 4:8 (7–8); D&C 101:16. TG God, Knowledge about; Objectives; Testimony.know thee the only true TG Godhead.God, and Jesus Christ, whom thou hast TG Jesus Christ, Messenger of the Covenant.sent.
I have D&C 65:6; 76:43 (40–43).glorified thee on the earth: I have D&C 19:2. TG Jesus Christ, Atonement through.finished the work which thou gavest me to do.

And now, O Father, glorify thou me with thine own self with the TG Celestial Glory; Jesus Christ, Glory of.glory which I had with thee TG Jesus Christ, Foreordained; Man, Antemortal Existence of.before the world was.
I have manifested thy name unto the men which thou gavest me 1 Kgs. 8:53; 3 Ne. 15:19 (19–20).out of the world: thine they were, and thou gavest them me; and they have kept thy word.
Now they have known that all things whatsoever thou hast TG Jesus Christ, Relationships with the Father.given me are of thee.

For I have given unto them the Deut. 18:18; John 6:68. TG Revelation.words which thou gavest me; and they have received them, and have known surely that I John 8:42; 16:28 (27–28, 30); Gal. 4:4.came out from thee, and they have TG Faith.believed that thou didst send me.
I TG Prayer.pray for them: I pray not for the world, but for them which D&C 27:14; 84:63.thou hast given me; for they are thine.
And all mine are thine, and thine are mine; and I am glorified in them.

And now I am no more in the world, but these are in the TG Earth, Purpose of.world, and I come to thee. Holy Father, keep through thine own name those whom thou hast given me, that they may be TG Unity.one, as we are.

While I was with them in the world, I kept them in thy name: those that thou gavest me I have kept, and John 6:39; 18:9.none of them is D&C 50:42.lost, but the son of 3 Ne. 27:32. TG Damnation; Death, Spiritual, Second; Hell.perdition; that the scripture might be fulfilled.
And now come I to thee; and these things I speak in the world, that they might have my TG Joy.joy fulfilled in themselves.
I have given them thy word; and the world hath hated them, because they are not of the world, even as I am not of the world.
I pray not that thou shouldest take them out of the TG World.world, but that thou shouldest TG Refuge.keep them from the TG Evil.evil.
They are not of the TG Worldliness.world, even as I am not of the world.
TG Sanctification.Sanctify them through thy Jacob 4:13.truth: thy word is 2 Sam. 7:28; Ps. 119:142. TG Truth.truth.
As thou hast TG Jesus Christ, Authority of.sent me into the world, even so have I also sent them into the world.
And for their sakes I TG Jesus Christ, Atonement through.sanctify myself, that they also might be sanctified through the truth.
Neither TG Prayer.pray I for these alone, but for them also which shall D&C 45:5.believe on me through their word;

That they all may be 4 Ne. 1:17 (15–17); D&C 38:27.one; as thou, TG Godhead; God the Father, Elohim.Father, art in me, and I in thee, that they also may be D&C 35:2; Moses 6:68.one in us: that the world may believe that thou hast sent me.
And the glory which thou gavest me I have given them; that they may be Gal. 2:20.one, even as we are D&C 93:3.one:
I in them, and thou in me, that they may be made TG Perfection.perfect in one; and that the world may know that thou hast sent me, and hast TG God, Love of.loved them, as thou hast loved me.
Father, I will that they also, whom thou hast given me, be with me where I am; that they may behold my glory, which thou hast given me: for thou lovedst me before the foundation of the world.
O righteous Father, the world hath not TG God, Knowledge about.known thee: but I have known thee, and these have known that thou hast sent me.

And I have TG Preaching.declared unto them thy TG Name.name, and will declare it: that the TG God, Love of.love wherewith thou hast TG Jesus Christ, Relationships with the Father.loved me may be in them, and I in them.

div.p: Jesus is betrayed and arrested—He is examined and maltreated first before Annas, then before Caiaphas—Peter denies knowing Jesus—Jesus is arraigned before Pilate.

chapter.verse: When Jesus had spoken these words, he went forth with his disciples over the brook Cedron, where was a garden, into the which he entered, and his disciples.
And Judas also, which betrayed him, knew the place: for Jesus ofttimes resorted thither with his disciples.
Judas then, having received a band of men and officers from the chief priests and Pharisees, cometh thither with lanterns and torches and weapons.
Jesus therefore, knowing all things that should come upon him, went forth, and said unto them, Whom seek ye?
They answered him, Jesus of Nazareth. Jesus saith unto them, I am he. And Judas also, which betrayed him, stood with them.
As soon then as he had said unto them, I am he, they went backward, and fell to the ground.
Then asked he them again, Whom seek ye? And they said, Jesus of Nazareth.
Jesus answered, I have told you that I am he: if therefore ye seek me, let these go their way:
That the saying might be fulfilled, which he spake, Of them which thou gavest me have I lost John 6:39; 17:12.none.
Then Simon Peter having a sword drew it, and smote the high priest’s servant, and TG Rashness.cut off his right ear. The servant’s name was Matt. 26:51.Malchus.
Then said Jesus unto Peter, Put up thy sword into the sheath: the 3 Ne. 11:11.cup which my Father hath given me, shall I not drink it?
Then the band and the captain and officers of the Jews took Jesus, and bound him,
And led him away to Matt. 26:57; Luke 3:2. TG Jesus Christ, Trials of.Annas first; for he was father in law to Caiaphas, which was the high priest that same year.
Now John 19:11.Caiaphas was he, which gave counsel to the Jews, that it was expedient that one man should die for the people.
¶ And Simon Peter followed Jesus, and so did another disciple: that disciple was known unto the high priest, and went in with Jesus into the palace of the high priest.
But Peter stood at the door without. Then went out that other disciple, which was known unto the high priest, and spake unto her that kept the door, and brought in Peter.
Then saith the damsel that kept the door unto Peter, Art not thou also one of this man’s disciples? He saith, I am not.
And the servants and officers stood there, who had made a fire of coals; for it was cold: and they warmed themselves: and Peter stood with them, and warmed himself.
¶ The high TG Jesus Christ, Trials of.priest then asked Jesus of his disciples, and of his doctrine.
Jesus answered him, I spake openly to the world; I ever taught in the synagogue, and in the temple, whither the Jews always resort; and in secret have I said nothing.
Why askest thou me? ask them which heard me, what I have said unto them: behold, they know what I said.
And when he had thus spoken, one of the officers which stood by Acts 23:2.struck Jesus with the palm of his hand, saying, Answerest thou the high priest so?
Jesus answered him, If I have spoken evil, bear witness of the evil: but if well, why smitest thou me?
Now Annas had sent him bound unto Caiaphas the high priest.
And Simon Peter stood and warmed himself. They said therefore unto him, Art not thou also one of his disciples? He denied it, and said, I am not.
One of the servants of the high priest, being his kinsman whose ear Peter cut off, saith, Did not I see thee in the garden with him?
Peter then denied again: and immediately the cock crew.

¶ Then led they Jesus from Caiaphas unto the hall of judgment: and it was early; and they themselves went not into the judgment hall, lest they should be defiled; but that they might eat the John 19:14.passover.
TG Jesus Christ, Trials of.Pilate then went out unto them, and said, What accusation bring ye against this man?
They answered and said unto him, If he were not a malefactor, we would not have delivered him up unto thee.
Then said Pilate unto them, Take ye him, and judge him according to your law. The Jews therefore said unto him, It is not lawful for us to put any man to death:
That the saying of Jesus might be fulfilled, which he spake, signifying what death he should die.
Then Pilate entered into the judgment hall again, and called Jesus, and said unto him, Art thou the King of the Jews?
Jesus answered him, Sayest thou this thing of thyself, or did others tell it thee of me?
Pilate answered, Am I a Jew? Thine own nation and the chief priests have delivered thee unto me: what hast thou done?

Jesus answered, My John 6:15.kingdom is not of this TG World.world: if my kingdom were of this world, then would my servants fight, that I should not be delivered to the Jews: but now is my kingdom not from hence.

1 Tim. 6:13.Pilate therefore said unto him, Art thou a Isa. 55:4.king then? Jesus answered, Thou sayest that I am a John 19:12 (11–13).king. To this end was I born, and for this cause came I into the world, that I should bear witness unto the 3 Ne. 11:31 (31–41). TG Truth.truth. Every one that is of the truth heareth my voice.
Pilate saith unto him, What is truth? And when he had GR said this again.said this, he went out again unto the Jews, and saith unto them, I find in him no Matt. 27:24 (11–26); John 19:4.fault at all.
But ye have a custom, that I should release unto you one at the passover: will ye therefore that I release unto you the King of the Jews?
Then cried they all again, saying, Not this man, but Barabbas. Now Luke 23:19.Barabbas was a robber.

div.p: Jesus is scourged and crucified—He places His mother in John’s care—He dies and His side is pierced with a spear—He is buried in the tomb of Joseph of Arimathæa.

chapter.verse: Then Pilate therefore took Jesus, and Mosiah 15:5.scourged him.
And the soldiers plaited a crown of thorns, and put it on his head, and they put on him a purple robe,
And said, Hail, D&C 45:53.King of the Jews! and they smote him with their hands.
Pilate therefore went forth again, and saith unto them, Behold, I bring him forth to you, that ye may know that I find no GR cause, crime, case. John 18:38; Mosiah 14:9.fault in him.
Then came Jesus forth, wearing the crown of thorns, and the purple robe. And Pilate saith unto them, Behold the man!

When the chief priests therefore and officers saw him, they Acts 3:13.cried out, saying, Crucify him, crucify him. Pilate saith unto them, Take ye him, and crucify him: for I find no GR cause, crime, motive.fault in him.
The Jews answered him, We have a Lev. 24:16.law, and by our law he ought to die, because he made himself the John 5:18 (17–18); 10:33; Mosiah 15:2.Son of God.
¶ When Pilate therefore heard that saying, he was the more afraid;
And went again into the judgment hall, and saith unto Jesus, Whence art thou? But Jesus gave him no Mosiah 14:7; 15:6.answer.
Then saith Pilate unto him, Speakest thou not unto me? knowest thou not that I have power to crucify thee, and have power to release thee?
Jesus answered, Thou couldest have no Rom. 13:1; D&C 134:1.power at all against me, except it were given thee from above: therefore he that delivered me unto thee hath the greater John 18:14.sin.

And from thenceforth Pilate sought to release him: but the Jews cried out, saying, If thou let this man go, thou art not Cæsar’s friend: whosoever maketh himself a John 18:37 (33, 36–37).king speaketh against Cæsar.
¶ When Pilate therefore heard that saying, he brought Jesus forth, and sat down in the judgment seat in a place that is called the Pavement, but in the Hebrew, Gabbatha.
And it was the preparation of the John 18:28. TG Passover.passover, and about the sixth hour: and he saith unto the Jews, Behold your D&C 45:53.King!

But they cried out, Away with him, away with him, TG Jesus Christ, Trials of.crucify him. Pilate saith unto them, Shall I crucify your King? The chief priests answered, We have no 2 Ne. 10:14.king but Cæsar.
Then delivered he him therefore unto them to be crucified. And they took Jesus, and led him away.
And he Gen. 22:6.bearing his Luke 14:27.cross went forth into a place called the place of a JST John 19:17 … burial … Heb. 13:12 (11–16).skull, which is called in the Hebrew Golgotha:
Where they crucified him, and two other with him, on either side one, and Jesus in the midst.
¶ And Pilate wrote a title, and put it on the cross. And the writing was, Jesus of Nazareth the King of the Jews.
This title then read many of the Jews: for the place where Jesus was crucified was nigh to the city: and it was written in Hebrew, and Greek, and Latin.
Then said the chief priests of the Jews to Pilate, Write not, The King of the Jews; but that he said, I am King of the Jews.
Pilate answered, What I have written I have written.
¶ Then the soldiers, when they had crucified Jesus, took his garments, and made four parts, to every soldier a part; and also his coat: now the coat was without seam, woven from the top throughout.

They said therefore among themselves, Let us not rend it, but cast lots for it, whose it shall be: that the scripture might be fulfilled, which saith, They parted my Ps. 22:18.raiment among them, and for my vesture they did cast lots. These things therefore the soldiers did.
¶ Now there stood by the cross of Jesus his Luke 2:35 (34–35). TG Marriage, Motherhood.mother, and his mother’s sister, Mary the wife of Luke 24:18.Cleophas, and Mary Magdalene.
When Jesus therefore saw his mother, and the John 20:2 (2–9); D&C 7:1.disciple standing by, whom he loved, he saith unto his mother, TG Woman.Woman, behold thy son!
Then saith he to the disciple, Behold thy TG Family, Love within; Honoring Father and Mother; Jesus Christ, Family of.mother! And from that hour that disciple took her unto his own home.
¶ After this, Jesus knowing that all things were now accomplished, that the scripture might be fulfilled, saith, I thirst.
Now there was set a vessel full of vinegar: and they filled a sponge with Ps. 69:21.vinegar, and put it upon hyssop, and put it to his mouth.
When Jesus therefore had received the vinegar, he said, It is TG Jesus Christ, Atonement through.finished: and he bowed his head, and gave up the ghost.

The Jews therefore, because it was the preparation, that the bodies should not remain upon the TG Jesus Christ, Crucifixion of.cross on the sabbath day, (for that Ex. 12:16 (15–17).sabbath day was an Jesus arose on the first day of the week. The previous day was the weekly Sabbath. The day before the Sabbath, being also the day after the Passover meal, could be the “high” day. Ex. 12:16; Lev. 23:7; Mark 15:42.high Ex. 12:14 (12–16).day,) besought Pilate that their legs might be Deut. 21:23 (22–23).broken, and that they might be taken away.
Then came the soldiers, and brake the legs of the first, and of the other which was crucified with him.
But when they came to Jesus, and saw that he was dead already, they TG Jesus Christ, Types of, in Anticipation; Passover.brake not his legs:
But one of the soldiers with a spear pierced his side, and forthwith came there out blood and water.
And he that John 1:14; 21:24.saw it bare John 1:7 (6–8).record, and his record is true: and he knoweth that he saith true, that ye might believe.
For these things were done, that the scripture should be fulfilled, A Ex. 12:46; Num. 9:12.bone of him shall not be broken.
And again another scripture saith, They shall look on him whom they TG Jesus Christ, Crucifixion of.pierced.

¶ And after this Matt. 27:57 (57–61).Joseph of Arimathæa, being a disciple of Jesus, but secretly for Prov. 29:25; John 7:13; 12:42 (42–43).fear of the Jews, besought Pilate that he might take away the body of Jesus: and Pilate gave him leave. He came therefore, and took the body of Jesus.
And there came also John 3:1 (1–10).Nicodemus, which at the first came to Jesus by night, and brought a mixture of Ps. 45:8.myrrh and aloes, about an hundred pound weight.
Then took they the body of Jesus, and wound it in linen clothes with the spices, as the manner of the Jews is to bury.
Now in the place where he was crucified there was a garden; and in the garden a new Luke 23:53; 2 Ne. 25:13.sepulchre, wherein was never man yet laid.
There laid they Jesus therefore because of the Jews’ TG Passover.preparation day ; for the sepulchre was nigh at hand.

div.p: Mary Magdalene, Peter, and John find the empty tomb—The risen Christ appears to Mary Magdalene in the garden—He appears to the disciples and shows His resurrected body—Thomas feels the wounds in Jesus’ hands, feet, and side—Jesus is the Christ, the Son of God.

chapter.verse: The TG Sabbath.first day of the week cometh Mary Magdalene early, when it was yet Mark 16:2.dark, unto the sepulchre, and seeth the Matt. 27:60.stone taken away from the JST John 20:1 … sepulcher, and two angels sitting thereon.sepulchre.

Then she runneth, and cometh to Simon Peter, and to the other John 13:23; 19:26 (26–27); 21:24 (20–24); 1 Ne. 14:27 (18–27); D&C 7:1.disciple, whom Jesus loved, and saith unto them, They have taken away the TG Jesus Christ, Lord.Lord out of the sepulchre, and we know not where they have laid him.
Peter therefore went forth, and that other disciple, and came to the sepulchre.
So they ran both together: and the other disciple did outrun Peter, and came first to the sepulchre.
And he stooping down, and looking in, saw the linen clothes lying; yet went he not in.
Then cometh Simon Peter following him, and went into the sepulchre, and seeth the linen clothes lie,
And the John 11:44.napkin, that was about his head, not lying with the linen clothes, but wrapped together in a place by itself.
Then went in also that other disciple, which came first to the sepulchre, and he saw, and believed.
For as yet they knew not the scripture, that he must Mark 9:10 (9–10); Hel. 14:16 (15–17); D&C 18:12 (11–12).rise again from the Isa. 25:8.dead.
Then the disciples went away again unto their own home.
¶ But Mary stood without at the sepulchre weeping: and as she wept, she stooped down, and looked into the sepulchre,
And seeth two TG Angels.angels in white sitting, the one at the head, and the other at the feet, where the body of Jesus had lain.
And they say unto her, Woman, why weepest thou? She saith unto them, Because they have taken away my Lord, and I know not where they have laid him.
And when she had thus said, she turned herself back, and TG Jesus Christ, Appearances, Postmortal.saw Jesus standing, and knew not that it was Jesus.

Jesus saith unto her, Woman, why weepest thou? whom seekest thou? She, supposing him to be the gardener, saith unto him, Sir, if thou have borne him hence, tell me where thou hast laid him, and I will take him away.
Jesus saith unto her, Mary. She turned herself, and saith unto him, Rabboni; which is to say, GR Teacher.Master.

Jesus saith unto her, JST John 20:17 … Hold me not …Touch me not; for I am not yet Luke 23:43 (39–43); Alma 40:11 (6–15). TG Jesus Christ, Ascension of.ascended to my TG God the Father, Elohim.Father: but go to my brethren, and say unto them, I ascend unto my TG Godhead.Father, and your Father; and to my God, and your God.
Mary Magdalene came and told the disciples that she had seen the Lord, and that he had spoken these things unto her.

¶ Then the same day at evening, being the first day of the week, when the doors were shut where the disciples were TG Assembly for Worship; Meetings.assembled for fear of the Jews, came Jesus and TG Jesus Christ, Appearances, Postmortal.stood in the midst, and saith unto them, TG Peace of God.Peace be unto you.
And when he had so said, he shewed unto them his TG Jesus Christ, Resurrection.hands and his side. Then were the disciples glad, when they TG Jesus Christ, Appearances, Postmortal.saw the Lord.
Then said Jesus to them again, Peace be unto you: as my TG Jesus Christ, Divine Sonship.Father hath TG Jesus Christ, Authority of.sent me, even so Mark 16:15 (14–18). TG Authority.send I you.
And when he had said this, he breathed on them, and saith unto them, Receive ye the Holy Ghost:
Whose soever sins ye TG Priesthood, Keys of; Remission of Sins.remit, they are remitted unto them; and whose soever sins ye retain, they are retained.
¶ But Thomas, one of the twelve, called Didymus, was not with them when Jesus came.

The other disciples therefore said unto him, We have seen the Lord. But he said unto them, Except I shall see in his hands the TG Jesus Christ, Crucifixion of.print of the nails, and put my finger into the print of the nails, and thrust my hand into his side, I will not believe.
¶ And after eight days again his disciples were within, and Thomas with them: then came Jesus, the doors being shut, and stood in the midst, and said, Peace be unto you.
Then saith he to Thomas, Reach hither thy finger, and behold my hands; and reach hither thy hand, and 3 Ne. 11:14.thrust it into my side: and be not TG Doubt.faithless, but TG Unbelief.believing.
And Thomas answered and said unto him, My Lord and my God.

Jesus saith unto him, Thomas, because thou hast TG Jesus Christ, Appearances, Postmortal.seen me, thou hast believed: D&C 34:4.blessed are they that have not seen, and yet have 1 Pet. 1:8; Alma 32:21 (17–21). TG Faith.believed.
¶ And many other TG Signs.signs truly did Jesus in the presence of his disciples, which are not John 21:25; 1 Ne. 14:25 (18–30); D&C 93:6 (6, 18).written in this book:

But these are 1 Jn. 5:13 (10–21).written, that ye might Luke 1:4 (3–4); John 1:7 (6–8); 1 Ne. 6:4; Morm. 5:14. TG Faith.believe that Jesus is the TG Jesus Christ, Messiah.Christ, the Son of God; and that believing ye might have John 5:24.life through his TG Jesus Christ, Taking the Name of; Name.name.

div.p: Jesus appears to the disciples at the sea of Tiberias—He says, Feed my sheep—He foretells Peter’s martyrdom and that John will not die.

chapter.verse: After these things Jesus TG Jesus Christ, Resurrection.shewed himself again to the disciples at the sea of Tiberias; and on this wise shewed he himself.
There were together Simon Peter, and Thomas called Didymus, and Nathanael of Cana in Galilee, and the sons of Matt. 4:21.Zebedee, and two other of his disciples.
