Simon Peter saith unto them, I go a fishing. They say unto him, We also go with thee. They went forth, and entered into a ship immediately; and that night they caught nothing.
But when the morning was now come, Jesus TG Jesus Christ, Appearances, Postmortal.stood on the shore: but the disciples knew not that it was Jesus.
Then Jesus saith unto them, Children, have ye any meat? They answered him, No.
And he said unto them, Cast the net on the right side of the ship, and ye shall find. They cast therefore, and now they were not able to draw it for the multitude of fishes.

Therefore that disciple whom Jesus loved saith unto Peter, It is the Lord. Now when Simon Peter heard that it was the Lord, he girt his fisher’s coat unto him, (for he was naked,) and did cast himself into the sea.
And the other disciples came in a little ship; (for they were not far from land, but as it were two hundred cubits,) dragging the net with fishes.
As soon then as they were come to land, they saw a fire of coals there, and fish laid thereon, and bread.
Jesus saith unto them, Bring of the fish which ye have now caught.
Simon Peter went up, and drew the net to land full of great fishes, an hundred and fifty and three: and for all there were so many, yet was not the net broken.
Jesus saith unto them, Come and dine. And none of the disciples durst ask him, Who art thou? knowing that it was the Lord.
Jesus then cometh, and taketh bread, and giveth them, and fish likewise.
This is now the 3 Ne. 26:13; 27:2.third time that Jesus TG Jesus Christ, Appearances, Postmortal.shewed himself to his disciples, after that he was risen from the dead.

¶ So when they had dined, Jesus saith to Simon Peter, Simon, son of Jonas, lovest thou me more than these? He saith unto him, Yea, Lord; thou knowest that I love thee. He saith unto him, Feed my lambs.
He saith to him again the second time, Simon, son of Jonas, lovest thou me? He saith unto him, Yea, Lord; thou knowest that I love thee. He saith unto him, 1 Pet. 5:2; D&C 112:14.Feed my sheep.

He saith unto him the third time, Simon, son of Jonas, lovest thou me? Peter was grieved because he said unto him the third time, Lovest thou me? And he said unto him, Lord, thou knowest all things; thou knowest that I love thee. Jesus saith unto him, TG Fellowshipping; Jesus Christ, Teaching Mode of; Missionary Work.Feed my TG Sheep.sheep.

Verily, verily, I say unto thee, When thou wast young, thou girdedst thyself, and walkedst whither thou wouldest: but when thou shalt be TG Old Age.old, thou shalt stretch forth thy hands, and another shall gird thee, and carry thee whither thou wouldest not.
This spake he, signifying by what 2 Pet. 1:14.death he should glorify God. And when he had spoken this, he saith unto him, Follow me.
Then Peter, turning about, seeth the disciple whom Jesus loved following; which also leaned on his breast at supper, and said, Lord, which is he that betrayeth thee?
Peter seeing him saith to Jesus, Lord, and what shall this man do?
Jesus saith unto him, If I will that he Rev. 10:11; D&C 7:3 (3–4). TG Translated Beings.tarry till I come, what is that to thee? TG Jesus Christ, Exemplar.follow thou me.

Then went this saying abroad among the brethren, that that disciple should not Luke 9:27.die: yet Jesus said not unto him, He shall not die; but, If I will that he tarry till I come, what is that to thee?
This is the John 1:14; 13:23; 19:35; 20:2.disciple which testifieth of these things, and TG Scriptures, Writing of.wrote these things: and we know that his testimony is 3 Ne. 8:1.true.

And there are also many other things which Jesus did, the which, if they should be John 20:30 (30–31); 1 Ne. 14:25 (18–30).written every one, I suppose that even the world itself could not contain the 3 Ne. 26:6.books that should be written. Amen.

div.p: Jesus ministers for forty days after His resurrection—The kingdom is to be restored to Israel at a later time—The Twelve are to bear witness in Jerusalem, Judæa, Samaria, and the uttermost parts of the earth—Jesus ascends into heaven—Matthias is chosen to fill the vacancy in the Twelve.

chapter.verse: The former treatise have 2 Tim. 4:11 (10–11).I made, O Luke 1:3.Theophilus, of all that Jesus began both to do and teach,
Until the day in which he was taken up, after that he through the Holy Ghost had given TG Holy Ghost, Mission of.commandments unto the apostles whom he had chosen:

To whom also he TG Jesus Christ, Appearances, Postmortal; Jesus Christ, Resurrection.shewed himself alive after his GR suffering. JST Acts 1:3 … sufferings …passion by many infallible proofs, being seen of them forty days, and speaking of the things D&C 100:11.pertaining to the kingdom of God:

And, being assembled together with them, commanded them that they should not depart from Jerusalem, but wait for the D&C 95:9 (8–10). TG Promise.promise of the Father, which, saith he, ye have heard of me.
For John truly baptized with water; but ye shall be TG Holy Ghost, Baptism of; Holy Ghost, Gift of.baptized with the Holy Ghost not many days hence.
When they therefore were come together, they asked of him, saying, Lord, wilt thou at this time TG Dispensations.restore again the kingdom to Israel?
And he said unto them, It is not for you to know the times or the Dan. 2:21 (19–22, 28); D&C 68:11 (7–12); 121:12 (12, 27, 31).seasons, which the Father hath put in his own power.

But ye shall receive 2 Tim. 1:7. TG Holy Ghost, Gifts of.power, after that the Holy Ghost is come upon you: and ye shall be Luke 24:48; D&C 27:12.witnesses unto me both in Jerusalem, and in all Judæa, and in Acts 8:5 (5, 14).Samaria, and unto the uttermost part of the earth.

And when he had spoken these things, while they beheld, he was 3 Ne. 10:18 (18–19); 11:8 (8–10). TG Jesus Christ, Resurrection.taken Ps. 68:18; Eph. 4:8.up; and a cloud received him out of their sight.
And while they looked steadfastly toward heaven as he went up, behold, two men stood by them in white apparel;

Which also said, Ye men of Galilee, why stand ye gazing up into 1 Thes. 1:10.heaven? this same Jesus, which is TG Jesus Christ, Ascension of.taken up from you into heaven, shall so John 14:3 (1–4).come in like TG Jesus Christ, Second Coming.manner as ye have seen him go into heaven.
Then returned they unto Jerusalem from the mount called Olivet, which is from Jerusalem a sabbath day’s journey.

And when they were come in, they went up into an upper room, where abode both Peter, and James, and John, and Andrew, Philip, and Thomas, Bartholomew, and Matthew, James the son of Alphæus, and Simon Zelotes, and Judas the brother of James.
These all continued with TG Unity.one accord in prayer and Moro. 6:5 (1–5).supplication, with the women, and Mary the mother of Jesus, and with his TG Jesus Christ, Family of.brethren.
¶ And in those days Peter stood up in the midst of the disciples, and said, (the number of names together were about an hundred and twenty,)

Men and brethren, this scripture must needs have been fulfilled, which the Holy Ghost by the mouth of David TG Holy Ghost, Mission of.spake before concerning Ps. 41:9; Luke 22:48 (47–48).Judas, which was guide to them that took Jesus.
For he was numbered with us, and had obtained part of this ministry.
Now this man purchased a field with the reward of iniquity; and falling headlong, he Matt. 27:5 (3–10).burst asunder in the midst, and all his bowels gushed out.
And it was known unto all the dwellers at Jerusalem; insomuch as that field is called in their GR own.proper tongue, Aceldama, that is to say, The field of blood.
For it is written in the book of Psalms, Let his habitation be Ps. 69:25.desolate, and let no man dwell therein: and his GR overseership, office. Ps. 109:8; D&C 114:2.bishoprick let another take.
Wherefore of these men which have companied with us all the time that the Lord Jesus went in and out among us,
Beginning from the baptism of John, unto that same day that he was taken up from us, must one GR become a witness.be ordained to be a TG Witness.witness with us of his TG Resurrection.resurrection.
And they appointed two, Joseph called Barsabas, who was surnamed Justus, and Matthias.
And they D&C 9:8 (7–9).prayed, and said, Thou, Lord, which TG God, Omniscience of.knowest the hearts of all men, shew GR which one.whether of these two thou hast TG Called of God.chosen,
That he may take part of this ministry and TG Church Organization.apostleship, from which Judas by transgression 1 Cor. 10:12; D&C 3:9.fell, that he might go to his own place.
And they gave forth their lots; and the lot fell upon TG Apostles.Matthias; and he was numbered with the eleven apostles.

div.p: The Spirit is poured out on the day of Pentecost—Peter testifies of Jesus’ resurrection—He tells how to gain salvation and speaks of the gift of the Holy Ghost—Many believe and are baptized.

chapter.verse: And when the day of Ex. 23:17; 34:22 (16, 22); Lev. 23:16 (15–16); Acts 20:16.Pentecost was fully come, they were all with one accord in one place.
And suddenly there came a sound from heaven as of a rushing mighty D&C 109:37.wind, and it filled all the house where they were sitting.
And there appeared unto them D&C 109:36.cloven tongues like as of fire, and it sat upon each of them.

And they were all filled with the Acts 4:31. TG Holy Ghost, Baptism of.Holy Ghost, and began to speak with other TG Holy Ghost, Gifts of; Holy Ghost, Mission of.tongues, as the D&C 14:8.Spirit gave them utterance.
And there were dwelling at Jerusalem Jews, devout men, out of every nation under heaven.
Now when this was noised abroad, the multitude came together, and were confounded, because that every man heard them speak in his own TG Language.language.
And they were all amazed and marvelled, saying one to another, Behold, are not all these which speak Galilæans?
And how hear we every man in our own tongue, wherein we were born?
Parthians, and Medes, and Elamites, and the dwellers in Mesopotamia, and in Judæa, and Cappadocia, in Pontus, and Asia,
Phrygia, and Pamphylia, in Egypt, and in the parts of Libya about Cyrene, and strangers of Rome, Jews and Acts 13:43.proselytes,
Cretes and Arabians, we do hear them speak in our 1 Cor. 14:22.tongues the wonderful works of God.
And they were all amazed, and were in doubt, saying one to another, What meaneth this?
Others 1 Cor. 14:23.mocking said, These men are full of new wine.
¶ But Peter, standing up with the eleven, lifted up his voice, and said unto them, Ye men of Judæa, and all ye that dwell at Jerusalem, be this known unto you, and hearken to my words:
For these are not drunken, as ye suppose, seeing it is but the third hour of the day.
But this is that which was spoken by the prophet JS—H 1:41.Joel;

And it shall come to pass in the TG Last Days.last days, saith God, I will Joel 2:28.pour out of my Isa. 44:3; Ezek. 36:27 (26–27); D&C 19:38; 44:2; 95:4.Spirit upon all flesh: and your sons and your daughters shall Acts 21:9. TG Holy Ghost, Gifts of.prophesy, and your young men shall see visions, and your old men shall TG Dream.dream dreams:
And on my servants and on my handmaidens I will pour out in those days of my Spirit; and they shall Acts 11:28 (27–28); 21:11 (4, 10–11).prophesy:
And I will shew TG Last Days.wonders in heaven above, and TG Signs.signs in the earth beneath; blood, and fire, and vapour of smoke:
The sun shall be turned into TG Darkness, Physical.darkness, and the moon into blood, before that great and GR glorious.notable TG Day of the Lord.day of the Lord come:
And it shall come to pass, that whosoever shall call on the name of the Lord shall be TG Jesus Christ, Savior; Salvation.saved.

Ye men of Israel, hear these words; Jesus of Nazareth, a man John 9:33 (31–33).approved of God among you by Mosiah 3:5 (5–6).miracles and wonders and signs, which Ex. 8:19; Luke 11:20; John 3:2; Acts 10:38.God did by him in the midst of you, as ye yourselves also know:

Him, being delivered by the GR appointed plan, purpose.determinate counsel and TG Foreordination; God, Foreknowledge of.foreknowledge of God, ye have taken, and by wicked hands have Matt. 26:24; Luke 22:22. TG Jesus Christ, Crucifixion of.crucified and slain:
Whom God hath TG Resurrection.raised up, having loosed the TG Pain.pains of TG Jesus Christ, Death of.death: because it was not possible that he should be holden of it.
For David speaketh concerning him, I foresaw the Ps. 16:8 (8–11).Lord always before my face, for he is on my right hand, that I should not be moved:
Therefore did my heart rejoice, and my tongue was glad; moreover also my flesh shall rest in hope:
Because thou wilt not leave my soul in JST Acts 2:27 … prison …hell, neither wilt thou suffer thine Holy One to see Acts 13:37 (34–37).corruption.
Thou hast made known to me the ways of life; thou shalt make me full of joy with thy countenance.
Men and brethren, let me freely speak unto you of the TG Patriarch.patriarch David, that he is both dead and 1 Kgs. 2:10.buried, and his sepulchre is with us unto this day.

Therefore being a prophet, and knowing that God had sworn with an TG Oath.oath to him, that of the fruit of his loins, according to the flesh, he would raise up TG Jesus Christ, Davidic Descent of.Christ to sit on his throne;
He seeing this before spake of the resurrection of Christ, that his soul was not left in hell, neither his flesh did see corruption.
This Jesus hath God Eph. 1:20. TG Jesus Christ, Resurrection.raised up, whereof we all are TG Witness.witnesses.

Therefore being GR to, at.by the TG Godhead.right hand of God Heb. 2:9.exalted, and having received of the Father the promise of the Holy Ghost, he hath D&C 53:2.shed forth this, which ye now see and hear.
For D&C 132:39.David is not Ps. 30:3; 86:13.ascended into the heavens: but he saith himself, The Lord said unto my Ps. 110:1.Lord, Sit thou on my right hand,
Until I make thy foes thy footstool.

Therefore let all the house of Israel know assuredly, that God hath made that same Jesus, whom ye have TG Jesus Christ, Crucifixion of.crucified, both TG Jesus Christ, Lord; Jesus Christ, Power of.Lord and Christ.

¶ Now when they heard this, they were Alma 22:3 (3–11). TG Conscience; Holy Ghost, Mission of; Holy Ghost, Source of Testimony.pricked in their TG Conversion.heart, and said unto Peter and to the rest of the apostles, Men and brethren, Alma 22:15.what shall we do?

Then Peter D&C 49:11 (11–14).said unto them, TG Baptism, Qualifications for; Repent; Salvation.Repent, and be TG Baptism; Baptism, Essential.baptized every one of you in the TG Name.name of Jesus Christ for the TG Remission of Sins.remission of sins, and ye shall receive the TG Holy Ghost, Gift of.gift of the Acts 8:17 (14–17). TG Holy Ghost, Baptism of.Holy Ghost.
For the TG Promise.promise is unto you, and to your children, and to all that are Isa. 57:19.afar off, even as many as the Lord our God shall call.
And with many other words did he testify and exhort, saying, D&C 36:6.Save yourselves from this GR crooked.untoward generation.
¶ Then they that gladly TG Teachable.received his TG Baptism, Qualifications for.word were baptized: and the same day there were added unto them about Acts 4:4.three thousand souls.

And they continued TG Commitment; Steadfastness; Sustaining Church Leaders.steadfastly in the apostles’ doctrine and TG Fellowshipping.fellowship, and in breaking of TG Bread; Sacrament.bread, and in prayers.
And TG Courage; Fearful.fear came upon every soul: and many wonders and TG Signs.signs were done by the apostles.
And all that believed were together, and had all things TG Consecration.common;
And sold their possessions and goods, and parted them to all men, as every man had Acts 4:35 (32–35); D&C 51:3.need.
And they, continuing daily with one accord in the Luke 24:53.temple, and breaking bread from house to house, did eat their meat with gladness and D&C 36:7.singleness of heart,
Praising God, and having favour with all the people. And the Lord Acts 5:14.added to the TG Church.church daily such as should be saved.

div.p: Peter and John heal a man lame since birth—Peter preaches repentance—He also speaks of the age of restoration preceding the Second Coming—He identifies Christ as the prophet of whom Moses spoke.

chapter.verse: Now Peter and John went up together into the temple at the hour of prayer, being the ninth hour.
And a certain man Acts 14:8 (8–10).lame from his mother’s womb was carried, whom they laid daily at the gate of the temple which is called Beautiful, to ask alms of them that entered into the temple;
Who seeing Peter and John about to go into the temple asked an TG Almsgiving.alms.
And Peter, fastening his eyes upon him with John, said, Look on us.
And he gave heed unto them, expecting to receive something of them.
Then Peter said, Silver and gold have I none; but such as I have TG Benevolence; Generosity.give I thee: In the Acts 4:10; Jacob 4:6; 3 Ne. 8:1.name of Jesus Christ of Nazareth rise up and walk.
And he took him by the right hand, and Mark 9:27. TG Heal.lifted him up: and immediately his feet and ankle bones received strength.
And he leaping up stood, and walked, and entered with them into the temple, walking, and leaping, and praising God.
And all the people Acts 4:16.saw him walking and praising God:
And they knew that it was he which sat for alms at the Beautiful gate of the temple: and they were filled with wonder and amazement at that which had happened unto him.
And as the lame man which was healed held Peter and John, all the people ran together unto them in the 1 Kgs. 6:3; John 10:23.porch that is called Solomon’s, greatly wondering.

¶ And when Peter saw it, he answered unto the people, Ye men of Israel, why marvel ye at this? or why look ye so earnestly on us, as though by our Gen. 41:16; Dan. 2:30.own power or holiness we had made this man to walk?

The God of Abraham, and of Isaac, and of Jacob, the God of our fathers, hath John 16:14; 1 Pet. 1:21 (17–21).glorified his Son Jesus; whom ye Matt. 27:20; Acts 13:28.delivered up, and John 19:6.denied him in the presence of Pilate, when he was determined to let him go.
But ye denied the Holy One and the Just, and desired a Luke 23:25 (17–19, 25).murderer to be granted unto you;
And killed the TG Jesus Christ, Messiah.Prince of life, whom God hath Acts 10:40.raised from the dead; whereof we are TG Witness.witnesses.

And his TG Name.name through faith in his name hath made this man strong, whom ye see and know: yea, the TG Faith.faith which is by him hath given him this perfect soundness in the presence of you all.
And now, brethren, JST Acts 3:17 … I know that through ignorance ye have done this, as also your rulers.I OR know.wot that through D&C 6:21.ignorance ye did it, as did also your rulers.
But those things, which God before had TG Foreordination.shewed by the mouth of all his Luke 24:27; Acts 28:23; Alma 18:36.prophets, that Christ should 1 Cor. 15:3.suffer, he hath so fulfilled.

¶ TG Repent.Repent ye therefore, and be TG Conversion.converted, that your sins may be Ps. 51:1; Mosiah 26:30; Alma 5:14 (11–15, 21).blotted out, when the times of refreshing shall come from the TG God, Presence of.presence of the Lord;
And he shall send TG Jesus Christ, Second Coming.Jesus Christ, which before was preached unto JST Acts 3:20 … you, whom ye have crucified;you:

Whom the heaven must receive until the times of TG Dispensations; Restoration of the Gospel.restitution of all things, which God hath TG Prophets, Mission of.spoken by the mouth of all his holy prophets since the world began.

For Moses truly said unto the fathers, A Deut. 18:15 (15–19); Acts 7:37; 1 Ne. 22:20 (20–22); JS—H 1:40.prophet shall the Lord your God raise up unto you of your brethren, like unto me; him shall ye hear in all things whatsoever he shall say unto you.
And it shall come to pass, that every soul, which will not hear that prophet, shall be D&C 1:14 (14–16); 133:63.destroyed from among the people.
Yea, and all the prophets from Samuel and those that follow after, as many as have spoken, have likewise TG Jesus Christ, Prophecies about.foretold of these days.

Ye are the TG Seed of Abraham.children of the prophets, and of the TG Abrahamic Covenant; Mission of Early Saints.covenant which God made with our fathers, saying unto Abraham, And in thy seed shall all the kindreds of the earth be TG Israel, Mission of.blessed.
Unto you first God, having raised up his TG God, Body of, Corporeal Nature.Son Jesus, sent him to bless you, in turning away every one of you from his iniquities.

div.p: Peter and John are arrested and brought before the council—Peter testifies that salvation comes because of Christ—The Sadducees strive to silence Peter and John—The Saints glory in the testimony of Jesus—They have all things in common.

chapter.verse: And as they spake unto the people, the priests, and the captain of the temple, and the Sadducees, came upon them,
Being grieved that they taught the people, and preached through Jesus the Acts 23:8.resurrection from the dead.
And they laid hands on them, and put them in hold unto the next day: for it was now eventide.
Howbeit many of them which heard the word believed; and the number of the men was about Acts 2:41.five thousand.
¶ And it came to pass on the morrow, that their rulers, and elders, and TG Scribe.scribes,
And Annas the high priest, and Caiaphas, and John, and Alexander, and as many as were of the kindred of the high TG Priestcraft.priest, were gathered together at Jerusalem.
And when they had set them in the midst, they asked, By what TG Priesthood, Power of.power, or by what TG Name.name, have ye done this?
Then Peter, filled with the TG Teaching with the Spirit.Holy Ghost, said unto them, Ye rulers of the people, and elders of Israel,
If we this day be examined of the good deed done to the impotent man, by what means he is made whole;

Be it known unto you all, and to all the people of Israel, that by the Acts 3:6.name of Jesus Christ of Nazareth, whom ye crucified, whom God raised from the dead, even by him doth this man stand here before you whole.
This is the TG Cornerstone; Rock.stone which was set at nought of you builders, which is become the head of the corner.

Neither is there TG Gospel.salvation in any other: for there is none other Ps. 9:10; Hosea 13:4; 1 Jn. 2:12; 2 Ne. 25:20; Mosiah 4:8. TG Jesus Christ, Taking the Name of.name under heaven given among men, whereby we must be TG Jesus Christ, Atonement through; Jesus Christ, Savior; Salvation; Salvation, Plan of.saved.

¶ Now when they saw the boldness of Peter and John, and perceived that they were D&C 1:19; 35:13.unlearned and GR ordinary, plain.ignorant men, they marvelled; and they took knowledge of them, that they had been with Jesus.
And beholding the man which was healed standing with them, they could say nothing against it.
But when they had commanded them to go aside out of the council, they conferred among themselves,
Saying, What shall we do to these men? for that indeed a notable Acts 3:9 (6–9).miracle hath been done by them is manifest to all them that dwell in Jerusalem; and we cannot deny it.
But that it spread no further among the people, let us straitly threaten them, that they speak henceforth to no man in this name.
And they called them, and Acts 5:28 (27–28).commanded them not to speak at all nor teach in the name of Jesus.
But Peter and John answered and said unto them, Whether it be right in the sight of God to D&C 3:7.hearken unto you more than unto God, judge ye.
For we cannot but Jer. 20:9; Amos 3:8.speak the things which we have TG Witness.seen and heard.
So when they had further threatened them, they let them go, finding nothing how they might punish them, because of the people: for all men glorified God for that which was done.
For the man was above forty years old, on whom this miracle of healing was shewed.
¶ And being let go, they went to their own company, and reported all that the chief priests and elders had said unto them.
And when they heard that, they lifted up their voice to God with one accord, and said, Lord, thou art God, which hast made heaven, and D&C 14:9.earth, and the sea, and all that in them is:
Who by the mouth of thy servant David hast said, Why did the heathen rage, and the people imagine vain things?
The Ps. 2:2 (1–2).kings of the earth stood up, and the rulers were gathered together against the Lord, and against his Christ.

For of a truth against thy holy child Jesus, whom thou hast TG Anointing; Jesus Christ, Authority of; Jesus Christ, Messiah.anointed, both Herod, and Pontius Pilate, with the Gentiles, and the people of Israel, were gathered together,
For to do whatsoever thy hand and thy counsel determined before to be done.
And now, Lord, behold their threatenings: and grant unto thy servants, that with all boldness they may speak thy word,
By stretching forth thine hand to heal; and that signs and wonders may be done by the name of thy holy child Jesus.

¶ And when they had prayed, the place was shaken where they were TG Assembly for Worship; Meetings.assembled together; and they were all filled with the Acts 2:4 (2–4); D&C 68:4 (3–4).Holy Ghost, and they spake the word of God with boldness.

And the multitude of them that believed were of 1 Cor. 1:10 (10–13); 3 Ne. 11:28 (28–30); Moses 7:18. TG Unity.one heart and of one soul: neither said any of them that GR any.ought of the things which he D&C 49:20.possessed was his own; but they had all things TG Consecration.common.
And with great power gave the apostles 1 Cor. 15:15. TG Witness.witness of the resurrection of the Lord Jesus: and great TG Grace.grace was upon them all.
Neither was there any among them that lacked: for as many as were possessors of lands or houses Acts 5:1 (1–11).sold them, and brought the prices of the things that were sold,
And laid them down at the apostles’ feet: and Acts 6:1; D&C 83:6 (1–6).distribution was made unto every man according as he had Acts 2:45. TG Welfare.need.
And Joses, who by the apostles was surnamed Acts 11:22 (22–24); Gal. 2:1.Barnabas, (which is, being interpreted, The son of consolation,) a Levite, and of the country of Cyprus,
Having land, sold it, and brought the D&C 19:34.money, and laid it at the apostles’ feet.

div.p: Ananias and Sapphira lie to the Lord and lose their lives—The Apostles continue the miracles of Jesus—Peter and John are arrested, an angel delivers them from prison, and they testify of Christ—Gamaliel counsels moderation.

chapter.verse: But a certain man named Ananias, with Sapphira his wife, Acts 4:34 (32, 34–35); D&C 19:26 (25–26).sold a possession,

And TG Selfishness.kept back Josh. 7:21 (1–26); D&C 105:3 (2–10). TG Consecration. part of the price, his wife also being privy to it, and brought a certain TG Welfare.part, and laid it at the apostles’ feet.
But Peter said, Ananias, why hath Satan filled thine heart to TG Honesty.lie to the Holy Ghost, and to TG Selfishness.keep back part of the price of the land?

Whiles it remained, was it not thine own? and after it was sold, was it not in thine own power? why hast thou conceived this thing in thine heart? thou hast not TG Lying.lied unto men, but unto Josh. 7:20.God.
And Ananias hearing these words fell down, and Lev. 10:2.gave up the ghost: and great fear came on all them that heard these things.
And the young men arose, wound him up, and carried him out, and buried him.
And it was about the space of three hours after, when his wife, not knowing what was done, came in.
And Peter answered unto her, Tell me whether ye sold the land for so much? And she said, Yea, for so much.

Then Peter said unto her, How is it that ye have agreed together to TG Test.tempt the Spirit of the Lord? behold, the feet of them which have buried thy husband are at the door, and shall carry thee out.
Then fell she down OR immediately.straightway at his feet, and yielded up the ghost: and the young men came in, and found her dead, and, carrying her forth, buried her by her husband.
And great fear came upon all the church, and upon as many as heard these things.
¶ And by the hands of the apostles were many Heb. 2:4. TG Signs.signs and wonders wrought among the people; (and they were all with one accord in Solomon’s porch.
And of the JST Acts 5:13 … rulers …rest durst no man join himself to them: but the people magnified them.
And believers were the more Acts 2:47.added to the Lord, multitudes both of men and women.)

Insomuch that they brought forth the sick into the streets, and laid them on beds and couches, that at the least the Luke 8:44 (43–48); Acts 19:12 (11–12).shadow of Peter passing by might overshadow some of them.
There came also a multitude out of the cities round about unto Jerusalem, bringing sick folks, and them which were vexed with unclean spirits: and they were TG Heal.healed every one.
¶ Then the high priest rose up, and all they that were with him, (which is the sect of the Sadducees,) and were filled with GR envy, malice.indignation,
And laid their hands on the apostles, and put them in the common prison.
But the Acts 12:7; Moro. 7:29 (29–33). TG Angels.angel of the Lord by night opened the Acts 16:26.prison doors, and brought them forth, and said,
Go, stand and speak in the temple to the people all the John 6:68 (63, 68).words of this life.

And when they heard that, they entered into the temple early in the morning, and taught. But the high priest came, and they that were with him, and called the council together, and all the senate of the children of Israel, and sent to the prison to have them brought.
But when the officers came, and found them not in the prison, they returned, and told,
Saying, The prison truly found we shut with all safety, and the keepers standing without before the doors: but when we had opened, we found no man within.
Now when the high priest and the captain of the temple and the chief priests heard these things, they doubted of them whereunto this would grow.
Then came one and told them, saying, Behold, the men whom ye put in prison are standing in the temple, and teaching the people.
Then went the captain with the officers, and brought them without violence: for they feared the people, lest they should have been stoned.
And when they had brought them, they set them before the council: and the high priest asked them,

Saying, Did not we straitly Acts 4:18 (15–18).command you that ye should not teach in this TG Name.name? and, behold, ye have filled Jerusalem with your doctrine, and intend to bring this man’s Matt. 27:25.blood upon us.
¶ Then Peter and the other apostles answered and said, We ought to Ex. 1:17 (16–17); D&C 30:1. TG Courage; Duty.obey God rather than men.
The God of our fathers raised up Jesus, whom ye slew and TG Jesus Christ, Crucifixion of.hanged on a tree.

Him hath God exalted GR at.with his right hand to be a TG Jesus Christ, Messiah.Prince and a TG Jesus Christ, Atonement through.Saviour, for to give repentance to Israel, and TG Forgive.forgiveness of sins.

And we are his Luke 1:2; John 1:14; Acts 26:16. TG Holy Ghost, Mission of; Witness.witnesses of these things; and so is also the TG Holy Ghost, Source of Testimony.Holy Ghost, whom God hath TG Holy Ghost, Gift of.given to them that obey him.
¶ When they heard that, they were 1 Ne. 16:2.cut to the heart, and took counsel to slay them.
Then stood there up one in the council, a Pharisee, named Acts 22:3.Gamaliel, a doctor of the law, had in reputation among all the people, and commanded to put the apostles forth a little space;
And said unto them, Ye men of Israel, take heed to yourselves what ye intend to do as touching these men.

For before these days rose up Theudas, boasting himself to be somebody; to whom a number of men, about four hundred, joined themselves: who was slain; and all, as many as GR were persuaded by, believed him.obeyed him, were scattered, and brought to nought.
After this man rose up Judas of Galilee in the days of the taxing, and drew away much people after him: he also perished; and all, even as many as obeyed him, were dispersed.
And now I say unto you, Acts 19:35 (35–41).Refrain from these men, and let them alone: for if this counsel or this work be of D&C 3:3; JS—H 1:23 (22–23).men, it will come to nought:
But if it be of God, ye cannot overthrow it; JST Acts 5:39 … be careful, therefore, lest ye be …lest haply ye be found even to Acts 23:9.fight against God.
And to him they agreed: and when they had called the apostles, and beaten them, they commanded that they should not speak in the name of Jesus, and let them go.

¶ And they departed from the presence of the council, Luke 6:23 (22–23); Col. 1:11; 1 Thes. 1:6; 1 Pet. 4:13.rejoicing that they were counted worthy to TG Persecution; Self-Sacrifice; Suffering.suffer TG Shame.shame for his TG Jesus Christ, Taking the Name of.name.
And daily in the temple, and in every house, they ceased not to teach and TG Missionary Work.preach Jesus Christ.

div.p: The Apostles choose seven to assist them—Stephen is tried before the council.

chapter.verse: And in those days, when the number of the disciples was multiplied, there arose a murmuring of the Grecians against the Hebrews, because their TG Widows.widows were TG Apathy; Welfare.neglected in the daily Acts 4:35.ministration.
Then the twelve called the multitude of the disciples unto them, and said, It is not reason that we should leave the word of God, and serve tables.

Wherefore, brethren, look ye out among you seven men of TG Honesty; Priesthood, Qualifying for.honest 1 Tim. 3:7.report, full of the Holy Ghost and TG Holy Ghost, Gifts of.wisdom, whom we may appoint over this D&C 107:68 (14, 68).business.
But we will give ourselves continually to prayer, and to the Mark 16:15 (15–19); D&C 107:8.ministry of the word.

¶ And the saying pleased the whole multitude: and they TG Called of God.chose Stephen, a man full of faith and of the TG Holy Ghost, Gifts of.Holy Ghost, and Acts 21:8.Philip, and Prochorus, and Nicanor, and Timon, and Parmenas, and Nicolas a proselyte of Antioch:
Whom they set before the apostles: and when they had prayed, they TG Church Organization; Hands, Laying on of; Priesthood; Priesthood, Authority.laid their TG Setting Apart.hands on them.

And the word of God increased; and the number of the disciples multiplied in Jerusalem greatly; and a great company of the John 12:42. TG Priest, Aaronic Priesthood. BD Priests.priests were obedient to the faith.
And Stephen, full of faith and TG Priesthood, Power of.power, did great wonders and TG Miracle.miracles among the people.

¶ Then there arose certain of the synagogue, which is called the synagogue of the GR Freed-men.Libertines, and Cyrenians, and Alexandrians, and of them of Cilicia and of Asia, TG Disputations.disputing with Stephen.
And they were not able to resist the Isa. 54:17; Luke 21:15; D&C 100:5 (5–6).wisdom and the spirit by which he spake.
Then they suborned men, which said, We have heard him speak TG Blaspheme.blasphemous words against Moses, and against God.
And they stirred up the people, and the elders, and the scribes, and came upon him, and caught him, and brought him to the council,
And set up TG Slander.false witnesses, which said, This man ceaseth not to speak blasphemous words against this holy place, and the law:
For we have heard him say, that this Jesus of Nazareth shall destroy this place, and shall change the Acts 21:21.customs which Moses delivered us.
And all that sat in the council, looking steadfastly on him, saw his face as it had been the Hel. 5:36.face of an TG Angels.angel.

div.p: Stephen recounts the history of Israel and names Moses as a prototype of Christ—He testifies of the apostasy in Israel—He sees Jesus on the right hand of God—Stephen’s testimony is rejected, and he is stoned to death.

chapter.verse: Then said the high priest, Are these things so?

And he said, Men, brethren, and fathers, hearken; The God of glory appeared unto our father Gen. 12:1; Abr. 2:3 (3–4).Abraham, when he was in Mesopotamia, before he dwelt in Gen. 11:31 (31–32).Charran,
And said unto him, Get thee out of thy Abr. 1:1 (1, 5).country, and from thy kindred, and come into the land which I shall shew thee.
Then came he out of the land of the Gen. 11:31.Chaldæans, and dwelt in Charran: and from thence, when his father was dead, he removed him into this land, wherein ye now dwell.

And he gave him none inheritance in it, no, not so much as to set his foot on: yet he TG Promised Lands.promised that he would give it to him for a possession, and to his seed after him, when as yet he had no child.

And God spake on this wise, That his seed should Gen. 26:3.sojourn in a strange land; and that they should bring them into TG Israel, Bondage of, in Egypt.bondage, and entreat them evil four hundred years.
And the nation to whom they shall be in bondage will I judge, said God: and after that shall they come forth, and serve me in this place.

And he gave him the covenant of TG Circumcision.circumcision: and so Abraham begat Isaac, and circumcised him the eighth day; and Isaac begat Jacob; and Jacob begat the twelve TG Patriarch.patriarchs.
And the patriarchs, moved with TG Envy.envy, sold Alma 46:24.Joseph into Egypt: but God was with him,
And D&C 24:1.delivered him out of all his TG Affliction.afflictions, and gave him favour and wisdom in the sight of Pharaoh king of Egypt; and he made him governor over Egypt and all his house.
Now there came a OR famine. TG Drought; Famine.dearth over all the land of Egypt and Chanaan, and great affliction: and our fathers found no sustenance.
But when Jacob heard that there was Gen. 42:1.corn in Egypt, he sent out our fathers first.
And at the second time Gen. 45:3.Joseph was made known to his brethren; and Joseph’s kindred was made known unto Gen. 47:2.Pharaoh.
Then sent TG Israel, Joseph, People of.Joseph, and called his Gen. 45:13.father TG Israel, Origins of.Jacob to him, and all his kindred, threescore and fifteen souls.
So Jacob went down into Egypt, and died, he, and our fathers,
And were carried over into Sychem, and laid in the sepulchre that Abraham Gen. 23:17.bought for a sum of money of the sons of Emmor the father of Sychem.
But when the time of the promise drew nigh, which God had sworn to Abraham, the people grew and multiplied in Egypt,
Till another Ex. 1:8.king arose, which knew not Joseph.
The same dealt subtilly with our kindred, and evil entreated our fathers, so that they cast out their young children, to the end they might not live.
In which time Moses was born, and was GR comely, beautiful to God.exceeding fair, and nourished up in his father’s house Ex. 2:2.three months:
And when he was cast out, Pharaoh’s daughter took him up, and nourished him for her own Ex. 2:10.son.
And Moses was TG Education.learned in all the wisdom of the Egyptians, and was mighty in words and in deeds.
And when he was full Ex. 7:7; Deut. 31:2 (1–2); 34:7.forty years old, it came into his heart to visit his Ex. 2:11 (11–15); Heb. 11:25 (24–27).brethren the children of Israel.
And seeing one of them suffer wrong, he defended him, and avenged him that was TG Oppression.oppressed, and Ex. 2:12 (11–12).smote the Egyptian:
For he supposed his brethren would have Ex. 2:14; 3:13 (13–15); 4:1.understood how that God by his hand would TG Deliver.deliver them: but they understood not.
And the next day he shewed himself unto them as they strove, and would have set them at one again, saying, Sirs, ye are brethren; why do ye Ex. 2:13. TG Injustice.wrong one to another?
But he that did his neighbour wrong thrust him away, saying, Who made thee a 1 Ne. 17:26 (23–37).ruler and a judge over us?
Wilt thou kill me, as thou diddest the Egyptian yesterday?
Then Ex. 2:15.fled Moses at this saying, and was a stranger in the land of Madian, where he begat two Ex. 2:22; 18:3.sons.
And when forty years were expired, there appeared to him in the wilderness of mount Sina an Ex. 3:2.angel of the Lord in a flame of fire in a bush.
When Moses saw it, he wondered at the OR vision.sight: and as he drew near to behold it, the Ex. 3:4 (3–6).voice of the Lord came unto him,
Saying, I am the Ex. 3:15.God of thy fathers, the God of Abraham, and the God of Isaac, and the God of Jacob. Then Moses trembled, and durst not behold.
Then said the Lord to him, Put off thy shoes from thy feet: for the place where thou standest is holy ground.
I have seen, I have seen the affliction of my people which is in Egypt, and I have heard their groaning, and am come down to deliver them. And now come, I will send thee into Egypt.

This Moses whom they refused, saying, Who made thee a ruler and a judge? the same did God send to be a ruler and a TG Deliver; Jesus Christ, Savior; Salvation.deliverer by the hand of the angel which appeared to him in the bush.
He Ex. 12:51.brought them out, after that he had shewed wonders and signs in the land of Egypt, and in the Red sea, and in the wilderness forty years.

¶ This is that Moses, which said unto the children of Israel, A Deut. 18:15 (15–19); Acts 3:22 (22–23); JS—H 1:40. TG Jesus Christ, Prophecies about.prophet shall the Lord your God raise up unto you of your brethren, like unto me; him shall ye hear.
This is he, that was in the church in the wilderness with the angel which spake to him in the mount Sina, and with our fathers: who received the lively Rom. 3:2; D&C 90:5.oracles to give unto us:
To whom our fathers would not TG Disobedience.obey, but thrust him from them, and in their hearts turned back again into Egypt,
Saying unto Aaron, Make us Ex. 32:1, 23.gods to go before us: for as for this Moses, which brought us out of the land of Egypt, we wot not what is become of him.
And they made a Ex. 32:4; Hosea 8:5 (5–7).calf in those days, and offered sacrifice unto the idol, and rejoiced in the works of their own hands.

Then God turned, and Ps. 81:12; Rom. 1:24.gave them up to worship the host of heaven; as it is written in the book of the prophets, O ye house of Israel, have ye offered to me slain beasts and sacrifices by the space of forty years in the wilderness?
Yea, ye took up the tabernacle of Moloch, and the star of your god Remphan, figures which ye made to worship them: and I will carry you away beyond Babylon.

Our fathers had the tabernacle of witness in the wilderness, as he had appointed, speaking unto Moses, that he should make it according to the 1 Chr. 28:19 (11, 19); Heb. 8:5.fashion that he had seen.
Which also our fathers that came after brought in with IE Joshua. Heb. 4:8.Jesus into the possession of the Gentiles, whom God drave out before the face of our fathers, unto the days of David;
Who found favour before God, and desired to find a tabernacle for the God of Jacob.
But Solomon built him an house.
Howbeit the most High Acts 17:24.dwelleth not in temples made with hands; as saith the prophet,
Heaven is my TG Kingdom of God, in Heaven.throne, and earth is my footstool: what house will ye build me? saith the Lord: or what is the place of my rest?
Hath not my hand TG Jesus Christ, Creator.made all these things?

¶ Ye TG Stiffnecked.stiffnecked and Jer. 6:10; Rom. 2:29 (28–29).uncircumcised in heart and ears, ye do always TG Rebellion.resist the Neh. 9:30.Holy Ghost: as your Mal. 3:7; Matt. 23:32; Hel. 13:25 (25–29).fathers did, so do ye.

Which of the prophets have not your fathers TG Apostasy of Israel; Prophets, Rejection of.persecuted? and they have slain them which shewed before of the coming of the Just One; of whom ye have been now the betrayers and murderers:
Who have received the law by the disposition of Moses 5:58.angels, and have not TG Disobedience.kept it.
¶ When they heard these things, they were 1 Ne. 16:2 (1–4).cut to the heart, and they gnashed on him with their teeth.

But he, being full of the TG Holy Ghost, Comforter; Holy Ghost, Gifts of.Holy Ghost, looked up steadfastly into heaven, and saw the TG Jesus Christ, Glory of.glory of TG Godhead.God, and D&C 137:3.Jesus TG Jesus Christ, Appearances, Postmortal.standing on the Heb. 1:3. TG Jesus Christ, Relationships with the Father.right hand of God,

And said, Behold, I TG God, Presence of; God, Privilege of Seeing.see the heavens Ezek. 1:1; D&C 50:45.opened, and the TG Jesus Christ, Son of Man.Son of man standing on the right TG God, Body of, Corporeal Nature.hand of TG God, Manifestations of; God the Father, Elohim.God.
Then they cried out with a loud voice, and stopped their ears, and ran upon him with one accord,

And cast him out of the city, and stoned him: and the witnesses laid down their Acts 22:20.clothes at a The Greek word used identifies a man who is younger than forty years of age.young man’s feet, whose name was Saul.

And they TG Persecution.stoned TG Martyrdom.Stephen, JST Acts 7:59 … and he, calling upon God, said …calling upon 3 Ne. 19:22 (18–36). God , and saying, Lord Jesus, receive my TG Man, a Spirit Child of Heavenly Father; Spirits, Disembodied.spirit.

And he kneeled down, and Luke 6:28; 23:34 (33–34).cried with a loud voice, Matt. 5:44 (44–47).Lord, lay not this sin to their 2 Tim. 4:16; 3 Ne. 12:44.charge. And when he had said this, he fell asleep.

div.p: Saul persecutes the Church—Philip’s ministry in Samaria is described—Philip performs miracles and baptizes men and women—Peter and John come to Samaria and confer the gift of the Holy Ghost by the laying on of hands—Simon seeks to buy this gift and is rebuked by Peter—Philip preaches about Christ and baptizes an Ethiopian eunuch.

chapter.verse: And Saul was consenting unto his death. And at that time there was a great TG Persecution.persecution against the TG Church.church which was at Jerusalem; and they were all scattered abroad throughout the regions of Judæa and Samaria, except the apostles.
And devout men carried Stephen to his burial, and made great TG Mourning.lamentation over him.
As for Saul, he made Acts 22:4; 1 Cor. 15:9; 1 Tim. 1:13; Mosiah 27:10 (9–11).havoc of the church, entering into every house, and haling men and women committed them to Acts 22:19; 4 Ne. 1:30.prison.
Therefore they that were scattered abroad went every where preaching the word.
Then Philip went down to the city of Acts 1:8.Samaria, and preached Christ unto them.
And the people with one accord gave heed unto those things which Philip TG Counsel.spake, hearing and seeing the miracles which he did.

For TG Spirits, Evil or Unclean.unclean spirits, crying with loud voice, came out of many that were possessed with them: and many GR paralytic and lame.taken with palsies, and that were lame, were 3 Ne. 7:22.healed.
And there was great joy in that city.

But there was a certain man, called Simon, which beforetime in the same city used TG Sorcery.sorcery, and TG Superstitions.bewitched the people of Samaria, giving out that himself was some 2 Ne. 26:29.great one:
To whom they all gave heed, from the least to the greatest, saying, This man is the great power of God.
And to him they had regard, because that of long time he had bewitched them with sorceries.

But when they believed Philip TG Missionary Work; Preaching.preaching the things concerning the kingdom of God, and the TG Name.name of Jesus Christ, they were TG Baptism, Essential.baptized, both men and women.
Then Simon himself believed also: and when he was baptized, he continued with Philip, and OR was amazed, astonished.wondered, beholding the miracles and signs which were done.
Now when the apostles which were at Jerusalem heard that Samaria had received the Acts 11:1 (1–18).word of God, they sent unto them Peter and John:
Who, when they were come down, prayed for them, that they might receive the Holy Ghost:
(For as yet he was fallen upon none of them: only they were baptized in the name of the Lord Jesus.)
Then TG Hands, Laying on of.laid they their hands on them, and they received the Acts 2:38.Holy Ghost.
And when Simon saw that through TG Priesthood, Authority.laying on of the apostles’ hands the Holy Ghost was given, he offered them money,
Saying, Give me also this TG Priesthood, Power of; Selfishness; Unrighteous Dominion.power, that on whomsoever I lay hands, he may receive the Holy Ghost.
But Peter said unto him, Thy money perish with thee, because thou hast thought that the TG God, Gifts of.gift of God may be TG Bribe.purchased with money.
Thou hast neither part nor lot in this matter: for thy heart is not D&C 49:2.right in the sight of God.
Repent therefore of this thy TG Wickedness.wickedness, and pray God, if perhaps the TG Motivations.thought of thine 3 Ne. 12:29.heart may be forgiven thee.
For I perceive that thou art in the Alma 41:11; Morm. 8:31.gall of bitterness, and in the TG Bondage, Spiritual.bond of iniquity.
Then answered Simon, and said, Pray ye to the Lord for me, that none of these things which ye have spoken come upon me.
And they, when they had testified and preached the word of the Lord, returned to Jerusalem, and preached the gospel in many villages of the Samaritans.
And the TG Angels.angel of the Lord spake unto Philip, saying, Arise, and go toward the south unto the way that goeth down from Jerusalem unto Gaza, which is desert.

And he arose and went: and, behold, a man of Ethiopia, an eunuch of great authority under Candace queen of the Ethiopians, who had the charge of all her treasure, and had come to Jerusalem for to John 12:20.worship,
Was returning, and sitting in his chariot read Esaias the prophet.
Then the TG Holy Ghost, Gifts of.Spirit said unto Philip, Go near, and join thyself to this chariot.
And Philip ran thither to him, and heard him read the prophet Esaias, and said, Understandest thou what thou readest?
And he said, How can I, except some man should guide me? And he desired Philip that he would come up and sit with him.

The place of the scripture which he read was this, He was led as a sheep to the slaughter; and like a TG Jesus Christ, Lamb of God; Jesus Christ, Trials of.lamb dumb before his shearer, so opened he not his mouth:
In his humiliation his judgment was taken away: and who shall declare his generation? for his life is taken from the earth.
And the eunuch answered Philip, and said, I pray thee, of whom speaketh the prophet this? of himself, or of some other man?
Then Philip opened his mouth, and began at the same scripture, and preached unto him Gal. 3:14 (13–14).Jesus.
And as they went on their way, they came unto a certain water: and the eunuch said, See, here is water; what doth hinder me to be baptized?
And Philip said, If thou TG Baptism, Qualifications for.believest with all thine heart, thou mayest. And he answered and said, I believe that Jesus Christ is the Son of God.
And he commanded the chariot to stand still: and they went down both into the water, both Philip and the eunuch; and he TG Baptism; Baptism, Immersion.baptized him.
And when they were come up out of the water, the TG God, Spirit of.Spirit of the Lord caught away Philip, that the eunuch saw him no more: and he went on his way rejoicing.
But Philip was found at Azotus: and passing through he preached in all the cities, till he came to Acts 21:8.Cæsarea.

div.p: Jesus appears to Saul—Saul is a chosen vessel—Ananias restores Saul’s sight—Saul is baptized and begins his ministry—Peter heals Æneas and raises Dorcas from death.

chapter.verse: And Saul, yet breathing out Acts 26:10.threatenings and slaughter against the disciples of the Lord, went unto the high priest,
And desired of him Acts 22:5.letters to Damascus to the synagogues, that if he found any of this way, whether they were men or women, he might bring them bound unto Jerusalem.
And as he journeyed, he came near Damascus: and suddenly there shined round about him a TG Light [noun].light from heaven:
And he Ezek. 1:28; Ether 3:6 (6–8); Moses 1:9.fell to the earth, and heard a voice saying unto him, Acts 22:7 (6–16); 26:14 (9–17).Saul, Saul, why persecutest thou me?
And he said, Who art thou, Lord? And the Lord said, I am TG Jesus Christ, Appearances, Postmortal.Jesus whom thou persecutest: it is hard for thee to D&C 121:38.kick against the pricks.
And he trembling and astonished said, Lord, TG Conversion.what wilt thou have me to do? And the Lord said unto him, Arise, and go into the city, and it shall be told thee what thou must do.

JST Acts 9:7 And they who were journeying with him saw indeed the light, and were afraid; but they heard not the voice of him who spake to him.And the men which journeyed with him stood speechless, hearing a Acts 22:9.voice, but seeing Dan. 10:7; Alma 36:11 (6–11).no man.
And Saul arose from the earth; and when his eyes were opened, he saw no man: but they led him by the hand, and brought him into Damascus.
And he was three days without sight, and neither did eat nor drink.
¶ And there was a certain disciple at Damascus, named Acts 22:12.Ananias; and to him said the Lord in a TG Jesus Christ, Appearances, Postmortal.vision, Ananias. And he said, Behold, I am here, Lord.
And the Lord said unto him, Arise, and go into the street which is called Straight, and inquire in the house of Judas for one called Saul, of Tarsus: for, behold, he prayeth,
And hath seen in a vision a man named Ananias coming in, and putting his hand on him, that he might receive his sight.
Then Ananias answered, Lord, I have heard by many of this man, how much Acts 26:10.evil he hath done to thy saints at Jerusalem:
And here he hath authority from the chief priests to bind all that call on thy name.

But the Lord said unto him, Go thy way: for he is a 1 Thes. 3:3.chosen vessel unto me, to Acts 22:15; 26:16.bear my TG Name.name before the Rom. 15:10 (8–21); 1 Tim. 2:7.Gentiles, and Ps. 119:46; Matt. 10:18; D&C 1:23; 124:3 (3, 16, 107). TG Kings, Earthly.kings, and the children of Israel:
For I will shew him how great things he must TG Self-Sacrifice.suffer for my name’s sake.

And Ananias went his way, and entered into the house; and putting his TG Administrations to the Sick; Hands, Laying on of.hands on him said, Brother Saul, the Lord, even Jesus, that 1 Cor. 9:1.appeared unto thee in the way as thou camest, hath TG Called of God; Delegation of Responsibility.sent me, that thou mightest receive thy sight, and be filled with the TG Holy Ghost, Gift of.Holy Ghost.
And immediately there fell from his eyes as it had been scales: and he received TG Sight.sight forthwith, and arose, and was TG Baptism, Essential.baptized.
And when he had received meat, he was strengthened. Then was Saul certain days with the disciples which were at Damascus.
And straightway he preached Christ in the synagogues, that he is the Son of God.

But all that heard him were amazed, and said; Is not this he that Gal. 1:13.destroyed them which called on this name in Jerusalem, and came hither for that intent, that he might bring them Acts 26:10.bound unto the chief priests?
But Saul increased the more in strength, and confounded the Jews which dwelt at Damascus, proving that this is very Christ.
¶ And after that many days were fulfilled, the Jews took counsel to 2 Cor. 11:32.kill him:
But their GR plot, conspiracy was known.laying await was known of Saul. And they watched the gates day and night to kill him.
Then the disciples took him by night, and let him down by the wall in a Josh. 2:15.basket.
And when Saul was come to Gal. 1:18 (15–18).Jerusalem, he assayed to join himself to the disciples: but they were all afraid of him, and believed not that he was a disciple.

But Barnabas took him, and brought him to the apostles, and declared unto them how he had TG Jesus Christ, Appearances, Postmortal.seen the Lord in the way, and that he had spoken to him, and how he had preached boldly at Damascus in the name of Jesus.
And he was with them coming in and going out at Jerusalem.
And he spake boldly in the name of the Lord Jesus, and TG Disputations.disputed against the Grecians: but they went about to slay him.
Which when the brethren knew, they brought him down to Cæsarea, and sent him forth to Tarsus.

Then had the churches rest throughout all Judæa and Galilee and Samaria, and were TG Edification.edified; and walking in the fear of the Lord, and in the TG Comfort; Holy Ghost, Comforter; Holy Ghost, Gifts of.comfort of the Holy Ghost, were multiplied.
¶ And it came to pass, as Peter passed throughout all quarters, he came down also to the TG Saints.saints which dwelt at Lydda.
And there he found a certain man named Æneas, which had kept his bed eight years, and was GR paralyzed.sick of the palsy.
And Peter said unto him, Æneas, Jesus Christ maketh thee whole: arise, and make thy bed. And he arose immediately.
And all that dwelt at Lydda and Saron saw him, and turned to the Lord.
¶ Now there was at Joppa a certain disciple named Tabitha, which by interpretation is called Dorcas: this TG Woman.woman was full of good works and almsdeeds which she did.
And it came to pass in those days, that she was sick, and died: whom when they had washed, they laid her in an upper chamber.
And forasmuch as Lydda was nigh to Joppa, and the disciples had heard that Peter was there, they sent unto him two men, desiring him that he would not delay to come to them.

Then Peter arose and went with them. When he was come, they brought him into the upper chamber: and all the widows stood by him weeping, and shewing the coats and garments which Dorcas made, while she was with them.
But Peter put them all forth, and kneeled down, and prayed; and turning him to the body said, Tabitha, TG Death, Power over.arise. And she opened her eyes: and when she saw Peter, she sat up.
And he gave her his hand, and lifted her up, and when he had called the TG Saints.saints and widows, presented her John 14:12 (12–14).alive.
And it was known throughout all Joppa; and many believed in the Lord.
And it came to pass, that he tarried many days in Joppa with one Simon a tanner.

div.p: An angel ministers to Cornelius—Peter, in a vision, is commanded to take the gospel to the Gentiles—The gospel is taught by witnesses—The Holy Ghost falls upon the Gentiles.

chapter.verse: There was a certain man in Cæsarea called Cornelius, a centurion of the band called the Italian band,
A devout man, and one that feared God with all his house, which gave much TG Almsgiving.alms to the people, and prayed to God alway.
He saw in a TG Vision.vision GR clearly, distinctly.evidently about the ninth hour of the day an 2 Ne. 32:3; Moro. 7:22 (22–38).angel of God coming in to him, and saying unto him, Cornelius.
And when he looked on him, he was afraid, and said, What is it, Lord? And he said unto him, Thy prayers and thine alms are come up for a memorial before God.
And now send men to Joppa, and call for one Simon, whose surname is Peter:
He lodgeth with one Simon a tanner, whose house is by the sea side: he shall tell thee what thou oughtest to do.
And when the angel which spake unto Cornelius was departed, he called two of his household servants, and a devout soldier of them that waited on him continually;
And when he had declared all these things unto them, he sent them to Joppa.
¶ On the morrow, as they went on their journey, and drew nigh unto the city, Peter went up upon the 1 Sam. 9:25 (25–27).housetop to pray about the sixth hour:
And he became very hungry, and would have eaten: but while they made ready, he fell into a Acts 22:17.trance,
And saw D&C 107:19.heaven opened, and a certain vessel descending unto him, as it had been a great sheet GR bound.knit at the four corners, and let down to the earth:
Wherein were all manner of fourfooted beasts of the earth, and wild beasts, and creeping things, and fowls of the air.
And there came a voice to him, Rise, Peter; kill, and eat.
But Peter said, Not so, Lord; for I have never eaten any thing that is common or Lev. 11:2 (2–47).unclean.
And the voice spake unto him again the second time, What God hath Lev. 20:25. TG Cleanliness; Purification.cleansed, that call not thou Acts 11:9; 1 Ne. 17:35 (32–41).common.
This was done thrice: and the vessel was received up again into heaven.
Now while Peter doubted in himself what this vision which he had seen should mean, behold, the men which were sent from Cornelius had made inquiry for Simon’s house, and stood before the gate,
And called, and asked whether Simon, which was surnamed Peter, were lodged there.
¶ While Peter thought on the vision, the TG Holy Ghost, Gifts of.Spirit said unto him, Behold, three men seek thee.
Arise therefore, and get thee down, and Acts 15:7.go with them, doubting nothing: for I have sent them.
Then Peter went down to the men which were sent unto him from Cornelius; and said, Behold, I am he whom ye seek: what is the cause wherefore ye are come?

And they said, Cornelius the centurion, a just man, and one that feareth God, and of good report among all the nation of the Jews, was TG Warn.warned from God by an holy angel to send for thee into his house, and to hear words of thee.
Then called he them in, and lodged them. And on the morrow Peter went away with them, and certain brethren from Joppa accompanied him.
And the morrow after they entered into Cæsarea. And Cornelius waited for them, and had called together his kinsmen and near friends.
And as Peter was coming in, Cornelius met him, and fell down at his feet, and worshipped him.
But Peter took him up, saying, Stand up; I myself also am a Rev. 19:10; D&C 20:19.man.
And as he talked with him, he went in, and found many that were come together.

And he said unto them, Ye know how that it is an unlawful thing for a man that is a Jew to keep company, or come unto one of another nation; but God hath shewed me that I should not call any man 2 Ne. 26:33; Hel. 3:28 (27–30).common or TG Uncleanness.unclean.
Therefore came I unto you without gainsaying, as soon as I was sent for: I ask therefore for what intent ye have sent for me?
And Cornelius said, Four days ago I was TG Fast, Fasting.fasting until this hour; and at the ninth hour I prayed in my house, and, behold, a TG Angels.man stood before me in bright clothing,
And said, Cornelius, thy prayer is heard, and thine alms are had in remembrance in the sight of God.
Send therefore to Joppa, and call hither Simon, whose surname is Peter; he is lodged in the house of one Simon a tanner by the sea side: who, when he cometh, shall speak unto thee.
Immediately therefore I sent to thee; and thou hast well done that thou art come. Now therefore are we all here present before God, to hear all things that are commanded thee of God.
¶ Then Peter opened his mouth, and said, Of a truth I perceive that God is no Acts 15:9; 17:26 (25–26); Rom. 2:11; 1 Ne. 17:35; D&C 1:35; 38:16. TG Judgment.respecter of persons:
But in every Rom. 10:12 (12–13); Alma 26:37 (36–37). TG Nations.nation he that TG Reverence.feareth him, and TG Good Works.worketh TG Righteousness.righteousness, is 1 Sam. 2:30.accepted with him.
The 3 Ne. 27:13 (13–22).word which God sent unto the children of Israel, preaching peace by Jesus Christ: (he is Lord of all:)
That word, I say, ye know, which was published throughout all Judæa, and began from Galilee, after the baptism which John preached;

How God TG Anointing; Jesus Christ, Messiah.anointed Jesus of Nazareth with the TG Holy Ghost, Gifts of.Holy Ghost and with power: who went about doing good, and healing all that were TG Oppression.oppressed of the devil; for Ex. 8:19; Luke 11:20; John 3:2; Acts 2:22.God was with him.
And we are Acts 13:31 (29–33). TG Witness.witnesses of all things which he did both in the land of the Jews, and in Jerusalem; whom they slew and hanged on a tree:
Him God Acts 3:15 (15, 26); 17:31. TG Jesus Christ, Resurrection; Resurrection.raised up the third day, and TG Jesus Christ, Appearances, Postmortal.shewed him openly;
Ether 12:7.Not to all the people, but unto witnesses chosen before of God, even to us, who did eat and drink with him after he rose from the dead.

And he commanded us to TG Missionary Work.preach unto the people, and to testify that it is he which was TG Jesus Christ, Authority of; Priesthood, Authority.ordained of God to be the TG Jesus Christ, Judge.Judge of quick and dead.
To him give all the prophets TG Prophets, Mission of.witness, that through his TG Name.name whosoever TG Faith.believeth in him shall receive TG Remission of Sins.remission of sins.
¶ While Peter yet spake these words, the Holy Ghost fell on all them which heard the word.

And they of the circumcision which believed were astonished, as many as came with Peter, because that on the Matt. 8:11 (11–12); Luke 13:29 (28–30); 2 Ne. 10:18 (9–18); D&C 45:9 (7–30). TG Gentiles.Gentiles also was poured out the TG Holy Ghost, Gift of.gift of the Holy Ghost.
For they heard them speak with TG Holy Ghost, Gifts of.tongues, and magnify God. Then answered Peter,
Can any man forbid water, that these should not be baptized, which have received the TG Holy Ghost, Baptism of.Holy Ghost as well as we?
And he commanded them to be TG Baptism, Essential.baptized in the name of the Lord. Then prayed they him to tarry certain days.

div.p: God grants the gift of repentance to the Gentiles—The disciples are first called Christians at Antioch—The Church is guided by revelation.

chapter.verse: And the apostles and brethren that were in Judæa heard that the D&C 109:60.Gentiles had also received the Acts 8:14 (14–20).word of God.
And when Peter was come up to Jerusalem, they that were of the TG Circumcision.circumcision TG Contention.contended with him,
Saying, Thou wentest in to men uncircumcised, and didst eat with them.
But Peter rehearsed the matter from the beginning, and expounded it by order unto them, saying,
I was in the city of Joppa praying: and in a Acts 22:17.trance I saw a vision, A certain vessel descend, as it had been a great sheet, let down from heaven by four corners; and it came even to me:
Upon the which when I had fastened mine eyes, I considered, and saw fourfooted beasts of the earth, and wild beasts, and creeping things, and fowls of the air.
And I heard a voice saying unto me, Arise, Peter; slay and eat.
But I said, Not so, Lord: for nothing common or unclean hath at any time entered into my mouth.
But the voice answered me again from heaven, What God hath TG Purification; Uncleanness.cleansed, that call not thou Acts 10:15 (3, 15).common.
And this was done three times: and all were drawn up again into heaven.
And, behold, immediately there were three men already come unto the house where I was, sent from Cæsarea unto me.
And the Spirit bade me go with them, nothing doubting. Moreover these six brethren accompanied me, and we entered into the man’s house:
And he shewed us how he had seen an angel in his house, which stood and said unto him, Send men to Joppa, and call for Simon, whose surname is Peter;
Who shall tell thee words, whereby thou and all thy house shall be saved.
And as I began to speak, the Holy Ghost fell on them, as on us at the beginning.
Then remembered I the word of the Lord, how that he said, John indeed baptized with water; but ye shall be baptized with the Holy Ghost.
Forasmuch then as God gave them the like TG Holy Ghost, Gift of; Holy Ghost, Gifts of.gift as he did unto us, who believed on the Lord Jesus Christ; what was I, that I could withstand God?

When they heard these things, they held their peace, and glorified God, saying, Then hath God also to the Mark 16:15 (15–16); Acts 21:19; D&C 84:62 (62–64).Gentiles granted TG Repent.repentance unto life.

¶ Now they which were scattered abroad upon the TG Persecution.persecution that arose about Stephen travelled as far as Phenice, and Cyprus, and Antioch, preaching the word to none but unto the Jews only.
And some of them were men of Cyprus and Cyrene, which, when they were come to Antioch, spake unto the Grecians, preaching the Lord Jesus.
And the hand of the Lord was with them: and a great number believed, and turned unto the Lord.
¶ Then tidings of these things came unto the ears of the church which was in Jerusalem: and they sent forth Acts 4:36.Barnabas, that he should go as far as Antioch.
Who, when he came, and had seen the grace of God, was glad, and exhorted them all, that with purpose of heart they would cleave unto the Lord.
For he was a good man, and full of the Holy Ghost and of faith: and much people was added unto the Lord.
Then departed Barnabas to Tarsus, for to seek Saul:

And when he had found him, he brought him unto Antioch. And it came to pass, that a whole year they TG Assembly for Worship; Meetings.assembled themselves with the church, and taught much people. And the disciples were called Acts 26:28; 1 Pet. 4:16; Mosiah 5:8 (8–11); Alma 46:15 (13–15). TG Jesus Christ, Taking the Name of.Christians first in Acts 14:26.Antioch.
¶ And in these days came Acts 13:1 (1–13).prophets from Jerusalem unto Antioch.

And there stood up one of them named Agabus, and signified by the Acts 2:18; 21:11 (4, 10–11).Spirit that there should be great OR famine. TG Drought.dearth throughout all the world: which came to pass in the days of Claudius Cæsar.
Then the disciples, every man according to his ability, determined to send TG Almsgiving; Welfare.relief unto the brethren which dwelt in Judæa:
Which also they did, and sent it to the TG Elder, Melchizedek Priesthood.elders by the hands of Barnabas and Saul.

div.p: The martyrdom of James is described—An angel frees Peter from prison—The Lord slays Herod by disease—The Church grows.

chapter.verse: Now about that time Herod the king stretched forth his hands to vex certain of the church.
And he TG Tyranny.killed TG Martyrdom.James the brother of John with the sword.
And because he saw it pleased the Jews, he proceeded further to take Peter also. (Then were the days of TG Bread, Unleavened.unleavened bread.)

And when he had apprehended him, he put him in Alma 14:27 (22–29); Hel. 5:21 (21–50).prison, and delivered him to four GR squads; i.e., detachments of four men each.quaternions of soldiers to keep him; intending after IE Passover.Easter to bring him forth to the people.
Peter therefore was kept in prison: but prayer was made without ceasing of the church unto God for him.
And when Herod would have brought him forth, the same night Peter was sleeping between two soldiers, bound with two chains: and the keepers before the door kept the prison.

And, behold, the Acts 5:19.angel of the Lord came upon him, and a light shined in the prison: and he smote Peter on the side, and raised him up, saying, Arise up quickly. And his chains fell off from his hands.
And the angel said unto him, Gird thyself, and bind on thy sandals. And so he did. And he saith unto him, Cast thy garment about thee, and follow me.
And he went out, and followed him; and OR knew.wist not that it was true which was done by the angel; but thought he saw a vision.

When they were past the first and the second ward, they came unto the iron gate that leadeth unto the city; which opened to them of his own accord: and they went out, and passed on through one street; and forthwith the angel departed from him.

And when Peter was come to himself, he said, Now I know of a surety, that the Lord hath sent his angel, and hath delivered me out of the hand of Herod, and from all the expectation of the people of the Jews.
And when he had considered the thing, he came to the house of Mary the mother of John, whose surname was Mark; where many were gathered together praying.
And as Peter knocked at the door of the gate, a damsel came to hearken, named Rhoda.
And when she knew Peter’s voice, she opened not the gate for gladness, but ran in, and told how Peter stood before the gate.
And they said unto her, Thou art mad. But she constantly affirmed that it was even so. Then said they, It is his angel.
But Peter continued knocking: and when they had opened the door, and saw him, they were astonished.

But he, Acts 21:40.beckoning unto them with the hand to hold their peace, declared unto them how the Lord had brought him out of the prison. And he said, Go shew these things unto James, and to the brethren. And he departed, and went into another place.
Now as soon as it was day, there was no small stir among the soldiers, what was become of Peter.
And when Herod had sought for him, and found him not, he examined the keepers, and commanded that they should be put to TG Persecution.death. And he went down from Judæa to Cæsarea, and there abode.

¶ And Herod was highly displeased with them of Tyre and Sidon: but they came with one accord to him, and, having made Blastus the king’s chamberlain their friend, desired peace; because their country was nourished by the king’s country.
And upon a set day Herod, arrayed in royal apparel, sat upon his throne, and made an oration unto them.
And the people gave a shout, saying, It is the voice of a god, and not of a man.
And immediately the TG Angels.angel of the Lord smote him, because he gave not God the glory: and he was eaten of worms, and gave up the ghost.
¶ But the word of God grew and multiplied.
And Barnabas and Saul returned from Jerusalem, when they had fulfilled their Acts 15:36 (36–37).ministry, and took with them John, whose surname was Mark.

div.p: Saul and Barnabas are called to missionary service—Saul, now called Paul, curses a sorcerer—Christ is a descendant of David—Paul offers the gospel to Israel, then to the Gentiles.

chapter.verse: Now there were in the church that was at Antioch certain Acts 11:27. TG Church Organization.prophets and teachers; as Barnabas, and Simeon that was called Niger, and Lucius of Cyrene, and Manaen, which had been brought up with Herod the tetrarch, and Saul.

As they ministered to the Lord, and Luke 5:35. TG Fast, Fasting.fasted, the TG Holy Ghost, Gifts of.Holy Ghost said, TG Setting Apart.Separate me Barnabas and Saul for the work whereunto I have TG Called of God.called them.
And when they had fasted and prayed, and TG Authority; Priesthood, Authority.laid their TG Hands, Laying on of; Priesthood, Ordination.hands on them, they sent them away.
¶ So they, being sent forth by the Holy Ghost, departed unto Seleucia; and from thence they sailed to Cyprus.
And when they were at Salamis, they preached the word of God in the synagogues of the Jews: and they had also John to their minister.
And when they had gone through the isle unto Paphos, they found a certain TG Sorcery.sorcerer, a false prophet, a Jew, whose name was Bar-jesus:
Which was with the deputy of the country, Sergius Paulus, a TG Prudence.prudent man; who called for Barnabas and Saul, and desired to hear the word of God.
But Elymas the sorcerer (for so is his name by interpretation) withstood them, seeking to turn away the deputy from the faith.
Then Saul, (who also is called Paul,) filled with the TG Holy Ghost, Gifts of.Holy Ghost, set his eyes on him,

And said, O full of all subtilty and all mischief, thou 1 Jn. 3:10 (10–18); Alma 11:23.child of the devil, thou TG Enemies.enemy of all TG Righteousness.righteousness, wilt thou not cease to pervert the right ways of the Lord?

And now, behold, the hand of the Lord is upon thee, and thou Gen. 19:11.shalt be Alma 30:49 (48–60).blind, not seeing the sun for a season. And immediately there fell on him a mist and a darkness; and he went about seeking some to lead him by the hand.
Then the deputy, when he saw what was done, believed, being astonished at the doctrine of the Lord.
Now when Paul and his company loosed from Paphos, they came to Perga in Pamphylia: and Acts 15:37 (36–40).John departing from them returned to Jerusalem.
¶ But when they departed from Perga, they came to Antioch in Pisidia, and went into the synagogue on the sabbath day, and sat down.
And after the reading of the law and the prophets the rulers of the synagogue sent unto them, saying, Ye men and brethren, if ye have any word of exhortation for the people, say on.
Then Paul stood up, and beckoning with his hand said, Men of Israel, and ye that Moro. 7:3 (3–4).fear God, give audience.
The God of this people of Israel chose our fathers, and exalted the people when they dwelt as TG Stranger.strangers in the land of Egypt, and with an high arm brought he them out of it.
And about the time of forty years suffered he their manners in the wilderness.
And when he had Num. 21:3 (1–3); Ps. 80:8 (8–10).destroyed seven nations in the TG Israel, Land of.land of Chanaan, he divided their land to them by lot.
And after that he gave unto them judges about the space of four hundred and fifty years, until Samuel the prophet.
And afterward they desired a king: and God gave unto them Saul the son of Cis, a man of the tribe of Benjamin, by the space of forty years.

And when he had removed him, he raised up unto them David to be their king; to whom also he gave testimony, and said, I have found David the son of Jesse, a man after mine own TG Heart.heart, which shall fulfil all my will.
Of this man’s TG Jesus Christ, Davidic Descent of.seed hath God according to his TG Promise.promise raised unto Israel a TG Jesus Christ, Savior.Saviour, Jesus:
When John had first preached before his coming the baptism of repentance to all the people of Israel.
And as John fulfilled his course, he said, Whom think ye that I am? I am not he. But, behold, there cometh one after me, whose shoes of his feet I am not worthy to loose.
Men and brethren, children of the stock of Abr. 2:9 (9–10).Abraham, and whosoever among you feareth God, to you is the word of this salvation sent.
For they that dwell at Jerusalem, and their rulers, because they knew him not, nor yet the voices of the prophets which are read every sabbath day, they have fulfilled them in condemning him.
And though they found no cause of death in him, yet desired they Pilate that he should be Matt. 27:20; Acts 3:13.slain.
And when they had fulfilled all that was written of him, they took him down from the tree, and laid him in a sepulchre.
But God raised him from the dead:
And he was TG Jesus Christ, Appearances, Postmortal.seen many days of them which came up with him from Galilee to Jerusalem, who are his Acts 10:39 (39–43); 22:15 (14–16).witnesses unto the people.
And we declare unto you glad tidings, how that the promise which was made unto the TG Family, Patriarchal.fathers,

God hath fulfilled the same unto us their children, in that he hath raised up Jesus again; as it is also written in the second psalm, Thou art my TG Jesus Christ, Divine Sonship.Son, this day have I begotten thee.
And as concerning that he raised him up from the dead, now no more to return to corruption, he said on this wise, I will give you the Isa. 55:3.sure mercies of David.
Wherefore he saith also in another psalm, Thou shalt not suffer thine Holy One to see corruption.
For David, after he had served his own generation by the will of God, fell on sleep, and was laid unto his fathers, and saw corruption:
But he, whom God TG Jesus Christ, Resurrection.raised again, saw no Acts 2:27 (27, 31).corruption.

¶ Be it known unto you therefore, men and brethren, that through this man is preached unto you the TG Forgive; Jesus Christ, Atonement through; Jesus Christ, Redeemer; Remission of Sins.forgiveness of sins:
And by him all that believe are TG Justification.justified from all things, from which ye could not be Rom. 2:13.justified by the TG Law of Moses.law of Moses.
Beware therefore, lest that come upon you, which is spoken of in the prophets;
Behold, ye despisers, and wonder, and perish: for I work a work in your days, a Hab. 1:5; 3 Ne. 21:9 (9–11).work which ye shall in no wise believe, though a man declare it unto you.
And when the Jews were gone out of the synagogue, the Gentiles besought that these words might be preached to them the next TG Sabbath.sabbath.
Now when the congregation was broken up, many of the Jews and religious Acts 2:10.proselytes followed Paul and Barnabas: who, speaking to them, persuaded them to continue in the grace of God.
¶ And the next sabbath day came almost the whole city together to hear the word of God.
