Saying, Go unto this people, and say, Hearing ye shall hear, and shall not understand; and seeing ye shall see, and not Isa. 6:9.perceive:

For the TG Hardheartedness.heart of this people is waxed gross, and their ears are dull of hearing, and their TG Spiritual Blindness.eyes have they closed; lest they should see with their eyes, and hear with their ears, and understand with their heart, and should be converted, and I should heal them.
Be it known therefore unto you, that the salvation of God is sent unto the Acts 13:46.Gentiles, and that they will hear it.
And when he had said these words, the Jews departed, and had great reasoning among themselves.
And Paul dwelt two whole years in his own hired house, and received all that came in unto him,
Preaching the kingdom of God, and teaching those things which concern the Lord Jesus Christ, with all confidence, no man forbidding him.

div.title: The Epistle of Paul the Apostle
to the

div.p: The gospel is the power of God unto salvation through Jesus Christ—The wrath of God rests on those guilty of murder, homosexual practices, fornication, and other sins if the guilty do not repent.

chapter.verse: Paul, a TG Servant.servant of Jesus Christ, called to be an 1 Cor. 4:9 (1–21); D&C 18:9.apostle, GR set apart. TG Setting Apart.separated unto the gospel of God,
(Which he had promised afore by his prophets in the holy scriptures,)

Concerning his Son The Greek text omits the name and title at this point.Jesus Christ our Lord, which was made of the TG Jesus Christ, Birth of.seed of TG Jesus Christ, Davidic Descent of.David according to the flesh;

And GR appointed, decreed, set forth.declared to be the D&C 10:57; 11:28; 14:9; 45:52.Son of God with TG Jesus Christ, Power of.power, according to the spirit of holiness, by the resurrection GR from the dead of Jesus Christ our Lord.from the dead:

By whom we have received TG Grace.grace and apostleship, JST Rom. 1:5–6 … through obedience, and faith in his name, to preach the gospel among all nations; among whom ye also are called of Jesus Christ;for TG Obedience.obedience to the faith among all nations, for his name:
Among whom are ye also the called of Jesus Christ:
To all that be in Rome, beloved of God, called to be TG Saints.saints: Grace to you and peace from God our Father, and the Lord Jesus Christ.
First, I thank my God through Jesus Christ for you all, that your Rom. 16:19.faith is GR proclaimed.spoken of throughout the whole world.
For God is my witness, whom I serve with my spirit in the gospel of his Son, that without ceasing I make mention of you always in my prayers;
Making request, if by any means now at length I might have a prosperous journey by the will of God to come unto you.
For I long to see you, that I may impart unto you some spiritual D&C 46:11 (8–32).gift, to the end ye may be established;
That is, that I may be TG Sustaining Church Leaders.comforted together with you by the mutual faith both of you and me.

Now I would not have you ignorant, brethren, that oftentimes I purposed to come unto you, (but was GR hindered, restrained, prevented. Rom. 15:22.let hitherto,) that I might have some D&C 111:2 (2–11).fruit among you also, even as among other Gentiles.
I am debtor both to the Greeks, and to the Acts 28:2.Barbarians; both to the wise, and to the unwise.
So, as much as in me is, I am TG Commitment.ready to preach the gospel to you that are at Rome also.

For I am not Ps. 119:46; Matt. 10:33 (32–33); 2 Tim. 1:12; 2:12 (10–15); 2 Ne. 31:14 (12–21); D&C 101:5 (1–5). TG Loyalty; Shame.ashamed of the TG Gospel.gospel of Christ: for it is the 1 Cor. 1:18; 1 Pet. 1:5; D&C 68:4. TG Jesus Christ, Power of.power of God unto 1 Cor. 15:2. TG Salvation.salvation to every one that believeth; to the Jew first, and also to the Greek.

For therein is the Ps. 4:1; 2 Cor. 5:21. TG God, the Standard of Righteousness.righteousness of God revealed JST Rom. 1:17 … through faith on his name; as it is written …from faith to faith: as it is written, The just shall live by TG Faith.faith.

For the TG God, Indignation of.wrath of God is revealed from heaven against all ungodliness and unrighteousness of men, JST Rom. 1:18 … who love not the truth, but remain in unrighteousness,who IE restrain the truth by unrighteousness.hold the truth in TG Unrighteous Dominion.unrighteousness;
Because that which may be known of God is manifest GR among, within.in them; for God hath shewed it unto them.

For the Acts 14:17.invisible things of him from the creation of the world are clearly seen, being understood by the things that are made, even his TG God, Eternal Nature of.eternal TG Jesus Christ, Power of.power and Godhead; so that they are without excuse:

Because that, when they knew God, they glorified him not as God, neither were TG Ingratitude.thankful; but became GR corrupt in their reasonings, deliberations. 2 Kgs. 17:15.vain in their imaginations, and their Isa. 44:20; Hosea 4:12.foolish heart was TG Darkness, Spiritual.darkened.
Professing themselves to be 2 Ne. 9:28. TG Learn.wise, they became fools,

And changed the glory of the GR incorruptible, immortal.uncorruptible God into an Ex. 32:4.image made like to GR (also) perishable.corruptible man, and to birds, and fourfooted beasts, and creeping things.
Wherefore God also Acts 7:42.gave them up to TG Uncleanness.uncleanness through the lusts of their own hearts, to dishonour their own bodies between themselves:
Who changed the truth of God into a lie, and worshipped and served the creature more than the Creator, who is blessed for ever. Amen.

For this cause God GR abandoned, delivered.gave them up unto GR sufferings, passions of dishonor. 2 Tim. 3:3.vile Judg. 19:22.affections: for even their TG Woman.women did change the natural use into that which is against nature:

And likewise also the men, leaving the natural use of the woman, burned in their TG Homosexual Behavior; Lust.lust one toward another; men with men working that which is unseemly, and receiving in themselves that recompence of their error which was meet.

And even as they did not like to GR discern, choose.retain God in their knowledge, God Ps. 81:12.gave them over to a GR worthless, unable to stand test.reprobate mind, to do those things which are not convenient;

Being filled with all unrighteousness, fornication, TG Wickedness.wickedness, covetousness, TG Malice.maliciousness; full of envy, murder, GR strife, discord.debate, TG Deceit.deceit, malignity; whisperers,

GR Slanderers. TG Backbiting.Backbiters, haters of God, GR violent, overbearing.despiteful, TG Pride.proud, TG Boast; Haughtiness.boasters, inventors of evil things, TG Disobedience; Family, Children, Duties of.disobedient to parents,
Without TG Understanding.understanding, covenantbreakers, without natural affection, implacable, unmerciful:

Who knowing the judgment of God, that they which commit such things are worthy of TG Capital Punishment.death, not only do the same, but GR approve of them, sympathize with them.have Jer. 5:31.pleasure in them that do them.

div.p: God will render to every person according to his or her deeds—Both Jews and Gentiles will be judged by gospel laws.

chapter.verse: Therefore thou art inexcusable, O man, whosoever thou art that judgest: for wherein thou TG Judgment.judgest another, thou condemnest thyself; for thou that judgest doest the same things.
But we GR know.are sure that the TG Judgment, the Last.judgment of God is according to truth against them which commit such things.
And thinkest thou this, O man, that Ezek. 16:52.judgest them which do such things, and doest the same, that thou shalt escape the D&C 10:28. TG Judgment, the Last.judgment of God?
Or despisest thou the TG Treasure.riches of his Mosiah 4:20 (19–20).goodness and TG Forbear.forbearance and longsuffering; not knowing that the goodness of God leadeth thee to repentance?

But GR in accordance with.after thy TG Hardheartedness; Stiffnecked.hardness and impenitent heart treasurest up unto thyself wrath against the day of wrath and revelation of the righteous judgment of God;
Who will GR give back, recompense, restore.render to every man TG Accountability.according to his Rom. 11:6 (5–6). TG Good Works.deeds:
To them who by TG Patience.patient D&C 67:13. TG Perseverance.continuance in well doing seek for glory and honour and TG Immortality.immortality, eternal life:
But unto them that are TG Contention.contentious, and do not TG Disobedience.obey the truth, but obey unrighteousness, TG God, Indignation of.indignation and wrath,
Ps. 32:10.Tribulation and TG Sorrow.anguish, upon every soul of man that doeth evil, of the Jew first, and also of the GR Greek.Gentile;
But glory, honour, and TG Peace of God.peace, to every man that TG Work, Value of.worketh good, to the Jew first, and also to the GR Greek.Gentile:
For there is no GR partiality. Deut. 10:17; Acts 10:34; Rom. 10:12 (12–13); Hel. 3:28 (27–30). TG God, Access to.respect of 1 Ne. 17:35 (35–40); 2 Ne. 26:33 (23–33).persons with God.
For as many as have sinned without Luke 12:48. TG Accountability; Law of Moses.law shall also perish without law: and as many as have sinned in the law shall be judged by the law;
(For not the hearers of the law are just before God, but the TG Good Works.doers of the law shall be Acts 13:39 (26–41).justified.
For when the Gentiles, which have not the law, do by nature the things contained in the law, these, having not the law, are a law unto themselves:

GR Who.Which shew the work of the Jer. 31:33 (31–34); D&C 84:46 (44–47).law written in their hearts, their 2 Ne. 32:5. TG Conscience.conscience also bearing D&C 6:23 (22–23).witness, and their thoughts the mean while accusing or else excusing one another;)
In the day when God shall 1 Cor. 4:5. TG Jesus Christ, Judge.judge the secrets of men by Jesus Christ according to my TG Gospel.gospel.
Behold, thou art called a Jew, and restest in the law, and makest thy boast of God,
And knowest his will, and approvest the things that are more excellent, being instructed out of the law;
And art confident that thou thyself art a guide of the blind, a light of them which are in darkness,
An instructor of the foolish, a teacher of babes, which hast the GR system, appearance. 2 Tim. 3:5.form of knowledge and of the truth in the law.
Thou therefore which teachest another, TG Hypocrisy.teachest thou not thyself? thou that 1 Cor. 9:14 (13–14); Alma 39:11 (11–12).preachest a man should not TG Stealing.steal, dost thou steal?
Thou that sayest a man should not commit TG Adulterer.adultery, dost thou commit adultery? thou that abhorrest idols, dost thou GR rob shrines, temples.commit TG Sacrilege.sacrilege?
Thou that makest thy boast of the law, through breaking the law dishonourest thou God?
For the name of God is TG Blaspheme.blasphemed among the Gentiles through you, as it is written.
For Gal. 5:3. TG Circumcision.circumcision verily profiteth, if thou keep the law: but if thou be a breaker of the law, thy circumcision is made uncircumcision.
Therefore if the uncircumcision keep the righteousness of the law, shall not his uncircumcision be counted for circumcision?
And shall not uncircumcision which is by nature, if it fulfil the law, judge thee, who by the letter and circumcision dost transgress the law?
For he is not a Jew, which is one outwardly; neither is that circumcision, which is outward in the flesh:

But he is a Jew, which is one inwardly; and Acts 7:51; 2 Ne. 9:33. TG Circumcision.circumcision is that of the heart, in the Rom. 7:6.spirit, and not in the letter; whose praise is not of men, but of God.

div.p: Man is not justified by the law of Moses—He is justified through righteousness, which comes through faith in Christ, made possible through Christ’s atoning sacrifice.

chapter.verse: JST Rom. 3:1–2 What advantage then hath the Jew over the Gentile? or what profit of circumcision, who is not a Jew from the heart? But he who is a Jew from the heart, I say hath much every way …What GR preeminence.advantage then hath the Jew? or what profit is there of TG Circumcision.circumcision?
Much every way: chiefly, because that unto them were committed the Acts 7:38; 2 Ne. 29:4 (4–6).oracles of God.
For what if some did not believe? shall their 1 Ne. 8:28 (26–28).unbelief make the faith of God without effect?

GR May it not be!God forbid: yea, let God be true, but every man a Alma 30:42 (37–42).liar; as it is written, That thou mightest be Ps. 51:4.justified in thy sayings, and mightest overcome when thou art judged.

JST Rom. 3:5–8 (Appendix).But if our unrighteousness GR recommend.commend the righteousness of God, what shall we say? Is God 2 Ne. 26:7; Alma 42:1 (1, 13–25).unrighteous who taketh vengeance? (I speak as a man)
GR May it not be!God forbid: for then how shall God TG Jesus Christ, Judge.judge the world?
For if the truth of God hath more abounded GR in, by, because of.through my lie unto his glory; why yet am I also judged as a sinner?
And not rather, (as we be slanderously reported, and as some affirm that we say,) Let us do evil, that good may come? whose TG Damnation.damnation is just.
What then? are we better than they? No, in no wise: for we have before proved both Jews and Gentiles, that they are all under sin;
As it is written, There is Ps. 14:2–3; 53:1 (1–3).none TG Man, Natural, Not Spiritually Reborn.righteous, no, not one:
There is none that understandeth, there is none that seeketh after God.
They are all gone out of the D&C 82:6.way, they are together become Luke 17:10 (7–10); Mosiah 2:21 (20–21).unprofitable; there is none that doeth D&C 33:4; 35:12.good, no, not one.
Their Ps. 5:9.throat is an open sepulchre; with their tongues they have used deceit; the Ps. 140:3 (1–13).poison of asps is under their lips:
Whose mouth is full of cursing and Alma 41:11; Morm. 8:31.bitterness:
Their feet are swift to Isa. 59:7 (7–8).shed blood:
Destruction and Mosiah 3:25; Morm. 8:38.misery are in their ways:
And the way of 1 Ne. 20:22 (18–22).peace have they not known:
There is no D&C 10:56 (55–56).fear of God before their eyes.
Now we know that what things soever the law saith, it saith to them who are under the law: that every Ezek. 16:63.mouth may be stopped, and all the world may become TG Guilt.guilty before God.
Therefore by the 2 Ne. 25:23.deeds of the TG Law of Moses.law there shall no flesh be 2 Ne. 2:5 (5–8).justified in his sight: for GR through.by the law is the knowledge of sin.
But now the TG God, the Standard of Righteousness.righteousness of God GR apart from, without intervention of.without the law is manifested, being witnessed by the law and the prophets;
Even the righteousness of God which is by faith of Jesus Christ unto all and upon all them that believe: for there is no difference:
For all have Prov. 20:9; 1 Ne. 10:6. TG Man, Natural, Not Spiritually Reborn; Sin.sinned, and come short of the glory of God;
JST Rom. 3:24 Therefore being justified only by his grace …Being D&C 20:30.justified freely by his TG Grace.grace through the TG Jesus Christ, Redeemer.redemption that is in Christ Jesus:

Whom God hath GR purposed, designed beforehand.set forth to be a GR mercy seat. TG Jesus Christ, Atonement through; Redemption.propitiation through faith in his TG Blood, Symbolism of.blood, to declare his righteousness for the TG Remission of Sins.remission of sins that are past, through the TG Forbear.forbearance of God;
To declare, I say, at this time his righteousness: that he might be TG God, Justice of.just, and the justifier of him which believeth in Jesus.
Where is Alma 26:36 (10–12, 36); D&C 3:4; 84:73.boasting then? It is excluded. By what law? of works? Nay: but by the law of faith.
Therefore we conclude that a man is TG Justification.justified by TG Faith.faith GR apart from, without intervention of.without the deeds of the law.
Is he the 2 Ne. 30:2 (1–2).God of the Jews only? is he not also of the Gentiles? Yes, of the TG Gentiles.Gentiles also:
Seeing it is 1 Tim. 2:5; D&C 121:28.one God, which shall justify the TG Circumcision.circumcision by faith, and uncircumcision through faith.
Do we then make void the law through faith? GR May it not be!God forbid: GR but.yea, we establish the law.

div.p: Abraham’s faith was accounted to him for righteousness—Man is justified by faith, righteous works, and grace.

chapter.verse: What shall we say then that Abraham our father, as pertaining to the flesh, hath found?
JST Rom. 4:2–5 (Appendix).For if Abraham were justified by works, he hath GR ground, reason for boasting. whereof to glory; but not before God.
For what saith the scripture? Abraham See JST Gen. 15:9–12 (Appendix). TG Faith.believed God, and it was counted unto him for TG Righteousness.righteousness.
Now to him that worketh is the GR wage, hire.reward not GR considered a favor but as his due.reckoned of grace, but of debt.
But to him that worketh not, but believeth on him that justifieth the ungodly, his faith is counted for righteousness.
Even as David also describeth the blessedness of the man, unto whom God imputeth righteousness without works,
Saying, Ps. 32:1.Blessed are they whose iniquities are forgiven, and whose sins are covered.
Blessed is the man to whom the Lord will not impute sin.
Cometh this blessedness then upon the circumcision only, or upon the uncircumcision also? for we say that faith was reckoned to Abraham for righteousness.
How was it then reckoned? when he was in circumcision, or in uncircumcision? Not in circumcision, but in uncircumcision.

And he received the TG Signs.sign of TG Circumcision.circumcision, a seal of the righteousness of the faith which he had yet being uncircumcised: that he might be the Abr. 2:10.father of all them that believe, though they be not circumcised; that righteousness might be imputed unto them also:

And the father of circumcision to them who are not of the circumcision only, but who also TG Walking with God.walk in the steps of that faith of our father Abraham, which he had being yet uncircumcised.
For the promise, that he should be the TG Birthright.heir of the world, was not to Abraham, or to his TG Seed of Abraham.seed, through the law, but through the righteousness of faith.
For if they which are of the law be heirs, faith is made void, and the promise made of none effect:
Because the law worketh wrath: for where no Rom. 5:13; 2 Ne. 9:25; Alma 42:17 (12–24).law is, there is no TG Transgress.transgression.

JST Rom. 4:16 Therefore ye are justified of faith and works, through grace, to the end the promise might be sure to all the seed; not to them only who are of the law, but to them also who are of the faith of Abraham; who is the father of us all,Therefore it is of faith, that it might be by TG Grace.grace; to the end the promise might be sure to all the seed; not to that only which is of the law, but to that also which is of the TG Faith.faith of Abraham; who is the TG Family, Patriarchal.father of us all,

(As it is written, I have made thee a father of many nations,) before him whom he believed, even God, who TG God, Power of; Resurrection.quickeneth the dead, and calleth those things which be not as though they were.
Who against hope believed in TG Hope.hope, that he might become the father of many TG Nations.nations, according to that which was spoken, So shall thy TG Seed of Abraham.seed be.
And being not weak in faith, he considered not his own body now dead, when he was about an hundred years TG Old Age.old, neither yet the deadness of Sara’s womb:
He GR doubted, hesitated.staggered not at the promise of God through unbelief; but was strong in faith, giving glory to God;
And being fully persuaded that, what he had Gen. 18:14; Luke 1:37. TG Promise.promised, he was able also to perform.
And therefore it was imputed to him for righteousness.
Now it was not written for his sake alone, that it was imputed to him;
But for 3 Ne. 20:25 (24–27).us also, to whom it shall be imputed, if we believe on him that raised up Jesus our Lord from the dead;
Who was delivered for our Isa. 53:5; Mark 10:45.offences, and was raised again for our TG Justification.justification.

div.p: Man is justified through the blood of Christ—Adam fell, and Christ atoned that man might be saved.

chapter.verse: Therefore being TG Justification.justified by TG Faith.faith, we have TG Peace of God.peace with God through our Lord Jesus Christ:
By whom also we have access by Gal. 5:5 (5–6).faith into this grace wherein we stand, and rejoice in hope of the glory of God.
And not only so, but we glory in 2 Cor. 4:17.tribulations also: knowing that TG Tribulation.tribulation worketh TG Patience.patience;
And patience, experience; and experience, hope:
And Ps. 119:116. TG Hope.hope maketh not ashamed; because the TG Love.love of God is shed abroad in our hearts by the Holy Ghost which is given unto us.
For when we were yet without TG Strength.strength, in due time Christ died for the ungodly.
For scarcely for a righteous man will one die: yet peradventure for a good man some would even dare to die.
But God commendeth his love toward us, in that, while we were yet Mosiah 4:11.sinners, TG Salvation, Plan of.Christ died for us.
Much more then, being now TG Jesus Christ, Atonement through.justified by his TG Blood, Symbolism of.blood, we shall be saved from wrath through him.

For if, when we were enemies, we were TG Jesus Christ, Redeemer; Reconciliation.reconciled to God by the TG Jesus Christ, Death of.death of his Son, much more, being reconciled, we shall be saved by his life.

And not only so, but we also joy in God through our Lord Jesus Christ, by whom we have now received the GR reconciliation, restoration to favor. TG Jesus Christ, Atonement through; Redemption.atonement.

Wherefore, as by one man Gen. 3:6. TG Fall of Man; Sin.sin entered into the world, and 1 Cor. 15:21.death by sin; and so TG Death.death passed upon all men, for that all have TG Man, Natural, Not Spiritually Reborn.sinned:
(For until the law sin was in the world: but TG Sin.sin is not imputed when there is no Rom. 4:15; 2 Ne. 9:25; Alma 42:17 (12–24).law.

Nevertheless death reigned from Adam to Moses, even over them that had not sinned after the similitude of Adam’s TG Transgress.transgression, who is the GR type, pattern. 1 Cor. 15:45.figure of him that was to come.

But not as the offence, so also is the free gift. For if through the offence of one many be dead, much more the grace of God, and the gift by grace, which is by 2 Cor. 5:14.one man, Jesus Christ, hath abounded unto many.
And not as it was by one that sinned, so is the gift: for the judgment was by one to condemnation, but the free gift is of many offences unto justification.
For if by one man’s offence death reigned by one; much more they which receive abundance of TG Grace.grace and of the gift of righteousness shall reign in life by one, Jesus Christ.)

Therefore as by the offence of one judgment came upon all men to condemnation; even so by the TG Righteousness.righteousness of one the free gift came upon all men unto TG Justification.justification of life.
For as by one man’s TG Disobedience.disobedience many were made sinners, so by the TG Obedience.obedience of one shall many be made righteous.
Moreover the law entered, that the offence might abound. But where sin abounded, grace did much more abound:
That as sin hath reigned unto death, even so might grace reign through TG Righteousness.righteousness unto TG Eternal Life.eternal life by Jesus Christ our Lord.

div.p: Baptism is in similitude of the death, burial, and resurrection of Christ—The wages of sin is death—Christ brings eternal life.

GR May it not be!God forbid. How shall we, that are dead to Mosiah 2:41.sin, live any longer therein?

For if we have been planted together in the TG Jesus Christ, Types of, in Memory.likeness of his Col. 2:20; 3:3; 2 Tim. 2:11.death, we shall be also in the likeness of his TG Resurrection.resurrection:

Knowing this, that our Eph. 4:22 (22–24); Col. 3:9.old man is crucified with him, that the Col. 2:11.body of sin might be GR brought to an end, freed.destroyed, that henceforth we should not serve Moro. 10:32 (32–33).sin.

Knowing that Christ being TG Jesus Christ, Resurrection.raised from the dead dieth no more; death hath no more dominion over him.

For the TG Wages.wages of TG Sin.sin is TG Death; Death, Spiritual, First.death; but the TG God, Gifts of.gift of God is TG Eternal Life.eternal life through Jesus Christ our Lord.

div.p: The law of Moses is fulfilled in Christ—Paul delights in the law of God after the inward man.

chapter.verse: Know ye not, brethren, (for I speak to them that know the law,) how that the Mosiah 13:30 (29–32).law hath dominion over a man as long as he liveth?

For the TG Marriage, Wives.woman which hath an TG Marriage, Marry.husband is bound by the law to her husband so long as he liveth; but if the husband be dead, she is loosed from the law of her husband.

So then if, while her husband liveth, she be married to another man, she shall be called an adulteress: but if her husband be dead, she is free from that law; so that she is no adulteress, though she be married to another man.

Wherefore, my brethren, ye also are become Gal. 2:19; 2 Ne. 25:25 (24–27); D&C 74:5 (2–6).dead to the Mosiah 13:28 (27–29).law by the body of Christ; that ye should be married to another, even to him who is raised from the dead, that we should bring forth fruit unto God.

JST Rom. 7:5–27 (Appendix).For when we were in the flesh, the GR sufferings, afflictions.motions of sins, which were by the law, did work in our members to bring forth fruit unto 2 Ne. 2:5 (4–10); 25:23; Alma 42:14 (12–16).death.

But now we are GR released, freed from.delivered from the law, that being 2 Cor. 5:17.dead wherein we were held; that we should serve in newness of Rom. 2:29 (28–29).spirit, and not in the oldness of the letter.

What shall we say then? Is the law sin? GR May it not be!God forbid. Nay, I had not known sin, but by the D&C 82:3 (3–4). TG Law of Moses.law: for I had not known TG Lust.lust, except the law had said, Thou shalt not GR lust for, set the heart upon. TG Covet.covet.

For I was alive without the law once: but when the commandment came, sin revived, and I died.
And the commandment, which was ordained to life, I found to be unto 2 Cor. 3:7.death.
For sin, GR having taken opportunity.taking occasion by the commandment, deceived me, and by it slew me.
Wherefore the law is holy, and the commandment holy, and just, and good.

Was then that which is good made death unto me? GR May it not be!God forbid. But sin, that it might appear sin, working death in me by that which is good; that sin by the commandment might become exceeding sinful.
For we know that the D&C 29:34.law is spiritual: but I am TG Carnal Mind.carnal, GR devoted to, a slave to.sold under sin.
JST Rom. 7:15–17 (Appendix).For GR what I produce, achieve.that which I do I GR know, understand.allow not: for what I GR choose, intend, design.would, that do I not; but what I hate, that do I.
If then I do that which I GR choose, intend, design.would not, I consent unto the law that it is good.
Now then it is no more I that GR produce, work.do it, but sin that dwelleth in me.

For I know that in me (that is, in my TG Man, Natural, Not Spiritually Reborn.flesh,) Alma 22:15.dwelleth no good thing: for to will is present with me; but how to 2 Ne. 4:19 (17–20, 26).perform that which is good I find not.
For the TG Opposition.good that I GR intend, choose.would I do not: but the evil which I would not, that I do.
Now if I do GR what I do not intend, choose.that I would not, it is no more I that do it, but sin that dwelleth in me.
I find then a law, that, when I would do good, evil is Heb. 12:1; 2 Ne. 4:18 (17–19).present with me.
For I TG Spirituality.delight in the law of God after the inward man:
But I see another law in my members, Alma 13:28.warring against the law of my mind, and bringing me into TG Bondage, Spiritual.captivity to the law of sin which is in my members.
O 2 Ne. 4:17. TG Sorrow.wretched man that I am! who shall deliver me from the body of this death?
I thank God through Jesus Christ our Lord. So then with the mind I myself serve the law of God; but with the flesh the law of sin.

div.p: The law of Christ brings life and peace—Those adopted as children of God become joint heirs with Christ—God’s elect are foreordained to eternal life—Christ makes intercession for man.

chapter.verse: There is therefore now no condemnation to them which are in Christ Jesus, who TG Walking with God.walk not after the TG Trust Not in the Arm of Flesh.flesh, but after the Alma 13:12 (12, 28); D&C 11:12 (12–13).Spirit.

For what the law could not do, in that it was Heb. 7:18.weak through the flesh, God sending his own Son in the likeness of sinful TG Jesus Christ, Condescension of.flesh, and for sin, condemned sin in the flesh:
That the righteousness of the law might be fulfilled in us, who walk not after the flesh, but after the Mosiah 3:19.Spirit.
For they that are after the flesh do GR watch, guard.mind the things of the flesh; but they that are after the Spirit the things of the Spirit.

For to be TG Carnal Mind; Chastity; Man, Natural, Not Spiritually Reborn.carnally minded is TG Death; Death, Spiritual, First.death; but to be TG Spirituality.spiritually minded is life and TG Peace of God.peace.
Because the TG Fall of Man.carnal mind is TG Opposition.enmity against God: for it is not subject to the law of God, neither indeed can be.
So then they that are JST Rom. 8:8 … after the flesh …in the flesh cannot please God.

Therefore, brethren, we are debtors, not to the flesh, to live after the flesh.
For if ye live after the flesh, ye GR are at the point of withering, dying.shall die: but if ye through the Spirit do GR put to death, subdue. Col. 3:5.mortify the deeds of the body, ye shall live.
For as many as are TG Leadership.led by the TG God, Spirit of.Spirit of God, they are the TG Sons and Daughters of God.sons of God.
For ye have not received the spirit of TG Bondage, Spiritual.bondage again to fear; but ye have received the Spirit of Isa. 56:3 (3–8).adoption, whereby we cry, Abba, Father.
The Spirit itself beareth TG Witness.witness with our TG Spirit Creation.spirit, that we are the TG Man, a Spirit Child of Heavenly Father.children of God:

And if children, then heirs; Luke 12:44 (42–44). TG Birthright; Election; Eternal Life; Exaltation; Man, Potential to Become like Heavenly Father.heirs of God, and joint-heirs with Christ; if so be that we TG Persecution; Suffering.suffer with him, that we may be also glorified together.

For the GR eager hope.earnest expectation of the GR creation, material universe.creature waiteth for the GR revelation.manifestation of the sons of God.
For the creature was made subject to JST Rom. 8:20 … tribulation … TG Vanity.vanity, not willingly, but by reason of him who hath subjected the same in hope,
Because the GR creation, material universe.creature itself also shall be TG Deliver.delivered from the bondage of 1 Cor. 15:42.corruption into the glorious TG Liberty.liberty of the children of God.
For we know that the whole creation TG Earth, Curse of.groaneth and travaileth in pain together until now.

And not only they, but ourselves also, GR who.which have the Gal. 5:22 (16–26).firstfruits of the Spirit, even we ourselves groan within ourselves, waiting for the 2 Cor. 5:2.adoption, to wit, the redemption of our body.
For we are saved by TG Hope.hope: but hope that is 2 Cor. 4:18.seen is not hope: for what a man seeth, why doth he yet hope for?
But if we hope for that we see not, then do we with patience wait for it.

Likewise the Spirit also TG Holy Ghost, Gifts of.helpeth our infirmities: for we know not what we should James 4:3; D&C 46:28 (28–30); 88:65 (64–65).pray for as we ought: but the Spirit itself maketh TG Communication.intercession for us with GR sighings.groanings which cannot be uttered.

JST Rom. 8:29–30 (Appendix).For whom he did TG God, Foreknowledge of; Man, Antemortal Existence of.foreknow, he also GR appointed beforehand, foreordained. He that spared not his own TG Jesus Christ, Atonement through.Son, but Isa. 50:8.delivered him up for us all, how shall he not with him also freely give us D&C 84:38 (33–41).all things?
Who shall GR accuse God’s elect.lay any thing to the charge of God’s elect? It is God that justifieth.
Who is he that condemneth? It is Christ that died, yea rather, that is risen again, who is even at the right hand of God, who also maketh Isa. 53:12; Heb. 7:25.intercession for us.
Who shall separate us from the D&C 29:5.love of Christ? shall TG Tribulation.tribulation, or distress, or TG Persecution.persecution, or famine, or nakedness, or peril, or sword?
As it is written, For thy sake we are Ps. 44:22; 2 Cor. 4:11.killed all the day long; we are accounted as TG Sheep.sheep for the slaughter.
Nay, in all these things we are GR abundantly victorious.more than 2 Cor. 2:14.conquerors through him that loved us.
For I am persuaded, that neither death, nor life, nor angels, nor principalities, nor powers, nor things present, nor things to come,
Nor height, nor depth, nor any other creature, shall be able to Matt. 10:31 (29–31); D&C 121:33.separate us from the 2 Ne. 1:15.love of God, which is in Christ Jesus our Lord.

div.p: Paul explains how the law of election (foreordination) operates—The people of Israel are chosen (foreordained) to receive the adoption, covenants, promises, and blessings of the gospel; yet they are not all Israel who are of Israel—They must seek their blessings by faith—The Gentiles also attain to righteousness and salvation by faith.

chapter.verse: I say the truth in Christ, I 1 Tim. 2:7.lie not, my TG Conscience.conscience also bearing me witness in the Holy Ghost,
That I have great heaviness and continual sorrow in my heart.

JST Rom. 9:3 (For once I could have wished that myself were accursed from Christ,) …For I could wish that myself were Ex. 32:32.accursed from Christ for my brethren, my kinsmen according to the flesh:

Who are Israelites; to whom pertaineth the TG Election; Sons and Daughters of God.adoption, and the glory, and the TG Abrahamic Covenant.covenants, and the giving of the law, and the service of God, and the promises;
Whose are the fathers, and of whom as concerning the TG Jesus Christ, Condescension of.flesh Christ came, who is over all, God blessed for ever. Amen.
Not as though the word of God GR has been fruitless, ineffectual.hath taken none effect. For they are not all Israel, which are of 2 Ne. 30:2.Israel:

JST Rom. 9:7 Neither, because they are all children of Abraham, are they the seed; but, In Isaac …Neither, because they are the seed of Abraham, are they all children: but, In TG Seed of Abraham.Isaac shall thy seed be called.
That is, They which are the children of the flesh, these are not the TG Sons and Daughters of God.children of God: but the children of the promise are counted for the seed.
For this is the word of promise, At this time will I come, and Sara shall have a TG Seed of Abraham.son.
And not only this; but when Gen. 22:23.Rebecca also had conceived by one, even by our father Isaac;

(For the children being not yet born, neither having done any good or evil, that the purpose of God according to TG Foreordination; God, Foreknowledge of.election might stand, not of works, but of him that calleth;)
It was said unto her, The Gen. 25:23.elder shall serve the younger.
As it is written, Jacob have I Mal. 1:2 (2–3).loved, but Esau have I hated.
What shall we say then? Is there Ps. 92:15. TG God, Perfection of.unrighteousness with God? GR May it not be!God forbid.
For he saith to Moses, I will have mercy on whom I will have mercy, and I will have TG Compassion.compassion on whom I will have compassion.
So then it is not of him that willeth, nor of him that runneth, but of God that sheweth TG God, Mercy of.mercy.

For the scripture saith unto Pharaoh, Even for this same Ex. 9:16 (15–17).purpose have I raised thee up, that I might shew my power in thee, and that my name might be declared throughout all the earth.
Therefore hath he TG Forgive.mercy on whom he will have mercy, and whom he will he GR leaves to stubbornness, hardness. Deut. 2:30.hardeneth.
Thou wilt say then unto me, Why doth he yet find fault? For who hath resisted his will?
Nay but, O man, who art thou that GR contradictest, disputest.repliest against God? Shall the thing formed say to him that formed it, Why hast thou Job 9:12.made me thus?
Hath not the Isa. 45:9; Jer. 18:6.potter power over the clay, of the same lump to make one vessel unto honour, and another unto dishonour?
What if God, willing to shew his wrath, and to make his power known, endured with much longsuffering the vessels of wrath fitted to 1 Pet. 2:8 (4–8).destruction:
And that he might make known the TG Treasure.riches of his TG Celestial Glory; God, Glory of.glory on the vessels of mercy, which he had afore prepared unto glory,
Even us, whom he hath called, not of the Jews only, but also of the Gentiles?
As he saith also in IE Hosea.Osee, I will call them my people, which were not my Hosea 2:23; Zech. 13:9.people; and her beloved, which was not beloved.
And it shall come to pass, that in the place where it was said unto them, Ye are not my people; there shall they be called the GR sons. TG Sons and Daughters of God.children of the living God.

IE Isaiah.Esaias also crieth concerning Israel, Though the Isa. 10:22.number of the children of TG Israel, Blessings of.Israel be as the sand of the sea, a TG Israel, Remnant of.remnant shall be saved:
For he will finish the work, and cut it short in D&C 52:11.righteousness: because a short work will the Lord make upon the earth.
And as Esaias said before, Except the Lord of HEB hosts.Sabaoth had left us a GR posterity.seed, we had been as Sodoma, and been made like unto Gomorrha.
What shall we say then? That the Gentiles, which followed not after righteousness, have attained to righteousness, even the TG Righteousness.righteousness which is of faith.
But TG Apostasy of Israel.Israel, which followed after the law of righteousness, hath not attained to the law of righteousness.
GR Why?Wherefore? Because they sought it not by faith, but as it were by the works of the law. For they Isa. 8:14 (13–15); Matt. 21:44 (43–45); Luke 2:34.stumbled at that stumblingstone;
As it is written, Behold, I lay in Sion a TG Cornerstone; Jesus Christ, Prophecies about.stumblingstone and TG Rock.rock of TG Offense.offence: and whosoever believeth on him shall not be ashamed.

div.p: Salvation comes through righteousness to those who believe in Christ—Faith comes by hearing the gospel taught by legal administrators sent of God.

chapter.verse: Brethren, my heart’s desire and prayer to God for Israel is, that they might be TG Reconciliation.saved.
For I bear them record that they have a TG Zeal.zeal of God, but not according to knowledge.

For they being ignorant of God’s TG God, the Standard of Righteousness.righteousness, and GR desiring, endeavoring.going about to establish their own righteousness, have not TG Apostasy of Israel; Submissiveness.submitted themselves unto the righteousness of God.
For Christ is the 2 Ne. 2:7 (7–10).end of the law for righteousness to every one that believeth.
For Moses describeth the righteousness which is of the law, That the man which doeth those things shall live by them.
But the TG Righteousness.righteousness which is of faith speaketh on this wise, Say not in thine heart, Who shall ascend into heaven? (that is, to bring Christ down from above:)
Or, Who shall descend into the deep? (that is, to bring up Christ again from the dead.)
But what saith it? The word is nigh thee, even in thy mouth, and in thy heart: that is, the word of faith, which we preach;
That if thou shalt confess with thy mouth the Lord Jesus, and shalt believe in thine heart that God hath raised him from the dead, thou shalt be saved.
For with the TG Heart.heart man D&C 46:14 (11–14).believeth unto righteousness; and with the mouth confession is made unto TG Salvation.salvation.
For the scripture saith, Whosoever believeth on him shall not be ashamed.

For there is no Acts 10:35 (34–35); Rom. 2:11; Gal. 3:28 (26–29).difference between the Jew and the Greek: for the same Lord over all is TG Treasure.rich unto all that TG God, Access to.call upon him.
For whosoever shall TG Prayer.call upon the name of the Lord shall be saved.

How then shall they call on him in whom they have not TG Apostasy of Israel; Hypocrisy.believed? and how shall they believe in him of whom they have not heard? and how shall they hear without a D&C 42:6.preacher?

And how shall they TG Missionary Work; Preaching.preach, except they be TG Called of God.sent? as it is written, How beautiful are the Nahum 1:15.feet of them that preach the TG Gospel.gospel of TG Peacemakers; Peace of God.peace, and bring glad tidings of good things!
But they have not all TG Apostasy of Israel.obeyed the gospel. For Esaias saith, Lord, who hath Isa. 53:1; John 12:38.believed our report?
So then TG Faith.faith cometh by Gal. 3:2; 2 Ne. 33:1 (1–4); Alma 31:5 (5, 8–11); D&C 100:8 (7–8).hearing, and hearing by the word of God.
But I say, Have they not heard? Yes verily, their sound went into all the earth, and their Ps. 19:4.words unto the ends of the D&C 23:2; 24:10.world.
But I say, Did not Israel know? First Moses saith, I will provoke you to Deut. 32:21. TG Jealous.jealousy by them that are no people, and by a foolish nation I will anger you.
But IE Isaiah.Esaias is very bold, and saith, I was found of them that Isa. 65:1.sought me not; I was made manifest unto them that asked not after me.
But to Israel he saith, All day long I have stretched forth my hands unto a Isa. 65:2. TG Apostasy of Israel; Disobedience.disobedient and GR contradicting, opposing.gainsaying people.

div.p: Israel was chosen (foreordained) according to the election of grace—But some harden their hearts against it—The Gentiles are adopted into the house of Israel—The gospel goes preferentially to the Gentiles until the fulness of the Gentiles.

chapter.verse: I say then, Hath God cast away his people? GR May it not be!God forbid. For I also am an Acts 22:3; 2 Cor. 11:22.Israelite, of the seed of Abraham, of the tribe of Benjamin.

God hath not cast away his TG Israel, Blessings of.people which he TG Foreordination; God, Foreknowledge of.foreknew. GR Know.Wot ye not what the scripture saith of IE Elijah.Elias? how he maketh intercession to God against Israel, saying,
Lord, they have TG Apostasy of Israel; Martyrdom.killed thy prophets, and digged down thine altars; and I am 1 Kgs. 19:14 (10–18).left alone, and they seek my life.
But what saith the answer of God unto him? I have D&C 49:8.reserved to myself seven thousand men, who have not bowed the knee to the image of Baal.
Even so then at this present time also there is a remnant according to the TG Election.election of grace.
And if by TG Grace.grace, then is it no more of Rom. 2:6 (5–10).works: otherwise grace is no more grace. But if it be of works, then is it no more grace: otherwise work is no more work.
What then? Israel hath not obtained that which he seeketh for; but the election hath obtained it, and the rest GR became callous.were blinded

(According as it is written, God hath given them the spirit of GR deep sleep, stupor. Isa. 29:10.slumber, eyes that they should not see, and ears that they should not Matt. 13:14; 2 Ne. 16:10.hear;) unto this day.
And David saith, Let their table be made a Ps. 69:22.snare, and a trap, and a stumblingblock, and a GR retribution, requital.recompence unto them:
Let their eyes be darkened, that they may not see, and bow down their back alway.
I say then, Have they stumbled that they should fall? GR May it not be!God forbid: but rather through their fall salvation is come unto the Gentiles, for to provoke them to TG Jealous.jealousy.
Now if the fall of them be the riches of the world, and the GR default, failure.diminishing of them the riches of the Gentiles; how much more their fulness?
For I speak to you Acts 22:21.Gentiles, inasmuch as I am the apostle of the Gentiles, I Jacob 2:2 (2–3); D&C 24:3; 66:11.magnify mine office:
If by any means I may provoke to emulation them which are my flesh, and might save some of them.
For if the casting away of them be the TG Reconciliation.reconciling of the world, what shall the receiving of them be, but life from the dead?
For if the firstfruit be holy, the lump is also holy: and if the TG Israel, Mission of.root be holy, so are the branches.
And if some of the branches be broken off, and thou, being a wild olive TG Vineyard of the Lord.tree, wert grafted in among them, and with them partakest of the root and fatness of the olive tree;
TG Boast.Boast not against the branches. But if thou boast, thou bearest not the root, but the root thee.
Thou wilt say then, The branches were broken off, that I might be grafted in.

Well; because of TG Apostasy of Israel; Unbelief.unbelief they were Ps. 80:12 (12–16). TG Israel, Scattering of.broken off, and thou standest by 2 Cor. 1:24.faith. Be not highminded, but TG Reverence.fear:
For if God spared not the Jacob 5:3 (1–77); 6:1 (1–13).natural branches, take heed lest he also TG Apostasy of the Early Christian Church.spare not thee.
Behold therefore the goodness and severity of God: on them which fell, severity; but toward thee, goodness, if thou continue in his goodness: otherwise thou also shalt be cut off.
And they also, if they abide not still in unbelief, shall be grafted in: for God is able to graft them in again.

For if thou wert cut out of the olive tree which is wild by nature, and wert grafted contrary to nature into a good olive tree: how much more shall these, which be the natural branches, be grafted into their own olive tree?

For I would not, brethren, that ye should be ignorant of this mystery, lest ye should be wise in your own Prov. 26:12.conceits; that GR callousness. Jacob 4:14.blindness in part is happened to Israel, until the TG Restoration of the Gospel.fulness of the TG Gentiles.Gentiles be come in.

And so all TG Israel, Restoration of.Israel shall be saved: as it is written, There shall come out of TG Zion.Sion the Isa. 59:20; Alma 11:40 (37–41).Deliverer, and shall turn away ungodliness from Jacob:
For this is my Isa. 59:21 (20–21); Heb. 10:16 (16–17); D&C 49:9 (5–9). TG Abrahamic Covenant.covenant unto them, when I shall take away their sins.
As concerning the gospel, they are enemies for your sakes: but as touching the election, they are beloved for the fathers’ sakes.
For the gifts and calling of God are without repentance.
For as ye in times past GR were disbelieving, disobedient to.have not believed God, yet have now obtained mercy through their GR disobedience, disbelief.unbelief:
Even so have these also now not GR obeyed.believed, that through your mercy they also may obtain mercy.
For God hath GR closed up together.concluded them all in unbelief, that he might have mercy upon all.

O the depth of the TG Treasure.riches both of the TG God, Intelligence of.wisdom and TG God, Omniscience of.knowledge of God! how Isa. 40:28.unsearchable are his judgments, and his ways past Prov. 25:2.finding out!
For who hath known the Jacob 4:8 (8–13); Mosiah 4:9.mind of the Lord? or who hath been his 1 Cor. 2:16; D&C 22:4. TG Counselor.counsellor?
Or who hath first given to him, and it shall be recompensed unto him again?
For of him, and D&C 76:24 (22–24); 93:10.through him, and to him, are all things: to whom be glory for ever. Amen.

div.p: Paul counsels the Saints to present their bodies as a living sacrifice, to use their own grace-given gifts, and to live as Saints should live.

chapter.verse: I beseech you therefore, brethren, by the mercies of God, that ye present your TG Body, Sanctity of.bodies a living Mosiah 2:34; Moro. 10:32 (30–33); D&C 93:1. TG Self-Sacrifice.sacrifice, holy, Lev. 1:4.acceptable unto God, which is your reasonable TG Service.service.

And be not conformed to this TG World; Worldliness.world: but be ye Eph. 5:8; 1 Jn. 5:4 (1–5). TG Self-Mastery.transformed by the TG Man, New, Spiritually Reborn.renewing of your TG Mind.mind, that ye may GR test, try, prove.prove what is that good, and acceptable, and perfect, TG God, Will of.will of God.

For I say, through the grace given unto me, to every man that is among you, not to TG Humility.think of himself more highly than he ought to think; but to think D&C 18:21; 43:35.soberly, according as 1 Cor. 7:17.God hath dealt to every man the measure of faith.
For as we have many members in one body, and all members have not the same GR function, operation.office:
So we, being many, are TG Unity.one TG Church.body in Christ, and every one members one of another.
Having then gifts differing according to the TG Grace.grace that is given to us, whether prophecy, let us prophesy according to the proportion of TG Faith.faith;
Or ministry, let us wait on our ministering: or he that teacheth, on teaching;
Or he that exhorteth, on exhortation: he that giveth, let him do it with simplicity; he that ruleth, with diligence; he that sheweth mercy, with TG Cheerful.cheerfulness.
Let love be GR sincere, unfeigned, real.without dissimulation. Abhor that which is Ps. 26:4; 2 Ne. 4:31; Alma 13:12; D&C 38:42. TG Evil.evil; cleave to that which is good.
Be kindly Philip. 2:2 (1–4).affectioned one to another with TG Brotherhood and Sisterhood.brotherly TG Love.love; in honour preferring one another;
Not slothful in GR haste, diligence.business; TG Zeal.fervent in spirit; serving the Lord;
Rejoicing in hope; D&C 24:8; 31:9.patient in TG Adversity.tribulation; GR constantly persisting in.continuing instant in prayer;
Distributing to the necessity of saints; given to TG Hospitality.hospitality.
Bless them which TG Enemies; Persecution.persecute you: bless, and TG Curse.curse not.
Rejoice with them that do rejoice, and TG Compassion.weep with them that weep.

Be of the same mind one toward another. Mind not high things, but GR conforming willingly with the humble. 3 Ne. 12:5.condescend to men of low estate. Be not wise in your own Prov. 26:12. TG Pride.conceits.
TG Forgive; Retribution.Recompense to no man TG Evil.evil for evil. Provide things TG Honesty.honest in the 2 Cor. 8:21.sight of all men.
If it be possible, as much as lieth in you, live Prov. 3:30. TG Peace; Peacemakers.peaceably with all men.
Dearly beloved, TG Forbear; Retribution.avenge not yourselves, but rather give place unto wrath: for it is written, Vengeance is mine; I will repay, saith the Lord.
Therefore if thine 2 Kgs. 6:22. TG Enemies.enemy hunger, feed him; if he thirst, give him drink: for in so doing thou shalt heap coals of fire on his head.
Be not overcome of evil, but Rev. 2:17.overcome evil with good.

div.p: Paul counsels, Be subject unto God’s ministers; keep the commandments; love one another; righteousness leads to salvation.

chapter.verse: Let every soul GR be submissive, render obedience. D&C 58:22 (21–22). TG Citizenship; Governments.be subject unto the higher GR authorities.powers. For there is no John 19:11 (10–12).power JST Rom. 13:1 … in the church but of God …but of God: the TG Governments.powers that be are ordained of God.
Whosoever therefore resisteth the power, resisteth the ordinance of God: and they that TG Rebellion.resist shall receive to themselves TG Damnation.damnation.
For rulers are not a terror to good works, but to the evil. Wilt thou then not be afraid of the GR authority.power? do that which is TG Retribution.good, and thou shalt have praise of the same:

For he is GR a servant.the minister of God to thee for good. But if thou do that which is evil, be afraid; for he beareth not the sword in vain: for he is the minister of God, a revenger to execute wrath upon him that doeth evil.
Wherefore ye must needs be subject, not only for wrath, but also for TG Conscience.conscience sake.
JST Rom. 13:6–7 (Appendix).For for this cause pay ye tribute also: for they are God’s ministers, attending continually upon this very thing.
Render therefore to all their dues: tribute to whom tribute is due; custom to whom custom; fear to whom fear; D&C 134:6. TG Honor.honour to whom honour.
TG Debt.Owe no man any thing, but to love one another: for he that TG Love.loveth another hath fulfilled the law.

For this, Thou shalt not commit TG Adulterer.adultery, Thou shalt not kill, Thou shalt not TG Stealing.steal, Thou shalt not bear false witness, Thou shalt not TG Covet.covet; and if there be any other commandment, it is briefly comprehended in this saying, namely, Thou shalt love thy TG Neighbor.neighbour as thyself.
Moro. 7:47 (1, 44–47). TG Motivations.Love worketh no ill to his neighbour: therefore love is the fulfilling of the Matt. 22:40 (36–40); Gal. 5:14 (13–14).law.
And that, knowing the time, that now it is high TG Time.time to awake out of TG Apathy; Sleep.sleep: for now is our salvation nearer than when we believed.

The night is far spent, the John 9:4; Alma 34:31 (31–34).day is at hand: let us therefore cast off the works of 2 Cor. 5:17; 1 Thes. 5:4. TG Darkness, Spiritual.darkness, and let us put on the Isa. 59:17 (16–17); 2 Cor. 6:7; Eph. 6:11 (10–24); 1 Thes. 5:8; D&C 27:15 (15–18).armour of TG Children of Light; Light [noun].light.

Let us walk GR with propriety, decently, gracefully. TG Honesty.honestly, as in the day; not in TG Rioting and Reveling.rioting and TG Drunkenness.drunkenness, not in GR lewdness, whoredoms.chambering and wantonness, not in strife and TG Envy.envying.
But Gal. 3:27 (24–29).put ye on the Lord Jesus Christ, and make not provision for the flesh, to fulfil the TG Lust.lusts thereof.

div.p: Avoid quarreling about opinions and making unrighteous judgment of each other—Every knee will bow to Christ—The kingdom of God embraces righteousness, peace, and joy in the Holy Ghost.

chapter.verse: Him that is Isa. 35:3; Rom. 15:1 (1–3); 1 Cor. 8:9; Gal. 6:1; D&C 81:5; 84:106.weak in the faith receive ye, but not to TG Doubt.doubtful TG Disputations.disputations.
For one believeth that he may eat all things: another, who is weak, eateth GR vegetables.herbs.
Let not him that eateth despise him that eateth not; and let not him which eateth not Col. 2:16.judge him that eateth: for God hath received him.
Who art thou that D&C 11:12.judgest another man’s servant? to his own master he standeth or falleth. Yea, he shall be holden up: for God is able to make him stand.
One man esteemeth one Gal. 4:10 (9–10).day above another: another esteemeth every day alike. Let every man be fully persuaded in his own mind.

He that regardeth the day, regardeth it unto the Lord; and he that regardeth not the day, to the Lord he doth not regard it. He that eateth, eateth to the Lord, for he giveth God thanks; and he that eateth not, to the Lord he eateth not, and giveth God thanks.
For none of us 2 Cor. 5:15 (14–16); Gal. 2:20.liveth to himself, and no man dieth to himself.
For whether we live, we live unto the Lord; and whether we die, we D&C 42:44 (44–47); 63:49.die unto the Lord: whether we live therefore, or die, we are the Lord’s.

For to this end Christ both died, and rose, and revived, that he might be TG Jesus Christ, Authority of; Jesus Christ, Power of.Lord both of the Luke 20:38. TG Genealogy and Temple Work; Salvation for the Dead.dead and living.
But why dost thou TG Judgment.judge thy brother? or why dost thou set at nought thy brother? for we shall all stand before the TG Judgment, the Last.judgment seat of Christ.
For it is written, As I live, saith the Lord, every Isa. 45:23; Philip. 2:10.knee shall bow to me, and every tongue shall GR praise, profess openly.confess to God.
So then every one of us shall give account of himself to God.

Let us not therefore judge one another any more: but judge this rather, that no man put a Lev. 19:14; 1 Cor. 8:9 (9–13); 10:32 (24–33); 2 Cor. 6:3; Alma 39:11.stumblingblock or an occasion to fall in his brother’s way.

I know, and am persuaded by the Lord Jesus, that there is nothing GR ceremonially unclean. TG Uncleanness.unclean of itself: but to him that TG Motivations.esteemeth any thing to be unclean, to him it is unclean.

But if thy brother be grieved with thy meat, JST Rom. 14:15 … thou walkest not charitably if thou eatest. Therefore destroy not him with thy meat …now walkest thou not GR according to love.charitably. Destroy not him GR on account of food.with thy meat, for whom Christ died.
Let not then your good be evil spoken of:
For the kingdom of God is not GR food. TG Food.meat and drink; but TG Righteousness.righteousness, and TG Contentment; Peace of God.peace, and TG Joy.joy in the Holy Ghost.
For he that in these things serveth Christ is acceptable to God, and approved of men.
Let us therefore follow after the things which make for TG Peace; Peacemakers.peace, and things wherewith one may TG Edification.edify another.
GR Because of food.For meat destroy not the TG God, Works of.work of God. All things indeed are pure; but it is evil for that man who eateth with offence.
It is good neither to eat flesh, nor to drink wine, nor any thing whereby thy brother stumbleth, or is 1 Cor. 8:13.offended, or is made weak.
Hast thou faith? have it to thyself before God. Happy is he that condemneth not himself GR by what he tries, approves.in that thing which he alloweth.
And he that TG Doubt.doubteth is damned if he eat, because he eateth not of faith: for whatsoever is not of TG Faith.faith is TG Sin.sin.

div.p: True Saints fellowship one another—Paul recounts his diligence in preaching the gospel—The gifts of the Spirit are poured out upon the Gentiles.

chapter.verse: We then that are strong ought to GR remove, endure, bear with. Alma 31:33.bear the infirmities of the Rom. 14:1 (1–3).weak, and not to please ourselves.
Let every one of us please his TG Neighbor.neighbour for his good to TG Edification.edification.
For even Christ pleased not himself; but, as it is written, The GR revilings, insults. TG Reproach.reproaches of them that reproached thee fell on me.

For whatsoever things were 1 Cor. 10:11. TG Scriptures, Writing of.written aforetime were 1 Cor. 9:10.written for our TG Scriptures, Value of; Understanding.learning, that we through TG Patience.patience and comfort of the TG Scriptures, Study of.scriptures might have TG Hope.hope.
Now the God of patience and GR comfort.consolation grant you to be 1 Cor. 1:10 (9–16); 1 Thes. 5:13 (13–15); Mosiah 18:21.likeminded one toward another according to Christ Jesus:
That ye may with one mind and one mouth glorify God, even the TG Jesus Christ, Divine Sonship.Father of our Lord Jesus Christ.
Wherefore receive ye one another, as Christ also received us to the glory of God.
Now I say that Jesus Christ was a minister of the TG Circumcision.circumcision for the truth of God, to GR establish, make constant.confirm the 2 Cor. 1:20.promises made unto the fathers:

And that the Gentiles might glorify God for his TG God, Mercy of.mercy; as it is written, For this cause I will GR profess openly, praise.confess to thee among the Ps. 18:49.Gentiles, and sing unto thy name.
And again he saith, Rejoice, ye Acts 9:15 (1–19); 3 Ne. 30:2 (1–2).Gentiles, with his people.
And again, Praise the Lord, all ye Gentiles; and laud him, all ye people.
And again, IE Isaiah.Esaias saith, There shall be a root of TG Jesus Christ, Davidic Descent of.Jesse, and he that shall rise to reign over the Gentiles; in him shall the Gentiles trust.
Now the God of hope fill you with all joy and TG Peace of God.peace in believing, that ye may abound in TG Hope.hope, through the power of the Holy Ghost.
And I myself also am persuaded of you, my brethren, that ye also are full of goodness, filled with all TG Knowledge.knowledge, able also to TG Warn.admonish one another.
Nevertheless, brethren, I have written the more boldly unto you in GR part.some sort, as putting you in mind, because of the TG Grace.grace that is given to me of God,

That I should be the GR servant at one’s own expense.minister of Jesus Christ to the Gentiles, ministering the gospel of God, that the GR sacrifice, offering.offering up of the Gentiles might be acceptable, being TG Holy Ghost, Gifts of.sanctified by the Holy Ghost.
I have therefore whereof I may Alma 26:16 (16, 35).glory through Jesus Christ in those things which pertain to God.
For I will not dare to speak of any of those things which Christ hath not wrought by me, to make the Gentiles obedient, by word and deed,

Through mighty Morm. 9:19 (19–21); D&C 63:10 (7–10).signs and wonders, by the power of the TG God, Spirit of.Spirit of God; so that from Jerusalem, and round about unto Illyricum, I have fully preached the gospel of Christ.
Yea, so have I strived to preach the gospel, not where Christ was named, lest I should build upon another man’s 2 Cor. 10:15; D&C 52:33.foundation:
But as it is written, To whom he was not Isa. 52:15.spoken of, they shall see: and they that have not heard shall understand.
For which cause also I have been much Rom. 1:13.hindered from coming to you.
But now having no more GR opportunity.place in these parts, and having a great desire these many years to come unto you;
Whensoever I take my journey into Spain, I will come to you: for I trust to see you in my journey, and to be brought on my way thitherward by you, if first I be somewhat filled with your company.
But now I go unto Jerusalem to minister unto the saints.
For it hath pleased them of Macedonia and Achaia to make a certain TG Almsgiving; Welfare.contribution for the poor saints which are at Jerusalem.

It hath pleased them verily; and their debtors they are. For if the Gentiles have been made partakers of their 1 Cor. 9:11.spiritual things, their TG Duty.duty is also to minister unto them in GR material, temporal.carnal things.
When therefore I have performed this, and have sealed to them this Philip. 4:17; Titus 3:14.fruit, I will come by you into Spain.
And I am sure that, when I come unto you, I shall come in the D&C 1:23; 14:10; 35:17; 39:11 (11–18); 42:12; JS—H 1:34.fulness of the blessing of the gospel of Christ.
Now I beseech you, brethren, for the Lord Jesus Christ’s sake, and for the love of the Spirit, that ye strive together with me in your prayers to God for me;
That I may be delivered from them that GR refuse belief or obedience.do not believe in Judæa; and that my service which I have for Jerusalem may be accepted of the saints;
That I may come unto you with joy by the will of God, and may with you be 2 Cor. 7:13.refreshed.
Now the God of peace be with you all. Amen.

div.p: Paul sends greetings to various Saints—He counsels the Saints to avoid those who cause divisions—The Saints should be wise concerning good and innocent concerning evil.

chapter.verse: I commend unto you Phebe our sister, which is a servant of the church which is at Cenchrea:
That ye receive her in the Lord, as becometh saints, and that ye assist her in whatsoever business she hath need of you: for she hath been a succourer of many, and of myself also.
Greet Priscilla and Aquila my helpers in Christ Jesus:
Who have for my life laid down their own necks: unto whom not only I give thanks, but also all the churches of the Gentiles.
Likewise greet the church that is in their house. Salute my wellbeloved Epænetus, who is the firstfruits of Achaia unto Christ.
Greet Mary, who bestowed much labour on us.
Salute Andronicus and Junia, my kinsmen, and my fellowprisoners, who are of note among the apostles, who also were in Christ before me.
Greet Amplias my beloved in the Lord.
Salute Urbane, our helper in Christ, and Stachys my beloved.
Salute Apelles approved in Christ. Salute them which are of Aristobulus’ JST Rom. 16:10 … church. household .
Salute Herodion my kinsman. Greet them that be of the JST Rom. 16:11 … church … household of Narcissus, which are in the Lord.
Salute Tryphena and Tryphosa, who labour in the Lord. Salute the beloved Persis, which laboured much in the Lord.
Salute Rufus chosen in the Lord, and his mother and mine.
Salute Asyncritus, Phlegon, Hermas, Patrobas, Hermes, and the brethren which are with them.
Salute Philologus, and Julia, Nereus, and his sister, and Olympas, and all the saints which are with them.
Salute one another with an holy JST Rom. 16:16 … salutation …kiss. The TG Jesus Christ, Head of the Church.churches of Christ salute you.

Now I beseech you, brethren, GR watch, beware of.mark them which cause Acts 20:30 (28–30); 1 Cor. 1:10 (10–13); 2 Thes. 3:6 (2–6); 2 Ne. 26:21 (20–21).divisions and GR stumbling blocks, scandals.offences contrary to the 3 Ne. 11:40 (31–41).doctrine which ye have learned; and 1 Cor. 5:11.avoid them.
For they that are such serve not our Lord Jesus Christ, but their own belly; and by good words and 2 Pet. 2:3.fair speeches deceive the hearts of the GR innocent, guileless.simple.

For your Rom. 1:8.obedience is come abroad unto all men. I am glad therefore on your behalf: but yet I would have you TG Wisdom.wise unto that which is good, and GR blameless, innocent.simple concerning evil.

And the God of Moro. 7:3 (3–4).peace shall GR break the power of, crush. Gen. 3:15. TG Jesus Christ, Prophecies about; Redemption.bruise TG Devil.Satan under your feet shortly. The grace of our Lord Jesus Christ be with you. Amen.
Timotheus my workfellow, and Lucius, and Jason, and Sosipater, my kinsmen, salute you.
I Tertius, who wrote this epistle, salute you in the Lord.
Gaius mine host, and of the whole church, saluteth you. Erastus the chamberlain of the city saluteth you, and Quartus a brother.
The Rev. 22:21 (17–21).grace of our Lord Jesus Christ be with you all. Amen.

Now to him that is of power to 1 Thes. 3:2.stablish you according to my gospel, and the preaching of Jesus Christ, according to the revelation of the Eph. 3:4; Col. 1:26 (25–26); D&C 6:7 (7–11); 42:65 (61–65); 121:27 (25–27).mystery, which was kept secret since the world began,
But now is made manifest, and by the scriptures of the prophets, according to the commandment of the Gen. 21:33.everlasting God, made known to all nations for the TG Obedience.obedience of faith:
To God only wise, be glory through Jesus Christ for ever. Amen.

div.p: True Saints are perfectly united in the same mind and in the same judgment—Preach the gospel and save souls—The gospel is preached by the weak and the simple.

chapter.verse: Paul, called to be an apostle of Jesus Christ through the will of God, and Sosthenes our brother,

Unto the church of God which is at Corinth, to them that are TG Sanctification.sanctified in Christ Jesus, called to be TG Mission of Early Saints; Saints.saints, with all that in every place call upon the name of Jesus Christ our Lord, both theirs and ours:
Grace be unto you, and TG Peace of God.peace, from God our Father, and from the Lord Jesus Christ.
I thank my God always on your behalf, for the grace of God which is given you by Jesus Christ;
That in every thing ye are enriched by him, in all utterance, and in all knowledge;
Even as the TG Testimony.testimony of Christ was GR established, strengthened.confirmed in you:
So that ye GR come short, miss, need.come behind in no gift; waiting for the GR revelation. 1 Thes. 3:13; D&C 45:44 (25–44).coming of our Lord Jesus Christ:
Who shall also GR secure, establish, strengthen.confirm you unto the end, that ye may be 3 Ne. 27:20; D&C 4:2.blameless in the day of our Lord Jesus Christ.
God is faithful, by whom ye were called unto the TG Fellowshipping.fellowship of his Son Jesus Christ our Lord.

Now I beseech you, brethren, by the name of our Lord Jesus Christ, that ye all speak the same thing, and that there be no GR factions, schisms. Acts 4:32; Rom. 15:5 (1–7); 16:17 (17–18); Eph. 4:13 (11–14); 3 Ne. 11:28 (28–30). TG Church.divisions among you; but that ye be perfectly joined together in the same 1 Cor. 12:25; 2 Cor. 13:11; D&C 38:27. TG Mind.mind and in the same judgment.

For it hath been declared unto me GR about, concerning.of you, my brethren, by them which are of the house of Chloe, that there are TG Apostasy of the Early Christian Church; Contention.contentions among you.
Now this I say, that every one of you saith, I am of Paul; and I of Apollos; and I of Cephas; and I of 3 Ne. 27:8 (4–9).Christ.
Is Christ 2 Ne. 28:3 (3–5, 12–13).divided? was Paul crucified for you? or were ye baptized in the name of Paul?
I thank God that I baptized none of you, GR except.but Acts 18:8.Crispus and Gaius;
Lest any should say that I had baptized in mine own name.
And I baptized also the household of 1 Cor. 16:15.Stephanas: besides, I know not whether I baptized any other.
For Christ sent me not to baptize, but to preach the gospel: not with TG Wisdom.wisdom of words, lest the cross of Christ should be made of none effect.
For the 1 Pet. 2:8 (4–8).preaching of the cross is to them that perish foolishness; but unto us which are saved it is the Rom. 1:16; 1 Cor. 4:20.power of God.
For it is written, I will destroy the wisdom of the wise, and will bring to nothing the TG Understanding.understanding of the TG Prudence.prudent.

Where is the wise? where is the TG Scribe.scribe? where is the TG Disputations.disputer of this GR age.world? hath not God made foolish the Isa. 44:25; Jer. 8:8 (8–9); D&C 133:58.wisdom of this TG Worldliness.world?
For after that in the wisdom of God the world GR by means of, through.by wisdom knew not God, it pleased God by the foolishness of TG Gospel.preaching to save them that believe.
For the Jews require a TG Sign Seekers.sign, and the Greeks seek after wisdom:
But we TG Missionary Work.preach Christ TG Jesus Christ, Crucifixion of.crucified, unto the Jews a Jacob 4:15.stumblingblock, and unto the Greeks foolishness;

But unto them JST 1 Cor. 1:24 … who believe, both Jews and Greeks …which are called, both Jews and Greeks, Christ the TG God, Power of.power of God, and the TG Jesus Christ, Relationships with the Father.wisdom of God.
Because the TG Foolishness.foolishness of God is wiser than men; and the weakness of God is stronger than men.

For ye see your calling, brethren, how that not many wise men after the TG Man, Natural, Not Spiritually Reborn.flesh, not many mighty, not many noble, are JST 1 Cor. 1:26 … chosen … John 12:43 (42–43). TG Called of God. called :

But God hath chosen the James 2:5 (1–9); D&C 1:23 (18–20, 23).foolish things of the world to GR shame, frustrate. Alma 37:7 (6–7); D&C 133:58 (58–59).confound the wise; and God hath chosen the Ether 12:27 (23–29); D&C 35:13; 124:1.weak things of the world to confound the things which are mighty;
And base things of the world, and things which are despised, hath God chosen, yea, and things which are not, to bring to nought things that are:
That no flesh should glory in his presence.
But of him are ye in Christ Jesus, who of God is made unto us wisdom, and righteousness, and TG Sanctification.sanctification, and redemption:
That, according as it is written, He that glorieth, let him Jer. 9:24 (23–24); 1 Cor. 5:6; Alma 26:16 (16, 35).glory in the Lord.

div.p: The gospel is preached by the power of the Spirit—The Spirit reveals all things to the Saints—The unrepentant natural man cannot receive the things of the Spirit of God.

chapter.verse: And I, brethren, when I came to you, came not with excellency of speech or of wisdom, declaring unto you the TG Testimony.testimony of God.
For I determined not to know any thing among you, GR except.save Jesus Christ, and him TG Jesus Christ, Crucifixion of.crucified.
And I was with you in Gal. 4:13; Ether 12:23 (23–29).weakness, and in fear, and in much trembling.

And my 1 Thes. 1:5.speech and my preaching was not with GR persuasive.enticing words of man’s wisdom, but in demonstration of the TG Teaching with the Spirit.Spirit and of 2 Ne. 28:4 (3–5). TG Priesthood, Power of.power:
That your faith should not stand in the wisdom of men, but in the 2 Cor. 4:7.power of God.
Howbeit we speak wisdom among them that are TG Perfection.perfect: yet not the wisdom of this world, nor of the princes of this world, that come to nought:
But we speak the TG Wisdom.wisdom of God in a TG Mysteries of Godliness.mystery, even the hidden Col. 2:3. wisdom , which God GR foreordained.ordained before the world unto our glory:
Which none of the princes of this world knew: for had they known it, they would not have TG Jesus Christ, Crucifixion of.crucified the Lord of glory.
But as it is written, Isa. 64:4; D&C 76:10.Eye hath not seen, nor ear heard, neither have entered into the heart of man, the things which God hath TG Blessing; Reward.prepared for them that love him.

But God hath Alma 5:46 (45–48). TG Revelation.revealed them unto us by his 1 Thes. 4:8; 1 Jn. 3:24; D&C 42:17.Spirit: for the D&C 35:19.Spirit GR explores, investigates. D&C 63:59.searcheth all things, yea, the deep things of God.

For what man TG Education.knoweth the things of a man, save the spirit of man which is in him? even so the things of God Mark 6:52; John 1:5; Jacob 4:8 (8–10, 13); Alma 26:21 (21–22).knoweth no man, JST 1 Cor. 2:11 … except he has the Spirit of God.but the TG Holy Ghost, Source of Testimony.Spirit of God.
Now we have received, not the D&C 50:15.spirit of the world, but the spirit which is of God; that we might know the things that are freely given to us of God.
Which things also we speak, not in the words which man’s 2 Cor. 1:12.wisdom teacheth, but which the Holy Ghost TG Holy Ghost, Mission of.teacheth; comparing spiritual things with spiritual.

But the 2 Ne. 9:43 (42–43). TG Learn; Man, Natural, Not Spiritually Reborn.natural man TG Teachable.receiveth not the things of the TG God, Spirit of.Spirit of God: for they are Ether 12:25 (23–28, 38). TG Foolishness.foolishness unto him: neither can he TG God, Knowledge about; Knowledge.know them, because they are TG Spirituality.spiritually GR examined, tried, judged. TG Discernment, Spiritual.discerned.
But he that is spiritual GR examines, tries, judges.judgeth all things, yet he himself is judged of no man.
For who hath known the mind of the Lord, that he may Rom. 11:34; D&C 22:4. TG Counsel.instruct him? But we have the 1 Jn. 3:24 (19–24); 4:12 (7–21); Moses 4:6.mind of Christ.

div.p: Milk comes before meat in the Church—Men’s works will be tried by fire—The Saints are the temple of God, and if they are faithful, they will inherit all things.

chapter.verse: And I, brethren, could not speak unto you as unto spiritual, but as unto carnal, even as unto babes in Christ.
I have fed you with Heb. 5:12 (11–14); 1 Pet. 2:2 (1–3); D&C 19:22; 50:40.milk, and not with meat: for hitherto ye were not able to bear it, neither yet now are ye able.

For ye are yet TG Carnal Mind; Man, Natural, Not Spiritually Reborn.carnal: for whereas there is among you TG Envy.envying, and TG Strife.strife, and TG Apostasy of the Early Christian Church.divisions, are ye not carnal, and walk as men?
For while one saith, I am of Paul; and another, I am of Apollos; are ye not carnal?
Who then is Paul, and who is Apollos, but GR servants. 2 Cor. 3:6.ministers by whom ye believed, even as the Lord gave to every man?
I have John 4:37 (36–38); 1 Cor. 9:1.planted, Apollos watered; but God GR caused, gave growth.gave the Mark 4:27 (26–29).increase.
So then neither is he that planteth any thing, neither he that watereth; but God that giveth the increase.
Now he that planteth and he that watereth are one: and every man shall receive his own TG Reward; Wages.reward according to his own labour.
For we are 2 Cor. 6:1.labourers together with God: ye are God’s GR cultivated field, farm.husbandry, ye are God’s building.

According to the TG Grace.grace of God which is given unto me, as a wise masterbuilder, I have laid the foundation, and another buildeth thereon. But let every man take heed how he buildeth thereupon.
For other Matt. 16:18; 2 Ne. 4:30. TG Cornerstone.foundation can no man lay than that is laid, which is Jesus Christ.
Now if any man build upon this foundation gold, silver, precious stones, wood, hay, stubble;

Every man’s work shall be made manifest: for the day shall declare it, because it shall be revealed by fire; and the Mal. 3:2 (2–3).fire shall GR test, put to proof. TG Test.try every man’s work of what sort it is.
If any man’s work abide which he hath built thereupon, he shall receive a reward.
If any man’s work shall be burned, he shall suffer loss: but he himself JST 1 Cor. 3:15 … may …shall be saved; yet so as by fire.
Know ye not that ye are the TG Body, Sanctity of; Life, Sanctity of; Temple.temple of God, and that the TG God, Spirit of.Spirit of God dwelleth GR in, within, among.in you?

If any man GR spoil, corrupt, deprave. TG Cleanliness; Filthiness; Holy Ghost, Loss of; Pollution; Sacrilege; Uncleanness; Word of Wisdom.defile the temple of God, him shall God destroy; for the temple of God is TG Holiness; Sacred.holy, which temple ye are.
Let no man deceive himself. If any man among you TG Boast.seemeth to be wise in this world, let him become a 2 Ne. 9:42 (42–43).fool, that he may be TG Learn.wise.

For the TG God, Wisdom of; Knowledge; Wisdom.wisdom of this TG World; Worldliness.world is TG Foolishness.foolishness with God. For it is written, He taketh the wise in their own GR cunning, villainy.craftiness.
And again, The Lord TG God, Omniscience of.knoweth the thoughts of the wise, that they are GR deceptive, fruitless.vain.
Therefore let no man 1 Cor. 5:6; D&C 76:61 (58–62).glory in men. For all things are yours;
Whether D&C 76:99.Paul, or Apollos, or Cephas, or the world, or life, or death, or things present, or things to come; Luke 12:44.all are yours;
And ye are 2 Cor. 10:7.Christ’s; and Christ is God’s.

div.p: Christ’s ministers must be faithful—The Apostles suffer, minister, and keep the faith—The kingdom of God is not in word but in power.

chapter.verse: Let a man so account of us, as of the ministers of Christ, and stewards of the TG God, Knowledge about; Mysteries of Godliness.mysteries of God.
Moreover it is required in TG Delegation of Responsibility; Stewardship.stewards, that a man be found TG Trustworthiness.faithful.
But with me it is a very small thing that I should be judged of you, or of man’s judgment: yea, I judge not mine own self.
For I know nothing JST 1 Cor. 4:4 … against myself …by myself; yet am I not hereby justified: but he that TG Jesus Christ, Judge.judgeth me is the Lord.

Therefore judge nothing before the time, until the Lord come, who both will bring to light the D&C 123:13.hidden things of darkness, and will make Rom. 2:16.manifest the counsels of the hearts: and then shall every man have praise of God.

And these things, brethren, I have in a figure transferred to myself and to Apollos for your sakes; that ye might learn in us not to think of men above that which is written, that no one of you be GR proud, arrogant. D&C 38:24.puffed up for one against another.

For who maketh thee GR different from, superior to.to differ from another? and what hast thou that thou didst not Mosiah 4:19.receive? now if thou didst receive it, why dost thou glory, as if thou hadst not received it?
Now ye are full, now ye are rich, ye have reigned as kings without us: and I would to God ye did reign, that we also might reign with you.

For I think that God hath set forth us the GR last apostles. Rom. 1:1; 1 Ne. 11:34 (33–34).apostles last, as it were D&C 42:48.appointed to death: for we are made a spectacle unto the world, and to angels, and to men.
We are fools for Christ’s sake, but ye are wise in Christ; we are weak, but ye are strong; ye are honourable, but we are despised.
Even unto this present hour we both hunger, and thirst, and are naked, and are GR roughly treated, afflicted.buffeted, and have no certain dwellingplace;
