And TG Labor.labour, working with our TG Self-Sacrifice.own D&C 38:40.hands: being TG Malice; Reviling.reviled, we bless; being TG Persecution.persecuted, we GR endure patiently.suffer it:
Being defamed, we entreat: we are made as the filth of the world, and are the offscouring of all things unto this day.
I write not these things to TG Shame.shame you, but as my beloved 1 Thes. 2:11.sons I TG Warn.warn you.
For though ye have ten thousand instructors in Christ, yet have ye not many fathers: for in Christ Jesus I have begotten you through the TG Gospel.gospel.
Wherefore I beseech you, be ye GR imitators. 1 Cor. 11:1.followers of me.

For this cause have I sent unto you Acts 16:1; 1 Tim. 1:2.Timotheus, who is my beloved son, and faithful in the Lord, who shall bring you into remembrance of my ways which be in Christ, as I teach every where in every church.
Now some are TG Apostasy of the Early Christian Church.puffed up, as though I would not come to you.
But I will come to you shortly, if the Lord will, and will know, not the speech of them which are puffed up, but the power.
For the kingdom of God is not in word, but in 1 Cor. 1:18 (17–18); D&C 18:47; 42:14. TG Priesthood, Power of.power.
IE Which do you choose?What will ye? shall I come unto you with a rod, or in love, and in the spirit of GR gentleness, kindness.meekness?

div.p: The Church cannot fellowship sinners—Christ, our passover, was sacrificed for us.

chapter.verse: It is reported commonly that there is GR sexual immorality. TG Sexual Immorality.fornication among you, and such fornication as is not so much as named among the Gentiles, that one should have his father’s wife.
And ye are Alma 5:53 (53–56); 6:3.puffed up, and have not rather mourned, that he that hath done this deed might be taken away from among you.
For I verily, GR as it were.as absent in body, but present in spirit, have judged already, as though I were present, concerning him that hath so done this deed,
In the name of our Lord Jesus Christ, when ye are gathered together, JST 1 Cor. 5:4 … and have the Spirit, with …and my spirit, with the power of our Lord Jesus Christ,

To 1 Tim. 1:20; D&C 78:12 (11–12).deliver such an one unto Satan for the TG Punish.destruction of the flesh, that the spirit may be saved in the D&C 2:1; 19:3; 39:21; 45:12 (12–34); Moses 7:65 (59–67).day of the Lord Jesus.
Your GR boasting. 1 Cor. 1:31; 3:21 (18–21).glorying is not good. Know ye not that a little leaven leaveneth the whole lump?

D&C 43:11.Purge out therefore the old leaven, that ye may be a new lump, as ye are TG Bread, Unleavened.unleavened. For even Christ our TG Jesus Christ, Types of, in Anticipation; Passover.passover is sacrificed for us:
Therefore let us keep the Ex. 12:14 (14–17).feast, not with old TG Leaven.leaven, neither with the leaven of malice and wickedness; but with the unleavened bread of TG Sincere.sincerity and truth.
I wrote unto you in an TG Scriptures, Lost.epistle not to 2 Thes. 3:6 (6, 11–15).company with GR sexually immoral persons, male prostitutes. TG Fornication.fornicators:
Yet not altogether with the fornicators of this world, or with the TG Covet.covetous, or GR swindlers, robbers.extortioners, or with idolaters; for then must ye needs go out of the world.

But now I have written unto you not to keep Rom. 16:17.company, if any man that is called a brother be a fornicator, or covetous, or an idolater, or a railer, or a TG Drunkenness.drunkard, or an extortioner; with such an one GR not even.no not to 2 Jn. 1:10.eat.
For what have I to do to judge them also that are GR outside, foreign, not belonging.without? do not ye 1 Cor. 6:1.judge them that are within?
But them that are without God judgeth. Therefore put away from among yourselves that wicked person.

div.p: Church members should not fight one another in the courts—The unrighteous will not be saved—True Saints are the temple of the Holy Ghost.

chapter.verse: Dare any of you, having a Ex. 18:16.matter against another, go to Matt. 18:17; 1 Cor. 5:12; D&C 64:12 (9–12).law before the unjust, and not before the saints?

Do ye not know that the saints shall See JST Matt. 7:1–2 (Matt. 7:1 note a). Dan. 7:22; Rev. 20:4. TG Judgment, the Last.judge the TG World.world? and if the world shall be judged by you, are ye unworthy to judge the smallest matters?
Know ye not that we shall judge angels? how much more things that pertain to this life?
If then ye have judgments of things pertaining to this life, set them to judge who are least esteemed in the church.
I speak to your TG Shame.shame. Is it so, that there is not a wise man among you? no, not one that shall be able to judge between his brethren?
But brother goeth to law with brother, and that before the unbelievers.

Now therefore there is utterly a fault among you, because ye go to law one with another. Why do ye not rather take TG Retribution.wrong? why do ye not rather TG Forbear. suffer yourselves to be Luke 6:29 (29–30).defrauded?
Nay, ye do wrong, and TG Fraud; Injustice; Selfishness.defraud, and that your brethren.

Know ye not that the 1 Ne. 10:21; Alma 11:37; 40:26; Moses 6:57. TG Worthiness.unrighteous shall not John 3:5; 1 Cor. 15:50.inherit the kingdom of God? Be not deceived: neither GR sexually immoral persons, male prostitutes. TG Fornication.fornicators, nor idolaters, nor TG Adulterer; Sexual Immorality.adulterers, nor GR catamites.effeminate, nor GR male homosexuals. TG Homosexual Behavior.abusers of themselves with mankind,
Nor TG Stealing.thieves, nor covetous, nor TG Drunkenness; Word of Wisdom.drunkards, nor TG Reviling.revilers, nor GR robbers.extortioners, shall inherit the kingdom of God.

And such were some of you: but ye are TG Baptism; Wash.washed, but ye are TG Sanctification.sanctified, but ye are justified in the TG Jesus Christ, Taking the Name of; Name.name of the Lord Jesus, and by the TG God, Spirit of.Spirit of our God.

JST 1 Cor. 6:12 All these things are not lawful unto me, and all these things are not expedient. All things are not lawful for me, therefore I will not …All things are lawful unto me, but all things are not GR beneficial, advantageous.expedient: all things are lawful for me, but I will not be brought under the power of any.

Meats for the belly, and the belly for meats: but God shall destroy both it and them. Now the body is not for GR sexual immorality. TG Chastity; Fornication.fornication, but for the Lord; and the Lord for the body.
And God hath both raised up the Lord, and will also TG Resurrection.raise up us by his own power.
Know ye not that your bodies are the Eph. 5:30.members of Christ? shall I then take the members of Christ, and make them the members of an harlot? God forbid.
What? know ye not that he which is joined to an harlot is one body? for two, saith he, shall be TG Marriage, Marry.one flesh.
But he that is joined unto the Lord is 3 Ne. 19:23 (23, 29).one spirit.
Flee fornication. Every sin that a man doeth is without the body; but he that committeth fornication sinneth against his own body.
What? know ye not that your TG Body, Sanctity of; Chastity.body is the TG Temple.temple of the Holy Ghost which is in you, which ye have of God, and ye are not your 1 Cor. 7:23.own?
For ye are Ex. 15:16; 1 Pet. 1:18 (17–21); 2 Pet. 2:1.bought with a price: therefore glorify God in your body, and in your spirit, which are God’s.

div.p: Paul answers special questions about marriage among those called on missions—Paul praises self-discipline.

chapter.verse: Now concerning the things whereof ye wrote unto me: JST 1 Cor. 7:1 … saying, It is good … It is good for a man not to touch a woman.
Nevertheless, JST 1 Cor. 7:2 … I say, to avoid … to avoid TG Fornication.fornication, let every man have his own TG Marriage, Marry; Marriage, Wives.wife, and let every woman have her own husband.

Let the TG Marriage, Husbands.husband render unto the TG Marriage, Wives.wife due TG Benevolence; Family, Love within; Marriage, Continuing Courtship in.benevolence: and likewise also the wife unto the husband.
The wife hath not power of her own body, but the husband: and likewise also the husband hath not power of his own body, but the wife.

JST 1 Cor. 7:5 Depart ye not one from the other … TG Family, Love within; Fraud.Defraud ye not one the other, except it be with consent for a time, that ye may give yourselves to fasting and prayer; and come together again, that TG Devil.Satan TG Test.tempt you not for your incontinency.
But I speak this by permission, and not of commandment.
For I would that all men were even as I myself. But every man hath his GR own.proper TG God, Gifts of; Holy Ghost, Gifts of.gift of God, one after this manner, and another after that.
I say therefore to the unmarried and TG Widows.widows, It is good for them if they abide even as I.

JST 1 Cor. 7:9 But if they cannot abide, let them marry; for it is better to marry than that any should commit sin.But if they cannot GR use self-control.contain, let them marry: for it is better to marry than to GR burn with lust.burn.
And unto the married I command, yet not I, but the Lord, Let not the Matt. 5:32. TG Chastity; Family, Patriarchal.wife TG Divorce.depart from her husband:
But and if she depart, let her remain unmarried, or be TG Peacemakers; Reconciliation.reconciled to her husband: and let not the husband TG Divorce.put away his wife.
But to the rest speak I, not the Lord: If any brother hath a wife that believeth not, and she be pleased to dwell with him, let him not put her away.
And the woman which hath an TG Marriage, Husbands.husband that believeth not, and if he be pleased to dwell with her, let her not leave him.

D&C 74.For the unbelieving TG Marriage, Interfaith.husband is TG Sanctification.sanctified by the wife, and the unbelieving wife is GR cleansed.sanctified by the husband: else were your children unclean; but now are they holy.
But if the unbelieving depart, let him depart. A brother or a sister is not under bondage in such cases: but God hath called us GR in.to peace.
For what knowest thou, O wife, whether thou shalt TG Family, Love within.save thy husband? or how knowest thou, O man, whether thou shalt save thy wife?
But as Rom. 12:3.God hath distributed to every man, as the Lord hath called every one, so let him walk. And so GR order, prescribe.ordain I in all churches.
Is any man called being circumcised? let him not GR conceal, obliterate circumcision.become uncircumcised. Is any called in uncircumcision? let him not be circumcised.
TG Circumcision.Circumcision is nothing, and uncircumcision is nothing, but the keeping of the commandments of God.
Let every man abide in the same calling wherein he was called.
Art thou called being a servant? care not for it: but if thou mayest be made free, use it rather.
For he that is called in the Lord, being a servant, is the Lord’s Mosiah 5:8; D&C 88:86.freeman: likewise also he that is called, being free, is Christ’s Eph. 6:6; Col. 3:24.servant.
Ye are 1 Cor. 6:19 (19–20).bought with a price; be not ye the GR slaves.servants of men.
Brethren, let every man, wherein he is called, therein abide with God.
Now concerning virgins I have no commandment of the Lord: yet I give my judgment, as one that hath obtained 1 Tim. 1:13.mercy of the Lord to be faithful.

I suppose therefore that this is good for the present GR trial, affliction. JST 1 Cor. 7:26 … distress, for a man so to remain that he may do greater good.distress, I say, that it is good for a man so to be.
Art thou bound unto a wife? seek not to be loosed. Art thou loosed from a wife? seek not a wife.
But and if thou marry, thou hast not sinned; and if a virgin marry, she hath not sinned. Nevertheless such shall have trouble in the flesh: but I spare you.
JST 1 Cor. 7:29–33, 38 (Appendix).But this I say, brethren, the time is short: it remaineth, that both they that have wives be as though they had none;
And they that weep, as though they wept not; and they that rejoice, as though they rejoiced not; and they that buy, as though they possessed not;
And they that use this world, as not abusing it: for the James 4:14; 1 Jn. 2:17.fashion of this TG Worldliness.world passeth away.
But I would have you without GR cares.carefulness. He that is unmarried careth for the things that belong to the Lord, how he may please the Lord:
But he that is married careth for the things that are of the world, how he may please his wife.

There is difference also between a wife and a virgin. The unmarried woman careth for the things of the Lord, that she may be holy both in body and in spirit: but she that is married careth for the things of the world, how she may please her husband.
And this I speak for your own profit; not that I may cast a snare upon you, but for that which is comely, and that ye may attend upon the Lord without distraction.
But if any man think that he behaveth himself uncomely toward his virgin, if she pass the flower of her age, and need so require, let him do what he will, he sinneth not: let them marry.
Nevertheless he that standeth steadfast in his heart, having no necessity, but hath power over his own will, and hath so decreed in his heart that he will keep his virgin, doeth well.

JST 1 Cor. 7:38 So then he that giveth himself in marriage doeth well; but he that giveth himself not in marriage doeth better.So then he that giveth her in marriage doeth well; but he that giveth her not in marriage doeth better.
The TG Marriage, Wives.wife is bound by the TG Marriage, Temporal.law as long as her husband liveth; but if her husband be dead, she is at liberty to be married to whom she will; only in the Lord.
But she is happier if she so abide, after my judgment: and I think also that I have the Spirit of God.

div.p: There are many gods and many lords—To us there is one God (the Father) and one Lord, who is Christ.

chapter.verse: Now GR concerning.as touching things offered unto Acts 15:29.idols, we know that we all have knowledge. Knowledge puffeth up, but GR love. TG Charity.charity GR builds up, strengthens, establishes, repairs. TG Edification.edifieth.
And if any man think that he knoweth any thing, he knoweth nothing yet as he ought to TG Education; Study.know.
But if any man love God, the same is John 10:14.known of him.

As concerning therefore the eating of those things JST 1 Cor. 8:4 … which are in the world offered in sacrifice unto idols, we know that an idol is nothing, and …that are offered in sacrifice unto Gal. 4:8. TG Idolatry.idols, we know that an 1 Cor. 10:19 (19–20).idol is nothing in the world, and that there is none other God but one.
For though there be that are called gods, whether in heaven or in earth, (as there be gods many, and lords many,)

But to us there is but one TG Godhead.God, the Dan. 2:47; Eph. 4:6.Father, of whom are all things, and we in him; and one TG Jesus Christ, Lord.Lord Jesus Christ, by whom are TG Jesus Christ, Power of.all things, and we by him.
Howbeit there is not in every man that knowledge: for some with conscience of the idol unto this hour eat it as a thing offered unto an idol; and their TG Conscience.conscience being weak is defiled.
But TG Food.meat commendeth us not to God: for neither, if we eat, are we the better; neither, if we eat not, are we the worse.
But take heed lest by any means this TG Liberty; Motivations.liberty of yours become a Rom. 14:13; 1 Cor. 10:32 (24–33).stumblingblock to them that are Rom. 14:1.weak.
For if any man see thee which hast knowledge sit at meat in the idol’s temple, shall not the conscience of him which is weak be emboldened to eat those things which are offered to idols;
And through thy knowledge shall the weak brother perish, for whom Christ died?
But when ye sin so against the 1 Jn. 3:14 (10–18).brethren, and wound their weak conscience, ye sin against Christ.
Wherefore, if meat GR cause my brother to stumble, falter.make my brother to Rom. 14:21 (20–21).offend, I will TG Abstain.eat no flesh while the world standeth, lest I make my brother to offend.

div.p: Paul rejoices in his Christian liberty—He preaches the gospel to all without charge—He is all things to all men to gain converts.

chapter.verse: Am I not an 2 Cor. 12:11 (11–12); Gal. 2:7 (7–8); 1 Tim. 2:7.apostle? am I not free? have I not Acts 9:17 (3, 17); 2 Cor. 12:1 (1, 7); Gal. 1:12.seen Jesus Christ our Lord? are not ye my 1 Cor. 3:6.work in the Lord?
If I be not an apostle unto others, yet doubtless I am to you: for the GR certification, proof, token. 2 Cor. 3:2.seal of mine apostleship are ye in the Lord.
Mine GR defense.answer to them that do GR question, call into account, judge.examine me is this,
Have we not power to eat and to drink?
Have we not power to lead about a sister, a Matt. 8:14.wife, as well as other apostles, and as the Matt. 12:46; John 7:3 (3, 5, 10).brethren of the Lord, and Cephas?
Or I only and Barnabas, have not we power to forbear working?

Who goeth a 2 Cor. 10:4.warfare any time at his own charges? who planteth a Deut. 20:6.vineyard, and Prov. 27:18.eateth not of the fruit thereof? or who feedeth a flock, and eateth not of the milk of the flock?
Say I these things as a man? or saith not the law the same also?
For it is written in the law of Moses, Thou shalt not Deut. 25:4.muzzle the mouth of the ox that treadeth out the corn. Doth God take care for oxen?

Or saith he it altogether for our sakes? For our sakes, no doubt, this is Rom. 15:4.written: that he that ploweth should plow in hope; and that he that thresheth in hope should be 2 Tim. 2:6 (5–6).partaker of his hope.
If we have sown unto you Rom. 15:27.spiritual things, is it a great thing if we shall reap your carnal things?
If others be partakers of this power over you, are not we rather? Nevertheless we have not used this GR authority.power; but suffer all things, lest we should hinder the gospel of Christ.
Do ye not know that they which Deut. 18:1 (1–2).minister about holy things GR eat.live of the things of the temple? and they which wait at the altar are partakers with the altar?
Even so hath the Lord ordained that they which Rom. 2:21 (21–23); Alma 39:11 (11–12).preach the TG Gospel.gospel should Matt. 10:10.live of the gospel.
But I have used none of these things: neither have I written these things, that it should be so done unto me: for it were better for me to die, than that any man should make my glorying void.
For though I preach the TG Gospel.gospel, I have nothing to glory of: for TG Duty.necessity is laid upon me; yea, woe is unto me, if I TG Missionary Work; Preaching.preach not the gospel!
For if I do this thing TG Initiative.willingly, I have a TG Reward.reward: but if against my will, a Gal. 2:7. TG Dispensations.dispensation of the gospel is committed unto me.

What is my reward then? Verily that, when I preach the gospel, I may make the gospel of Christ without Acts 20:34 (33–34); 2 Cor. 11:7; 1 Thes. 2:9; Mosiah 2:14 (12, 14–18).charge, that I abuse not my power in the gospel.
For though I be free from all men, yet have I made myself 2 Cor. 4:5. TG Servant.servant unto all, that I might gain the more.
And unto the Jews I became as a Jew, that I might gain the Jews; to them that are under the law, as under the law, that I might gain them that are under the law;
To them that are without law, as without law, (being not without law to God, but under the law to Christ,) that I might gain them that are without law.
To the weak became I as 2 Cor. 11:29.weak, that I might gain the weak: I am made 1 Cor. 10:33 (32–33).all things to all men, that I might by all means save some.
And this I do for the gospel’s sake, that I might be partaker thereof with you.
Know ye not that they which run in a race run all, but one receiveth the prize? So run, that ye may obtain.

And every man that striveth for the 2 Tim. 2:5. TG Self-Mastery.mastery is TG Temperance.temperate in all things. Now they do it to obtain a corruptible TG Reward.crown; but we an 1 Pet. 1:4 (1–16).incorruptible.
I therefore so run, not as uncertainly; so fight I, not as one that beateth the air:
But I GR rigorously discipline.keep under my TG Chastity.body, and bring it into subjection: lest that by any means, when I have preached to others, I myself should be a castaway.

div.p: Christ is the God of Israel and the spiritual Rock that guided them—Ancient Israel rebelled against Christ—Paul contrasts true and false sacraments.

chapter.verse: Moreover, brethren, I would not that ye should be ignorant, how that all our fathers were under the Ps. 99:7; D&C 103:20 (19–20).cloud, and all passed through the TG Israel, Deliverance of.sea;
And were all TG Baptism; Baptism, Essential.baptized unto Moses in the cloud and in the sea;
And did all eat the same spiritual Ex. 16:15.meat;

And did all drink the same spiritual drink: for they drank of that spiritual Rock that followed them: and that 3 Ne. 15:5. TG Cornerstone; Jesus Christ, Jehovah; Jesus Christ, Types of, in Anticipation; Rock.Rock was Christ.
But with many of them Num. 21:5; Ezek. 20:13 (10–26).God was not well D&C 68:31.pleased: for they were GR strewn, buried. Heb. 3:17.overthrown in the Num. 26:65 (64–65); Jude 1:5.wilderness.
Now these things were our GR types.examples, to the intent we should not GR desire, long for. TG Lust.lust after evil things, as they also lusted.
Neither be ye idolaters, as were some of them; as it is written, The Ex. 32:6.people sat down to eat and drink, and rose up to play.
Neither let us commit TG Fornication.fornication, as some of them committed, and fell in one day three and twenty thousand.
Neither let us TG Test.tempt Christ, as some of them also tempted, and were destroyed of serpents.
Neither TG Murmuring.murmur ye, as some of them also murmured, and were Num. 14:37.destroyed of the destroyer.

Now all these things happened unto them for GR types. TG Example.ensamples: JST 1 Cor. 10:11 … and they were written for our admonition also, and for an admonition for those upon whom the end of the world shall come.and they are Rom. 15:4.written for our TG Warn.admonition, upon whom the ends of the world are come.
Wherefore let him that thinketh he Philip. 4:1 (1–7).standeth take heed lest he Acts 1:25; D&C 3:9; 58:15.fall.

There hath no temptation GR seized upon.taken you but such as is common to man: but God is faithful, who will not suffer you to be Job 4:7 (1, 2, 7); 37:23; Ps. 34:17 (17, 19); 1 Ne. 22:19; Alma 14:11. TG Test.tempted above that ye are able; but will with the TG Temptation.temptation also make a way to D&C 95:1.escape, that ye may be able to TG Strength.bear it.
Wherefore, my dearly beloved, flee from TG Idolatry.idolatry.
I speak as to wise men; judge ye what I say.

The Matt. 26:27 (26–29).cup of TG Blessing.blessing which we bless, is it not the GR aid, fellowship, partnership.communion of the blood of Christ? The bread which we break, is it not the communion of the body of Christ?
For we being many are one bread, and one body: for we are all partakers of that one bread.
Behold Israel after the flesh: are not they which eat of the sacrifices GR sharers, partners.partakers of the altar?
What say I then? that the idol is any thing, or that which is offered in sacrifice to 1 Cor. 8:4.idols is any thing?
But I say, that the things which the Gentiles sacrifice, they Deut. 32:17. TG Sacrifice; Superstitions.sacrifice to devils, and not to God: and I would not that ye should have fellowship with devils.
Ye cannot drink the 2 Cor. 6:16 (14–17).cup of the Lord, and the cup of devils: ye cannot be partakers of the Lord’s table, and of the table of devils.
Do we provoke the Lord to TG Jealous.jealousy? are we stronger than he?

JST 1 Cor. 10:23 All things are not lawful for me, for all things are not expedient; all things are not lawful, for all things edify not.All things are lawful for me, but all things are not GR advantageous, appropriate, beneficial.expedient: all things are lawful for me, but all things TG Edification.edify not.
Let no man seek his Philip. 2:21 (17–30).own, but every man another’s JST 1 Cor. 10:24 … good. wealth .
Whatsoever is sold in the GR market.shambles, that eat, asking no question for conscience sake:
For the Ps. 24:1. TG God, Works of.earth is the Lord’s, and the fulness thereof.
If any of them that believe not bid you to a Ex. 34:15. feast , and ye be disposed to go; whatsoever is set before you, eat, asking no question for conscience sake.
But if any man say unto you, This is offered in sacrifice unto idols, eat not for his sake that shewed it, and for conscience sake: for the earth is the Lord’s, and the fulness thereof:
Conscience, I say, not thine own, but of the other: for why is my liberty judged of another man’s TG Conscience.conscience?
For if I GR with gratitude, graciousness.by grace be a partaker, why am I evil spoken of for that for which I give thanks?
Whether therefore ye eat, or drink, or whatsoever ye do, Col. 3:17 (17–23).do all to the glory of God.
Give none Rom. 14:13 (11–21); 1 Cor. 8:9 (7–13). TG Offense.offence, neither to the Jews, nor to the Gentiles, nor to the church of God:
Even as I please all men in all things, not 2 Cor. 12:14.seeking mine own profit, but the GR benefit, advantage. profit of many, that they may be 1 Cor. 9:22 (19–23).saved.

div.p: Paul speaks of certain customs of hair and grooming—Heresies will arise that test and prove the faithful—The sacramental emblems are partaken in remembrance of the flesh and blood of Christ—Beware of partaking unworthily.

chapter.verse: Be ye GR imitators. 1 Cor. 4:16; Eph. 5:1 (1–5).followers of me, even as I also am of Christ.
Now I praise you, brethren, GR because.that ye remember me in all things, and keep the GR precepts, doctrines, traditions. TG Ordinance.ordinances, as I delivered them to you.

But I would have you know, that the head of every man is Christ; and the TG Marriage, Husbands.head of the TG Marriage, Wives; Woman.woman is the man; and the TG Jesus Christ, Relationships with the Father.head of Christ is God.
Every man praying or prophesying, having his head covered, dishonoureth his head.
But every woman that prayeth or prophesieth with her head uncovered dishonoureth her head: for that is GR one and the same as.even all one as if she were shaven.
For if the woman be not covered, let her also be shorn: but if it be a TG Shame.shame for a woman to be shorn or shaven, let her be covered.
For a man indeed ought not to cover his head, forasmuch as he is the image and glory of God: but the woman is the glory of the man.
For the man is not of the woman; but the woman of the man.
Neither was the man created for the woman; but the woman for the man.
For this cause ought the woman to have power on her head because of the angels.

Nevertheless neither is the TG Family, Eternal; Marriage, Celestial; Marriage, Continuing Courtship in; Marriage, Husbands.man without the woman, neither the TG Marriage, Wives.woman without the man, in the Lord.
For as the woman is of the man, even so is the man also by the woman; but all things of God.
Judge in yourselves: is it GR proper, fitting, becoming.comely that a woman pray unto God uncovered?
Doth not even nature itself teach you, that, if a man have long Ezek. 44:20.hair, it is a shame unto him?
But if a woman have long hair, it is a glory to her: for her hair is given her for a covering.
But if any man seem to be 1 Cor. 14:33 (33, 40); 3 Ne. 11:22. TG Contention.contentious, we have no such custom, neither the churches of God.
Now in this that I declare unto you I praise you not, that ye come together not for the better, but for the worse.

For first of all, when ye come together in the TG Assembly for Worship; Meetings.church, I hear that there be GR dissensions, schisms. TG Apostasy of the Early Christian Church; Church.divisions among you; and I partly believe it.
For there must be also GR sects, factions.heresies among you, that they which are approved may be made manifest among you.
When ye come together therefore into one place, JST 1 Cor. 11:20 … is it not to eat the Lord’s supper? this is not to eat the Lord’s supper.
For in eating every one taketh before other his own supper: and one is hungry, and another is drunken.

What? have ye not houses to eat and to drink in? or despise ye the TG Church.church of God, and TG Shame.shame them that have not? What shall I say to you? shall I praise you in this? I praise you not.
For I have received of the Lord that which also I delivered unto you, That the Lord Jesus the same night in which he was betrayed took 3 Ne. 20:8 (3–9).bread:
And when he had given thanks, he brake it, and said, Take, eat: this is my body, which is broken for you: this do in TG Jesus Christ, Types of, in Memory.remembrance of me.
After the same manner also he took the cup, when he had supped, saying, This cup is the new testament in my blood: this do ye, as oft as ye drink it, in remembrance of me.
For as often as ye eat this TG Sacrament.bread, and drink this cup, ye do GR proclaim, announce.shew the Lord’s TG Jesus Christ, Death of.death till he come.

Wherefore whosoever shall eat this TG Bread of Life.bread, and drink this cup of the Lord, 3 Ne. 18:29 (28–32); Morm. 9:29.unworthily, shall GR offend against.be TG Guilt.guilty of the body and blood of the Lord.
But let a man 2 Cor. 13:5 (5–7).examine himself, and so let him eat of that bread, and drink of that cup.
For he that eateth and drinketh TG Sacrament.unworthily, eateth and drinketh GR condemnation, judgment. TG Damnation.damnation to himself, not discerning the Lord’s body.
For this cause many are weak and TG Sickness.sickly among you, and many sleep.
For if we would GR scrutinize, examine.judge ourselves, we should not be judged.
But when we are judged, we are TG Chastening.chastened of the Lord, that we should not be condemned with the world.
Wherefore, my brethren, when ye come together to eat, tarry one for another.
And if any man hunger, let him eat at home; that ye come not together unto condemnation. And the rest will I set in order when I come.

div.p: The Holy Ghost reveals that Jesus is the Christ—Spiritual gifts are present among the Saints—Apostles, prophets, and miracles are found in the true Church.

chapter.verse: Now concerning spiritual gifts, brethren, I would not have you ignorant.
Ye know that ye were Gentiles, GR led astray.carried away unto these dumb TG Idolatry.idols, even as ye were led.

Wherefore I give you to understand, that no man speaking by the Spirit of God calleth Jesus accursed: and that no man can TG Revelation; Testimony.say that Jesus is the TG Jesus Christ, Lord.Lord, but by the John 6:44 (44, 63–65). TG Holy Ghost, Mission of; Holy Ghost, Source of Testimony; Inspiration.Holy Ghost.
Now there are diversities of TG God, Gifts of; Holy Ghost, Gifts of; Holy Ghost, Mission of.gifts, but the same Spirit.
And there are differences of administrations, but the same Lord.
And there are diversities of operations, but it is the same God which worketh all in all.
But the manifestation of the D&C 46:16 (8–18). TG God, Spirit of.Spirit is given to every man to profit withal.
For to one is D&C 35:23.given by the D&C 25:7; 35:19.Spirit the D&C 89:2.word of TG Wisdom.wisdom; to another the word of Moro. 10:10. TG Holy Ghost, Gifts of; Knowledge.knowledge by the same Spirit;
To another TG Faith.faith by the same Spirit; to another the gifts of TG Heal.healing by the same Spirit;

To another the working of TG Miracle.miracles; to another TG Prophecy.prophecy; to another TG Discernment, Spiritual.discerning of spirits; to another divers kinds of tongues; to another the interpretation of TG Language.tongues:
But all these worketh that one and the selfsame Spirit, dividing to every man severally as he will.
For as the body is one, and hath many members, and all the members of that one body, being many, are one body: so also is Christ.

For by one Spirit are we all baptized into one TG Church.body, whether we be Jews or GR Greeks. Col. 3:11.Gentiles, whether we be GR slaves. D&C 24:11; 43:20.bond or free; and have been all made to drink into one Spirit.
For the body is not one member, but many.
If the foot shall say, Because I am not the hand, I am not of the body; is it therefore not of the body?
And if the ear shall say, Because I am not the eye, I am not of the body; is it therefore not of the body?
If the whole body were an eye, where were the hearing? If the whole were hearing, where were the smelling?
But now hath God set the members every one of them in the body, as it hath pleased him.
And if they were all one member, where were the body?
But now are they many members, yet but one body.
And the eye cannot say unto the hand, I have no need of thee: nor again the D&C 84:109. TG Leadership.head to the feet, I have no need of you.
Nay, much more those members of the body, which seem to be GR weaker.more feeble, are necessary:
And those members of the body, which we think to be less honourable, upon these we bestow more abundant honour; and our uncomely parts have more abundant comeliness.
For our comely parts have no need: but God hath tempered the body together, having given more abundant honour to that part which lacked:
That there should be no schism in the body; but that the members should have the same 1 Cor. 1:10 (10–15).care one for another.
And whether one member TG Suffering.suffer, all the members TG Compassion.suffer with it; or one member be honoured, all the members rejoice with it.
Now ye are the body of TG Jesus Christ, Head of the Church.Christ, and TG Church Organization.members in particular.

And God hath set some in the church, first TG Apostles.apostles, secondarily TG Prophets, Mission of.prophets, thirdly TG Teacher.teachers, after that miracles, then gifts of healings, helps, TG Governments.governments, diversities of tongues.
Are all apostles? are all prophets? are all teachers? are all workers of TG Miracle.miracles?
Have all the gifts of healing? do all speak with tongues? do all interpret?
But GR seek earnestly, be zealous for. D&C 46:8 (8–9). TG Covet.covet earnestly the best TG Holy Ghost, Gifts of.gifts: and yet shew I unto you a more Ether 12:11 (11–12, 32).excellent way.

div.p: Paul discusses the high status of charity—Charity, a pure love, excels and exceeds almost all else.

chapter.verse: Though I speak with the tongues of men and of angels, and have not GR love.charity, I am become as sounding brass, or a tinkling cymbal.

And though I have the gift of TG Prophecy.prophecy, and understand all TG Mysteries of Godliness.mysteries, and all knowledge; and though I have all faith, so that I could remove mountains, and have not charity, I am nothing.
And though I bestow all my goods to feed the TG Welfare. poor , and though I give my body to be burned, and have not charity, it profiteth me nothing.
TG Charity; Courtesy.Charity TG Forbear; Suffering.suffereth long, and is TG Benevolence.kind; charity TG Envy.envieth not; charity vaunteth not itself, is not puffed up,
Doth not behave itself GR indecently, unbecomingly.unseemly, TG Selfishness.seeketh not her own, is not easily TG Anger; Provoking; Self-Mastery.provoked, thinketh no evil;
Rejoiceth not in GR injustice, unrighteousness.iniquity, but rejoiceth in the TG Truth.truth;
Beareth all things, believeth all things, hopeth all things, endureth all things.
Charity never TG Dependability.faileth: but whether there be prophecies, they shall fail; whether there be tongues, they shall cease; whether there be knowledge, it shall vanish away.
For we know in part, and we prophesy in part.
But when that which is perfect is come, then that which is in part shall be done away.
When I was a child, I spake as a child, I understood as a child, I thought as a child: but when I became a man, I put away childish things.
For now we see through a 2 Cor. 3:18; James 1:23 (22–27). TG Veil.glass, GR obscurely, enigmatically.darkly; but then face to face: now I know in part; but then shall I know even as also I am known.
And now abideth TG Faith.faith, TG Hope.hope, TG Love.charity, these three; but the greatest of these is charity.

div.p: People should desire spiritual gifts—Tongues and prophecy are compared—Prophecy is the greater gift—Paul says, You may all prophesy; covet to prophesy.

chapter.verse: GR Pursue, follow eagerly, earnestly.Follow after charity, and desire spiritual TG Holy Ghost, Gifts of. gifts , but rather that ye may prophesy.

For he that speaketh in JST 1 Cor. 14:2 … another tongue … (Note: JST uses “another” in place of “an unknown” in every instance in vv. 4, 13, 14, 19, 27.)an unknown tongue speaketh not unto men, but unto God: for no man understandeth him; howbeit in the spirit he speaketh mysteries.
But he that TG Prophecy.prophesieth speaketh unto men to TG Edification.edification, and exhortation, and comfort.
He that speaketh in an unknown tongue edifieth himself; but he that prophesieth edifieth the church.

I GR wish.would that ye all spake with tongues, but rather that ye TG Prophecy.prophesied: for greater is he that prophesieth than he that speaketh with tongues, except he interpret, that the church may receive edifying.

Now, brethren, if I come unto you speaking with tongues, what shall I profit you, GR unless.except I shall speak to you either by TG Revelation.revelation, or by knowledge, or by prophesying, or by doctrine?
And even things without life giving sound, whether pipe or harp, except they give a distinction in the sounds, how shall it be known what is piped or harped?
For if the trumpet give an uncertain sound, who shall prepare himself to the battle?
So likewise ye, except ye utter by the tongue words easy to be understood, how shall it be known what is spoken? for ye shall speak into the air.
There are, it may be, so many kinds of TG Revelation.voices in the world, and none of them is without signification.
Therefore if I know not the meaning of the voice, I shall be unto him that speaketh a barbarian, and he that speaketh shall be a barbarian unto me.
Even so ye, GR since.forasmuch as ye are zealous of spiritual D&C 46:10. gifts , seek that ye may excel to the TG Edification.edifying of the church.
Wherefore let him that speaketh in an unknown tongue pray that he may interpret.
For if I pray in an unknown tongue, my spirit prayeth, but my GR mind.understanding is unfruitful.
What is it then? I will pray with the spirit, and I will pray with the understanding also: I will sing with the spirit, and I will sing with the understanding also.
Else when thou shalt bless with the spirit, how shall he that occupieth the room of the unlearned say Amen at thy giving of thanks, seeing he understandeth not what thou sayest?
For thou verily givest thanks well, but the other is not edified.
I thank my God, I speak with tongues more than ye all:
Yet in the church I had rather TG Language.speak five words with my understanding, that by my voice I might teach others also, than ten thousand words in an unknown tongue.

Brethren, be not Matt. 18:3 (1–6); Eph. 4:14.children in TG Understanding.understanding: howbeit in GR wickedness, depravity. TG Malice.malice be ye children, but in TG Education.understanding be men.
In the law it is written, With men of other tongues and other lips will I speak unto this people; and yet for all that will they not GR listen to, heed.hear me, saith the Lord.

Wherefore Acts 2:11.tongues are for a TG Signs.sign, not to them that believe, but to them that believe not: but TG Prophecy.prophesying serveth not for them that believe not, but for them which believe.

If therefore the whole church be come together into one place, and all speak with tongues, and there come in those that are unlearned, or Acts 2:13 (12–13).unbelievers, will they not say that ye are mad?
But if all prophesy, and there come in one that believeth not, or one unlearned, he is convinced of all, he is judged of all:
And thus are the Heb. 4:12 (12–13).secrets of his heart made manifest; and so falling down on his face he will worship God, and report that God is in you of a truth.

How is it then, brethren? when ye come together, every one of you hath a psalm, hath a doctrine, hath a tongue, hath a revelation, hath an interpretation. Let all things be done unto 2 Cor. 12:19; D&C 50:23.edifying.
If any man speak in an unknown tongue, let it be by two, or at the most by three, and that by course; and let one interpret.
But if there be no interpreter, let him keep TG Silence.silence in the church; and let him speak to himself, and to God.
Let the prophets speak two or three, and let the other judge.
If any thing be revealed to another that sitteth by, let the first hold his peace.
For ye may all prophesy one by one, that all may learn, and all may be comforted.
And the spirits of the prophets are subject to the prophets.
For God is not the 1 Cor. 11:16 (16–19). author of Eph. 4:5 (3–6), 13; D&C 1:30.confusion, but of TG Peace; Peace of God.peace, as in all TG Church.churches of the TG Saints.saints.

Let your TG Woman.women keep silence in the churches: for it is not permitted unto them to JST 1 Cor. 14:34 … rule …speak; but they are commanded GR to be submissive.to be under TG Obedience.obedience, as also saith the law.
And if they will learn any thing, let them ask their husbands at home: for it is a shame for women to JST 1 Cor. 14:35 … rule …speak in the church.
What? came the word of God out from you? or came it unto you only?
If any man think himself to be a prophet, or spiritual, let him acknowledge that the things that I write unto you are the commandments of the Lord.
But if any man be ignorant, let him be ignorant.
Wherefore, brethren, IE be eager, zealous. TG Covet.covet to prophesy, and forbid not to speak with tongues.
Let all things be done decently and in TG Order.order.

div.p: Christ died for our sins—He rose from the dead and was seen by many—All men will be resurrected—Paul speaks of baptism for the dead—The three degrees of glory are described—Victory over death comes through Christ.

chapter.verse: Moreover, brethren, I TG Missionary Work.declare unto you the TG Gospel.gospel which I preached unto you, which also ye have received, and wherein ye stand;
By which also ye are Rom. 1:16.saved, if ye GR hold fast to, retain.keep in memory what I preached unto you, unless ye have believed in vain.

For I delivered unto you first of all that which I also received, how that Christ D&C 54:1. TG Jesus Christ, Death of; Jesus Christ, Redeemer.died for our Acts 3:18; Alma 5:27.sins according to the scriptures;
And that he was buried, and that he TG Jesus Christ, Resurrection.rose again the third day according to the scriptures:
And that he was TG Jesus Christ, Appearances, Postmortal.seen of Luke 24:34 (33–36).Cephas, then of the twelve:

After that, he was TG Jesus Christ, Appearances, Postmortal.seen of GR more than, over.above five hundred brethren at once; of whom the greater part remain unto this present, but some are fallen asleep.
After that, he was seen of James; then of all the apostles.
And last of all he was TG Jesus Christ, Appearances, Postmortal.seen of me also, as of one born out of due time.
For I am the least of the apostles, that am not GR adequate, sufficient, competent.meet to be called an apostle, because I Acts 8:3.persecuted the church of God.

But by the TG Grace.grace of God I am what I am: and his grace which was bestowed upon me was not in vain; but I 2 Cor. 11:23.laboured more abundantly than they all: yet not I, but the grace of God which was with me.
Therefore whether it were I or they, so we preach, and so ye believed.
Now if Christ be preached that he rose from the dead, how say some among you that there is no resurrection of the dead?
But if there be no resurrection of the dead, then is Christ not risen:
And if Christ be not risen, then is our preaching vain, and your faith is also vain.
Yea, and we are found false witnesses of God; because we have Acts 4:33.testified of God that he raised up Christ: whom he raised not up, if so be that the dead rise not.
For if the dead rise not, then is not Christ raised:
And if Christ be not raised, your faith is GR useless, empty, ineffective.vain; ye are yet in your sins.
Then they also which are fallen asleep in Christ are GR lost, destroyed.perished.
If in this TG Salvation for the Dead.life only we have TG Hope.hope in Christ, we are of all men most miserable.
But now is 1 Pet. 1:3.Christ Eph. 1:20.risen from the dead, and become the GR firstling.firstfruits of them that slept.
For since by man came Rom. 5:12. TG Death.death, by man came also the TG Resurrection.resurrection of the dead.

For as in TG Adam; Fall of Man.Adam all Ps. 82:7.die, even so in TG Salvation, Plan of.Christ shall all be made TG Immortality; Jesus Christ, Atonement through; Jesus Christ, Mission of; Jesus Christ, Resurrection; Salvation.alive.
But every man in his own GR rank. TG Order.order: Christ the GR firstling. TG Resurrection.firstfruits; afterward they that are Christ’s at his TG Jesus Christ, Second Coming.coming.

Then cometh the end, when he shall have delivered up the kingdom to God, even the Father; when he shall have GR brought to an end, abolished.put down all Isa. 60:12; Dan. 2:44; 7:9.rule and all TG Authority.authority and TG Jesus Christ, Power of.power.
For he must TG Jesus Christ, Millennial Reign.reign, till he hath put all D&C 88:106.enemies under his Heb. 2:8.feet.
The last TG Enemies.enemy that shall be TG Death, Power over.destroyed is 2 Tim. 1:10; Rev. 20:14 (11–15).death.
For he hath put all things under his feet. But when he saith all things are put under him, it is GR clear, plain, evident.manifest that he is excepted, which did put all things under him.

And when all things shall be Philip. 3:21; D&C 76:106.subdued unto him, then shall the Son also himself be TG Jesus Christ, Relationships with the Father.subject unto him that put all things under him, that God may be all in all.

Else what shall they do which are TG Baptism, Essential; Baptism for the Dead; Genealogy and Temple Work.baptized GR in behalf of, for the sake of.for the dead, if the dead TG Immortality.rise not at all? why are they then baptized for the TG Salvation for the Dead.dead?
And why stand we in GR danger, peril.jeopardy every hour?

JST 1 Cor. 15:31 I protest unto you the resurrection of the dead; and this is my rejoicing which I have in Christ Jesus our Lord daily, though I die.I protest by your rejoicing which I have in Christ Jesus our Lord, I die daily.

If after the manner of men I have fought with beasts at Ephesus, what advantageth it me, if the dead rise not? let us Isa. 22:13; Luke 12:19; 2 Ne. 28:7.eat and drink; for TG Procrastination.to morrow we die.
Be not deceived: evil GR conversations, associations.communications corrupt good manners.
Awake to righteousness, and sin not; for some have not the knowledge of God: I speak this to your TG Shame.shame.
But some man will say, How are the dead raised up? and with what body do they come?
Thou fool, that which thou sowest is not quickened, except it John 12:24 (24–29).die:
And that which thou sowest, thou sowest not that body that shall be, but bare grain, IE whether it be.it may chance of wheat, or of some other grain:
But God giveth it a body as it hath pleased him, and to every seed his own body.
All flesh is not the same flesh: but there is one kind of flesh of men, another flesh of beasts, another of fishes, and another of birds.

JST 1 Cor. 15:40 Also celestial bodies, and bodies terrestrial, and bodies telestial; but the glory of the celestial, one; and the terrestrial, another; and the telestial, another. There are also celestial bodies, and bodies terrestrial: but the glory of the TG Celestial Glory.celestial is one, and the glory of the TG Terrestrial Glory.terrestrial is another.
There is one glory of the TG Astronomy.sun, and another glory of the moon, and another glory of the TG Telestial Glory.stars: for one star D&C 76:98.differeth from another star in TG Glory.glory.
So also is the resurrection of the dead. It is sown in Rom. 8:21; Mosiah 16:10.corruption; it is raised in incorruption:
It is sown in dishonour; it is raised in Col. 3:4.glory: it is sown in weakness; it is raised in power:
It is sown a D&C 29:43.natural body; it is raised a Alma 11:45; D&C 88:27.spiritual body. There is a natural body, and there is a spiritual body.
And so it is written, The D&C 84:16.first man TG Adam.Adam was made a living soul; the last Rom. 5:14.Adam was made a John 5:21.quickening spirit.
Howbeit that was not D&C 29:32; 128:14 (13–14).first which is spiritual, but that which is natural; and afterward that which is spiritual.
The first man is of the earth, earthy: the second man is the Lord from heaven.
As is the earthy, such are they also that are earthy: and as is the heavenly, such are they also that are heavenly.
And as we have borne the image of the earthy, we shall also bear the image of the heavenly.
Now this I say, brethren, that TG Flesh and Blood.flesh and Lev. 17:14 (11, 14).blood cannot John 3:5; 1 Cor. 6:9.inherit the kingdom of God; neither doth Alma 41:4.corruption inherit incorruption.
Behold, I shew you a mystery; We shall not all GR die, sleep in death. 1 Thes. 4:17.sleep, but we shall all be Philip. 3:21; D&C 43:32.changed,

In a moment, in the D&C 63:51 (50–51).twinkling of an eye, at the last trump: for the D&C 29:26.trumpet shall sound, and the TG Death.dead shall be raised incorruptible, and we shall be D&C 101:31.changed.
For this Alma 41:4.corruptible must put on incorruption, and this TG Mortality.mortal must put on immortality.

So when this corruptible shall have put on incorruption, and this mortal shall have put on TG Immortality.immortality, then shall be brought to pass the saying that is written, Isa. 25:8.Death is swallowed up in victory.
O Mosiah 16:8 (7–8).death, where is thy sting? O GR Hades, hell.grave, where is thy victory?
The sting of D&C 42:45–46.death is sin; and the GR power.strength of sin is the law.
But TG Thanksgiving.thanks be to God, which giveth us the 1 Jn. 5:4 (4–5).victory through our Lord Jesus Christ.
Therefore, my beloved brethren, be ye TG Perseverance; Steadfastness.steadfast, unmoveable, always abounding in the work of the Lord, forasmuch as ye know that your labour is not in vain in the Lord.

div.p: Paul counsels, Stand fast in the faith; let all things be done with charity.

chapter.verse: Now concerning the Acts 24:17 (17, 26).collection for the saints, as I have GR directed, arranged.given order to the churches of Galatia, even so do ye.
Upon the TG Sabbath.first day of the week let every one of you lay by him in store, as God hath Deut. 16:10.prospered him, that there be no gatherings when I come.
And when I come, whomsoever ye shall approve by your letters, them will I send to bring your GR gift. TG Generosity.liberality unto Jerusalem.
And if it be GR suitable, worthwhile.meet that I go also, they shall go with me.
Now I will come unto you, when I shall pass through Macedonia: for I do pass through Macedonia.
And it may be that I will abide, yea, and winter with you, that ye may bring me on my journey whithersoever I go.
For I will not see you now GR in passing.by the way; but I GR hope.trust to 2 Cor. 1:15 (15–16).tarry a while with you, if the Lord permit.
But I will tarry at Ephesus until Pentecost.
For a great Col. 4:3.door and GR energetic, efficient.effectual is opened unto me, and there are many adversaries.
Now if 2 Cor. 1:19; 1 Thes. 3:2.Timotheus come, see that he may be with you without fear: for he worketh the work of the Lord, as I also do.
Let no man therefore 1 Tim. 4:12.despise him: but conduct him forth in peace, that he may come unto me: for I look for him with the brethren.

As touching our brother Acts 18:24.Apollos, I greatly desired him to come unto you with the brethren: but his will was not at all to come at this time; but he will come when he shall have convenient time.
TG Watch.Watch ye, Philip. 4:1 (1–7).stand fast in the D&C 9:14.faith, GR behave like men.quit you like 1 Sam. 4:9; Isa. 46:8.men, be strong.
Let all your things be done with TG Charity.charity.

I beseech you, brethren, (ye know the house of 1 Cor. 1:16.Stephanas, that it is the firstfruits of Achaia, and that they have GR devoted, appointed.addicted themselves to the ministry of the saints,)
That ye submit yourselves unto such, and to every one that helpeth with us, and laboureth.
I am glad of the coming of Stephanas and Fortunatus and Achaicus: for that which was 2 Cor. 11:9.lacking on your part they have supplied.
For they have refreshed my spirit and yours: therefore acknowledge ye them that are such.
The churches of Asia salute you. Acts 18:2.Aquila and Priscilla salute you much in the Lord, with the church that is in their house.
All the brethren greet you. Greet ye one another with an holy JST 1 Cor. 16:20 … salutation.kiss.
The salutation of me Paul with mine own hand.
If any man love not the Lord Jesus Christ, let him be Anathema Aramaic, meaning “The Lord will come!” or “Come, O Lord!”Maran-atha.
The grace of our Lord Jesus Christ be with you.
My love be with you all in Christ Jesus. Amen.

div.p: God comforts and cares for His Saints—The Saints are sealed and given assurance by the Spirit in their hearts.

chapter.verse: Paul, an D&C 21:1.apostle of Jesus Christ by the will of God, and Timothy our brother, unto the church of God which is at Corinth, with all the saints which are in all Achaia:
Grace be to you and peace from God our Father, and from the Lord Jesus Christ.
Blessed be God, even the Father of our Lord Jesus Christ, the Father of TG God, Mercy of.mercies, and the God of all TG Comfort.comfort;

Who comforteth us in all our TG Tribulation.tribulation, that we may be able to comfort them which are in any trouble, by the Philip. 3:10; 2 Thes. 2:16.comfort wherewith we ourselves are comforted of God.
For as the Col. 1:24. TG Suffering.sufferings of Christ abound in us, so our consolation also aboundeth by Christ.

And whether we be afflicted, it is for your consolation and salvation, which is GR active, operative.effectual in the D&C 63:20; 101:35.enduring of the same sufferings which we also suffer: or whether we be comforted, it is for your consolation and salvation.
And our hope of you is steadfast, knowing, that as ye are partakers of the TG Persecution; Suffering.sufferings, so shall ye be also of the consolation.
For we would not, brethren, have you ignorant of our trouble which came to us in Asia, that we were pressed out of measure, above strength, insomuch that we TG Despair.despaired even of life:
But we had the sentence of death in ourselves, that we should not trust in ourselves, but in God which TG Resurrection.raiseth the dead:
Who TG Deliver.delivered us from so great a death, and doth deliver: in whom we trust that he will yet deliver us;
Ye also helping together by Philip. 1:19; Philem. 1:22.prayer for us, that for the gift bestowed upon us by the means of many persons thanks may be given by many on our behalf.

For our rejoicing is this, the TG Testimony.testimony of our conscience, that in GR plainness, singleness of heart.simplicity and godly sincerity, not with fleshly 1 Cor. 2:13.wisdom, but by the grace of God, we have had our conversation in the world, and more abundantly to you-ward.
For we TG Scriptures, Writing of.write none other things unto you, than what ye read or acknowledge; and I trust ye shall acknowledge even to the end;
As also ye have acknowledged us in part, that we are your rejoicing, even as ye also are ours in the D&C 1:9 (8–10).day of the Lord Jesus.
And in this confidence I was minded to 1 Cor. 16:7 (5–7).come unto you before, that ye might have a second benefit;
And to pass by you into Macedonia, and to come again out of Macedonia unto you, and of you to be brought on my way toward Judæa.

When I therefore was thus minded, did I use GR light-mindedness, levity.lightness? or the things that I purpose, do I purpose according to the flesh, that with me there should be yea yea, and nay nay?
But as God is true, our word toward you was not yea and nay.

For the Luke 1:35.Son of God, Jesus Christ, who was preached among you by us, even by me and Acts 15:34 (32–34); 1 Thes. 1:1; 1 Pet. 5:12.Silvanus and 1 Cor. 16:10 (10–11); 1 Thes. 3:2.Timotheus, was not yea and nay, but in him was yea.
For all the Rom. 15:8.promises of God in him are yea, and in him Amen, unto the glory of God by us.
Now he which stablisheth us with you in Christ, and hath TG Anointing.anointed us, is God;
Who hath also TG Sealing.sealed us, and given the earnest of the Spirit in our hearts.
Moreover I call God for a record upon my soul, that to spare you I came not as yet unto Corinth.
Not for that we have dominion over your faith, but are helpers of your joy: for by Rom. 11:20.faith ye stand.

div.p: Saints should love and forgive one another—They always triumph in Christ.

chapter.verse: But I determined this with myself, that I would not come again to you in heaviness.
For if I make you sorry, who is he then that maketh me glad, but the same which is made sorry by me?
And I wrote this same unto you, lest, when I came, I should have sorrow from them of whom I ought to rejoice; having 2 Cor. 8:22.confidence in you all, that my joy is the joy of you all.
For out of much affliction and anguish of heart I wrote unto you with many tears; not that ye should be grieved, but that ye might know the love which I have more abundantly unto you.
But if any have caused grief, he hath not grieved me, but in part: that I may not overcharge you all.
Sufficient to such a man is this punishment, which was inflicted of many.
So that contrariwise ye ought rather to TG Forgive.forgive him, and comfort him, lest perhaps such a one should be swallowed up with overmuch sorrow.
Wherefore I beseech you that ye would confirm your love toward him.
For to this end also did I TG Scriptures, Writing of.write, that I might know the proof of you, whether ye be 2 Cor. 7:15.obedient in all things.
To whom ye forgive any thing, I forgive also: for if I forgave any thing, to whom I forgave it, for your sakes forgave I it in the GR presence.person of Christ;
Lest 2 Pet. 2:20 (20–22).Satan should get an advantage of us: for we are not ignorant of his devices.
Furthermore, when I came to Troas to preach Christ’s gospel, and a Acts 14:27; 1 Cor. 16:9.door was opened unto me of the Lord,
I had no rest in my spirit, because I found not Titus my brother: but taking my leave of them, I went from thence into Macedonia.
Now thanks be unto God, which always causeth us to Rom. 8:37.triumph in Christ, and maketh manifest the savour of his knowledge by us in every place.
For we are unto God a sweet savour of Christ, in them that are saved, and in them that perish:
To the one we are the savour of death unto death; and to the other the savour of life unto life. And who is 2 Cor. 3:5 (5–6).sufficient for these things?

For we are not as many, which 2 Cor. 4:2. TG Apostasy of the Early Christian Church.corrupt the TG Scriptures, Preservation of.word of God: but as of TG Sincere.sincerity, but as of God, in the sight of God speak we in Christ.

div.p: The gospel surpasses the law of Moses—Where the Spirit of the Lord is, there is liberty.

chapter.verse: Do we begin again to commend ourselves? or need we, as some others, epistles of commendation to you, or letters of commendation from you?
Ye are our 1 Cor. 9:2.epistle written in our hearts, known and read of all men:

Forasmuch as ye are manifestly declared to be the epistle of Christ ministered by us, Deut. 9:10 (10–11).written not with ink, but with the Spirit of the living God; not in Ex. 24:12; 31:18; 34:1; Deut. 4:13; Prov. 3:3.tables of stone, but in Ezek. 11:19 (19–20).fleshy tables of the Jer. 31:33; Heb. 8:10.heart.
And such trust have we through Christ to God-ward:
Not that we are sufficient of ourselves to think any thing as of ourselves; but our 2 Cor. 2:16; Alma 26:12.sufficiency is of God;
Who also hath made us able 1 Cor. 3:5.ministers of the new testament; not of the letter, but of the spirit: for the letter killeth, but the John 6:63.spirit giveth life.

But if the ministration of Rom. 7:10.death, written and engraven in stones, was glorious, so that the children of Israel could not steadfastly behold the face of Moses for the TG Transfiguration.glory of his countenance; which glory was to be done away:
How shall not the ministration of the spirit be rather glorious?
For if the ministration of condemnation be glory, much more doth the ministration of righteousness exceed in glory.
For even that which was made glorious had no glory in this respect, by reason of the glory that excelleth.
For if that which is done away was glorious, much more that which remaineth is glorious.
Seeing then that we have such hope, we use great GR boldness, frankness.plainness of speech:
And not as Moses, which put a veil over his face, that the children of Israel could not steadfastly look to the end of that which is abolished:

But their TG Mind.minds were TG Spiritual Blindness.blinded: for until this day remaineth the same veil untaken away in the Acts 15:21.reading of the old testament; which TG Jesus Christ, Types of, in Anticipation; Veil. veil is done away in Christ.
But even unto this day, when Moses is read, the veil is upon their TG Hardheartedness.heart.
Nevertheless when it shall turn to the Lord, the veil shall be taken away.
Now the Lord is GR the Spirit.that Spirit: and where the Alma 61:15. TG God, Spirit of.Spirit of the Lord is, there is Rom. 8:2. TG Governments; Liberty.liberty.

But we all, with open face beholding as in a 1 Cor. 13:12.glass the TG Celestial Glory.glory of the Lord, are changed into the same Rom. 8:29.image from TG Man, Potential to Become like Heavenly Father.glory to glory, even as by the Spirit of the Lord.

div.p: Gospel light shines on the Saints—Mortal trials are nothing as contrasted with eternal glory.

chapter.verse: Therefore seeing we have this ministry, as we have received Alma 12:34.mercy, we faint not;

But have TG Priesthood, Qualifying for.renounced the hidden things of GR shame, disgrace. TG Honesty.dishonesty, not walking in craftiness, nor handling the word of God 2 Cor. 2:17; 1 Thes. 2:3 (3, 5).deceitfully; but by manifestation of the D&C 19:37; 75:4.truth commending ourselves to every man’s TG Conscience.conscience in the sight of God.
But if our gospel be hid, it is hid to them that are lost:

In whom the Eph. 6:12. TG Devil.god of this TG Worldliness.world hath Alma 19:6; Moro. 7:12; D&C 93:39 (38–39). TG Spiritual Blindness.blinded the TG Mind.minds of them which TG Unbelief.believe not, lest the TG Light [noun].light of the glorious gospel of Christ, who is the TG God, Body of, Corporeal Nature.image of TG Godhead.God, should shine unto them.
For we Gal. 1:11 (11–12). TG Missionary Work.preach not ourselves, but Christ Jesus the Lord; and ourselves your 1 Cor. 9:19; Alma 26:3.servants for Jesus’ sake.

For God, who Ps. 33:9; Moses 2:5 (4–5).commanded the light to shine out of darkness, hath D&C 45:9.shined in our hearts, to give the TG Intelligence; Light of Christ.light of the knowledge of the TG God, Glory of; Jesus Christ, Relationships with the Father.glory of God in the face of Jesus Christ.
But we have this treasure in earthen vessels, that the excellency of the 1 Cor. 2:5; D&C 19:3; 88:13 (7–13).power may be of God, and not of us.
We are 2 Cor. 7:5. TG Adversity.troubled on every side, yet not distressed; we are perplexed, but not in TG Despair.despair;
2 Cor. 11:23 (23–27); D&C 101:35.Persecuted, but not forsaken; cast down, but not Ps. 129:2.destroyed;
Always bearing about in the body the dying of the Lord Jesus, that the life also of Jesus might be made manifest in our body.
For we which live are alway delivered unto Rom. 8:36. TG Martyrdom.death for Jesus’ sake, that the life also of Jesus might be made manifest in our TG Mortality.mortal flesh.
So then death worketh in us, but life in you.
We having the same spirit of faith, according as it is written, I D&C 14:8.believed, and therefore have I spoken; we also believe, and therefore D&C 18:21.speak;
Knowing that he which raised up the Lord Jesus shall raise up us also by Jesus, and shall present us with you.
For all things are for your sakes, that the abundant grace might through the TG Thanksgiving.thanksgiving of many redound to the glory of God.
For which cause we faint not; but though our outward man perish, yet the 2 Ne. 4:20; Mosiah 24:15.inward man is renewed day by day.
For our light Rom. 5:3. TG Persecution; Suffering.affliction, which is but for a moment, worketh for us a far more exceeding and TG Eternal Life.eternal D&C 63:66.weight of glory;

While we look not at the things which are Rom. 8:24.seen, but at the things which are not seen: for the things which are seen are temporal; but the things which are not Ether 12:6.seen are TG Eternity.eternal.

div.p: Saints walk by faith and seek tabernacles of immortal glory—The gospel reconciles man to God—God’s ministers carry the word of reconciliation to the world.

chapter.verse: For we know that if our earthly house of this tabernacle were dissolved, we have a building of God, an house not made with hands, eternal in the heavens.
For in this we groan, earnestly desiring to be Rom. 8:23.clothed upon with our house which is from heaven:
If so be that being clothed we shall not be found naked.
For we that are in this tabernacle do TG Mourning.groan, being burdened: not for that we would be unclothed, but clothed upon, that TG Mortality.mortality might be swallowed up of life.
Now he that hath wrought us for the selfsame thing is God, who also hath given unto us the earnest of the Spirit.
Therefore we are always GR of good courage, of good cheer.confident, knowing that, whilst we are at home in the body, we are absent from the Lord:
(For we walk by TG Faith.faith, not by TG Sight.sight:)
We are confident, I say, and willing rather to be absent from the body, and to be present with the Lord.
Wherefore we Moro. 9:6.labour, that, whether present or absent, we may be accepted of him.

For we must all appear before the TG Jesus Christ, Judge.judgment seat of Christ; that every one may receive the things done in his body, TG Accountability.according to that he hath TG Good Works.done, whether it be good or bad.
Knowing therefore the terror of the Lord, we persuade men; but we are made manifest unto God; and I trust also are made manifest in your TG Conscience.consciences.
For we commend not ourselves again unto you, but give you occasion to glory on our behalf, that ye may have somewhat to answer them which glory in appearance, and not in heart.

JST 2 Cor. 5:13 For we bear record that we are not beside ourselves; for whether we glory, it is to God, or whether we be sober, it is for your sakes.For whether we be beside ourselves, it is to God: or whether we be sober, it is for your cause.
For the love of Christ constraineth us; because we thus judge, that if Rom. 5:15.one D&C 19:16.died for all, then were all dead:
And that he died for all, that they which live should not henceforth Rom. 14:7 (7–9).live unto themselves, but unto him which died for them, and rose again.

JST 2 Cor. 5:16 Wherefore, henceforth live we no more after the flesh; yea, though we once lived after the flesh, yet since we have known Christ, now henceforth live we no more after the flesh.Wherefore henceforth know we no man after the flesh: yea, though we have known Christ after the flesh, yet now henceforth know we him no more.

Therefore if any man be 1 Jn. 2:5 (2–5).in Christ, he is a TG Man, New, Spiritually Reborn.new creature: Rom. 7:6; 13:12 (12–14); Heb. 8:13.old things are D&C 29:24; 63:49.passed away; behold, all things are become Rev. 21:5.new.
And all things are of God, who hath TG Reconciliation.reconciled us to himself by Jesus Christ, and hath given to us the ministry of reconciliation;
To wit, that God was in Christ, TG Reconciliation.reconciling the world unto himself, not imputing their trespasses unto them; and hath committed unto us the word of reconciliation.
Now then we are Luke 14:23.ambassadors for Christ, as though God did beseech you by us: we pray you in Christ’s stead, be ye reconciled to God.
For he hath made him to be Isa. 53:6.sin for us, who knew no 1 Jn. 3:3.sin; that we might be made the Rom. 1:17.righteousness of God in him.

div.p: Now is the day of salvation—God’s ministers must walk uprightly and bear all things—Saints should not be unequally yoked with unbelievers.

chapter.verse: We then, as 1 Cor. 3:9.workers together with JST 2 Cor. 6:1 … Christ … him , beseech you also that ye receive not the TG Grace.grace of God in vain.

(For he saith, I have heard thee in a Isa. 49:8.time accepted, and in the Alma 34:32 (31–34).day of TG Salvation.salvation have I succoured thee: behold, now is the accepted time; behold, now is the day of salvation.)
Giving no Rom. 14:13. TG Offense.offence in any thing, that the ministry be not blamed:

But in all things approving ourselves as the 2 Cor. 11:23. TG Priesthood, Magnifying Callings within.ministers of God, in much TG Patience.patience, in D&C 127:2 (2–3).afflictions, in necessities, in distresses,
In stripes, in imprisonments, in TG Rioting and Reveling.tumults, in labours, in watchings, in fastings;
By TG Purity.pureness, by knowledge, by TG Forbear.longsuffering, by TG Kindness.kindness, by the Holy Ghost, by D&C 121:41 (41–42).love unfeigned,
By the word of truth, by the power of God, by the Rom. 13:12.armour of TG Righteousness.righteousness on the right hand and on the left,
By honour and dishonour, by evil report and good report: as deceivers, and yet true;
As unknown, and yet well known; as dying, and, behold, we live; as chastened, and not killed;
As TG Mourning.sorrowful, yet alway rejoicing; as 2 Cor. 8:9.poor, yet making many TG Treasure.rich; as having nothing, and yet possessing all things.
O ye Corinthians, our mouth is open unto you, our heart is Ps. 119:32 (25–32).enlarged.
Ye are not straitened in us, but ye are straitened in your own D&C 121:45.bowels.
Now for a recompence in the same, (I speak as unto my children,) be ye also enlarged.

Be ye not unequally TG Marriage, Interfaith; Marriage, Temporal.yoked together with unbelievers: for what TG Fellowshipping.fellowship hath righteousness with 2 Kgs. 3:13; Ezek. 14:3.unrighteousness? and what communion hath TG Light [noun].light with darkness?
And what concord hath Christ with Belial? or what part hath he that believeth with an infidel?

And what 1 Cor. 10:21 (19–22).agreement hath the temple of God with idols? for ye are the TG Body, Sanctity of; Temple.temple of the living God; as God hath said, I will Lev. 26:12. TG God, Presence of.dwell in them, and walk in them; and I will be their God, and they shall be my people.

Wherefore D&C 25:10; 53:2; 84:50 (49–53).come out from among them, and be ye TG Separation.separate, saith the Lord, and touch not the Isa. 52:11. TG Uncleanness.unclean thing ; and I will receive you,
And will be a Jer. 31:9 (1, 9).Father unto you, and ye shall be my TG Sons and Daughters of God.sons and daughters, saith the Lord TG God, Power of.Almighty.

div.p: Godly sorrow for sin leads to repentance—The sorrow of the world leads to death.

chapter.verse: Having therefore these promises, dearly beloved, let us TG Chastity; Cleanliness; Purification.cleanse ourselves from all GR pollution, stain, defilement. TG Filthiness.filthiness of the flesh and spirit, perfecting TG Holiness.holiness in the fear of God.
Receive us; we have 1 Thes. 2:3 (3–10).wronged no man, we have corrupted no man, we have TG Fraud.defrauded no man.
I speak not this to condemn you: for I have said before, that ye are in our hearts to die and live with you.
Great is my boldness of speech toward you, great is my glorying of you: I am filled with TG Comfort.comfort, I am exceeding TG Joy.joyful in all our 3 Ne. 12:12 (11–12).tribulation.
For, when we were come into Macedonia, our flesh had no rest, but we were 2 Cor. 4:8.troubled on every side; without were fightings, within were D&C 10:55.fears.
Nevertheless God, that comforteth those that are cast down, comforted us by the coming of Titus;

And not by his coming only, but by the consolation wherewith he was TG Comfort.comforted in you, when he told us your earnest desire, your mourning, your fervent mind toward me; so that I rejoiced the more.
For though I made you sorry with a letter, I do not repent, though I did repent: for I perceive that the same epistle hath made you sorry, though it were but for a season.
Now I rejoice, not that ye were made sorry, but that ye sorrowed to repentance: for ye were made TG Poor in Spirit.sorry after a godly manner, that ye might receive damage by us in nothing.

For TG Godliness.godly Eccl. 7:3; Isa. 22:12; Joel 2:17; James 4:9 (8–10); Alma 36:17 (5–28); 42:29. TG Mourning; Sorrow.sorrow worketh TG Repent.repentance to TG Salvation.salvation not to be repented of: but the Morm. 2:13.sorrow of the world worketh death.

For behold this selfsame thing, that ye sorrowed after a godly sort, what carefulness it wrought in you, yea, what clearing of yourselves, yea, what indignation, yea, what fear, yea, what vehement desire, yea, what zeal, yea, what revenge! In all things ye have approved yourselves to be clear in this matter.
Wherefore, though I wrote unto you, I did it not for his cause that had done the wrong, nor for his cause that suffered wrong, but that our care for you in the sight of God might appear unto you.
Therefore we were comforted in your comfort: yea, and exceedingly the more joyed we for the joy of Titus, because his spirit was Rom. 15:32.refreshed by you all.
For if I have 2 Cor. 9:2.boasted any thing to him of you, I am not ashamed; but as we spake all things to you in truth, even so our TG Boast.boasting, which I made before Titus, is found a truth.

And his GR heart, tender affections, compassion.inward affection is more abundant toward you, whilst he remembereth the 2 Cor. 2:9; 10:6. TG Obedience.obedience of you all, how with fear and trembling ye received him.
I rejoice therefore that I have confidence in you in all things.

div.p: True Saints impart of their substance to the poor—Christ, out of His poverty, brought eternal riches.

chapter.verse: Moreover, brethren, GR we make known (or declare) to you the grace.we do you to wit of the grace of God bestowed on the churches of Macedonia;

How that in a great TG Probation; Test.trial of TG Affliction.affliction the abundance of their joy and their deep Mark 12:44 (43–44).poverty abounded unto the riches of their GR sincerity, purity of mind. TG Generosity.liberality.
For to their power, I bear record, yea, and beyond their power they were willing of themselves;
Praying us with much entreaty that we would receive the gift, and take upon us the D&C 88:133.fellowship of the ministering to the saints.
And this they did, not as we hoped, but first TG Self-Sacrifice.gave their own selves to the Lord, and unto us by the will of God.
Insomuch that we desired Titus, that as he had begun, so he would also finish in you the same grace also.
Therefore, as ye abound in every thing, in faith, and utterance, and knowledge, and in all TG Diligence.diligence, and in your love to us, see that ye abound in this grace also.
I speak D&C 63:22.not by commandment, but by occasion of the forwardness of others, and to prove the TG Sincere.sincerity of your love.

For ye know the TG Grace.grace of our Lord Jesus Christ, that, though he was TG Treasure.rich, yet for your sakes he became Philip. 2:7 (5–8).poor, that ye through his 2 Cor. 6:10.poverty might be rich.
And herein I give my advice: for this is expedient for you, who have begun before, not only to do, but also to be forward a year ago.
Now therefore perform the doing of it; that as there was a readiness to will, so there may be a performance also out of that which ye have.
For if there be first a Ex. 25:2 (1–7).willing D&C 64:34.mind, it is accepted according to that a man hath, and not according to that he hath not.
For I mean not that other men be eased, and ye burdened:

But by an equality, that now at this time your abundance may be a supply for their want, that their Alma 1:30 (29–30).abundance also may be a supply for your want: that there may be TG Consecration.equality:
As it is written, He that had Ex. 16:18. gathered much had nothing over; and he that had gathered little had no lack.
But thanks be to God, which put the same earnest care into the heart of Titus for you.
For indeed he accepted the exhortation; but being more forward, of his own accord he went unto you.
And we have sent with him the brother, whose praise is in the gospel throughout all the churches;
And not that only, but who was also chosen of the churches to travel with us with this grace, which is administered by us to the glory of the same Lord, and declaration of your ready mind:
Avoiding this, that no man should blame us in this abundance which is administered by us:
Providing for TG Honesty.honest things, not only in the sight of the Lord, but also in the Rom. 12:17.sight of men.
And we have sent with them our brother, whom we have oftentimes proved diligent in many things, but now much more diligent, upon the great 2 Cor. 2:3.confidence which I have in you.
Whether any do inquire of Titus, he is my partner and fellowhelper concerning you: or our brethren be inquired of, they are the messengers of the churches, and the glory of Christ.
Wherefore shew ye to them, and before the churches, the proof of your love, and of our boasting on your behalf.

div.p: God loves and rewards a cheerful giver—Thanks be to God for His unspeakable gift.

chapter.verse: For as touching the ministering to the saints, it is superfluous for me to write to you:
For I know the forwardness of your mind, for which I 2 Cor. 7:14.boast of you to them of Macedonia, that Achaia was ready a year ago; and your zeal hath TG Provoking.provoked very many.
Yet have I sent the brethren, lest our boasting of you should be in vain in this behalf; that, as I said, ye may be ready:
Lest haply if they of Macedonia come with me, and find you unprepared, we (that we say not, ye) should be ashamed in this same confident boasting.

Therefore I thought it necessary to exhort the brethren, that they would go before unto you, and make up beforehand your GR gift, blessing, benevolence.bounty, whereof ye had notice before, that the same might be ready, as a matter of bounty, and not as of covetousness.
But this I say, He which soweth sparingly shall TG Harvest.reap also sparingly; and he which TG Accountability.soweth bountifully shall reap also bountifully.
Every man according as he D&C 6:33 (33–34).purposeth in his heart, so let him give; not TG Hypocrisy.grudgingly, or of necessity: for God loveth a TG Cheerful.cheerful TG Generosity.giver.
And God is able to make all Philip. 4:18 (15–23); Jacob 2:19 (17–19). TG Grace.grace abound toward you; that ye, always having all sufficiency in all things, may abound to every good work:
(As it is written, He hath dispersed abroad; he hath given to the poor: his righteousness remaineth for ever.
Now he that ministereth seed to the sower both minister bread for your food, and multiply your seed sown, and increase the fruits of your TG Righteousness.righteousness;)
Being enriched in every thing to all bountifulness, which causeth through us TG Thanksgiving.thanksgiving to God.
For the Heb. 13:16.administration of this service not only supplieth the want of the saints, but is abundant also by many thanksgivings unto God;
