Let no man Matt. 24:4 (4–5); Mosiah 26:6; D&C 123:12.beguile you of your reward in a voluntary TG Humility.humility and worshipping of angels, intruding into those things which he hath not seen, vainly puffed up by his fleshly mind,
And not holding the Eph. 4:15 (15–16).Head, from which all the body by joints and bands having nourishment ministered, and knit together, increaseth with the increase of God.
Wherefore if ye be Rom. 6:5 (2–5); Col. 3:3.dead with Christ from the rudiments of the world, why, as though living in the world, are ye subject to TG Ordinance.ordinances,
JST Col. 2:21–22 (Appendix).(Touch not; taste not; handle not;
Which all are to perish with the using;) after the Titus 1:14; D&C 46:7; JS—H 1:19. TG Apostasy of the Early Christian Church.commandments and Matt. 15:9; 2 Ne. 28:9.doctrines of men?
Which things have indeed a shew of wisdom in will worship, and humility, and neglecting of the body; not in any honour to the satisfying of the flesh.

div.p: Some lives are hidden with God in Christ—The Saints are told to be holy and to serve the Lord Jesus Christ.

chapter.verse: If ye then be Col. 2:12.risen with Christ, seek those things which are above, where Christ sitteth on the right hand of God.
Set your GR mind.affection on Matt. 6:33.things Prov. 15:24.above, not on things on the TG Worldliness.earth.
For ye are Rom. 6:5 (2–5); Col. 2:20.dead, and your life is hid with Christ in God.
When Christ, who is our Ether 4:12.life, shall 1 Jn. 3:2.appear, then shall ye also appear with him in 1 Cor. 15:43 (42–44).glory.

Rom. 8:13.Mortify therefore your Rom. 6:13.members which are upon the earth; fornication, uncleanness, TG Sexual Immorality.inordinate affection, evil concupiscence, and TG Covet.covetousness, which is TG Idolatry.idolatry:
For which things’ sake the TG God, Indignation of.wrath of God cometh on the children of TG Disobedience.disobedience:
In the which ye also walked GR formerly.some time, when ye lived in them.
But now ye also Eph. 4:22 (22–24).put off all these; TG Anger.anger, wrath, TG Malice.malice, TG Blaspheme.blasphemy, filthy TG Profanity.communication out of your mouth.
TG Honesty; Lying.Lie not one to another, seeing that ye have put off the Rom. 6:6; Mosiah 3:19.old man with his deeds;
And have put on the TG Man, New, Spiritually Reborn.new man , which is renewed in knowledge after the Rom. 8:29.image of him that TG Jesus Christ, Creator; Man, Physical Creation of.created him:
Where there is neither 1 Cor. 12:13.Greek nor Jew, TG Circumcision.circumcision nor uncircumcision, Barbarian, Scythian, bond nor free: but Christ is all, and in all.
Put on therefore, as the TG Election.elect of God, holy and beloved, Gal. 5:22.bowels of TG Mercy.mercies, TG Benevolence.kindness, TG Humility.humbleness of mind, TG Meek.meekness, longsuffering;
TG Forbear.Forbearing one another, and Mosiah 26:31 (30–31).forgiving one another, if any man have a quarrel against any: even as Christ forgave you, so also do ye.
And above all these things put on TG Charity.charity, which is the bond of perfectness.

div.p: The Saints are told to be holy, sanctify themselves, and love one another—The Lord will come, and the dead will rise.

Wherefore comfort one another with these words.

div.p: The Saints will know the season of the Second Coming of Christ—Live the way Saints should live—Rejoice evermore—Do not despise prophesyings.

And to Philip. 2:29. TG Respect.esteem them very highly in love for their work’s sake. And be at Mark 9:50; Rom. 15:5 (1–7); D&C 20:54 (53–55).peace among yourselves.

TG Prayer.Pray without ceasing.

GR Extinguish, Hinder, Suppress.Quench not the TG Holy Ghost, Gifts of; Holy Ghost, Loss of.Spirit.

div.p: At His Second Coming, the Lord Jesus will take vengeance upon the ungodly.

So that we ourselves glory in you in the 1 Thes. 1:8.churches of God for your GR endurance. D&C 58:2.patience and faith in all your 1 Thes. 2:14.persecutions and tribulations that ye endure:
Which is a manifest token of the righteous judgment of God, that ye may be counted worthy of the kingdom of God, for which ye also TG Suffering.suffer:
Seeing it is a righteous thing with God to recompense tribulation to them that trouble you;
And to you who are troubled TG Rest.rest with us, when the TG Day of the Lord.Lord Jesus shall be TG Jesus Christ, Second Coming.revealed from heaven with his mighty D&C 76:21.angels,
In flaming TG World, End of.fire taking 1 Thes. 4:6.vengeance on them that know not God, and that TG Disobedience.obey not the TG Gospel.gospel of our Lord Jesus Christ:
Who shall be TG Punish.punished with TG Eternity.everlasting TG Damnation; Sons of Perdition.destruction from the presence of the Lord, and from the glory of his power;
When he shall come to be Isa. 66:5.glorified in his TG Saints.saints, and to be admired in all them that believe (because our testimony among you was believed) in that day.
Wherefore also we pray always for you, that our God would count you TG Worthiness.worthy of this calling, and fulfil all the good pleasure of his goodness, and the work of faith with power:
That the name of our Lord Jesus Christ may be glorified in you, and ye in him, according to the TG Grace.grace of our God and the Lord Jesus Christ.

div.p: Apostasy is to precede the Second Coming—The gospel prepares men for eternal glory.

chapter.verse: Now we beseech you, brethren, GR concerning.by the coming of our Lord Jesus Christ, and by our gathering together unto him,

That ye be not soon shaken in mind, JST 2 Thes. 2:2 … or be troubled by letter, except ye receive it from us; neither by spirit, nor by word, as that the day of Christ is at hand.or be troubled, neither by spirit, nor by word, nor by 1 Thes. 4:15 (13–17).letter as from us, as that the Luke 19:11.day of Christ is at hand.

TG Apostasy of the Early Christian Church.falling away first, and that TG Antichrist; Devil.man of TG Sin.sin be revealed, the son of perdition;
Who TG Opposition.opposeth and exalteth himself above all that is called God, or that is worshipped; so that he as God sitteth in the temple of God, shewing himself that he is God.
Remember ye not, that, when I was yet with you, I told you these things?

That they all might be GR brought to account, trial. TG Damnation.damned who TG Unbelief.believed not the truth, but had pleasure in unrighteousness.

Therefore, brethren, stand fast, and hold the traditions which ye have been taught, whether by word, or our epistle.
Now our Lord Jesus Christ himself, and God, even our Father, which hath 1 Jn. 4:10.loved us, and hath given us everlasting 2 Cor. 1:4; Philip. 3:10.consolation and good hope through TG Grace.grace,
Comfort your hearts, and stablish you in every good word and work.

div.p: Pray for the triumph of the gospel cause—Paul preaches the gospel of work—Be not weary in well-doing.

And that we may be delivered from GR unsuitable, absurd, improper.unreasonable and wicked men: for all men have not faith.

Yet count him not as an TG Enemies.enemy, but TG Warn.admonish him as a TG Brotherhood and Sisterhood.brother.

The salutation of Paul with mine own Col. 4:18.hand, which is the token in every epistle: so I write.
The grace of our Lord Jesus Christ be with you all. Amen.

div.p: Counsel is given to teach true doctrine only—Christ came to save repentant sinners.

Henceforth there is laid up for me a TG Exaltation; Reward.crown of TG God, the Standard of Righteousness.righteousness, which the Lord, the righteous TG Jesus Christ, Judge.judge, shall give me at that day: and not to me only, but unto all them also that love his appearing.
Do thy diligence to come shortly unto me:

For Col. 4:14; Philem. 1:24.Demas hath TG Apostasy of Individuals.forsaken me, having loved this present TG Worldliness.world, and is departed unto Thessalonica; Crescens to Galatia, Titus unto Dalmatia.
Only Acts 1:1.Luke is with me. Take Acts 15:37 (37–39); Col. 4:10.Mark, and bring him with thee: for he is profitable to me for the ministry.
And Tychicus have I sent to Ephesus.
The cloak that I left at Troas with Carpus, when thou comest, bring with thee, and the Luke 1:1 (1–4); 1 Ne. 13:26 (24–26).books, but especially the parchments.
Alexander the coppersmith did me much evil: the Lord reward him according to his D&C 64:11.works:
Of whom be thou ware also; for he hath greatly GR opposed, resisted.withstood our words.
At my first GR defense.answer no man stood with me, but all men forsook me: I pray God that it may not be laid to their Acts 7:60 (59–60); 3 Ne. 12:44.charge.

Notwithstanding the John 14:18.Lord stood with me, and strengthened me; that by me the preaching might be fully known, and that all the Gentiles might hear: and I was delivered out of the mouth of the lion.
And the Lord shall deliver me from every evil work, and will GR save, rescue.preserve me unto his heavenly kingdom: to whom be glory for ever and ever. Amen.
Salute Prisca and Aquila, and the household of Onesiphorus.
Erastus abode at Corinth: but Trophimus have I left at Miletum sick.
Do thy diligence to come before winter. Eubulus greeteth thee, and Pudens, and Linus, and Claudia, and all the brethren.
The TG Jesus Christ, Lord.Lord Jesus Christ be with thy spirit. Grace be with you. Amen.

div.p: Eternal life was promised before the world began—The qualifications of bishops are given—Unto the pure, all things are pure.

chapter.verse: Paul, a TG Servant.servant of God, and an TG Apostles.apostle of Jesus Christ, according to the faith of God’s TG Election.elect, and the acknowledging of the truth which is after godliness;
In TG Hope.hope of TG Salvation, Plan of.eternal life, which God, that cannot TG God, Perfection of; Lying.lie, 2 Tim. 1:1; Abr. 3:26.promised before the world began;
But hath in due times manifested his word through preaching, which is committed unto me according to the commandment of God our Saviour;
To Titus, GR a true son according to. mine own son after the common faith: Grace, mercy, and peace, from God the Father and the Lord Jesus Christ our Saviour.

For this cause left I thee in Crete, that thou shouldest set in TG Order.order the things that are wanting, and TG Priesthood, Authority; Priesthood, Ordination.ordain TG Church Organization; Elder, Melchizedek Priesthood.elders in every city, as I had GR directed, ordered.appointed thee:
If any be blameless, the husband of one wife, having faithful children not accused of GR debauchery or insubordination.riot or unruly.

For a TG Bishop; Leadership.bishop must be blameless, as the TG Delegation of Responsibility; Stewardship.steward of God; not GR obstinate, arrogant.selfwilled, not soon TG Anger.angry, not given to wine, no striker, not given to filthy lucre;
But a lover of TG Hospitality.hospitality, a lover of GR what is good.good men, sober, just, holy, GR self-controlled. TG Temperance.temperate;
Holding fast the faithful word as he hath been taught, that he may be able by sound doctrine both to exhort and to D&C 11:21.convince the GR those who deny, contradict.gainsayers.
For there are many unruly and GR idle speakers, disputers.vain talkers and TG Apostasy of the Early Christian Church.deceivers, specially they of the TG Circumcision.circumcision:
Whose mouths must be stopped, who subvert whole houses, teaching things which they ought not, for filthy 1 Tim. 6:5; Mosiah 29:40; Alma 11:24.lucre’s sake.
One of themselves, even a prophet of their own, said, The Cretians are alway liars, evil beasts, GR lazy gluttons.slow bellies.
This witness is true. Wherefore D&C 84:117 (87, 117).rebuke them sharply, that they may be sound in the faith;
Not giving heed to Jewish 1 Tim. 1:4.fables, and Matt. 15:9; Col. 2:22; D&C 3:6 (6–7); 45:29; 46:7; JS—H 1:19.commandments of men, GR who reject, repudiate the truth.that turn from the truth.

JST Titus 1:15 Unto the pure, let all things be pure …Unto the pure all things are TG Purity.pure: but unto them that are TG Pollution.defiled and unbelieving is nothing pure; but even their Luke 11:39 (39–41). TG Mind.mind and TG Conscience.conscience is Mark 7:15; 2 Ne. 19:17; D&C 93:35.defiled.

They Matt. 7:21 (21–23). TG False Prophets; Hypocrisy.profess that they Hosea 8:2. TG Ignorance.know God; but in Matt. 15:8 (7–9).works they TG Apostasy of the Early Christian Church.deny him, being abominable, and TG Disobedience.disobedient, and unto every good work GR unfit, worthless.reprobate.

div.p: Saints should live righteously, deny ungodliness, and seek the Lord.

chapter.verse: But 1 Thes. 2:4; 1 Pet. 4:11. TG Teaching.speak thou the things which become sound D&C 88:77 (77–78).doctrine:
That the TG Old Age.aged men be GR circumspect.sober, grave, TG Temperance.temperate, sound in faith, in charity, in patience.
The aged women likewise, that they be in behaviour as becometh holiness, not GR slanderers, traitors, devils.false TG Gossip.accusers, not given to much wine, teachers of good things;
That they may teach the young TG Marriage, Wives.women to be sober, to TG Family, Love within.love their husbands, to love their children,
To be discreet, TG Chastity; Modesty.chaste, GR housekeepers, guards.keepers at 1 Tim. 5:14.home, good, obedient to their own husbands, that the word of God be not blasphemed.
Young men likewise exhort to be sober minded.
In all things shewing thyself a pattern of good works: in doctrine shewing uncorruptness, GR dignity, respectfulness.gravity, TG Sincere.sincerity,
1 Tim. 6:3.Sound speech, that cannot be 1 Pet. 2:12 (11–12).condemned; that he that is GR an opponent, enemy.of the contrary part may be ashamed, having no evil thing to say of you.

Exhort Eph. 6:5; Col. 3:22; 1 Tim. 6:1 (1–2); 1 Pet. 2:18.servants to be obedient unto their own masters, and to please them well in all things; not GR arguing against, opposing, contradicting.answering again;
Not GR misappropriating, robbing. TG Stealing.purloining, but shewing all good fidelity; that they may GR honor, put in order.adorn the doctrine of God our Saviour in all things.
For the TG Grace.grace of God JST Titus 2:11 … which bringeth salvation to all men, hath appeared;that bringeth salvation hath appeared to all men,

Teaching us that, denying TG Godliness.ungodliness and TG Worldliness.worldly TG Lust.lusts, we should live TG Levity.soberly, TG Abundant Life; Righteousness.righteously, and godly, in this present world;
Looking for that blessed hope, and the TG Glory.glorious appearing of the great God and our Saviour Jesus Christ;

Who gave TG Self-Sacrifice.himself for us, that he might TG Jesus Christ, Atonement through; Jesus Christ, Redeemer; Redemption.redeem us from all iniquity, and TG Purification; Purity.purify unto himself a TG Peculiar People.peculiar people, TG Zeal.zealous of good works.
These things speak, and exhort, and TG Chastening.rebuke with all TG Authority.authority. Let no man GR disregard.despise thee.

div.p: Saints must live righteously after baptism.

chapter.verse: Put them in mind to be TG Citizenship.subject to TG Governments.principalities and powers, to obey magistrates, to be ready to every good work,
To TG Slander.speak evil of no man, to be GR not quarrelsome.no brawlers, but gentle, shewing all D&C 38:41.meekness unto all men.

For we ourselves also were GR once, formerly.sometimes foolish, TG Disobedience.disobedient, deceived, serving divers TG Lust.lusts and pleasures, living in TG Malice.malice and envy, hateful, and hating one another.
But after that the kindness and love of God our Saviour toward man appeared,

Not by works of TG Righteousness.righteousness which we have done, but according to his TG God, Mercy of.mercy he saved us, by the TG Baptism, Essential.washing of regeneration, and TG Holy Ghost, Gifts of.renewing of the Holy Ghost;
Which he shed on us abundantly through Jesus Christ our Saviour;
That being TG Justification.justified by his grace, we should be made D&C 70:8.heirs according to the hope of eternal life.

This is a faithful saying, and these things I will that thou affirm constantly, that they which have believed in God might be careful to maintain good TG Good Works.works. These things are good and profitable unto men.
But avoid 2 Tim. 2:23 (23–26).foolish questions, and genealogies, and TG Contention.contentions, and strivings about the law; for they are unprofitable and vain.
A man that is an TG Apostasy of Individuals.heretick after the first and second Matt. 18:17.admonition reject;
Knowing that he that is such is GR perverted, changed.subverted, and sinneth, being condemned of himself.
When I shall send Artemas unto thee, or Tychicus, be diligent to come unto me to Nicopolis: for I have determined there to winter.
Bring Zenas the lawyer and Apollos on their journey diligently, that nothing be wanting unto them.
And let GR our people.ours also learn to maintain Rom. 15:28 (25–28); Philip. 4:17.good works for necessary uses, that they be not unfruitful.
All that are with me salute thee. Greet them that love us in the faith. Grace be with you all. Amen.

div.p: The gospel changes a servant into a brother.

chapter.verse: Paul, a prisoner of Jesus Christ, and Timothy our brother, unto Philemon our dearly beloved, and fellowlabourer,
And to our beloved Apphia, and Col. 4:17.Archippus our fellowsoldier, and to the church in thy house:
Grace to you, and peace, from God our Father and the Lord Jesus Christ.
I thank my God, making mention of thee always in my prayers,
Hearing of thy love and faith, which thou hast toward the Lord Jesus, and toward all saints;
That the GR participation, fellowship.communication of thy faith may become GR active.effectual by the acknowledging of every good thing which is in you in Christ Jesus.
For we have great joy and consolation in thy love, because the GR affections, compassion.bowels of the saints are refreshed by thee, brother.
Wherefore, though I might be much bold in Christ to enjoin thee that which is convenient,
Yet for love’s sake I rather beseech thee, being such an one as Paul the aged, and now also a prisoner of Jesus Christ.
I beseech thee for my son Col. 4:9.Onesimus, whom I have begotten in my bonds:
Which in time past was to thee unprofitable, but now profitable to thee and to me:
Whom I have sent again: thou therefore receive him, that is, mine own bowels:
Whom I would have retained with me, that in thy stead he might have ministered unto me in the bonds of the gospel:
But without thy GR assent, suggestion. TG Mind.mind would I do nothing; that thy benefit should not be as it were of necessity, but willingly.
For perhaps he therefore departed for a season, that thou shouldest receive him for ever;
Not now as a servant, but above a servant, a TG Brotherhood and Sisterhood.brother beloved, specially to me, but how much more unto thee, both in the flesh, and in the Lord?
If thou count me therefore a partner, receive him as myself.
If he hath wronged thee, or oweth thee GR something, anything.ought, put that on mine account;
I Paul have written it with mine own hand, I will repay it: albeit I do not say to thee how thou owest unto me even thine own self besides.
Yea, brother, let me have joy of thee in the Lord: refresh my bowels in the Lord.
Having confidence in thy obedience I wrote unto thee, knowing that thou wilt also do more than I say.
But withal prepare me also a lodging: for I trust that through your 2 Cor. 1:11; Philip. 1:19.prayers I shall be given unto you.
There salute thee Col. 1:7 (7–8); 4:12 (12–13).Epaphras, my fellowprisoner in Christ Jesus;
Marcus, Aristarchus, Col. 4:14; 2 Tim. 4:10.Demas, Lucas, my fellowlabourers.
The grace of our Lord Jesus Christ be with your spirit. Amen.

div.title: The Epistle of Paul the Apostle
to the

div.p: The Son is in the express image of the person of the Father—Christ is the Only Begotten Son and thus above the angels.

Hath in these last days spoken unto us by his TG Godhead; Jesus Christ, Divine Sonship; Jesus Christ, Jehovah.Son, whom he hath TG Jesus Christ, Authority of.appointed Matt. 21:38 (33–46). Who being the brightness of his John 1:14. TG Celestial Glory.glory, and the express image of his TG God, Body of, Corporeal Nature.person, and upholding all things by the word of his TG God, Power of.power, when he had by himself GR made purification, expiation for.purged our sins, sat down on the Acts 7:55 (55–56); D&C 76:23.right hand of the Heb. 12:2.Majesty on high;

For unto which of the angels said he at any time, Thou art my Heb. 5:5. TG Jesus Christ, Divine Sonship.Son, this day have I begotten thee? And again, I will be to him a 2 Sam. 7:14; 1 Chr. 17:13.Father, and he shall be to me a Son?

And again, when he bringeth in the TG Jesus Christ, Firstborn.firstbegotten into the world, he saith, JST Heb. 1:6–7 … And let all the angels of God worship him, who maketh his ministers as a flame of fire. And of the angels he saith, Angels are ministering spirits.And let all the angels of God TG Worship.worship him.
And of the angels he saith, Who maketh his angels spirits, and his ministers a flame of fire.
But unto the Son he saith, Thy TG Kingdom of God, in Heaven.throne, O God, is for D&C 88:104.ever and ever: a sceptre of righteousness is the sceptre of thy kingdom.
Thou hast loved TG Righteousness.righteousness, and hated iniquity; therefore God, even thy God, hath anointed thee with the oil of gladness above thy fellows.
And, Thou, Lord, in the beginning hast laid the TG Jesus Christ, Creator.foundation of the earth; and the heavens are the works of thine D&C 67:2.hands:
They shall perish; but thou remainest; and they all shall GR grow old, become worn.wax old as doth a garment;
And as a vesture shalt thou fold them up, and they shall be changed: but thou art the same, and thy years shall not fail.
But to which of the angels said he at any time, Sit on my Ps. 110:1.right hand, until I make thine enemies thy footstool?
Are they not all D&C 7:6. TG Angels.ministering Zech. 6:5 (4–8).spirits, sent forth GR for service.to minister for them who shall be heirs of salvation?

div.p: Jesus came to suffer death and save men—He came to make reconciliation for the sins of the people.

chapter.verse: Therefore we ought to give the more TG Zeal.earnest D&C 21:4.heed to the things which we have heard, lest at any time we should let them slip.
For if the word spoken by angels was steadfast, and every transgression and TG Disobedience.disobedience received a TG God, Justice of.just recompence of reward;
How shall we Heb. 10:29; 12:25.escape, if we neglect so great salvation; which at the first began to be spoken by the Lord, and was confirmed unto us by them that heard him;

God also bearing them witness, both with Acts 5:12 (12–23). TG Holy Ghost, Gifts of; Symbolism.signs and TG Miracle.wonders, and with divers miracles, and gifts of the Holy Ghost, according to his own TG God, Will of.will?
For unto the angels hath he not put in subjection the world to come, whereof we speak.
But one in a certain place testified, saying, What is Ps. 8:4 (4–6).man, that thou art mindful of him? or the son of man, that thou visitest him?
Thou madest him a little lower than the HEB gods; GR angels.angels; thou crownedst him with glory and honour, and didst set him over the works of thy hands:

Thou hast put all things in subjection under his Matt. 28:18; 1 Cor. 15:25; Eph. 1:22.feet. For in that he put all in subjection under him, he left nothing that is not put under him. But now we see not yet all things put under him.

But we see Jesus, who was made a little TG Jesus Christ, Condescension of.lower than the Philip. 2:7 (7–9).angels GR through.for the suffering of TG Jesus Christ, Death of.death, Acts 2:33.crowned with glory and honour; that he by the TG Grace.grace of God should taste TG Jesus Christ, Atonement through.death for every man.

For GR it was fitting, suitable, proper for Him.it became him, for whom are all things, and by whom are all Col. 1:16 (13–19).things, in bringing many sons unto glory, to make the TG Jesus Christ, Messiah.captain of their salvation TG Perfection.perfect through sufferings.
For both he that sanctifieth and they who are TG Sanctification.sanctified are all of TG Unity.one: for which cause he is not ashamed to call them TG Brotherhood and Sisterhood.brethren,
Saying, I will declare thy Ps. 22:22.name unto my brethren, in the midst of the church will I sing praise unto thee.
And again, I will put my Ps. 18:2.trust in him. And again, Behold I and the children which God hath given me.

GR Since.Forasmuch then as the children are partakers of TG Flesh and Blood.flesh and blood, he also himself likewise took part of the same; that through death he might Gen. 3:15.destroy him that had the power of 2 Tim. 1:10. TG Redemption.death, that is, the TG Devil.devil;
And deliver them who through fear of death were all their lifetime subject to TG Bondage, Spiritual; Law of Moses.bondage.
For verily he took not on him the nature of TG Angels.angels; but he took on him the TG Seed of Abraham.seed of Abraham.

Wherefore in all things it behoved him to be made like unto his brethren, that he might be a merciful and faithful high priest in things pertaining to God, to make TG Jesus Christ, Redeemer; Reconciliation.reconciliation for the sins of the people.
For in that he himself hath suffered being GR tried, subjected to trial. TG Jesus Christ, Temptation of; Test.tempted, he is able to Alma 7:12; D&C 62:1.succour them that are tempted.

div.p: Christ is the Apostle and High Priest of our profession—Jesus, being the Son, is more than a servant—Now is the time and the day of our salvation.

chapter.verse: Wherefore, holy brethren, partakers of the heavenly calling, TG Meditation.consider the TG Jesus Christ, Messenger of the Covenant.Apostle and TG High Priest, Melchizedek Priesthood.High Priest of our profession, Christ Jesus;

For every house is builded by some man; but he that built all things is God.
And Moses verily was faithful in all his house, as a TG Servant.servant, for a testimony of those things which were to be spoken after;
But Christ as a son over his own TG Church.house; whose 1 Pet. 2:5 (4–8).house are we, if we hold fast the confidence and the rejoicing of the hope firm unto the end.
Wherefore (as the Heb. 10:15.Holy Ghost saith, Ps. 95:7 (7–11).To day if ye will hear his voice,
TG Hardheartedness.Harden not your hearts, as in the Num. 14:23 (2–23); Jacob 1:7 (7–8); Alma 12:36.provocation, in the day of temptation in the wilderness:
When your fathers tempted me, TG Test.proved me, and saw my works forty years.
Wherefore I was grieved with that Prov. 30:12 (11–14).generation, and said, They do alway err in their heart; and they have not known my ways.
So I sware in my wrath, They shall not Ps. 95:11.enter into my D&C 84:24. TG God, Presence of; God, Privilege of Seeing; Rest.rest.)

Take heed, brethren, lest there be in any of you an evil Gen. 6:5; Matt. 15:19; Hel. 12:4.heart of TG Stiffnecked.unbelief, in GR apostatizing, withdrawing from. TG Apostasy of Individuals.departing from the living God.
But Alma 34:39; D&C 38:23. TG Chastening.exhort one another daily, while it is called D&C 45:6.To day; lest any of you be hardened through the deceitfulness of sin.
For we are made partakers of Christ, if we hold the beginning of our confidence TG Perseverance; Steadfastness.steadfast unto the end;
While it is said, To day if ye will hear his voice, TG Contrite Heart.harden not your hearts, as in the TG Provoking.provocation.
For some, when they had heard, did provoke: howbeit not all that came out of Egypt by Moses.
But with whom was he grieved forty years? was it not with them that had sinned, whose Num. 14:29 (29, 37); 1 Cor. 10:5 (2–7).carcases fell in the wilderness?
And to whom sware he that they should not enter into his rest, but to them that believed not?
So we see that they could not enter in because of TG Unbelief.unbelief.

div.p: The gospel was offered to ancient Israel—Saints enter into the rest of the Lord—Though tempted in all points, Jesus was without sin.

chapter.verse: Let us therefore fear, lest, a promise being left us of entering into his TG God, Glory of; Rest.rest, any of you should seem to come short of it.
For unto us was the TG Gospel.gospel preached, as well as unto them: but the word preached did not profit them, not being mixed with TG Faith.faith in them that heard it.

JST Heb. 4:3 (Appendix).For we which have believed do enter into rest, as he said, As I have sworn in my wrath, if they shall enter into my D&C 19:9.rest: although the works were finished from the foundation of the world.
For he spake in a certain place of the TG Sabbath.seventh day on this wise, And God did rest the seventh day from all his works.
JST Heb. 4:5 And in this place again, If they harden not their hearts they shall enter into my rest.And in this place again, If they shall enter into my rest.

Seeing therefore it remaineth that some must enter therein, and GR those who formerly were taught the gospel.they to whom it was first preached entered not in because of GR disobedience, unbelief, obstinacy.unbelief:

Again, he GR appoints, decrees, constitutes.limiteth a certain day, saying in David, To day, after so long a time; as it is said, To day if ye will Ps. 95:7.hear his voice, TG Hardheartedness.harden not your hearts.
For if Ex. 17:9; Acts 7:45.Jesus had given them rest, then would he not afterward have spoken of another day.
There remaineth therefore a rest to the people of God.
For he that is entered into his rest, he also hath ceased from his own works, as God did from his.
Let us GR hasten, be eager.labour therefore to enter into that rest, lest any man fall after the same example of GR obstinacy, disobedience. TG Unbelief.unbelief.

For the Alma 31:5; D&C 6:2; 11:2; 12:2; 14:2; 33:1.word of God is GR living.quick, and powerful, and sharper than any twoedged Isa. 49:2; Rev. 2:12 (12–17).sword, Hosea 6:5; D&C 85:6.piercing even to the dividing asunder of JST Heb. 4:12 … body …soul and spirit, and of the joints and marrow, and is a 1 Cor. 14:25 (24–25). TG God, Omniscience of.discerner of the D&C 6:16.thoughts and TG Motivations.intents of the heart.
Neither is there any creature that is not manifest in his sight: but all things are naked and opened unto the Prov. 5:21.eyes of him with whom we have to do.
Seeing then that we have a great high priest, that is passed into the Heb. 9:24.heavens, Jesus the Son of God, let us hold fast our profession.

For we have not an high priest GR unable to sympathize with our frailties, imperfections.which cannot be touched with the feeling of our infirmities; but was in all points TG Jesus Christ, Exemplar; Jesus Christ, Temptation of; Temptation; Test.tempted like as we are, yet without John 8:46; Heb. 7:26; D&C 45:4. TG Sin.sin.
Let us therefore come boldly unto the throne of TG Grace.grace, that we may obtain mercy, and find grace to help in time of need.

div.p: For a man to hold the priesthood, he must be called of God as was Aaron—Christ was a priest forever after the order of Melchizedek—Jesus Christ is the Author of eternal salvation.

chapter.verse: For every high priest taken from among men is TG Priesthood; Priesthood, Ordination.ordained for men in things pertaining to God, that he may offer both gifts and TG Sacrifice.sacrifices for sins:
Who can have TG Compassion.compassion on the ignorant, and on them that are GR straying, wandering.out of the way; for that he himself also is compassed with infirmity.

And no man taketh this TG Leadership.honour unto himself, but he that is TG Called of God; Priesthood, Authority; Priesthood, Qualifying for.called of God, as was Ex. 28:1. TG Priesthood, Aaronic.Aaron.
So also Christ glorified not himself to be made an high priest; but he that said unto him, Thou art my Heb. 1:5.Son, to day have I begotten thee.
As he saith also in another place, Thou art a TG Priesthood, Melchizedek.priest D&C 124:130.for ever after the order of TG Priesthood, History of.Melchisedec.

Who in the days of his flesh, when he had offered up Matt. 26:39 (39, 42, 44).prayers and supplications with GR mighty, powerful supplication.strong crying and tears unto him that was able to save him from death, and was heard GR because of his piety, reverence.in that he feared;
Though he were a Son, yet TG Jesus Christ, Exemplar.learned he TG Agency; Obedience.obedience by the things which he TG Jesus Christ, Temptation of; Pain; Suffering.suffered;

And being made TG God, Perfection of; Perfection.perfect, he became the TG Jesus Christ, Authority of.author of eternal TG Gospel; Jesus Christ, Atonement through; Jesus Christ, Mission of; Salvation; Salvation, Plan of.salvation unto all them that obey him;
TG Jesus Christ, Authority of.Called of God an TG High Priest, Melchizedek Priesthood; Jesus Christ, Types of, in Anticipation.high priest after the order of Melchisedec.
Of whom we have many things to say, and GR difficult to be explained.hard to be John 16:12 (12–13).uttered, seeing ye are dull of Ezek. 33:31 (30–33); Matt. 11:15; 2 Ne. 9:31; D&C 1:14.hearing.

For when for the time ye ought to be teachers, ye have need that one teach you again which be the first principles of the oracles of God; and are become such as have need of 1 Cor. 3:2 (2–3); D&C 19:22 (21–22); 50:40.milk, and not of strong meat.
For every one that useth 1 Pet. 2:2 (1–3).milk is unskilful in the word of righteousness: for he is a babe.
But strong meat belongeth to them that are of full age, even those who by reason of use have their senses exercised to discern both good and evil.

div.p: Let us go on to perfection—The sons of perdition crucify Christ anew—God swears with an oath that the faithful will be saved.

chapter.verse: Therefore GR having left behind the beginning of the doctrine. JST Heb. 6:1 … not leaving …leaving the principles of the doctrine of Christ, let us go on unto TG Perfection.perfection; not laying again the foundation of TG Repent.repentance from dead works, and of faith toward God,

And have 1 Pet. 2:3 (1–3).tasted the good word of God, and the powers of the TG World.world to come,

If they shall TG Apostasy of Individuals; Holy Ghost, Loss of; Sin.fall away, to renew them again unto repentance; seeing they TG Holy Ghost, Unpardonable Sin against; Jesus Christ, Crucifixion of.crucify to themselves the Son of God afresh, and put him to an open TG Shame.shame.

For the earth which drinketh in the rain that cometh oft upon it, and bringeth forth herbs GR suitable, fit, proper.meet for them by whom it is GR cultivated, tilled.dressed, receiveth blessing from God:
But that which beareth thorns and briers is rejected, and is nigh unto D&C 104:3.cursing; whose end is to be burned.
But, beloved, we are persuaded better things of you, and things that accompany salvation, though we thus speak.

For God is not TG God, Perfection of.unrighteous to forget your work and 1 Thes. 1:3.labour of Col. 1:4.love, which ye have shewed toward his TG Name.name, in that ye have ministered to the saints, and do minister.
And we desire that every one of you do shew the same TG Dependability; Diligence.diligence to the full assurance of hope unto the end:
That ye be not TG Idleness; Laziness.slothful, but followers of them who through Heb. 10:22 (22–24).faith and TG Patience.patience inherit the TG Promise.promises.
For when God made promise to Abraham, because he could GR promise, confirm by an oath.swear by no greater, he Jer. 44:26; Amos 6:8.sware by himself,
Saying, Surely blessing I will TG Abrahamic Covenant.bless thee, and multiplying I will multiply thee.
And so, after he had patiently TG Perseverance.endured, he obtained the promise.
For men verily swear by the greater: and an TG Oath.oath for confirmation is to them an end of all TG Strife.strife.
Wherein God, willing more abundantly to shew unto the heirs of promise the immutability of his GR design, purpose. TG Counselor.counsel, TG Vow.confirmed it by an TG Covenants.oath:

That by two immutable things, in which it was impossible for God to TG God, Perfection of.lie, we might have a strong consolation, who have fled for TG Refuge.refuge to lay hold upon the TG Hope.hope set before us:
Which hope we have as an Ether 12:4.anchor of the soul, both sure and steadfast, and which entereth into that within the TG Veil.veil;
Whither the forerunner is for us entered, even Jesus, made an TG High Priest, Melchizedek Priesthood.high priest for ever after the order of Melchisedec.

div.p: The Melchizedek Priesthood brings exaltation and administers the gospel—It is received with an oath and covenant—The superiority of the Melchizedek Priesthood over the Aaronic Priesthood is explained—Salvation comes through the intercession of Christ.

chapter.verse: For this Melchisedec, king of Salem, TG Priesthood, Melchizedek.priest of the most high God, who met Abraham returning from the slaughter of the kings, and blessed him;

To whom also Abraham gave a TG Tithing.tenth part of all; first being by interpretation King of TG Righteousness.righteousness, and after that also King of TG Jerusalem.Salem, which is, King of peace;

JST Heb. 7:3 (Appendix).Without father, without mother, without descent, having neither D&C 84:17.beginning of days, nor end of life; but made like unto the Son of God; abideth a TG Jesus Christ, Authority of.priest continually.
Now consider how great this man was, unto whom even the TG Family, Patriarchal; Patriarch.patriarch Abraham gave the tenth of the spoils.

And verily they that are of the sons of TG Priest, Aaronic Priesthood. BD Priests.Levi, who receive the TG Priesthood, Aaronic.office of the priesthood, have a commandment to take tithes of the people according to the law, that is, of their brethren, though they come out of the loins of Abraham:
But he whose descent is not counted from them received tithes of Abraham, and blessed him that had the promises.
And without all contradiction the less is blessed of the better.
And here men that die receive tithes; but there he receiveth them, of whom it is witnessed that he liveth.
And as I may so say, Levi also, who receiveth tithes, payed tithes in Abraham.
For he was yet in the loins of his father, when Melchisedec met him.

If therefore Heb. 8:7. TG Perfection.perfection were by the TG Priesthood, Aaronic.Levitical priesthood, (for under it the people received the law,) what further need was there that another priest should rise after the order of Melchisedec, and not be called after the order of Aaron?
For the TG Priesthood, History of.priesthood being changed, there is made of necessity a change also of the law.
For he of whom these things are spoken pertaineth to another tribe, of which no man gave attendance at the altar.
For it is evident that our Lord sprang out of Isa. 11:1; Micah 5:2. TG Israel, Judah, People of.Juda; of which tribe Moses spake nothing concerning priesthood.
And it is yet far more evident: for that after the similitude of Melchisedec there ariseth another priest,
Who is made, not after the law of a Heb. 9:10.carnal commandment, but after the power of an endless life.
For he testifieth, Thou art a priest for ever after the order of Ps. 110:4.Melchisedec.
For there is verily a disannulling of the commandment going before for the Rom. 8:3.weakness and unprofitableness thereof.
JST Heb. 7:19–21 (Appendix).For the law made nothing Heb. 9:9.perfect, but the bringing in of a Heb. 12:24.better hope did; by the which we draw nigh unto God.
And inasmuch as not without an TG Oath; Priesthood, Oath and Covenant.oath he was made priest:

(For those TG Priesthood, Aaronic.priests were made without an oath; but this with an TG Priesthood, Oath and Covenant.oath by him that said unto him, The Lord sware and will not repent, Thou art a priest for ever after the order of Melchisedec:)
By so much was Jesus made a surety of a better Heb. 8:6.testament.
And they truly were many priests, because they were not suffered to continue by reason of death:
But this man, because he continueth ever, hath an unchangeable TG Jesus Christ, Authority of; Priesthood.priesthood.
Wherefore he is able also to save them GR perfectly.to the uttermost that Isa. 55:3; Heb. 11:6.come unto God by him, seeing he ever liveth to make Isa. 59:16; Rom. 8:34.intercession for them.

JST Heb. 7:25–26 (Appendix).For such an TG High Priest, Melchizedek Priesthood.high priest became us, who is holy, harmless, undefiled, Lev. 3:6. TG Separation.separate from Heb. 4:15; D&C 45:4.sinners, and made TG Priesthood, Magnifying Callings within.higher than the Eph. 1:20.heavens;

Who needeth not Heb. 10:11.daily, as those high priests, to offer up TG Sacrifice.sacrifice, first for his own sins, and then for the people’s: for this he did Rom. 6:10; Heb. 10:10.once, when he TG Jesus Christ, Atonement through; Jesus Christ, Redeemer.offered up himself.

For the law maketh men high priests which have infirmity; but the word of the TG Oath.oath, which was since the law, maketh the TG Jesus Christ, Divine Sonship.Son, who is GR perfected.consecrated for evermore.

div.p: Christ offered Himself as a sacrifice for sin—God promised to make a new covenant with Israel.

chapter.verse: Now of the things which we have spoken this is the sum: We have such an TG High Priest, Melchizedek Priesthood; Jesus Christ, Authority of.high priest, who is set on the right hand of the throne of the Majesty in the heavens;
A minister of the sanctuary, and of the Heb. 9:24.true Ex. 40:2; Heb. 9:11 (11–24).tabernacle, which the Lord pitched, and not man.
For every high priest is ordained to offer gifts and sacrifices: wherefore it is of necessity that this man have somewhat also to Eph. 5:2.offer.

JST Heb. 8:4 Therefore while he was on the earth, he offered for a sacrifice his own life for the sins of the people. Now every priest under the law, must needs offer gifts, or sacrifices, according to the law.For if he were on earth, he should not be a priest, seeing that there are TG Priest, Aaronic Priesthood. BD Priests.priests that offer gifts according to the law:

Who serve unto the example and TG Jesus Christ, Types of, in Anticipation.shadow of heavenly things, as Moses was admonished of God when he was about to make the Ex. 35:11.tabernacle: for, See, saith he, that thou make all things according to the Acts 7:44.pattern shewed to thee in the mount.

But now hath he obtained a more excellent ministry, by how much also he is the TG Jesus Christ, Atonement through; Jesus Christ, Authority of.mediator of a better Heb. 7:22. TG Covenants; Priesthood, Melchizedek.covenant, which was established upon better TG Promise.promises.
For if that first covenant had been Heb. 7:11 (11–16).faultless, then should no place have been sought for the second.
For finding fault with them, he saith, Behold, the days come, saith the Lord, when I will make a new covenant with the house of Israel and with the house of TG Israel, Judah, People of.Judah:

Not according to the TG Law of Moses.covenant that I made with their fathers in the day when I took them by the hand to lead them out of the land of Egypt; because they continued not in my covenant, and I regarded them not, saith the Lord.

For this is the covenant that I will make with the house of Israel after those days, saith the Lord; I will put my TG God, Law of.laws into their TG Mind.mind, and write them in their Jer. 31:33; 2 Cor. 3:3.hearts: and I will be to them a God, and they shall be to me a people:
And they shall not teach every man his neighbour, and every man his brother, saying, Know the Lord: for all shall know me, from the least to the greatest.
For I will be merciful to their D&C 38:14.unrighteousness, and their sins and their iniquities will I TG Forgive.remember no more.

In that he saith, A Heb. 9:15; 3 Ne. 12:47 (46–47); 15:3 (2–10); Ether 13:9. TG New and Everlasting Covenant.new covenant , he hath made the first old. Now that which decayeth and waxeth 2 Cor. 5:17.old is ready to 3 Ne. 12:46 (19, 46).vanish away.

div.p: The Mosaic ordinances prefigured Christ’s ministry—Christ is the Mediator of the new covenant.

chapter.verse: Then verily the first TG Law of Moses. covenant had also TG Ordinance.ordinances of divine TG Service.service, and a worldly sanctuary.

For there was a Ex. 26:1 (1–37); 39:33.tabernacle made; the first, wherein was the Ex. 25:31 (31–39); 26:35.candlestick, and the Ex. 25:23 (23–29); Lev. 24:6.table, and the Ex. 25:30.shewbread; which is called the sanctuary.
And after the second TG Jesus Christ, Types of, in Anticipation; Veil.veil, the tabernacle which is called the GR Holy of Holies.Holiest of all;

Which had the golden censer, and the TG Ark of the Covenant.ark of the covenant overlaid round about with gold, wherein was the golden Ex. 16:33 (33–34).pot that had manna, and Num. 17:10.Aaron’s rod that budded, and the Ex. 25:16 (16, 20, 40).tables of the covenant;
And over it the TG Cherubim.cherubims of glory shadowing the Ex. 25:17.mercyseat; of which we cannot now speak particularly.
Now when these things were thus GR prepared, built.ordained, the TG Priest, Aaronic Priesthood. BD Priests.priests went always into the first tabernacle, accomplishing the service of God.
But into the second went the Heb. 9:25; 10:3.high priest alone Ex. 30:10.once every year, not without blood, which he offered for himself, and for the errors of the people:
The Holy Ghost this signifying, that the way into the holiest of all was not yet made manifest, while as the first tabernacle was yet standing:

Which was a GR similitude, type, parable. TG Jesus Christ, Types of, in Anticipation.figure for the time then present, in which were offered both gifts and sacrifices, that could not make him that did the service Heb. 7:19.perfect, as pertaining to the conscience;
Which stood only in meats and drinks, and divers TG Wash.washings, and Heb. 7:16.carnal TG Ordinance.ordinances, Mosiah 13:29 (27–32).imposed on them until the time of reformation.

But Christ being come an TG High Priest, Melchizedek Priesthood.high priest of good things to come, by a greater and more perfect Mark 14:58; Heb. 8:2.tabernacle, not made with hands, that is to say, not of this building;

Neither by the blood of goats and calves, but by his own Acts 20:28; Heb. 10:10.blood he entered in once into the Lev. 16:2 (2–4); Heb. 10:19.holy place, having obtained eternal D&C 138:23 (18–23). TG Jesus Christ, Redeemer; Redemption.redemption for us.
For if the Lev. 4:5.blood of bulls and of goats, and the ashes of an heifer sprinkling the unclean, sanctifieth to the TG Purification.purifying of the flesh:

How much more shall the TG Blood, Symbolism of.blood of Christ, who through the eternal Spirit offered himself GR blameless.without Lev. 1:3; 22:20.spot to God, TG Purification.purge your TG Conscience.conscience from dead works to serve the living God?

And for this cause he is the TG Jesus Christ, Authority of; Jesus Christ, Mission of.mediator of the Heb. 8:13.new JST Heb. 9:15 … covenant … (Note: JST uses “covenant” in place of “testament” in every instance in vv. 15–18, 20.)testament, that by means of TG Jesus Christ, Death of.death, for the redemption of the TG Transgress.transgressions that were under the first JST Heb. 9:15 … covenant …testament, they which are called might receive the Heb. 10:36.promise of eternal D&C 137:7 (7–10). TG Inheritance.inheritance.

For where a JST Heb. 9:16 … covenant …testament is, there must also of necessity be the Ex. 24:8 (3–8); Matt. 26:28 (26–28). TG Martyrdom.death of the JST Heb. 9:16 … victim. (Note: JST uses “victim” in place of “testator” also in v. 17.) 1 Tim. 2:5–6; D&C 135:5.testator.
For a TG Witness.testament is of force after men are dead: GR since.otherwise it is of no strength at all while the testator liveth.
Whereupon neither the first testament was dedicated without blood.

For when Moses had Ex. 24:7.spoken every precept to all the people according to the law, he took the Ex. 24:6.blood of calves and of goats, with water, and scarlet wool, and hyssop, and Ex. 24:8.sprinkled both the book, and all the people,
Saying, This is the blood of the testament which God hath enjoined unto you.
Moreover he sprinkled with blood both the tabernacle, and all the vessels of the ministry.
And almost all things are by the law purged with blood; and without TG Blood, Symbolism of.shedding of blood is no TG Remission of Sins.remission.
It was therefore necessary that the TG Symbolism.patterns of things in the heavens should be purified with these; but the heavenly things themselves with better sacrifices than these.

For Christ is not entered into the holy places made with hands, which are the figures of the Heb. 8:2.true; but into Heb. 4:14.heaven itself, now to appear in the TG Jesus Christ, Atonement through.presence of God for us:
Nor yet that he should offer himself often, as the Heb. 9:7.high priest entereth into the holy place every year with blood of others;

For then must he often have suffered since the foundation of the world: but now once JST Heb. 9:26 … in the meridian of time hath he …in the GR consummation of the ages.end of the world hath he appeared to put away sin by the TG Self-Sacrifice.sacrifice of himself.
And as it is appointed unto men once to die, but after this the judgment:

So Christ was once TG Jesus Christ, Atonement through; Jesus Christ, Redeemer; Remission of Sins.offered to D&C 76:41.bear the Rom. 6:10.sins of many; and unto them that look for him shall he 2 Pet. 3:12; Rev. 1:7.appear the second time without sin unto salvation.

div.p: We are sanctified by the shedding of the blood of Christ—The superiority of His sacrifice is explained—Those who fall from grace through willful sin are damned—The just will live by faith.

chapter.verse: For the Mosiah 3:15 (14–15); 13:28 (28–32).law having a TG Jesus Christ, Types of, in Anticipation; Symbolism.shadow of good things to come, and not the very image of the things, can never with those sacrifices which they offered JST Heb. 10:1 … continually year by year make …year by year continually make the comers thereunto perfect.
For then would they not have ceased to be offered? because that the worshippers once TG Remission of Sins.purged should have had no more TG Peace of God.conscience of sins.
But in those TG Sacrifice. sacrifices there is a Lev. 16:21; Heb. 9:7.remembrance again made of sins every year.
For it is not possible that the blood of bulls and of goats should take away sins.
Wherefore when he cometh into the world, he saith, Sacrifice and offering thou wouldest not, but a body hast thou prepared me:
In burnt offerings and 1 Sam. 15:22; Micah 6:7. sacrifices for sin thou hast had no pleasure.
Then said I, Lo, I come (in the volume of the book it is written of me,) to do thy will, O God.
Above when he said, Sacrifice and offering and burnt offerings and offering for sin thou wouldest not, neither hadst pleasure therein; which are offered by the law;
Then said he, Lo, I come to do thy will, O God. He taketh away the first, that he may establish the second.

By the which will we are TG Sanctification.sanctified through the TG Jesus Christ, Types of, in Anticipation.offering JST Heb. 10:10 … once of the body of Jesus Christ.of the John 6:51; Heb. 9:12.body of Jesus Christ Rom. 6:10.once for all.
And every priest standeth Heb. 7:27.daily ministering and offering oftentimes the same sacrifices, which can never take away Lev. 4:20 (20, 26).sins:
But this man, after he had offered one TG Sacrifice; Self-Sacrifice.sacrifice for sins for ever, sat down on the right hand of God;
JST Heb. 10:13 From henceforth to reign until his enemies …From henceforth expecting till his enemies be made his footstool.
For by one offering he hath perfected for ever them that are sanctified.
Whereof the Heb. 3:7.Holy Ghost also is a TG Holy Ghost, Mission of; Witness.witness to us: for after that he had said before,

This is the Isa. 59:21 (20–21); Rom. 11:27; D&C 49:9 (5–9).covenant that I will make with them after those days, saith the Lord, I will put my laws into their hearts, and in their Jer. 31:31–33.minds will I write them;
And their sins and iniquities will I remember no more.
Now where TG Remission of Sins.remission of these is, there is no more offering for sin.
Having therefore, brethren, GR license, authority.boldness to enter into the Lev. 16:2 (2–4); Heb. 9:12.holiest by the blood of Jesus,
By a new and living way, which he hath consecrated for us, through the TG Veil.veil, that is to say, his flesh;
And having an TG High Priest, Melchizedek Priesthood.high priest over the house of God;

Let us TG God, Presence of.draw TG God, Access to.near with a true heart in full assurance of Heb. 6:12 (10–12); 1 Pet. 1:5 (3–8).faith, having our hearts GR purified.sprinkled from an evil TG Conscience.conscience, and our bodies washed with pure water.
Let us hold fast the 1 Tim. 6:12 (12–13).profession of our GR hope.faith without wavering; (for he is faithful that promised;)
And let us GR understand.consider one another to TG Provoking.provoke unto love and to good works:
Not forsaking the TG Assembly for Worship; Meetings.assembling of ourselves together, as the manner of some is; but exhorting one another: and so much the more, as ye see the day approaching.
For if we TG Apostasy of Individuals; Sin.sin TG Holy Ghost, Unpardonable Sin against.wilfully after that we have received the knowledge of the truth, there remaineth no more sacrifice for sins,
But a certain fearful looking for of judgment and fiery TG God, Indignation of.indignation, which shall devour the adversaries.
He that GR rejected, violated.despised Moses’ law died without mercy under two or three witnesses:

Of how much sorer Heb. 2:3.punishment, suppose ye, shall he be thought worthy, who hath trodden under foot the Son of God, and hath counted the blood of the covenant, wherewith he was sanctified, an unholy thing, and hath done GR insult.despite unto the Spirit of grace?
For we know him that hath said, TG Retribution.Vengeance belongeth unto me, I will TG God, Justice of.recompense, saith the Lord. And again, The Lord shall TG Jesus Christ, Judge.judge his people.
It is a fearful thing to fall into the hands of the living God.
But call to remembrance the former days, in which, after ye were illuminated, ye endured a great Heb. 12:4.fight of afflictions;
Partly, whilst ye were made a gazingstock both by reproaches and afflictions; and partly, whilst ye became companions of them that were so used.
For ye had compassion of me in my bonds, and took TG Joy.joyfully the spoiling of your goods, knowing in yourselves that ye have in heaven a better and an enduring Matt. 6:20.substance.
Cast not away therefore your confidence, which hath great recompence of reward.
For ye have need of TG Patience.patience, that, after ye have done the TG God, Will of.will of God, ye might receive the Heb. 9:15; 1 Pet. 1:9 (1–16).promise.
For yet a little while, and he that shall come will come, and will not tarry.
Now the just shall live by faith: but if any man TG Apostasy of Individuals.draw back, my soul shall have no pleasure in him.
But we are not of them who 2 Pet. 2:21 (20–21).draw back unto GR ruin, destruction. TG Death, Spiritual, Second; Sons of Perdition.perdition; but of them that believe to the saving of the soul.

div.p: By faith we understand the word and work of God—The faith of the ancients was centered in Christ—By faith, men subdued kingdoms, wrought righteousness, and worked miracles.

chapter.verse: Now TG Faith.faith is the GR assurance, basis, foundation. JST Heb. 11:1 … assurance of things hoped for …substance of things TG Hope.hoped for, the GR proof.evidence of things not seen.
For by it the elders GR received witness, testimony.obtained a good Heb. 11:39.report.
Through faith we understand that the worlds were TG Creation.framed by the TG Jesus Christ, Creator.word of God, so that things which are seen were not made of things which do appear.

By faith Gen. 4:4.Abel offered unto God a more excellent TG Sacrifice.sacrifice than Cain, by which he obtained witness that he was righteous, God testifying of his gifts: and by it he being dead yet speaketh.

By faith Jude 1:14 (14–16).Enoch was TG Translated Beings; Zion.translated that he should not see death; and was not found, because God had translated him: for before his translation he had this TG Testimony.testimony, that he pleased God.

But without TG Faith.faith it is impossible to please him: for he that Heb. 7:25.cometh to God must believe that he is, and that he is a TG Reward.rewarder of them that TG Diligence.diligently Ps. 34:4 (4, 6, 10); Lam. 3:25; Alma 37:37 (36–37).seek him.

By faith Gen. 6:22; 8:1; 1 Pet. 3:20 (20–21); 2 Pet. 2:5.Noah, being TG Warn.warned of God of things not seen as yet, GR being cautious, reverent.moved with fear, prepared an ark to the saving of his house; by the which he condemned the world, and became heir of the TG Righteousness.righteousness which is by faith.

By faith Gen. 12:1.Abraham, when he was called to go out into a place which he should after receive for an TG Lands of Inheritance.inheritance, TG Obedience.obeyed; and he went out, not 2 Chr. 20:12; 1 Ne. 4:6.knowing whither he went.

By faith he Gen. 26:3.sojourned in the TG Israel, Land of; Promised Lands.land of D&C 38:18.promise, as in a strange country, dwelling in Gen. 25:27.tabernacles with Isaac and Jacob, the heirs with him of the same promise:
For he looked for a D&C 76:66.city which hath foundations, whose builder and maker is God.
Through faith also Sara herself received strength to conceive seed, and was delivered of a child when she was past age, because she judged him faithful who had promised.
Therefore sprang there even of one, and him as good as dead, so many as the stars of the sky in TG Seed of Abraham.multitude, and as the sand which is by the sea shore innumerable.

These all died in faith, not having received the Matt. 13:17; 1 Pet. 1:10 (10–11).promises, but having seen them afar off, and were persuaded of them, and embraced them, and confessed that they were TG Stranger.strangers and pilgrims on the earth.
For they that say such things declare plainly that they seek a GR homeland, native place.country.
And truly, if they had been mindful of that country from whence they came out, they might have had opportunity to have returned.
But now they desire a better D&C 45:12 (12–14). country , that is, an heavenly: wherefore God is not ashamed to be called their God: for he hath prepared for them a city.
By James 2:21 (21–23).faith Abraham, when he was TG Test.tried, TG Sacrifice.offered up Isaac: and he that had received the TG Promise.promises offered up his only begotten son,
Of whom it was said, That in TG Seed of Abraham.Isaac shall thy TG Abrahamic Covenant.seed be called:
GR Considering.Accounting that God was able to raise him up, even from the dead; from whence also he received him in a figure.
By faith Isaac Gen. 27:27.blessed Jacob and Esau concerning things to come.
By faith Gen. 48:2 (1–2, 5, 16).Jacob, when he was a dying, blessed both the sons of Joseph; and worshipped, leaning upon the top of his staff.
By faith TG Israel, Joseph, People of.Joseph, when he died, made mention of the departing of the children of Israel; and gave commandment concerning his bones.
By faith Ex. 2:2.Moses, when he was born, was hid three months of his parents, because they saw he was a GR handsome.proper child; and they were not afraid of the king’s commandment.
By faith Moses, when he was come to years, refused to be called the Ex. 2:10.son of Pharaoh’s daughter;
Choosing rather to TG Suffering.suffer affliction with the Ex. 2:11 (11–15); Acts 7:23 (22–36).people of God, than to enjoy the pleasures of sin for a season;
Esteeming the TG Reproach.reproach of Christ greater TG Treasure.riches than the treasures in Egypt: for he had respect unto the recompence of the reward.
By faith he forsook Egypt, not fearing the wrath of the king: for he endured, as seeing him who is invisible.
Through faith he kept the Ex. 12:28 (21–28).passover, and the sprinkling of Ex. 12:22 (21–22).blood, lest he that destroyed the firstborn should touch them.
By faith they passed through the TG Israel, Deliverance of.Red sea as by dry land: which the Egyptians assaying to do were drowned.
By faith the Josh. 6:20.walls of Jericho fell down, after they were compassed about seven days.
By James 2:25.faith the harlot Josh. 2:1.Rahab perished not with GR those who were unbelieving, disobedient.them that believed not, when she had received the spies with peace.
And what shall I more say? for the time would fail me to tell of Gedeon, and of Judg. 4:6 (1–24).Barak, and of Samson, and of Judg. 11:6.Jephthae; of David also, and Samuel, and of the prophets:
Who through faith subdued kingdoms, wrought righteousness, obtained TG Promise.promises, stopped the mouths of Dan. 6:22.lions,
Quenched the violence of Dan. 3:27.fire, escaped the edge of the sword, out of weakness were made Joel 3:10; Ether 12:27.strong, waxed valiant in fight, turned to flight the armies of the aliens.
Women received their dead raised to life again: and others were tortured, not accepting TG Deliver.deliverance; that they might obtain JST Heb. 11:35 … the first resurrection;a better resurrection:
And others had trial of TG Cruelty. cruel TG Mocking; Persecution.mockings and scourgings, yea, moreover of bonds and imprisonment:

They were TG Martyrdom; Prophets, Rejection of.stoned, they were sawn asunder, were tempted, were slain with the sword: they wandered about in sheepskins and goatskins; being destitute, afflicted, tormented;
(Of whom the world was not worthy:) they wandered in deserts, and in mountains, and in dens and caves of the earth.
And these all, having obtained a GR testimony, witness.good Heb. 11:2.report through faith, received not the promise:

JST Heb. 11:40 God having provided some better things for them through their sufferings, for without sufferings they could not be made perfect.God having GR provided beforehand.provided some better thing for us, that they without us should not be made TG Perfection.perfect.

div.p: Whom the Lord loves He chastens—God is the Father of spirits—To see God, follow peace and holiness—Exalted Saints belong to the Church of the Firstborn.

chapter.verse: Wherefore seeing we also are compassed about with so great a cloud of witnesses, let us lay aside every weight, and the TG Sin.sin which doth so easily Rom. 7:21 (15–25); 2 Ne. 4:18 (17–19).beset us, and let us run with TG Patience; Perseverance.patience the race that is set before us,

Looking unto Jesus the TG Jesus Christ, Authority of; Jesus Christ, Messiah.author and GR one who completes, perfects.finisher of our faith; who for the joy that was set before him 1 Pet. 1:11.endured the cross, despising the TG Shame.shame, and is set down at the right hand of the Heb. 1:3.throne of God.
For consider him that endured such GR rebellion, opposition.contradiction of sinners against himself, lest ye be wearied and faint in your minds.

And this word, Yet once more, signifieth the removing of those things that are shaken, as of things that are made, that those things which cannot be shaken may remain.

For our God is a consuming fire.

div.p: Marriage is honorable—Christ is the same everlastingly—Paul explains how the Saints are to offer acceptable sacrifices.

Be not 2 Cor. 11:3; Col. 2:8.carried about with divers and strange Eph. 4:14.doctrines. For it is a good thing that the heart be established with grace; not with meats, which have not profited them that have been occupied therein.

TG Sustaining Church Leaders.Obey them that have the rule over you, and TG Submissiveness.submit yourselves: for they watch for your souls, as they that must give account, that they may do it with joy, and not with grief: for that is unprofitable for you.

Know ye that our brother Timothy is set at liberty; with whom, if he come shortly, I will see you.
Salute all them that have the rule over you, and all the saints. They of Italy salute you.
Grace be with you all. Amen.

div.p: If any of you lack wisdom, let him ask of God—Resist temptation—Be doers of the word—James explains how to recognize pure religion.

Every Ps. 85:12.good Moro. 7:12 (12–13).gift and every perfect John 3:27; 2 Cor. 9:15. TG God, Gifts of.gift is from above, and D&C 67:9.cometh down from the D&C 67:4, 9.Father of lights, with whom is no TG God, Perfection of.variableness, neither shadow of turning.

div.p: God has chosen the poor of this world who are rich in faith—Salvation is gained by keeping the whole law—Faith without works is dead.

And ye have respect to him that weareth the GR splendid.gay clothing, and say unto him, Sit thou here in a good place; and say to the poor, Stand thou there, or sit here under my footstool:

Likewise also was not Josh. 2:1.Rahab the harlot Heb. 11:31.justified by works, when she had received the messengers, and had sent them out another way?

div.p: By governing the tongue, we gain perfection—Heavenly wisdom is pure, peaceable, and full of mercy.

Behold also the ships, which though they be so great, and are driven of fierce winds, yet are they turned about with a very small helm, whithersoever the GR helmsman, pilot.governor listeth.

And the Prov. 16:27; Mark 7:20 (19–23); Alma 12:14; D&C 88:121.tongue is a fire, a world of iniquity: so is the tongue among our members, that it defileth the whole body, and setteth on fire the course of nature; and it is set on fire of hell.
For every kind of beasts, and of birds, and of serpents, and of things in the sea, is tamed, and hath been tamed of mankind:
But the TG Gossip; Profanity.tongue can no man tame; it is an GR untameable, uncontrollable.unruly evil, full of deadly poison.
Therewith bless we God, even the Father; and therewith TG Curse.curse we men, which are made after the TG God, Body of, Corporeal Nature.similitude of God.
Out of the same Matt. 12:34; Luke 6:45. TG Hypocrisy.mouth proceedeth blessing and cursing. My brethren, these things ought not so to be.
Doth a GR spring, well. Moro. 7:11 (6–11).fountain send forth at the same place sweet water and bitter?
Can the fig tree, my brethren, bear olive berries? either a vine, figs? so can no fountain both yield salt water and fresh.

But if ye have bitter envying and TG Strife.strife in your hearts, GR do not assume superiority over.glory not, and lie not against the truth.

For where TG Envy.envying and TG Contention.strife is, there is confusion and every evil work.

But the TG God, Intelligence of; Wisdom.wisdom that is from above is first pure, then peaceable, gentle, and GR pliant, easily persuaded.easy to be entreated, full of TG God, Mercy of.mercy and good fruits, without partiality, and without D&C 121:42.hypocrisy.

div.p: Wars are born of lusts—The friends of the world are the enemies of God—Sin is failure to walk in the light we have received.

Ye TG Prayer.ask, and receive not, because ye Hel. 10:5 (4–6).ask GR wickedly, wrongly. Rom. 8:26 (26–27); D&C 46:30 (28–30); 88:65 (64–65).amiss, that ye may GR waste, expend. D&C 46:9.consume it upon your TG Lust.lusts.

Ye TG Whore.adulterers and adulteresses, know ye not that the friendship of the TG World; Worldliness.world is TG Opposition.enmity with God? whosoever therefore will be a friend of the world is the TG Enemies.enemy of God.
Do ye think that the scripture saith in vain, The spirit that dwelleth in us lusteth to TG Envy.envy?

TG Reconciliation; Submissiveness.Submit yourselves therefore to God. TG Abstain; Self-Mastery.Resist the TG Devil.devil, and he will flee from you.

GR Endure hardship, Suffer harassment.Be afflicted, and mourn, and Isa. 22:12; Joel 2:17; 2 Cor. 7:10.weep: let your laughter be turned to TG Mourning.mourning, and your joy to heaviness.
TG Contrite Heart; Humility.Humble yourselves in the sight of the Lord, and he shall lift you up.

TG Gossip.Speak not evil one of another, brethren. He that speaketh evil of his brother, and judgeth his brother, speaketh evil of the TG Citizenship.law, and judgeth the law: but if thou judge the law, thou art not a doer of the law, but a judge.

Go to now, ye that say, To day or to Prov. 27:1.morrow we will go into such a city, and continue there a year, and buy and sell, and get gain:
Whereas ye know not what shall be on the morrow. For what is your life? It is even a 1 Cor. 7:31 (29–31); Jacob 7:26.vapour, that appeareth for a little time, and then vanisheth away.
For that ye ought to say, If the Lord will, we shall live, and do this, or that.
But now ye rejoice in your TG Boast.boastings: all such rejoicing is evil.
Therefore to him that Luke 12:47; 2 Ne. 9:25 (25–27).knoweth to do good, and TG Disobedience; Good Works; Obedience.doeth it not, to him it is TG Sin.sin.

div.p: Misery awaits the wanton rich—Await the Lord’s coming with patience—The elders are to anoint and heal the sick.

Your TG Treasure.riches are corrupted, and your garments are motheaten.

Your 1 Tim. 6:10 (10, 17); Jacob 2:17 (17–19).gold and silver is GR rusted, tarnished. D&C 56:16.cankered; and the GR venom, poison.rust of them shall be a witness against you, and shall eat your flesh as it were fire. Ye have heaped treasure together for the last days.

Be patient therefore, brethren, unto the coming of the Lord. Behold, the husbandman waiteth for the precious fruit of the earth, and hath long patience for it, until he receive the early and latter rain.

Behold, we count them TG Happiness.happy which TG Perseverance; Steadfastness.endure. Ye have heard of the patience of Job 1:1.Job, and have seen the end of the Lord; that the Lord is very TG Compassion.pitiful, and of tender TG God, Mercy of; Kindness.mercy.

Is any among you TG Affliction.afflicted? let him pray. Is any merry? let him sing psalms.

And the D&C 104:80.prayer of Matt. 15:28 (25–28). TG Faith.faith shall save the sick, and the Lord shall raise him up; and if he have committed sins, they shall be Mark 2:5 (3–9). TG Forgive.forgiven him.

TG Confession.Confess your faults one to another, and TG Benevolence.pray one for another, that ye may be healed. Ex. 33:17.The effectual Jer. 29:13; 1 Ne. 1:5 (5–8); 2 Ne. 4:24 (23–25); Moro. 7:9.fervent TG Prayer.prayer of a righteous man availeth much.

Elias was a man subject to like Acts 14:15 (11–15).passions as we are, and he prayed earnestly that it might not rain: and it TG Drought.rained not on the earth by the space of three years and six months.
And he prayed again, and the heaven gave rain, and the earth brought forth her fruit.
Brethren, if any of you do err from the truth, and one convert him;

Let him know, that he which D&C 6:11. TG Conversion.converteth the sinner from the error of his way shall 1 Tim. 4:16.save a soul from death, and shall See JST 1 Pet. 4:8 (1 Pet. 4:8 note a).hide a multitude of sins.

div.p: The trial of our faith precedes salvation—Christ was foreordained to be the Redeemer.

Who are kept by the Rom. 1:16.power of God through Heb. 10:22 (22–24); 1 Pet. 1:21 (21–22).faith unto salvation GR prepared.ready to be revealed in the last time.

Whom having not seen, ye love; in whom, though now ye see him not, yet John 20:29.believing, ye rejoice with joy unspeakable and full of glory:

Unto whom it was revealed, that not unto themselves, but unto us they did minister the things, which are now reported unto you by them that have TG Holy Ghost, Mission of.preached the gospel unto you with the Holy Ghost sent down from heaven; which things the TG Angels.angels desire to look into.

But as he which hath called you is holy, so be ye 1 Thes. 2:12.holy in all manner of 2 Pet. 3:11; D&C 136:24 (23–24).conversation;
Because it is written, Be ye holy; for I am 1 Jn. 3:3.holy.
And if ye call on the Father, who without respect of persons judgeth according to every man’s TG Good Works.work, pass the time of your sojourning here in TG Reverence.fear:

Forasmuch as ye know that ye were not 1 Cor. 6:20. TG Jesus Christ, Redeemer.redeemed with corruptible things, as silver and gold, from your GR erroneous, fruitless conduct.vain conversation received by TG Traditions of Men.tradition from your fathers;

But with the precious TG Jesus Christ, Atonement through.blood of Christ, as of a TG Jesus Christ, Lamb of God; Jesus Christ, Types of, in Anticipation; Passover.lamb without Lev. 1:3; 3:6; 22:20; Deut. 15:21.blemish and without spot:

GR Having been foreknown before the foundation.Who verily was TG Foreordination; Jesus Christ, Authority of; Jesus Christ, Foreordained; Salvation, Plan of.foreordained before the foundation of the world, but was manifest in these last times for you,
Who by him do believe in God, that raised him up from the dead, and gave him Acts 3:13 (13–15).glory; that your Gal. 5:5 (5–6); 1 Pet. 1:5 (3–8).faith and TG Hope.hope might be in God.

Seeing ye have TG Purification; Purity.purified your TG Soul.souls in obeying the truth through the Spirit unto unfeigned Moro. 8:26 (25–26).love of the brethren, see that ye TG Love.love one another with a pure heart fervently:
Being TG Holy Ghost, Baptism of.born again, not of corruptible seed, but of incorruptible, by the word of God, which liveth and TG Steadfastness.abideth for ever.
For all TG Mortality.flesh is as grass, and all the glory of man as the flower of grass. The grass withereth, and the flower thereof falleth away:
But the Ps. 119:89.word of the Lord endureth for ever. And this is the word which by the gospel is preached unto you.

div.p: Converts are newborn babes in Christ—He is the chief cornerstone—Saints hold a royal priesthood and are a peculiar people—Saints are in subjection to the laws of man.

chapter.verse: Wherefore laying aside all TG Malice.malice, and all TG Guile.guile, and hypocrisies, and envies, and all evil TG Backbiting; Slander.speakings,
As TG Man, New, Spiritually Reborn.newborn babes, desire the GR pure, genuine.sincere 1 Cor. 3:2 (2–3); Heb. 5:13 (13–14); Mosiah 3:19.milk of the word, that ye may grow thereby:
If so be ye have GR experienced. Heb. 6:5 (4–6); Alma 36:26 (24–26).tasted that the Lord is gracious.
To whom coming, as unto a living stone, disallowed indeed of men, but D&C 50:44.chosen of God, and precious,

Unto you therefore which believe he is precious: but unto them which be TG Disobedience.disobedient, the stone which the builders Matt. 21:42 (42–46); Luke 9:22.disallowed, the same is made the head of the corner,

And a 1 Cor. 1:18 (18–24).stone of Isa. 28:16; 2 Ne. 18:14 (13–15).stumbling, and a TG Rock.rock of TG Offense.offence, even to them which Matt. 21:44 (33–46).stumble at the word, being disobedient: whereunto also they were Rom. 9:22 (9–23); 1 Thes. 5:9.appointed.

But ye are a TG Election.chosen generation, a TG Mission of Early Saints; Mission of Latter-day Saints.royal TG Priesthood; Priesthood, Melchizedek.priesthood, an Ex. 19:6. TG Israel, Blessings of.holy TG Nations.nation, a GR purchased, preserved; note that in Ex. 19:5 the Hebrew word is segullah, meaning “special possession or property.” TG Israel, Mission of; Modesty; Peculiar People.peculiar people; that ye should shew forth the praises of him who hath called you out of TG Walking in Darkness.darkness into his marvellous TG Light [noun].light:
Which in time past were not a people, but are now the people of God: which had not obtained mercy, but now have obtained mercy.
Dearly beloved, I beseech you as TG Stranger.strangers and GR resident aliens, sojourners.pilgrims, TG Abstain.abstain from fleshly TG Lust.lusts, which TG War.war against the soul;

Having your conversation TG Children of Light; Honesty.honest among the Gentiles: that, whereas they speak against you as evildoers, they may by your good Matt. 5:16 (14–16); Titus 2:8.works, which they shall behold, glorify God in the day of visitation.
TG Citizenship; Submissiveness.Submit yourselves to every TG Governments.ordinance of man for the Lord’s sake: whether it be to the king, as GR superior.supreme;
Or unto governors, as unto them that are sent by him for the punishment of evildoers, and for the praise of them that do well.
For so is the will of God, that with well doing ye may put to TG Silence.silence the TG Ignorance.ignorance of foolish men:
As free, and not using your TG Liberty.liberty for a cloak of TG Malice.maliciousness, but as the TG Servant.servants of God.
TG Respect.Honour all men. Love the TG Brotherhood and Sisterhood.brotherhood. Prov. 24:21.Fear God. Honour the TG Citizenship; Kings, Earthly.king.
Col. 3:22; 1 Tim. 6:1 (1–2); Titus 2:9 (9–10).Servants, be subject to your Eph. 6:5 (5–8).masters with all fear; not only to the good and gentle, but also to the GR crooked, wicked.froward.
For this is GR pleasing, gracious.thankworthy, if a man for conscience toward God endure TG Tribulation.grief, suffering wrongfully.

For what glory is it, if, when ye be buffeted for your faults, ye shall take it D&C 122:7 (7–8).patiently? but if, when ye do well, and TG Forbear; Suffering.suffer for it, ye take it Alma 12:24. TG Patience.patiently, this is GR pleasing, gracious.acceptable with God.

For even hereunto were ye called: because Christ also TG Jesus Christ, Atonement through; Redemption; Self-Sacrifice.suffered for us, leaving us an TG Example; Jesus Christ, Exemplar.example, that ye should follow his steps:
Who did no Isa. 53:9. TG Sin.sin, neither was TG Guile; Sincere.guile found in his mouth:

Who, when he was Isa. 53:3 (3–6). TG Jesus Christ, Trials of; Retribution; Reviling.reviled, reviled not again; when he suffered, he threatened not; but TG Commitment.committed himself to him that judgeth righteously:

Who his own self TG Accountability; Self-Sacrifice.bare our TG Jesus Christ, Atonement through; Jesus Christ, Redeemer.sins in his own body on the tree, that we, being dead to sins, should live unto righteousness: by whose Isa. 53:5.stripes ye were healed.
For ye were as TG Sheep.sheep going astray; but are now returned unto the TG Jesus Christ, Good Shepherd; Shepherd.Shepherd and TG Bishop.Bishop of your souls.

div.p: Husbands and wives should honor each other—Saints should live by gospel standards—Christ preached to the spirits in prison.

While they behold your TG Chastity.chaste JST 1 Pet. 3:2 … conduct …conversation coupled with fear.

For after this manner in the old time the holy TG Woman.women also, who TG Trust in God.trusted in God, adorned themselves, being GR obedient, submissive to.in subjection unto their own husbands:
Even as Sara obeyed Abraham, calling him lord: whose TG Seed of Abraham.daughters ye are, as long as ye do well, and are not afraid with any GR dismay, consternation.amazement.

Likewise, ye TG Family; Marriage, Husbands.husbands, dwell with them according to D&C 121:42.knowledge, giving TG Marriage, Continuing Courtship in.honour unto the TG Marriage, Wives; Woman.wife, as unto the weaker vessel, and as being TG Marriage, Celestial.heirs together of the grace of life; that your TG Prayer.prayers be not hindered.

Not TG Retribution.rendering TG Forbear.evil for evil, or railing for railing: but contrariwise blessing; knowing that ye are thereunto called, that ye should inherit a TG Blessing.blessing.

Let him GR turn away from, avoid.eschew evil, and do good; let him seek TG Peacemakers; Peace of God.peace, and GR pursue, follow eagerly.ensue it.

But and if ye Luke 6:22; James 1:12 (8–16). TG Suffering.suffer for TG Righteousness.righteousness’ sake, TG Happiness.happy are ye: and be not afraid of their terror, neither be troubled;

But GR reverence as holy.sanctify the Lord God in your hearts: and be ready always to give GR a defense.an answer to every man that asketh you a reason of the TG Hope.hope that is in you with TG Meek.meekness and GR reverence, awe.fear:
